So the problem, I think, sometimes is that patience and a plan or a strategy doesn’t come into effect. It just thinks, ‘I’m supposed to get this right now.’ And at some point, maybe you make that decision where it’s not going to work out here. Maybe I need to go som

item.title: Jaylen Brown: To me, the biggest misconception is that sports and politics don’t coexist
item.description: “What’s the biggest misconception from a fan about being an NBA player?” Jaylen Brown: “To me, the biggest misconception is that sports and politics don’t coexist. I think most people — I think the average mind thinks, ‘Oh, it’s just sports. It’s not connected to anything. It’s just entertainment.’ So untrue. So untrue. I’ve sat in a million meetings. We’re not supposed to discuss the discretion of what those meetings are, or their topics of conversation, but I will say the partners of our leagu

item.title: Jaylen Brown: Sometimes I wake up feeling like I’m a pawn meant to divert the attention from what’s actually going on
item.description: Jaylen Brown: “Sports is extremely political, whether people realize it or not. Our game, in a sense, is a means of entertainment. And it almost, at times, serves as a distraction from the other stuff that’s going on around in our country. Sometimes I wake up feeling like I’m a pawn, because I’m just a means to divert the attention from what’s actually going on. There are conversations that we’re not having that we should be having right now. But it’s the Super Bowl, it’s All-Star Game. We’re us

item.title: Danny Ainge asked Jaylen Brown: How can you say that sports are a mechanism for control?
item.description: Jaylen Brown: I was in Boston, and I’m thinking I’m talking to a normal beat writer. It happened to be this big writer for The Guardian. And this dude put me on the cover of The Guardian newspaper saying, ‘Jaylen Brown says sports is a mechanism for control.’ That’s when I was 19 years old. Then that went viral. That’s when I got invited to Harvard to go speak about it. So Harvard invited me, based on that article in The Guardian, to come speak about it. That’s why I became the youngest Harvard

item.title: Adam Silver on tanking: There's no question what we saw this season was unprecedented
item.description: Commissioner Silver: “In terms of the tanking, there's no question that what we saw this season was unprecedented. You talked a lot about it. Your colleagues have talked a lot about it. Again, I think a bit of a group think there, obviously by definition it can't be successful for all the teams that were engaging in that strategy. But on the other hand, the analytics, which all the sports are using today, suggest that it's potentially a winning strategy, and that is to race to the bottom in orde

item.title: You either gotta be great or you gotta let yourself …
item.description: Silver: “I think there's a bit of a perfect storm this year. You have a perception of a very deep draft class, which it appears to be, great players coming into the league. And then even fans in particular cities and even media in those cities to a certain degree were rooting for their teams to tank and saying, ‘how well can your team be managed if you're sort of just in the middle? You either gotta be great or you gotta let yourself get bad.’ And I think we had to respond not just to the narrat

item.title: “To the credit of our teams, I think in record time …
item.description: Silver: “To the credit of our teams, I think in record time they came together this season and said, alright, we're gonna fix this for beginning next season. I guess some people could have said, you could have fixed it for this draft, but I don't think it would've been fair to change the rules of the road in the middle of a season.”

item.title: “What we've essentially done, and we have a proposal …
item.description: Silver: “What we've essentially done, and we have a proposal that we're going to be bringing to our team owners at the end of May, and that is to create essentially a system of flat odds, so that you have no particular incentive to be bad. There's even something we're calling draft relegation, that if you're one of the bottom three teams in the league, you'll actually have worse odds than teams that sort of are four through up until teams make the playoffs. We're still playing a little bit with

item.title: “And also ultimately additional authority for the …
item.description: Silver: “And also ultimately additional authority for the league office that if we do see that type of behavior where there's a sense that teams aren't going all out to win, that we can actually take away draft lottery balls, we can change the order of the draft, that teams have to know it's not just about paying a financial fine, which they may think is worth it in order to get a top pick, but that it'll directly impact their ability to get a top draft pick.”

item.title: “The system that we're going to be putting in front of …
item.description: Silver: “The system that we're going to be putting in front of our teams will be in effect for three years. What we're telling our teams is, the term people use is it will ‘sunset.’ In other words, this is going to be a new flat-odd system. It'll be in place for three years and will give us time for additional study to see whether there are other creative ways to better distribute players. And it'll also give us an opportunity to see how our teams respond to the system, because one thing I've le

item.title: Adam Silver 'very satisfied' with NBA's 65-game rule
item.description: On the league’s 65-game rule: Stephen A.: “How satisfied are you with the early results of the NBA’s 65-game rule tied to awards eligibility?” Commissioner Silver: “I'm very satisfied. So the issue again was, going into the last collective bargaining negotiation, roughly a third of our All-NBA players had played fewer than 65 games, fewer than 80% of the games. And so the view was, and remember this was a provision that was put in place through a negotiation with the Players Association, everyon

item.title: “We always knew whatever line we drew that there would …
item.description: Silver: “We always knew whatever line we drew that there would be players that would fall on the other side of it, the low end of it and there might be a sense of unfairness, which is why we put in place a provision around extraordinary circumstances, that if a player missed the 65 games for a game or two, but that there was an unusual reason that something happened over the course of the season, there'd be a process for making an exception. So I'm happy where that came out. I understand a playe

Obviously, we have to prepare for um one of these two teams and then we've talked about the differences between the two and all that, but just focusing on what makes us the best thing we can be as well.

He was rising sophomore and I’m a rising senior, he supposed to be this great point guard and he never passed me the ball at Nike camp. And then they beat us in the legendary tournament matchup where Cal beat

item.title: McMenamin on LeBron: I did not get the sense it's max or bust
item.description: Dave McMenamin: I did not get the sense in my reporting for this story that it’s max or bust for LeBron. Certainly, no. However, I don’t know what the number is. The Lakers, I would think, would be better served here making the initial offer be one that is received well and feels like it comes with a level of respect, and also just a level of interest in wanting him to be part of their team. You know, part of the story is that LeBron hasn’t felt that way all that much since last February.

item.title: LeBron would be open to discussing where his number …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: I think, based on what we saw in 2024, LeBron would be open to discussing where his number lies, so long as the money that’s left on the table is earmarked for talent that can come and affect winning. That is incumbent upon Rob Pelinka and whomever else he’s working with in the front office between now and whenever that conversation happens with Rich Paul, to line up. That’s what the job is.

item.title: A report stating that Los Angeles Lakers stars Luka …
item.description: A report stating that Los Angeles Lakers stars Luka Doncic and LeBron James don’t get along is not true, sources told Dallas Hoops Journal. Doncic and James have a positive relationship and Doncic wants James to re-sign with the Lakers this summer, sources said. While growing up in Slovenia, Doncic looked up to James and values having him as a teammate, sources said.

item.title: Austin Reaves, who will decline his player option for …
item.description: Austin Reaves, who will decline his player option for next season, is expected to re-sign with the Lakers this summer, sources said. The undrafted guard has a close relationship with James as well and wants James to continue playing for the Purple and Gold if he doesn’t retire from the NBA, sources said.

Okorie, a 6-foot-2 guard, was one of the best freshmen in the country last season and is on track to go from an unranked three-star recruit to a one-and-done first-rounder. item.title: Boozer graduated from Christopher Columbus High School …
item.description: The NBA legacy and Sporting News Player of the Year has shined during his one-and-done college season. But all of his basketball accomplishments might be secondary to what he's done in the classroom. Boozer graduated from Christopher Columbus High School in Miami, Fla. with a 4.88 GPA, which is one of the highest marks set by a student-athlete. It also rivals the 4.86 GPA mark Fernando Mendoza set at CCHS years earlier. Hard to believe two of the best athletes coming out of college in the same y

item.title: Darius Acuff on his NBA ceiling: 'Definitely a superstar point guard for sure'
item.description: After putting in one of the best freshman guard campaigns in college basketball history, Acuff has excelled at this week’s draft combine. Asked what role he envisions for himself at the next level, the self-assured 19-year-old wasn’t shy. “Definitely a superstar point guard for sure,” Acuff said. “Bring a lot of excitement to any city I go to, and just bring a lot of excitement straight from Day 1.” And just how does the Arkansas freshman define a superstar point guard? “A lot of accolades, just

item.title: “Whatever team takes me, I’m good,” said Acuff. …
item.description: The Nets could use any wins — and swagger — Acuff could bring. And there’s a very real chance he’s on the board for them at No. 6. In 11 mock drafts surveyed, Acuff was the most linked to Brooklyn in six of them, including ESPN, Bleacher Report, The Ringer and The Athletic. “Whatever team takes me, I’m good,” said Acuff. “Getting to know [Brooklyn], it was a good experience for sure, just talking to them.”

item.title: “To be honest with you, I don’t really care,” said …
item.description: There have been questions about his smallish size and porous defense. He has helped his stock at the draft combine, finishing first in the three-quarter sprint and measuring 6-2 barefoot with a solid 6-7 wingspan. The measurements matched Damian Lillard, who Acuff emulates and has drawn comparisons to from scouts. Asked by The Post if his solid measurements changed opinions, he shrugged. “To be honest with you, I don’t really care,” said Acuff. “People are going to always have an opinion no matt

item.title: The WNBA and NBA board of governors unanimously …
item.description: The WNBA and NBA board of governors unanimously approved the sale and relocation of the Connecticut Sun to Houston Rockets owner Tilman Fertitta, the league announced Wednesday. The two sides had reached an agreement to sell the team for $300 million in March, according to a person familiar with the decision. The person spoke then on condition of anonymity because of the sensitive nature of the sale. The two sides had to wait for approval from the board of governors to make it official.

item.title: Jayson Tatum: 'I am the definition of what it means to be a mama’s boy'
item.description: Jayson Tatum: Me and my mom are best friends. She was 19 when she had me, and I grew up in a single-parent household my entire life. Seeing my mom struggle to raise me and put food on the table while going to school, while working — she is the most important person that I’m thankful for. I am who I am, and my life turned out the way it did, because of her. When I got drafted, my mom moved to Boston. We lived in the same apartment building, down the hall. We live on the same street now. I am the

item.title: He was born in December. He was literally born 12 …
item.description: Jayson Tatum: Yeah. Having two kids — I was 19 when Deuce was born, and that was the best thing that could have ever happened to me. Craig Melvin: At 19? Jayson Tatum: At 19. Craig Melvin: How so? Jayson Tatum: It wasn’t planned, and it was tough because I was afraid. I was just leaving Duke. I was entering my new career. I was moving to Boston, starting my new life, and now I was about to be a father. He was born in December. He was literally born 12 games into my NBA career. I was nervous. I d

item.title: One thing I’ve been thinking about is skydiving
item.description: Jaylen Brown: One thing I’ve been thinking about is skydiving. I would love to go skydiving over the pyramids in Egypt. I’m afraid of heights. Terrified.

item.title: When you retire, do you think you’ll become a …
item.description: You’re a great example. And you’ve given lectures, y’all, at Harvard. Isn’t that something? And at MIT. So, when you retire, do you think you’ll become a professor? Jaylen Brown: Maybe, perhaps. I don’t know. We’ll see. I feel like I love to teach. It’s kind of in my family line. My mom teaches, and my grandmother did, too.

item.title: Kevin Love: When I had the panic attack, my teammates …
item.description: Kevin Love: When I had the panic attack, my teammates didn’t know what the hell was going on. Then I had another one against Oklahoma City, and nobody knew what was going on, so my teammates turned against me. I didn’t want to share anything because I thought my livelihood would be taken away from me. I thought that it would compromise my entire career, and people wouldn’t trust me. The organization wouldn’t trust me. I’d be looked at as someone who was weak in this professional sports setting.

item.title: I felt like achieving something else, getting another …
item.description: Kevin Love: I always talk about trying to achieve my way out of depression, to achieve my way out of feeling these dark spells that I’d have for weeks and even months, primarily throughout my teens and my twenties. I felt like achieving something else, getting another accolade, or reaching a different height would cure me, or I wouldn’t be depressed anymore. My anxiety would go away, and then all of that would go away. Then my brain would go back to the same level that it was at prior. And it ju

item.title: You don’t feel like you have anybody to reach out to, …
item.description: Kevin Love: I was one of those dark-room, stare-at-the-ceiling-or-the-wall type of guys. In a lot of ways, that was me trying to say, “All right, if I am going to step outside, if I am going to face these 20,000-plus people arenas and be able to give all my energy to this and nothing else, then I need to just sit in a dark room in my bed and make sure that I eat, make sure that I fuel, make sure that I get my treatments, but just be in this dark room by myself with thoughts that were extremely u

item.title: There were a lot of times where I was not hard to read …
item.description: Kevin Love: There were a lot of times where I was not hard to read at all: low energy, no eye contact, shame shivers, didn’t want to talk to anybody, always just walking around perpetually tired, emotionally exhausted, nothing to give outside of putting on a fake facade, which is the most exhausting thing. People talk about playing a character. I was playing a character outwardly, and I wasn’t able to be myself as a person or emotionally. I wasn’t free or authentic in who I was. I wasn’t comfort

item.title: "That's a pretty man. He's a good looking man. I would …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Chuck on Max Strus: "That's a pretty man. He's a good looking man. I would not look him dead in the eyes. I made that mistake with Tom Brady one time. He's a better shooter than Tom Brady" ??

item.title: Rest in peace, Jason
item.description: Pau Gasol: Rest in peace, Jason

item.title: Charles Barkley: "We live in a homophobic society ... …
item.description: [Inside The NBA] While discussing Jason Collins' passing, Charles Barkley: "We live in a homophobic society ... anybody who think we ain't got a bunch of gay players in all sports, they're just stupid."

item.title: Nets name Makar Gevorkian assistant general manager
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Brooklyn Nets have named Makar Gevorkian assistant general manager. Gevorkian was elevated to the role following two seasons as vice president of basketball operations alignment and strategic planning.

item.title: Bulls to hire Stephen Mervis as senior VP of basketball operations
item.description: Shams Charania: The Chicago Bulls are finalizing a deal to hire Orlando Magic assistant GM Stephen Mervis as Senior Vice President of Basketball Operations under new EVP Bryson Graham, sources tell ESPN. Bulls will also hire Brooklyn Nets executive Acie Law as Vice President of Player Personnel.

item.title: Mervis spent 12 years rising in the Magic front …
item.description: Mervis spent 12 years rising in the Magic front office, specializing in strategy and player evaluation. Meanwhile, Law held scouting, draft-focused roles for the Kings and Thunder from 2018-25. He played four NBA seasons, including part of 2009-10 in Chicago, and was teammates with Graham on the men's basketball team at Texas A&M.

item.title: So, yes, you could hear about the framework of a …
item.description: Technically, the Heat isn’t eligible to trade its 2026 first-round pick (No. 13 overall) because NBA rules prevent teams from being without a first-round selection in two consecutive years (the Heat sent a top-14 protected first-round 2027 pick to the Charlotte Hornets in the Rozier trade). But the Heat could pick a player on behalf of another team at No. 13 in the first round of this year’s draft as part of a prearranged trade. This means the Heat and Bucks could agree to a trade prior to next

item.title: "Luka Doncic is starting to get in that territory of …
item.description: First Take: "Luka Doncic is starting to get in that territory of Joel Embiid. When you need him the most, and it comes postseason time, he's not available." 👀 — @KendrickPerkins on if the Lakers should prioritize Luka or LeBron

item.title: Sparks to honor Lisa Leslie with a statue outside Crypto.com arena
item.description: Kendra Andrews: The Sparks announce they will honor Lisa Leslie with a statue outside Crypto.com arena. It will be unveiled on Sept. 20. “To be cemented in Los Angeles, the city that raised me, I couldn’t be more proud to be a role model forever,” Lesli said in a statement.

item.title: Leslie tells Lakers fans they need to appreciate …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Lisa Leslie tells Lakers fans they need to appreciate LeBron James: “I’m a L.A. girl and LeBron filled a huge void for us in L.A. We lost the great Kobe Bryant and to have LeBron come into L.A. and perform the way he has at the level that he has for this amount of time. We have just been so critical of this man who has been nothing but perfection for the Purple and Gold” (Via @FirstTake )

item.title: “We live in a homophobic society, and that’s …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: “We live in a homophobic society, and that’s unfortunate. First of all, anybody who think we ain’t got a bunch of gay players in all sports, they’re just stupid.” - Charles Barkley (h/t @MrBuckBuckNBA )

item.title: ESPN remembers pioneering sports broadcaster Charlie …
item.description: ESPN PR: ESPN remembers pioneering sports broadcaster Charlie Neal, who passed away Wednesday Neal’s long career helped elevate #HBCU football & basketball on television In 2005, he called the first college football game on @ESPNU when it launched

item.title: Kingston Flemings compares his game to Tyrese Maxey and De'Aaron Fox
item.description: So, what was the advice he gave you going into this experience? Kingston Flemings: Yeah, definitely just putting my head down and working and just being me at the end of the day. You can't be someone else. He had a talk with me during the year that I was trying to be to Allen Iverson. Don't be Allen Iverson, be Kingston Flemings. So really just being myself and if myself is going to be good enough for a team and when that team drafts me, I'm going to show them who I am. So that's really what coa

item.title: Darryn Peterson breaks down what he's learning from Devin Booker, Anthony Edwards, SGA, Donovan Mitchell and Dame Lillard
item.description: Q. Some film you're watching of guys that you're trying to emulate at the next level? Darryn Peterson: Devin Booker, Anthony Edwards, Shai,, Donovan Mitchell, and Dame. Q. What are you taking from them particularly? Peterson: Just ways that they score. They all score in different ways. Let's see how they take space and most of them are getting guarded super hard during their game. How they get easy shots, how they get to free throw line.

item.title: An assistant with a stat sheet said they'd led for …
item.description: Kerr asked Khalid to go see whether Steph and Dray wanted to meet when the team landed in Phoenix. They said they did. Right away. Across the room, someone cracked that the chicken sandwiches earlier today had brought them this victory. "The win started at Honor Bar," someone said. "Started at Honor Bar," Kerr said. "You're welcome!" Jacob Rubin crowed. An assistant with a stat sheet said they'd led for only four minutes and six seconds. Everyone laughed and tried to stay in the moment. Steve lo

item.title: Does Bronny actually listen to you? LeBron James: …
item.description: Steve Nash: Does Bronny actually listen to you? LeBron James: No. Does any kid? No. He's 21 years old. Does any kid their parent at that point? No. No. He's a great kid. I don't even try to… I don't give him too much. Listen, I try to lead by example. When my voice is needed, you know, I feel like he'll reach out and he wants it. And other than that, I just hope that I'm doing my part by leading by example, leading by my voice, and he take what he want.

item.title: “He's playing a lot, right? Or as much as he …
item.description: LeBron’s golf game: “He's playing a lot, right? Or as much as he can. He'll play a lot more now that the season's over now, obviously. He's taking instruction, you know, he's got a coaching, and he's just applying the things that have made him great in basketball, like the commitment and the process.”

item.title: Warriors would like to keep 11th pick in upcoming draft, despite trade interest in Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: FullCourtPass: The Warriors are expected to keep the 11th pick of the draft, per @MarcJSpears "The Warriors have that 11th pick and people wondering can they get in the Giannis mix. From what I'm being told today from several people the Warriors like to keep that pick"

item.title: "We're looking forward to welcoming [Joel Embiid] back …
item.description: Austin Krell: Josh Harris: "We're looking forward to welcoming [Joel Embiid] back on our team."

item.title: "I'm a big believer in character and leadership. I'm …
item.description: Austin Krell: "I'm a big believer in character and leadership. I'm looking for a person that embodies those things." - Bob Myers on what he's looking for in the next President of BB Ops

item.title: Josh Harris says there is no issue with the luxury …
item.description: Ky Carlin: Josh Harris says there is no issue with the luxury tax. He added that the front office always has the green light to go into the tax. #Sixers

item.title: Bob Myers says at a Sixers press conference in the …
item.description: Marc Stein: Bob Myers says at a Sixers press conference in the wake of Daryl Morey's dismissal that he will communicate "five to seven days a week" with the new head of basketball operations hired to succeed Morey. The goal, Myers says, is to have a hire in place for the June 23-24 draft.

item.title: “Look, I think Nick had the team played hard, and …
item.description: Ky Carlin: Josh Harris on the job Nick Nurse has done so far: “Look, I think Nick had the team played hard, and there were a lot of things that happened. I think we all saw where there were various rosters that were available and rosters that weren't available.” #Sixers

item.title: “Tuesday was a difficult day for me. I consider Daryl …
item.description: Ky Carlin: Josh Harris: “Tuesday was a difficult day for me. I consider Daryl Morey a friend, and he did a lot of positive things for our franchise. Made the playoffs five or six years, including the conference semifinals.” #Sixers

item.title: Three problems Timberwolves are trying to correct in …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Three problems Timberwolves are trying to correct in Game 6 *Attacking the double teams against Anthony Edwards *Jaden McDaniels ill-timed foul trouble *Julius Randle’s slump (36% FG, eight assists, 18 turnovers in series)

item.title: Jazz owner Ryan Smith on potentially trading up for No. 1 pick to take AJ Dybantsa: Everything should be on the table
item.description: Days after winning the No. 2 pick in the draft lottery, Jazz owner Ryan Smith said "everything should be on the table" when asked directly in an interview with the Deseret News if the organization would trade up to No. 1 to presumably select Dybantsa. "We don't control that," Smith said. "We are trying to win a championship. So everything should be on the table. Austin and Danny (Ainge) have also been known to do some pretty crazy stuff on draft night."

item.title: Utah Jazz owner Ryan Smith on if they will trade up …
item.description: BYUtv Sports Nation: Utah Jazz owner Ryan Smith on if they will trade up for AJ Dybantsa.

item.title: Pistons upgrade Duncan Robinson to questionable for Game 6
item.description: The Pistons have upgraded Duncan Robinson (low back soreness) to questionable for Friday's Game 6 against Cleveland. Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) and Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) are also questionable.

item.title: "We should be graded on the ultimate result of …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Myers on the Jared McCain trade: "We should be graded on the ultimate result of transactions."

item.title: Asked about his and Myers' involvement in the McCain …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Asked about his and Myers' involvement in the McCain trade, Harris says the front office made the recommendation to ownership – including Myers. "We were involved in it. It was part of a bigger plan... We don't know the outcome of that trade right now."

item.title: "I think putting all of the focus on one player is the …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Josh Harris on Joel Embiid: "I think putting all of the focus on one player is the wrong place to be." Harris calls Embiid a "warrior" and says "we're looking forward to welcoming him back on our team."

item.title: Myers says the Sixers' draft history is strong and he …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Myers says the Sixers' draft history is strong and he does not want to mess it up. "Maxey is the ultimate success story in that range," he says of the Sixers' upcoming No. 22 overall pick.

item.title: Harris, asked about fans being thrown out of games for …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Harris, asked about fans being thrown out of games for yelling "SELL THE TEAM" and other frustrations in the city, says "nobody is more frustrated than I am." Says his goal remains "pushing through" the second round and into the NBA Finals. "I'm sweating it really hard."

item.title: "Depth may be more important than it's ever been... We …
item.description: Adam Aaronson: Myers, asked if it remains tenable to still have three max players and build enough depth to win a title, says this year's Sixers clearly could not do it. "Depth may be more important than it's ever been... We have to look at what happened this year and be honest about."

item.title: Bob Myers says he would not work for someone who will …
item.description: Austin Krell: Bob Myers says he would not work for someone who will not allow the moves necessary to improve the team because of luxury tax concerns.

item.title: "I want you to know, no one is more frustrated than me …
item.description: Erin Grugan: Josh Harris to the Sixers fans: "I want you to know, no one is more frustrated than me that we haven't achieved our goals ... We owe it to you and the city to be better."

item.title: "I think no matter what, we have to stay physical, we …
item.description: Dane Moore: Asked Rudy Gobert about the team having had more success with him next Naz Reid than next to Julius Randle thus far in this series, and what can be better in the minutes he plays next to Randle. "I think no matter what, we have to stay physical, we have to stay focused on the gameplan. We have to keep bringing the effort. And then offensively, I think a lot of it is spacing... Keep trusting one another, keep spacing for one another, keep screening for one another, keep running for on

item.title: Go to bed man
item.description: Brandon Jennings: Go to bed man. I torn my Achilles relax 100mill if that don’t happen. Congrats on your longer career and more money!

item.title: Thank you, Jason Collins, for being large -- large of …
item.description: Thank you, Jason Collins, for being large -- large of spirit, large of import, but also large of physical stature. That itself helped. It helped reinforce that gay people, an immutable portion of nature, breathe and thrive most everywhere, even in the merciless zones beneath NBA baskets among bruising collisions and untoward elbows and immovable giants. Thank you for much more, of course. Thank you for making life that much more breathable for people like myself. Thank you for a coming-out in 20

item.title: At one point near the end, you said you had no …
item.description: One week prior, as an "NBA Cares" ambassador, you had spoken to about 20 kids at a clinic in San Antonio on Final Four weekend there, and you had told them about everything from Sally Ride to the goodness of Jerry Sloan to your recent trip to Bhutan to teach basketball. At one point near the end, you said you had no championship rings -- 13 seasons, two NBA Finals, 830 games, 95 playoff games -- but, well, "I'll have a wedding ring in a couple months." The kids had cheered.

item.title: Celtics hire former star point guard Isaiah Thomas as pro and college scout
item.description: Isaiah Thomas, the fearless 5-foot-9-inch point guard who captivated fans at TD Garden and became one of the most beloved Celtics of this century, has rejoined the organization that helped turn him into a superstar. According to league sources, Thomas has been hired as a pro and college scout for the Celtics. He will be based in the Seattle area, his hometown, giving president of basketball operations Brad Stevens reinforcements on the West Coast. The team’s regional scouting staff includes form

item.title: DeMarcus Cousins on LeBron James: I think he's got another 10 years left
item.description: Damien Peters: I asked DeMarcus Cousins his thoughts on potentially seeing LeBron James’ last ever game in the NBA. He gave an incredibly thoughtful and reflective response: “I think he’s got another 10 years left. For a guy who I grew up watching as a kid, I was a part of his camps as a young kid – it will be a sad day. I’m a guy that appreciates LeBron James. I know a lot of people hate him, I’m a guy that appreciates his legacy and what he’s done for the game and young athletes. Creating a bl

item.title: My NBA international scouting friends are sick of the …
item.description: Fran Fraschilla: My NBA international scouting friends are sick of the these young college assistant coaches texting and asking if a certain international player is good enough. They get an answer & the scout doesn’t hear from them for three months. Not a way to develop relationships. 🤷🏼‍♂️🏀👍🏼

item.title: With the title “Sport as a Strategic Asset of the …
item.description: In a new analysis, posted on the 13th of May, the Spanish “Instituto Coordenadas – Governance and applied economics”, a think tank and research institution focused on the interaction between governance and applied economics, continues to poses questions about the impact of the NBA Europe model to European sports. With the title “Sport as a Strategic Asset of the European Union: Lessons from the NBA Europe Case” the paper sums up the public information about the NBA Europe project.

item.title: As Eurohoops reported a few days ago, this is not the …
item.description: It has to be noted however, that the analysis includes also outdated information. According to the paper “the details known so far — entry fees for clubs ranging between €500 million and €1 billion, strategic control shifted outside Europe, distribution of approximately 50% of operating profits to the NBA and its owners, and a semi-closed format with reserved spots — have sparked a debate that goes far beyond sports and enters directly into European economic and industrial policy”. As Eurohoops

item.title: What doesn’t change is the following part of the …
item.description: On the other hand, while the NBA and FIBA would at first own 52% of this new structure, the participating teams would eventually have a majority equity stake following expansion in future years. What doesn’t change is the following part of the analysis: “The NBA has acknowledged that NBA Europe would be built around the best players available after the roughly 450 players already competing in the NBA, suggesting a developmental platform rather than a final destination for elite talent. A strict

item.title: “The decisive question is not whether the project can …
item.description: According to the paper, the conclusion is simple: “The decisive question is not whether the project can be launched, but what would happen if it fails, because in that scenario the economic, sporting, and institutional costs would fall primarily on European basketball and, by extension, on the European sports model itself”.

item.title: Jazz have reached out to Wizards about trading up to take AJ Dybantsa No. 1 overall
item.description: AJ Dybantsa is the top prospect in the draft, according to ESPN’s Jeremy Woo. The Jazz have reached out to the Washington Wizards, who have the No. 1 pick, about potentially trading up to land the former BYU standout, a source said.

item.title: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa met with the Wizards in Chicago during the NBA pre-draft camp. Washington is supposed to visit with “multiple players” in DC for the top pick, source days. Dybantsa also met with Utah, Chicago, Sacramento, Brooklyn, Atlanta, Dallas, Memphis & the Clippers.

item.title: L2M report says there were zero incorrect calls late in Cavaliers-Pistons game, including contact between Jarrett Allen, Ausar Thompson
item.description: Danny Cunningham: The NBA’s Last 2 Minute Report says that there were zero incorrect calls in the final two minutes of regulation and overtime in last night’s #Cavs win over the Pistons.

item.title: “Allen and Thompson legally step to the same spot …
item.description: Chris Fedor: NBA Last Two Minute Report from #Cavs-#Pistons Game 5 shows no missed calls. The review of the final play: “Allen and Thompson legally step to the same spot while pursuing the loose ball [before either player has possession] & both lose their balance from the marginal contact”

item.description: Ramona Shelburne: When I spoke to Darryn Peterson he had a very clear answer on what current player he admired: Devin Booker. Joined NBA Today to discuss with @king .mclure @marcjspears @MJAcostaTV #nba #nbatoday

item.description: Milwaukee Bucks center Myles Turner revealed on his podcast with New York Liberty forward Breanna Stewart that former Bucks head coach Doc Rivers “didn't fine anybody ever” this past season, and that superstar forward Giannis Antetokounmpo was the teammate who was most likely to be late for team events. “Guys were late all the time,” Turner told Stewart on a new episode of “Game Recognize Game” that was released Thursday. “Guys were showing up to film whenever they wanted to show up. Guys were m

item.title: “Oh, that's easy,” he said. “Giannis. Giannis is going …
item.description: Antetokounmpo reportedly had one foot in and one foot out this season with the Bucks. That comes to mind when listening to Turner’s podcast comments. “If the plane took off at 2 o'clock, we weren't leaving till 4:30,” said Turner, who left the Pacers last offseason to sign a four-year deal worth more than $100 million with Milwaukee after it waived Damian Lillard. Turner continued: “I'm being so serious, bro. It was crazy, dawg. Guys were an hour late to the plane. It got to the point where I kn

item.title: Another day getting better and minding our business …
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo: Another day getting better and minding our business 😃🍪🤎

item.title: Warriors losing top assistants, Terry Stotts and Jerry Stackhouse
item.description: Despite the return of longtime head coach Steve Kerr, the Golden State Warriors have changes coming to their coaching staff. The top two assistants, Terry Stotts and Jerry Stackhouse, are departing the franchise, league sources told ESPN on Thursday. Their contracts expired after this past season.

item.title: Stotts just finished his second year as Kerr's lead …
item.description: Stotts just finished his second year as Kerr's lead assistant and offensive coordinator. His nearly three-decade coaching career includes a nine-season run as head coach of the Portland Trail Blazers. Stotts informed Kerr late in the regular season that he didn't intend to return and told ESPN he is leaving the Warriors on good terms and remains open to NBA head coaching opportunities. "I enjoyed my time with Steve, the staff and players," Stotts said. "My two years there were fulfilling. Nothin

item.title: The Warriors are in the early stages of searching for …
item.description: The Warriors are in the early stages of searching for replacements for Stotts and Stackhouse. Former New Orleans Pelicans head coach Willie Green is among the candidates, league sources said. Green started his NBA coaching career with the Warriors in 2016 and spent three seasons as an assistant on Kerr's staff. The two remain close.

item.title: Jamahl Mosley, who coached the Orlando Magic for five …
item.description: Jamahl Mosley, who coached the Orlando Magic for five seasons before he was let go earlier this month, was captured using smelling salts before Orlando’s first Play-In Tournament game against the Philadelphia 76ers last month. “It heightens your awareness,” Mosley said in April. He added it was only his second time using them.

item.title: Sixers owner on Knicks fans taking over Philly: We did everything we could, including buying tickets to keep them out
item.description: This was what happened in the 76ers-Knicks series, as New York took over Xfinity Mobile Arena in Game 4 with chants of “Knicks in four”. This was jarring to hear, as it did not seem like Philly was playing at home at all. 76ers owner Josh Harris made his feelings regarding the matter perfectly clear in a press conference on Thursday night. “Obviously I didn’t like it. It’s our homecourt. We did everything we could, including buying tickets to keep the [Knicks] fans out. It’s on us. The fans supp

item.title: Sixers owner Josh Harris "Obviously I didn't like …
item.description: New York Basketball: Q "How did it feel sitting…with all the Knicks fan that took over?" Sixers owner Josh Harris "Obviously I didn't like it…We did everything we could…to keep the fans out…Let's face it–we got swept–so…there were some Knicks fans…Our job's to come back & win that series next year"

item.title: Knicks playoff run could generate $140 million for Madison Square Garden Sports
item.description: The New York Knicks have punched their ticket to the Eastern Conference finals for the second season in a row after last playing in the ECF in 2000, producing a windfall for Madison Square Garden Sports, which owns the club and the NHL’s Rangers. The Knicks have already played five playoff home games, which generated nearly $50 million in gross revenue before the NBA took its cut from ticket receipts. The team is likely in line for at least five more home games, as the heavy favorite versus whoe

item.title: He estimates the Knicks earned $8 million per home …
item.description: The Knicks generated $115 million from the playoffs last year, according to Seaport Research Partners analyst David Joyce, who covers the publicly traded MSGS. He estimates the Knicks earned $8 million per home game during the first round of the 2026 playoffs from tickets, suites, concessions and merchandise. It jumped to $12 million per game in the second round when the Knicks swept the Philadelphia 76ers.

item.title: AC Milan owner Gerry Cardinale explained why he sees …
item.description: AC Milan owner Gerry Cardinale explained why he sees the NBA Europe project as a major opportunity for the Italian football giant. Speaking to La Gazzetta dello Sport, the RedBird Capital Partners founder said Milan want to be part of the potential collaboration between European football clubs and the NBA, viewing it as a way to grow the club's global brand, create new opportunities for fans, and strengthen its financial position. "The NBA is probably the only truly international sport that come

item.title: I think European football clubs should collaborate …
item.description: But here too, I think in terms of: 'How does this help AC Milan?' The EuroLeague has existed for 26 years, and most teams are not profitable. It is not sustainable. So I think European football clubs should collaborate with the NBA and bring NBA basketball to Europe. And that is what I will try to do in Milan," he said. "The goal is to continue elevating the Milan brand, offer opportunities to the fanbase, and continue improving our financial profile. So it is all part of an ecosystem in which e

item.title: Tristan Thompson explains how he invested in Anthropic …
item.description: Market Bubble: Tristan Thompson explains how he invested in Anthropic “It was a buddy of mine in Silicon Valley… just going to a couple of these dinners they were doing with the NBA, sitting there talking to these guys and really picking their brains” “They were telling me about the future of AI and technology… I was like hey man, I like what you’re talking about” “It was a bunch of SF guys and everyone kept telling me, so I was like alright man, here’s a check. I wanna get in the game” “From th

item.title: Chris Paul says you can become stressed just thinking …
item.description: Broku: Chris Paul says you can become stressed just thinking about being stressed. He says the worst gut issues of his career weren't caused by food. They were caused by stress. What Paul does when he feels the stress loop starting: • Gets around his family • Takes a break from work and slows down • Resets his nervous system before it spirals • Stops trying to be everything for everybody He says trying to be everything for everybody is what runs you ragged. Most people are stuck in a loop of bei

item.title: Drake shouts out Steph Curry on ICEMAN: “Back when …
item.description: Polymarket Hoops: Drake shouts out Steph Curry on ICEMAN: “Back when they was askin bout where Davison was at, now everybody got a blue 30 on they back”

item.title: Gui Santos shares a story about Luka Doncic cursing …
item.description: NBA Base: Gui Santos shares a story about Luka Doncic cursing him out in Portuguese after he complained to the ref about Luka flopping: “Shut up, you son of a b*tch." Gui was in "shock" that Luka knew how to trash talk in his native language. (h/t @NBAdabad )

item.title: Bam Adebayo, Harrison Barnes, Jaylen Brown, Tobias Harris, and Larry Nance Jr. the finalists for Social Justice Champion award
item.description: NBA: As we honor the sixth year of the NBA Social Justice Champion award, join us in recognizing Bam Adebayo, Harrison Barnes, Jaylen Brown, Tobias Harris, and Larry Nance Jr. as the finalists for the 2025-26 season. This award honors NBA Hall of Famer Kareem Abdul-Jabbar and his lifetime commitment to driving impactful change around social justice.

item.title: Insiders I spoke with this week think Dybantsa is the …
item.description: Let’s start at the top, because that’s what we do when it comes to the NBA Draft. And in the 2026 version of the draft, that “top” isn’t one or two players. It’s four. Talking to executives here for the league’s annual draft combine, including some from multiple teams picking near the top, the consensus opinion was that reasonable people could disagree on the top four picks, in virtually any order, but the identity of those four was beyond doubt: BYU forward AJ Dybantsa, Kansas guard Darryn Pete

item.title: West GM on Kawhi Leonard: There's no way Clippers are voiding his contract
item.description: But, of course, you can't ignore Kawhi Leonard and the LA Clippers being under investigation for possible salary cap circumvention. That leaves his short-term future somewhat up in the air. "There's no way they're voiding his contract even if the league finds him guilty," a West GM said. "That would be a reward, clearing the Clippers' books for them." The way Leonard is playing, the Clippers might want him on their books. "Them getting the No. 5 pick in the draft [via trade with the Indiana Pace

item.title: East exec on Lakers: They should sign LeBron James to a one-year deal with no-trade clause
item.description: So do the Lakers, a team that needs to add to its roster around Doncic, bid against themselves when making LeBron James an offer? "I'd pay LeBron whatever he wants as long as it's a one-year deal, no player option. Give him the no-trade clause," an East executive said. "Everything [new Lakers owner Mark] Walter has done so far has been about good business. "LeBron sells tickets. He keeps the [local] TV partner happy. Re-signing LeBron is good business."

item.title: The question instead comes down to whether James would …
item.description: Tim Bontemps: The "if not for his age" line from the strategist is a fairly important one, given James entered this season as the oldest player in the NBA, will turn 42 in September amid a presumed record-extending 24th season. The general sentiment from conversations during this week's draft combine was that James will suit up for 2026-27. Those facts, coupled with his still-elite performance, led multiple league insiders in Chicago to declare James deserves "as much money as he wants." But tha

item.title: East exec: Giannis Antetokounmpo trade likely happening before the draft
item.description: Tim Bontemps: In the wake of Milwaukee Bucks co-owner Jimmy Haslam's comments at coach Taylor Jenkins' introductory news conference earlier this month, it seems like the Giannis Antetokounmpo saga could finally get some clarity between now and the draft. And, after years of "will they, won't they," the growing belief around the league is that this time an Antetokoumpo trade will actually happen. "It just feels like they're done with the circus, more than anything," an East executive said. "They

item.title: "Run it back, load manage Joel [Embiid] as much as …
item.description: Tim Bontemps: As the Philadelphia 76ers embark on their search for a new team president after firing Daryl Morey on Tuesday, the consensus from sources around the league is that no matter whom Bob Myers chooses to run basketball operations, there aren't many levers to pull to significantly improve the team in the short term. "Run it back, load manage Joel [Embiid] as much as they can, be a playoff team and try to stay healthy," a West executive said. "I think there's stuff you can do on the marg

item.title: "He's not a max player, but they're probably going to …
item.description: With Cunningham, already on a well-earned supermax contract, at $270 million over five years, this isn't as much about playing hardball as it could be about protecting the upside of a roster loaded with other young players. "He's not a max player, but they're probably going to have to give him the max," an East executive said of Jalen Duren. "Because now [cap space] teams like Chicago or Brooklyn might see him as someone they could get with a max offer sheet and Detroit will have to match. "With

item.title: Danny Green says Detroit Pistons need to bench Jalen …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Danny Green says Detroit Pistons need to bench Jalen Duren: “I’m praying for him, I hope nothing going on whatever’s happening with Jalen Duren. Regardless of the fact we can’t play him, we got to win a playoff game if you have something going on you better communicate that we got to sit you regardless your not performing. Only thing else came to mind how do you get him to pick it up, the best translator something that computes to your brain is the bench. Bobby Knight a** on bench

item.title: Agent on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade talks: I'm still betting no deal happens
item.description: Zach Lowe: I had breakfast with an agent, a very high-powered agent the other day, and he was like ‘I’m still betting no trade’. And I said really? And he said ‘just go through the exercise. Who has enough stuff and would be good enough with Giannis Antetokounmpo to contend right away?’

item.title: Since the Game 4 adjustments against Atlanta, he’s …
item.description: Since the Game 4 adjustments against Atlanta, he’s averaging 99 touches per 100 possessions, according to Second Spectrum. Against the Sixers, that number climbed to 103. During the regular season, he averaged only 83. This is a different Karl-Anthony Towns, more in line with the one head coach Mike Brown tried to create early in the season but who could not get comfortable as the hopeful hub of the offense. Now, he’s adopted this style and been one of the best players in the NBA playoffs.

item.title: During the regular season, Towns took a shot on 26 …
item.description: The shots are way down because Towns is choosing for them to be, not because the Knicks are failing to include him. He’s never received the basketball this much since joining the Knicks, and he’s revamped what he does next. During the regular season, Towns took a shot on 26 percent of his touches, according to Second Spectrum. During the seven-game winning streak, that rate is down to 15 percent. He’s passing on 71 percent of his touches, compared to 61 percent during the regular season. In the

item.title: Real Madrid adding former NBA center
item.description: Real Madrid is in advanced talks to sign Omer Yurtseven, sources tell BasketNews. According to sources, the 27-year-old Turkish center would join Madrid for the remainder of the season to strengthen the frontcourt rotation for the Spanish league playoffs. Since Yurtseven is not eligible to be registered in the EuroLeague, he would only be available for domestic competition. The move comes after Madrid lost both Edy Tavares and Alex Len from its center rotation, leaving Usman Garuba as the only r

item.title: Clippers have options with No. 5 pick, including quick rebuild or pick up a star to play alongside Kawhi Leonard
item.description: Sam Amick: For the Clippers, I'm very curious to see how it changes things for them. I don't know specifics, but like a lot of people were talking about Kawhi Leonard quite a bit out here. And so when you get the number five pick, does that compel you to rebuild quicker and get off a guy like Kawhi? Any chance you would trade that pick to go get a guy like Giannis and add to Kawhi? I don't know what their game plan is, but when you have the stuff like the fifth pick and you didn't plan on being

item.title: Duncan Robinson, Caris LeVert and Kevin Huerter are …
item.description: Hunter Patterson: Duncan Robinson, Caris LeVert and Kevin Huerter are all present at shootaround this morning.

item.title: "If a team wants to hang a banner in the rafters, I’m …
item.description: Quote of the Week: Cameron Boozer, Duke freshman big "If a team wants to hang a banner in the rafters, I’m definitely the guy." Boozer said that to Andscape. That is one hell of a quote. And you know what, it could be true considering he checked in at 6-8 barefoot with a 7-2 wingspan on a 253-pound frame. That's prototype size for a power forward with enough length and bulk to play small-ball center in a pinch. Skeptics worried about his ability to defend in space. Well, Boozer ended up with a l

item.title: Rebounds, and 2 assists in 19 minutes with Reed …
item.description: Best Audition of the Week: Tarris Reed, UConn senior center Reed measured in with a 7-foot-4 wingspan on Monday, then put the rest of his game on tape in the scrimmages. Wednesday brought 9 points, 5 rebounds, and 2 assists in 19 minutes with Reed plowing through opponents inside and switching capably on defense. Thursday was even better with 17 points, 5 boards, and 2 blocks, plus the same impressive mobility. Playing defense in space is precisely what NBA teams wanted to see from Reed. The who

item.title: Mike Dunleavy Jr. open to trading Warriors’ 11th draft pick
item.description: Anthony Slater: Mike Dunleavy said the Warriors will look at all options with the 11th pick. Using it in a “strong draft” or: “If there’s offers for the pick to move up, move back, trade for a veteran player that can help us.”

item.title: Mike Dunleavy on the Warriors' season: "Pretty …
item.description: Dalton Johnson: Mike Dunleavy on the Warriors' season: "Pretty underwhelming."

item.title: Steph Curry came into the NBA as an undersized and …
item.description: Steph Curry came into the NBA as an undersized and undervalued point guard. It took years for him to find his voice, but a surprise player helped him out along the way. "Dorell Wright was a guy my second or third year that just had a charisma about him," Curry said on the "Fudd Around and Find Out" podcast. "He connected with pretty much everybody in the locker room, so I kind of got to see how he navigated that space. He was a champion in Miami, so he had been around a successful veteran team.

item.title: Mark Daigneault on Jalen Williams' hamstring strain: 'He's progressing, same stuff'
item.description: Justin Martinez: Jalen Williams was seen at practice today. Asked Mark Daigneault where Williams is at with his recovery from a Grade 1 left hamstring strain he sustained on April 22: “He’s progressing. Same stuff. I'm not going to give you the blow-by-blow on the details, but he continues to make progress and is doing a great job.”

item.title: Paul Pierce: "You're asking the guy (Taylor Jenkins) …
item.description: Paul Pierce: "You're asking the guy (Taylor Jenkins) to do basically what he did in Memphis, to come in here. They're in the middle of turmoil. You lose your coach. Your superstar, the best player that ever played for your franchise, wants out. And he's looking at the long run here. What does the long run look like? When you hire new coaches, you have reason to be optimistic. But this situation looks tough. If things don't change, he has a three-year window, probably, I'm going to guess, regardl

item.title: Someone paid more than $9 million to have lunch with …
item.description: Someone paid more than $9 million to have lunch with basketball player Stephen Curry and Warren Buffett, and the legendary investor also promised to match the winning bid so both their favorite charities will benefit. The auction on eBay was intended to revive an event that Buffett hosted for more than two decades that raised $53 million for the GLIDE Foundation homeless charity in San Francisco. This year's auction that wrapped up Thursday night also raised month for Curry's Eat.Learn.Play. Fou

item.title: A custom-built estate on Jett Road in Chastain Park …
item.description: A custom-built estate on Jett Road in Chastain Park traded hands for $6 million. Nine-season NBA player Malcolm Brogdon and his wife, Victoria, were the previous owners.

item.title: Sam Dekker joins South Carolina as assistant coach
item.description: South Carolina Men’s Basketball Coach Lamont Paris announced on Friday the hiring of former NBA player Sam Dekker as an assistant coach. Dekker completed an 11-year professional basketball career that included the NBA and overseas.

item.title: They now have to decide what to do with Jalen Duren, …
item.description: Tim Bontemps: They now have to decide what to do with Jalen Duren, pay him this summer, and this has been a very concerning month on that front. You have to do the same thing with Ausar Thompson, and it's been frankly a very concerning month on that front too because those two guys have shown that while they are talented players, if those are your three core players going forward, that's going to be a challenge to get where you hope to go with Cade Cunningham.

item.title: The Utah Jazz unveiled the Jazz Skill Lab, a youth …
item.description: The Utah Jazz unveiled the Jazz Skill Lab, a youth development hub that allows athletes ages 12-18 to train, test and track their abilities like prospects do in the NBA Draft Combine. "We want to be innovative and do things other teams aren't doing," Jazz Vice President of Youth Strategy Nate Martinez said. "It's another thing we've added to give kids another opportunity to connect with basketball, to connect with sport, connect with the Jazz brand in a new way."

item.title: Jaime Jaquez Jr. will be part of the NBA Finals, to be …
item.description: While the Heat failed to make the playoffs for the first time in seven years, forward Jaime Jaquez Jr. will be part of the NBA Finals, to be the featured guest during the NBA Finals at NBA House in Brazil. Jaquez, who joined the Heat the season after their trip to the 2023 NBA Finals, will be part of watch parties in Sao Paulo on June 5 and June 8.

item.title: Stephen Curry says he didn’t know where ‘Golden State’ was when Warriors drafted him
item.description: NBA draft night mostly was a blur for Warriors superstar Steph Curry. But he does remember the first thing he did when Golden State selected him with the No. 7 overall pick in 2009. "Getting that call from Larry Riley, the GM at the time, Don Nelson, who was the coach at the time, getting that call and being like, 'Hey, we're looking forward to having you, we're excited.' And then me quickly looking at my phone trying to figure out exactly where Golden State was," Curry recalled on WNBA star Azz

item.title: Aas he admitted, his head was fixated on being drafted …
item.description: Aas he admitted, his head was fixated on being drafted by the New York Knicks. "All I was thinking about was New York," Curry said. "But careful what you wish for. I'm glad it went my way for sure." Curry added that his favorite part of the night was being booed by New York fans inside The Theater at Madison Square Garden in New York City. He remembers initially questioning why they were booing him, but then he realized it was because they wanted him on the Knicks.

item.title: Michigan’s Nimari Burnett has garnered high NBA …
item.description: Jake Weingarten: Michigan’s Nimari Burnett has garnered high NBA interest and has several NBA workouts, including the Washington Wizards and Charlotte Hornets next week, a source told @stockrisers. He will have several NBA workouts following that at the end of May and into June.

item.title: Dallas Mavericks rookie Cooper Flagg is in a …
item.description: The rumors are true. Dallas Mavericks rookie Cooper Flagg is in a relationship with Duke University center Arianna Roberson. In a TikTok posted to her account on Thursday, May 14, Roberson, 20, and Flagg, 19, hold hands and embrace in a compilation of clips from their tropical vacation in the the Turks and Caicos Islands.

item.description: Knicks Videos: "He's done a great job of adjusting our team to give us the best chance to win. The spot we're in now is because of his courage and trust to change what we were doing and put us in a better position." - Karl-Anthony Towns on Mike Brown

item.title: "Go in there say how your feeling, with your feelings, …
item.description: Stefan Bondy: KAT implies he brought idea of changing offense to Mike Brown before Game 4 against the Hawks. That's when the Knicks started running offense thru KAT in midpost: "Go in there say how your feeling, with your feelings, your idea, and see if it's received. And it was received."

item.title: Wilt: 50.4 to 35.0 in 1961-62 (-15.4)
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Biggest scoring drops in the playoffs in an All-Star year Wilt: 50.4 to 35.0 in 1961-62 (-15.4) Jalen Duren: 19.5 to 10.1 this year (-9.4) Elgin Baylor: 24.8 to 15.4 in 1968-69 (-9.4) Flynn Robinson: 21.8 to 12.8 in 1969-70 (-9.0) Julius Randle: 25.1 to 16.6 in 2022-23 (-8.5)

item.title: Mike Dunleavy optimistic Draymond Green will remain in Golden State next season
item.description: Nick Friedell: Mike Dunleavy on Draymond Green: "The ball's in his court" as far as player option goes. Dunleavy sounds optimistic that Draymond will be here to start next season. He said there's a feeling on both sides that Draymond finishes his career with the Warriors.

item.title: Mike Dunleavy when asked if there will be changes to …
item.description: Dalton Johnson: Warriors GM Mike Dunleavy when asked if there will be changes to the offense: "We can play however we want, just don't turn the ball over."

item.title: "As crazy as it sounds," Curry said. "He’s younger …
item.description: Even though Draymond Green entered the NBA three years after Curry, the leadership was evident right away. "As crazy as it sounds," Curry said. "He’s younger than me, but it was Draymond. In my fourth year, can you imagine he was the loudest one in the room, even in his first year? As the relationship grew, it kind of became important for me to make sure I elevated my voice so that he wasn’t the only one that you heard on a daily basis."

item.title: Mike Dunleavy Jr on struggles during season: It wasn't the talent on the roster, it was injuries and things we can control
item.description: Anthony Slater: Mike Dunleavy: “This isn’t about the roster frankly. This year, I don’t think we came up short because of the talent on the roster. It was injuries and things we can control.”

item.title: “I don’t see under any scenario where we don’t want to …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Mike Dunleavy on Steph Curry’s eventual extension talks: “I don’t see under any scenario where we don’t want to figure out a way to have him finish here.”

item.title: The biggest takeaway from Kerr/Dunleavy press …
item.description: Nick Friedell: The biggest takeaway from Kerr/Dunleavy press conferences? It feels like everybody is aligned again. Kerr said part of what success looks like for Warriors next season is recommitting to the process of getting better each day again. Kerr, Dunleavy and Lacob all on same page.

item.title: Dunleavy on Steph contract extension discussions: "We …
item.description: Nick Friedell: Dunleavy on Steph contract extension discussions: "We want Steph to finish his career here." He sounds confident they'll be able to work something out -- but it doesn't sound like formal conversations have started yet.

item.title: Steve Kerr on returning: 'I'm thrilled to be back'
item.description: Nick Friedell: Kerr: “I’m thrilled to be back.”

item.title: "If I were tired and burned out, I would not be doing …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Steve Kerr: "If I were tired and burned out, I would not be doing this."

item.title: Said they won’t agree on everything. Lacob may not …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Steve Kerr goes in depth on his partnership with Joe Lacob. Said they won’t agree on everything. Lacob may not like a decision he makes. Kerr may not love a specific sideline reaction. But they are ultimately aligned.

item.title: "I gotta tighten the ship up next year."
item.description: Nick Friedell: Kerr is self-reflective in his first comments since new deal. "I gotta tighten the ship up next year." Both Kerr and Dunleavy made note of the fact they want the Warriors to fix their turnover problems that have plagued the organization for years.

item.title: Duncan Robinson, Kevin Huerter, Caris LeVert will be available against Cavs
item.description: The Pistons have upgraded Duncan Robinson (low back soreness), Kevin Huerter (left adductor strain) and Caris LeVert (right heel contusion) to available for Friday's Game 6 against Cleveland.

item.title: He received an injection to play in tonight’s Game 6, …
item.description: Jamal Collier: Pistons guard Kevin Huerter re-aggravated his adductor injury during Game 5, which is why he was pulled after playing just three minutes. He received an injection to play in tonight’s Game 6, sources told ESPN, and he is listed as available.

item.title: Those guys are gonna be a huge part of what we do for …

Those guys are gonna be a huge part of what we do for a long time, and what does it say about us if in their difficult times, we choose to give up on them?"

item.description: Skyed: Jalen Williams on his hamstring injury: “it’s good i haven’t had to rush back from my hamstring stuff at all, im actually taking extra days now then what was even originally planned cause we’re up 3-0 so there’s no point going into this series and possibly hurting myself”

item.description: Anthony Slater: Steve Kerr gets detailed on what must change as part of his decision to return “I know I have to be better. I didn’t have a great coaching year.” “We got a little too loose. I gotta tighten the ship up.”

item.title: “It usually does not work out well when a player wants …
item.description: Anthony Slater: Steve Kerr said Steph Curry did not put his foot down to ensure the Warriors brought Kerr back: “It usually does not work out well when a player wants to dictate what an organization does. Steph has always recognized the sanctity of that wall.”

item.title: Kerr shouts out former assistant coaches Terry Stotts …
item.description: Sam Gordon: Kerr shouts out former assistant coaches Terry Stotts and Jerry Stackhouse for their contributions and says he's looking externally and internally for their replacements on his coaching staff.

item.description: Lopez has a team option, while Konan Niederhäuser is not expected to be ready to start the season, though there is virtually no chance that the Clippers use a top pick on a center.

item.title: He would practice those things and work on them, even …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: JB on Harden, who he coached for four years in Houston: “He would foul bait me. We would play one on one. He would practice those things and work on them, even just playing one on one versus me. He has a great understanding of the rules and how to manipulate them in his favor.”

item.title: “It’s a feel. I wish I could say when he hits 32 …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Atkinson on how they decide to trap Cade: “It’s a feel. I wish I could say when he hits 32 points we’re going to start trapping. It’s a feel … at the end of the game you have to know when to go to it and when not to. Glad we got some reps at it.” Said they have to mix coverages.

item.title: I think all these guys' willingness to accept their …
item.description: Clemente Almanza: Mark Daigneault on Aaron Wiggins handling his limited role in these playoffs: “Like a total pro, just like everybody else. I think all these guys' willingness to accept their role at any given point in time, which is not permanent, it changes constantly. But their willingness to accept it and stay inside the team and continue to put the wind at their teammates' backs has applied pressure to everybody to continue to do that.”

item.title: “I feel like in the regular season, there's a level of …
item.description: Clemente Almanza: Chet Holmgren on OKC’s offense being better in the playoffs: “I feel like in the regular season, there's a level of experimentation that happens. As it should… I know Dub’s missing time, but we've been able to find a good amount of continuity.”

item.description: Clemente Almanza: Chet Holmgren on his first impressions of Jared McCain: “I watched a little bit of his Linsanity run last year before he unfortunately got hurt. I knew he could do more than just shoot.”

item.title: The Minnesota Timberwolves and Lynx today announced …
item.description: The Minnesota Timberwolves and Lynx today announced the appointment of Michael Dillon as Chief Financial Officer and Chief Operating Officer, effective immediately. Dillon joins the organization from the Pittsburgh Penguins, where he served as CFO and was instrumental in shaping and executing the club’s business strategy across finance and operations. A proven leader at the intersection of finance, technology, and data, Dillon brings a track record of driving performance, innovation, and organiz

item.title: The journey from Ron Artest to Metta World Peace is a …
item.description: The journey from Ron Artest to Metta World Peace is a great redemption story, and on Friday morning inside City Hall, the Los Angeles City Council officially celebrated “Metta World Peace Day,” honoring the former Lakers champion for his mental health advocacy. The recognition came after Councilmember Hugo Soto-Martinez introduced a resolution declaring May 15 as “Metta World Peace Day” throughout Los Angeles during Mental Health Awareness Month.

item.title: Kings interested in Mitchell Robinson?
item.description: Still, sources around the league have pegged the Sacramento Kings as a threat to pry away Robinson in free agency. Scott Perry, the GM of the Kings, has long been a fan of Robinson after drafting him the 2018 second round as a member of New York’s front office. It was probably Perry’s best move in a tenure of blunders before Rose arrived. Perry’s Kings have no cap space but could maneuver the roster to open a competitive offer for Robinson, who has mostly been underpaid throughout his career ($6

item.title: Cavs were +4 in 11 minutes with Donovan Mitchell OFF …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Cavs were +4 in 11 minutes with Donovan Mitchell OFF court in Game 6 tonight. They were -25 in his 37 minutes ON court. They lost the 8 minutes that Mitchell was on court without Harden by 16 points, shot 4-for-16 from the field in those minutes.

item.description: NBA on ESPN: “That’s a great question.” — James Harden on the Cavs not playing with the intensity needed to close out the series.

item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: James Harden is now 4-14 in Game 6s in his career, the second-worst record in Game 6 in NBA playoff history.

item.title: James Harden has tied the record for most games in a …

According to our sources, Frenchman Nicolas Batum will be among the panel of commentators appearing on the massive 1200 m² set, located in Culver City, amidst the MGM studios . item.title: Fred VanVleet on the Rockets: 'I know that we will be a better team with me on the team'
item.description: Fred VanVleet: "I know I add value. I know what I bring to the table, and I know that we will be a better team with me on the team. That has been the case for me on every team I’ve ever been on. I’m going to make the team better just by virtue of me being available, being around, and bringing my energy, before you even get to the game. My leadership and competitiveness, that’s what I do. I try to make everything I touch better. That’s my mindset. Not saying I do or I don’t, but that’s how I view

item.title: Fred VanVleet: "I think the gift and the curse with KD …
item.description: Fred VanVleet: "I think the gift and the curse with KD is that he’s so open, so authentic, so approachable, and so himself that it leaves room for clowns to jump in and add their own twist. And if he doesn’t come out and directly say, ‘No,’ or go against that — which is what they want — there’s just so much room for clickbait farming when it comes to him. He makes it easy. He makes it easy for that. I saw that for the first time this year with my own eyes. It’s something that I grew to appreciat

item.title: So, adding KD, it’s a whole new team. We didn’t get …
item.description: Fred VanVleet: “You’ve got to remember, bro, for what we built here in a quick turnaround, basically, we took four of our five starters out: me, Dillon, Jalen, Steven. Essentially, right? So, adding KD, it’s a whole new team. We didn’t get one practice together. I got hurt before we started practice, so we never even got to see what it looked like. There’s no continuity. Our system changed. Everything changed. I’m just proud of the guys for hanging in there during the season, and also proud of t

item.title: Fred VanVleet: “If you told me in September that I …
item.description: Fred VanVleet: “If you told me in September that I would miss the whole season, Stephen would miss 75% of the season, and we would win 52 games and play the Lakers in a four-five matchup, take them to six without KD, I would probably say, ‘Okay, that doesn’t sound crazy to me.’ And it’s not a failure. I wouldn’t feel bad about that. Now, while you’re in it, it’s crazy, right? It looks bad some nights. You’ve got the blown leads, you’ve got the mishaps, you’ve got guys’ performances up and down.

item.title: Wemby called Castle “amazing.”
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Wemby called Castle “amazing.” “The composure to make the late choice and to push the defense into as far as they can go to take care of all our strengths, he was great. He shot the ball and he should have.”

item.description: Rudy Gobert on whether he's surprised by Wemby being so good this quickly: "I wouldn't say surprised...it's credit to his work. I'm aware of that. Now it's gonna be interesting to see how he's ready to face that next challenge and see if he takes his team to the promised land this year"

item.title: The NBA Draft Lottery set a record last weekend, with …
item.description: The NBA Draft Lottery set a record last weekend, with ABC drawing 2.3 million viewers. That led into the Knicks finishing a sweep of the 76ers in the Eastern Conference semifinals. ABC was up 23% from the lottery last year. USA Network averaged 529,000 viewers for its debut WNBA game on Sunday afternoon, featuring Aces-Sparks. item.title: I'm about to go into another series healthy."
item.description: Williams has been sidelined since April 22 with a Grade 1 left hamstring strain, which typically carries a recovery time of 1-3 weeks. He has surpassed that window, all while OKC secured a second-round sweep of the Los Angeles Lakers without him. Williams was then seen at practice Friday, and he provided an update on his health status during a YouTube vlog he posted later that day. "I haven't had to rush back from my hamstring stuff at all," Williams said. "I'm actually taking extra days now the

item.title: Kon Knueppel continued his podcast tour this summer, …
item.description: Former Duke star Kon Knueppel continued his podcast tour this summer, making an appearance on the "Dale Jr. Download," hosted by none other than former two-time Daytona 500 winner Dale Earnhardt Jr. This comes not long after appearing on a podcast with Milwaukee Brewers star Christian Yelich. Earnhardt Jr. is the son of former racing icon Dale Earnhardt, who is tied with both Richard Petty and Jimmie Johnson for the most NASCAR championships (7) in the sport's history.

item.title: Spurs eliminate Minnesota
item.description: After standing head-and-shoulders above the rest of the Western Conference all season, it will indeed be the San Antonio Spurs and Oklahoma City Thunder in the conference finals. The Spurs eliminated the Minnesota Timberwolves with a lop-sided 139-109 win in Game 6 on Friday, setting up a heavyweight fight to reach the NBA Finals. Game 1 of Spurs-Thunder will take place on Monday. The Thunder swept the Los Angeles Lakers in the other Western Conference semifinal.

item.title: Game 1: OKC vs. San Antonio on Monday, May 18
item.description: Justin Martinez: The OKC Thunder will face the San Antonio Spurs in the Western Conference finals. Here's the schedule, with tipoff times TBD: Game 1: OKC vs. San Antonio on Monday, May 18 Game 2: OKC vs. San Antonio on Wednesday, May 20 Game 3: OKC at San Antonio on Friday, May 22 Game 4: OKC at San Antonio on Sunday, May 24 Game 5: OKC vs. San Antonio on Tuesday, May 26* Game 6: OKC at San Antonio on Thursday, May 28* Game 7: OKC vs. San Antonio on Saturday, May 30* * If necessary

item.title: A lot of NBA teams are also annoyed with the collapse …
item.description: You want to know a secret? A lot of NBA teams are also annoyed with the collapse of traditional jersey standards. Many hate having to use all these goofy-looking uniforms. The marketing is a pain in the ass and the options dilute the brands these franchises obsess over building. If you want to understand why tradition was abandoned, I will, again, direct you to Nike’s tanking stock price. I posted a piece about UBS research revealing that a decline in NBA popularity had hurt the apparel giant. T

item.title: “I’ve watched the NBA and been a part of it for a …
item.description: Nowitzki, a Naismith Memorial Basketball Hall of Famer, had a different opinion. “I’ve watched the NBA and been a part of it for a long, long time. I’ve never seen this,” Nowitzki said. “A guy walking into the huddle with eight minutes to go in the fourth quarter and dapping up the entire team? Too much for me. Obviously, you can do that after the game and show plenty of respect.” Griffin cosigned simply by saying, “I’ve never seen that before.”

It was the middle of March, and the Timberwolves had lost three in a row in decisive fashion. The team that they said they were, the fighters that never went down easy, were nowhere to be found. item.title: Team sources told The Athletic that there were never …
item.description: One bright spot for the Wolves is that they appear set to bring the front-office staff back next year intact. Connelly was a candidate for the top job in Dallas, but team sources told The Athletic that there were never any discussions between the Mavericks and Connelly before Masai Ujiri was hired.

item.title: Connelly will be entering the final season of his …
item.description: Connelly and Lloyd are two of the most respected executives in the league. Another season with them in charge should only help the Wolves’ efforts to close the gap on Oklahoma City and San Antonio, but there is work to be done. Connelly will be entering the final season of his contract, and Timberwolves ownership wants to get an extension done with him, team sources said. Finch has two more years left on his deal and will be back for his sixth full season next year.

item.title: Mike Conley to mull retirement
item.description: Mike Conley will mull retirement but could return on a cheaper deal. Bones Hyland will also be a free agent.
