He won in high school, won at Villanova this the guy who you want have on your team in those moments in the situation where it’s tough because he will figure it out he’s so intelligent and he’s tough physically, and mentally. So this doesn’t surprise me. I love Jalen Brunson want he stands for, what he’s capable of in the biggest m

item.title: Some extra attention has been brought to Karl-Anthony …
item.description: One quick hand motion has some in San Antonio up in arms. Some extra attention has been brought to Karl-Anthony Towns’ deflection on the final play of Wednesday’s miracle Knicks Game 4 Finals win after it appears he motioned to the fans sitting courtside to crowd Spurs rookie Dylan Harper for the critical inbounds pass. With the Knicks leading 107-106 with 1.2 seconds remaining, Towns walked toward Harper — positioned on the left sidelines if looking straight at the basket from midcourt — and di

item.title: Paris in talks with former NBA head coach Dave Joerger
item.description: Paris Basketball is exploring a high-profile coaching addition as the club prepares for the next chapter of its project. Paris is in talks with former NBA head coach Dave Joerger, sources tell ESPN's Olgun Uluc and BasketNews. The 52-year-old Minnesota native has been exploring opportunities outside the NBA and is widely expected around the market to emerge as Paris' next bench leader.

item.title: Paris is currently coached by 28-year-old Julius …
item.description: Paris is currently coached by 28-year-old Julius Thomas, who took over the team mid-season. However, BasketNews sources indicate that Thomas is expected to leave the organization this summer and is exploring opportunities to join an NBA coaching staff. Former Minnesota Timberwolves executive David Kahn oversees basketball operations in Paris, while the organization has established a growing reputation as a pathway between European basketball and the NBA.

item.title: Hawks, Pistons interested in Austin Reaves
item.description: Dan Woike on Austin Reaves: League sources said the Detroit Pistons and Atlanta Hawks are among a group of interested teams that can create space to make competitive offers. Other teams could also emerge.

item.title: Nets expected to offer Austin Reaves a four-year, $178.5 million contract
item.description: Multiple front-office sources around the league, granted anonymity to freely discuss an opposing player, expect Austin Reaves to have interest from the Brooklyn Nets, with a four-year, $178.5 million contract expected to be offered.

item.title: Draymond Green on Knicks' 29-point comeback: 'The greatest playoff game I've ever seen'
item.description: Draymond Green: By far craziest playoff game I've ever seen. I've been a part of some incredible playoff games myself. That had to be the greatest playoff game I've ever seen. To come back from… because the Spurs were dominating. They were rolling. They were doing everything right. They had to and the Knicks were playing pathetic. And it just started to split slowly but surely. And they kept going, kept going, kept going. When I tell you it got to the point to where when it was in single digits,

item.title: "As a basketball purist, I was happy to see the Knicks …
item.description: Run It Back: Isiah Thomas on Game 4 of the NBA Finals: "As a basketball purist, I was happy to see the Knicks comeback and win. I was terribly upset by the way Spurs played with a 29-point lead."

item.title: Magic Johnson on De'Aaron Fox: “He should’ve pulled …
item.description: Legion Hoops: Magic Johnson on De'Aaron Fox: “He should’ve pulled the ball back out… I think that play really hurt the Spurs. Wemby’s two free throws he missed hurt the spurs. De’Aaron is a point guard, you gotta know every situation. My job as a point guard was to know every situation.” (via @FirstTake , h/t @NBA__Courtside )

item.title: Green on apologizing to Jalen Brunson for saying he’s …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Draymond Green on apologizing to Jalen Brunson for saying he’s not a 1A guy in the NBA: “The reason I said that is because I’ve been very critical of Jalen Brunson just not thinking that, hey man, he’s this 1A guy that and here’s the thing what y’all have to understand about me. When I say 1A, when I say 1A, what I’m saying is no matter what you go to, you’re the guy. Allah Steph Curry, Allah LeBron James, Allah Luka Doncic, Kawhi, like there’s only really five to seven 1As in all

item.title: Paul Pierce on if OG Anunoby should be finals MVP: …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Paul Pierce on if OG Anunoby should be finals MVP: “I’m not giving OG Anunoby no goddamn flowers. I’m giving him the Finals MVP. TAKE THAT HOME, OG. I’m telling you, take home the finals MVP. He’s been dominant…. He’s been the most dominant player in this finals. Quite as is kept now after four games. When you look at the numbers and add them up.” (Via @NFGShow)

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: “If I go to DC, I’ll make the playoffs.”
item.description: Legion Hoops: AJ Dybantsa: “If I go to DC, I’ll make the playoffs.” (via @MrCongee)

item.title: Isiah Thomas on former NBA players not including LeBron James on their top-5 lists: 'It's jealousy, envy'
item.description: Run It Back: Isiah Thomas on former NBA players not including LeBron James on their top-5 lists: 'It's jealousy, envy... I don't mess around. I'm calling it out: Guys are jealous, feeling envious... They intentionally ignore the obvious. And when you intentionally ignore the obvious, then it has to come back to some jealousy, or some envy, or just some straight BS. Now this man has every basketball record on the books, and if he ain't in your Top 5, then you're full of sh*t.'

item.title: Ice Cube's The Big3 league eyeing $290 million valuation
item.description: The Big3 basketball league, co-founded by rapper Ice Cube, is eyeing a $290 million valuation as it plans to go public later this year. The league announced Friday that it has reached a deal with Graf Global Corp., described as a publicly traded special purpose acquisition company, that will offer fans a chance to buy stock in the league. They are targeting being available on one of the major stock exchanges in the fourth quarter of 2026 under the ticker TONT (for 3-on-3). "You can't participate

item.title: Rich Paul: 10 to 12 teams have called to show interest in LeBron James
item.description: NBA on ESPN: Despite rumors circulating, Rich Paul tells @PatMcAfeeshow that he hasn't had a conversation with LeBron James about next season, but has received calls from "about 10-12 teams" interested 👀 "Believe nothing that's out there because I haven't had one conversation with him."

item.title: I think the theme with Boston and Miami is, Giannis is …
item.description: Sam Amick on Giannis Antetokounmpo: I think the theme with Boston and Miami is, Giannis is ready for a really serious organization. I think he wants to be coached hard right now. — I do wonder how he feels about joining a Celtics organization that already has 17 banners. In Miami you’d maybe get a little more celebrated if you get them another one.

item.title: My informants told me again this morning a Bucks trade …
item.description: Gery Woelfel: My informants told me again this morning a Bucks trade involving Antetokounmpo will likely take place shortly after the conclusion of the NBA Finals. The Knicks could end the series Saturday night. The latest the Finals could run is Friday, June 19. The NBA draft commences June 23.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama: Everyone on the Spurs know we're going to come back and win this series
item.description: Tim Bontemps: Victor Wembanyama on whether his teammates believe they can come back from down 3-1: “Everybody knows we are going to do it.”

item.title: "I didn't really think much of it. I just saw that one …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Wemby is taking things being tossed at him in stride: "I didn't really think much of it. I just saw that one video of the eggs. I didn't see any other one. It's OK. I don't dislike it. Obviously it's not good at all. But it doesn't bother me."

item.title: "The ability, first and foremost. The fact that we've …
item.description: Maxime Aubin: I've asked Victor Wembanyama about what makes him still confident that the Spurs can bounce back and turn this NBA Finals around: "The ability, first and foremost. The fact that we've proven game after game that nobody beats us — we beat ourselves. The confidence comes from knowing that our destiny is in our own hands."

item.title: Spurs’ Victor Wembanyama on NBA Finals Game 4 loss to …
item.description: Ben Golliver: Spurs’ Victor Wembanyama on NBA Finals Game 4 loss to Knicks: “We’re over it.”

item.title: Mitch Johnson: De'Aaron Fox will have the ball in his hands at the end of the game tomorrow and I have the utmost confidence he will deliver
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Mitch Johnson: "I don't get into social media. I think I've probably been fired 212 times, and we've traded Fox 72 times. People have their opinions. I don't care. De'Aaron Fox will have the ball in his hands at the end of the game tomorrow and I have nothing but the utmost confidence that he's gonna deliver like he's done countless times for us"

item.title: "It's not like people have my phone number and can …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: De’Aaron Fox on the criticism he got after game 4: "It's not like people have my phone number and can call me. I don't watch those shows. It doesn't matter. It is what it is. You can't change it now. We're trying to move on from that"

item.title: "People making money. It's the economy that we live …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: De’Aaron Fox: "People making money. It's the economy that we live in. It's the world that we live in. Am I upset about it? No. Do I understand it? Sure. But I don't think that really changes kind of what happens on the court"

item.title: "I feel like we've made history all year. We've proven …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Stephon Castle on whether they can come back from 3-1: "I feel like we've made history all year. We've proven that with our backs against the wall that we can step up. I don't really expect this to be any different"

item.title: Josh Hart on Game 5: We can't keep getting into a hole we have to dig ourselves out of
item.description: SNY Knicks: "We can't keep getting into a hole and trying to dig ourselves out of a hole" Josh Hart talks about the Knicks' need to get off to a better start in Game 5:

item.title: "Definitely a factor but it's the playoffs. …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Wemby on whether fatigue played a factor at the end of game 4: "Definitely a factor but it's the playoffs. Everybody's just as tired. I mean, it shouldn't even be a factor in the game. I mean, now we got 2 days between games. It's not going to be a factor"

item.title: "I appreciate it more than I think I'll ever be able …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Jalen Brunson on hearing the Knicks fans on the road: "I appreciate it more than I think I'll ever be able to say"

item.title: "The narrative was placed on him by the outside world. …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Jalen on KAT: "The narrative was placed on him by the outside world. You never really know a person until obviously you meet them face to face and you see what he says and what his actions are. And being the teammate that he is and seeing his sacrifices and seeing what he's done his entire career puts him in a position to become a hall of famer. And honestly I wouldn't trade that for the world. And the things that he's done has been extraordinary and not really sure how he's goi

item.title: "Can't spell God without OG"
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: KAT: "Can't spell God without OG"

item.title: Jalen on being 1 win away from a title:
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Jalen on being 1 win away from a title: "0-0"

item.title: "My Raptors teammates that I used to play with in the …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: OG on who has reached out to him on his tip in GW: "My Raptors teammates that I used to play with in the past, they hit me up. They were really excited for me, happy for me"

item.title: "A lot of preparation like off the court with like …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: OG on taking his game to another level on the biggest stage: "A lot of preparation like off the court with like film study and all that things, and then just on the court, just being in the gym, working on my game, trying to grow and improve throughout the year or whatever. Just always trying to get better. And then also just opportunity. More opportunity to show more and do more things. And just being ready for it when you get the opportunity, just from the hard work"

item.title: "At the end of the day, it's just a basketball game. I …
item.description: Sirius XM NBA Radio: "At the end of the day, it's just a basketball game. I don't think anyone should be getting eggs tossed at them." @JulianChampagn2 on the Spurs dealing with Knicks fans outside their hotel

item.title: "Stop on De'Aaron Fox, he's playing with a high ankle …
item.description: Dan Patrick Show: "Stop on De'Aaron Fox, he's playing with a high ankle sprain – probably shouldn't be playing. He would normally dunk that layup – if he would've dunked it, he's the hero..." – John Calipari on people blaming De'Aaron Fox for the #Spurs loss

item.title: OG Anunoby says it's "really cool" to know that his …
item.description: SNY Knicks: OG Anunoby says it's "really cool" to know that his tip-in will be immortalized in New York sports history: "Everyone's been telling me how much it means. I obviously see how much it means. It's just really cool to be a part of it and I'm very grateful."

item.title: Spurs list Kornet as questionable for Game 5 due to an …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Spurs list Kornet as questionable for Game 5 due to an “illness” after he played just 4 minutes in Game 4.

item.title: Blazers, Rockets, Hawks interested in Jaylen Brown
item.description: Portland, Houston and Atlanta are teams routinely mentioned as suitors with Jaylen Brown interest, but the sheer salary numbers do add a layer of complexity to any talks. Brown, who turns 30 in October, just assembled a career season but also has three years and $183 million left on his current contract.

item.title: Miami might still be widely regarded as the most …
item.description: One of the loudest questions circulating throughout the league at the moment: Miami might still be widely regarded as the most likely destination for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: If Milwaukee does move forward with trading Giannis to …
item.description: Jake Fischer: If Milwaukee does move forward with trading Giannis to Miami, the Bucks will assuredly receive the Heat’s No. 13 pick along with other draft capital. League sources say Milwaukee is also calling rival teams in search of a third top-20 pick, and potentially another pick in the top-10 ahead of the Bucks’ current 10th selection.

item.title: Pistons, Timberwolves have interest in Kyrie Irving, Mavericks saying he's not available
item.description: The Mavericks, as we've been forecasting for some time in various appearances on the DLLS Mavs podcast, have indeed received trade inquiries for star guard Kyrie Irving, league sources say. However ... Sources tell The Stein Line that the Mavericks, thus far this offseason, have been messaging to teams that Irving is not available. Detroit and Minnesota are known to have Irving interest, but The Stein Line has learned that Houston — oft-mentioned as a potential suitor — has yet to make any overt

item.title: Pistons, Pacers pursuing Trey Murphy
item.description: External trade interest in New Orleans' Trey Murphy III remains intense, league sources say. Yet I was told this week that the inquiries have all been of an inbound nature. League sources say that the Pelicans have not offered Murphy anywhere and that this is the first time their new front office regime headed by Joe Dumars has been truly willing to field offers for Murphy. As one team interested in Murphy put it to me this week: "They want a lot." Two teams known to have expressed interest in t

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks will add Vince Legarza & Brad Jones as …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Milwaukee Bucks will add Vince Legarza & Brad Jones as assistant coaches. Legarza was an assistant with Mike Budenholzer on the Suns & Bucks. He & Taylor Jenkins both worked together under Budenholzer on the Hawks. Jones was an assistant for Jenkins on the Grizzlies.

item.title: "We love you and we need you."
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "We love you and we need you." —Pistons head coach JB Bickerstaff on the advice he would give De'Aaron Fox 👏

item.title: Jeremy Lin, Carmelo Anthony clear air on 7PM in Brooklyn podcast
item.description: Carmelo Anthony addressed his “ridiculous” remark, saying that Jeremy Lin deserved for the Knicks to match his offer from Houston instead of letting him walk over luxury tax concerns. “You got to think from my perspective, everything kind of came and went so fast and now I’m not on the Knicks anymore,” Lin said on the “7PM in Brooklyn,” a Wave Original. “And after that, I hear publicly a coach saying something and three teammates saying stuff publicly about like, ‘Oh, Linsanity didn’t vibe with

item.title: “I had to figure my s–t. I was injured. I was hurt,” …
item.description: Anthony also pushed back on the narrative that he was jealous of Lin during “Linsanity.” “I had to figure my s–t. I was injured. I was hurt,” Anthony said of the “Linsanity” era. “I had to think about how fast I’m going to come back, what I’m going to do, how the f–k I’m going to go help this team, how I got to lead this team. “I’m in a different position than [Jeremy] is. So I got to deal with a whole bunch of s–t. Forget being jealous of what [Jeremy’s] doing. I want him to keep going. Game wi

item.title: “Basically I asked every last question I had: ‘did …
item.description: During Friday’s installment of the “7PM in Brooklyn” podcast, Lin — whose rise to fame during the 2011-12 season sparked rumors that Anthony was jealous of his success — explained that the pair had an “honest and direct” conversation and finally reconciled. “Basically I asked every last question I had: ‘did that happen? What happened there? What happened here?’” Lin said. “But it was honest. It was direct, it was honest and we were able to definitely clear the air. Obviously perception is realit

item.title: “… And that’s the thing about that whole stretch was …
item.description: “… And that’s the thing about that whole stretch was there was a lot that I was unsure about. And so the narratives come, but to be able to talk it out, I appreciate that. And so my biggest thing was when I think back on my career, because I recently retired [last August], I’ve never wanted my story to be me versus anybody else, which we had talked about. “And I think at the end of all of this, if my name comes up or my story comes up and it’s me versus [Carmelo] or if we’re ever at a point wher

item.title: “These was questions that was built up for 14 years …
item.description: Anthony, who publicly invited Lin to be a guest on his “7PM In Brooklyn” podcast to “clear the air” last month, explained that it took him some time before he felt ready to answer Lin’s questions. “These was questions that was built up for 14 years that other people probably was adding on to your pressures and your mindset and your perspective,” Anthony said. “It took me to go through what I went through to have the time to come back and be at peace with being able to answer all your questions w

item.title: League sources believe Utah views the Ohio native as a …
item.description: Though Peterson described himself as a point guard at the NBA Combine, league sources believe Utah views the Ohio native as a potential backcourt partner for Keyonte George, sharing playmaking duties.

item.title: Sources indicated that trades could be on the table …
item.description: Before the draft lottery, sources indicated that trades could be on the table regardless of where the team was picking, but there has been no indication that a trade is in the works since moving up to the No. 2 selection.

item.title: Game 4 of the 2026 NBA Finals on ABC was the …
item.description: NBA Communications: Game 4 of the 2026 NBA Finals on ABC was the most-watched NBA Finals Game 4 since 1998, averaging 20.9M viewers and peaking with 23.2M viewers at 11:15 p.m. ET. The 2026 NBA Finals are the most-watched NBA Finals through four games since 1998.

item.title: Another record-setting audience for the '26 #NBAFinals …
item.description: ESPN PR: Another record-setting audience for the '26 #NBAFinals as ABC drew an audience of 20.9M 👏 🏀 Largest NBA Finals Game 4 audience since '98 🏀 Most-watched NBA Finals Game 4 EVER on ABC 🏀 Up 123% from last year's Game 4 🏀 23.2M peak viewers

item.title: Mavericks expected to turn to rising NBA assistant for head coaching job
item.description: In the wake of Dallas moving on from Jason Kidd, there have been rumblings of both Duke's Jon Scheyer and Michigan's Dusty May being linked to the vacancy. But ever since Masai Ujiri was hired to run basketball operations last month, the belief around the league is that the most likely outcome for Dallas is elevating one of the NBA's rising assistants. That's what Ujiri did the other two times he hired a coach, both with the Toronto Raptors: In 2018, he replaced Dwane Casey with Nick Nurse. In 2

item.title: Cam Johnson: I think the Knicks coaches are doing a …
item.description: Cam Johnson: I think the Knicks coaches are doing a really good job managing the series. I think the Knicks have shown a lot of mental resolve and togetherness that is enough to take you over the top in an entire playoff run and then especially in the finals. So in a sense they definitely deserve to be up 3-1.

item.title: 'Significant roster change' coming for Timberwolves?
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: What I think is going to happen is I do think there's going to be some sort of fairly significant roster change so that the starting lineup on opening night next season is not going to be the same starting lineup that finished this season.

item.title: You're hearing Kawhi Leonard. You're hearing maybe a …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: If I were to handicap all of the possibilities out there, I do think that Giannis is a really hard one to pull off. I think Kyrie is a more realistic possibility. You are hearing some other names start to just get kind of thrown around the rumor mill. You're hearing Kawhi Leonard. You're hearing maybe a little bit of Jalen Suggs, a few other names.

item.title: But throughout conversations with sources during the …
item.description: This time feels different. Bucks co-owner Jimmy Haslam has plainly said that either Giannis Antetokounmpo will be moved by the draft or the franchise will keep building around him, which would require a different set of moves. But throughout conversations with sources during the Finals, it appears Milwaukee has a new level of motivation to find a deal. And the rest of the league is waiting to see what will happen. "It seems like everyone else's business is on hold until we see what happens with

item.title: That led multiple observers from other teams to …
item.description: When talking with league insiders in the aftermath of Game 4, there was an emphasis on Spurs rookie guard Dylan Harper, and the potential for him to have greater involvement in the series moving forward, particularly after some late-game issues for De'Aaron Fox. Like throughout the regular season, Harper has come off the bench throughout the Finals, averaging 16.3 points and 6.8 rebounds in the series thus far, including 21 points on 8-for-12 shooting in 32 minutes in Game 4. But Harper was larg

item.title: There's a very real chance Jalen Brunson will win MVP …
item.description: Tim Bontemps: There's a very real chance Jalen Brunson will win MVP of the series if let's say the Knicks win in five or six and he has an incredible game and OG Anunoby has you know 12 or 15 points and is okay. Like sure he could win, but as of right now, Tim listed off the stats before, OG has one of the most iconic shots in NBA history. If the Knicks win the series, he's playing out of his mind. He's guarding De’Aaron Fox at the other end all the time. Like, as of right now, he would be my pi

item.title: Jalen Brunson always has just been a master of angles, …
item.description: Myles Turner: Jalen Brunson always has just been a master of angles, you know what I mean? Even had his height, even had his stature, his center of gravity is real low. Obviously he can get to wherever he wants with his handle or whatnot, but he just reads the floor really well. Like in today's day and age, like a pure point guard is just so hard to come across. there's only a few that are left in the league.

item.title: Jalen Brunson was asked during these finals what he …
item.description: Jalen Brunson was asked during these finals what he thought teams and scouts missed on him going through the draft process. With a smirk, he uttered one word: “Everything.” “I knew through college, but I really knew when he signed (with the Knicks),” Bridges, who played with Brunson at Villanova, said when asked when he realized his longtime friend would become an NBA star. “I knew what he was going to do, especially in the league we play in. Him having the ball and being able to be ball dominan

item.title: As one person with the organization defined the term, …
item.description: The scout realized a trait of Brunson’s that day, one that then-Mavericks general manager Donnie Nelson already valued when he insisted on plucking Jalen Brunson with that 33rd overall pick. Brunson, he told colleagues, had “gold dust” on him. The descriptor made its way around the Mavs scouting department. As one person with the organization defined the term, “He just f—ing wins.” Brunson, as plenty around him put it, was covered in gold dust. Especially these days, with a 3-1 lead in the NBA F

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns called passing the ball “one of my …
item.description: Karl-Anthony Towns called passing the ball “one of my greatest joys” because it makes his teammates happy. In the end, this Towns quote explains why the center is now viewed differently around the league and around the city he’s always wanted to play for: “I’ve always had to change the way I play so it could be most beneficial for the team. Taking all that experience this year, I’ve had to do it on the fly. It wasn’t game by game. It’s been quarter by quarter. That comes with experience, and jus

item.title: “It's straight,” Mitchell …
item.description: Ahead of Saturday night's Game 5, Mitchell Robinson was asked about the status of his injured finger as the series shifts to a potential close-out game. “It's straight,” Mitchell Robinson told reporters during the Knicks' media availability. “It's alright. I've got an appointment in the morning. It's alright. Looks well. I'm not worried about it.”

item.title: “He’s going to explode,” Swain told R.org. “He’s going …
item.description: After marveling at Caleb Wilson’s coachability, positional versatility and increasing height as just an eighth grader, Robert Swain already projected he would become a coveted NBA prospect. Swain, who trained Wilson since he was 10 years old at Swain Basketball Academy in Hapeville, Georgia, has become clairvoyant again. In an interview with R.org, Swain predicted that Wilson can mimic Magic Johnson’s playmaking, Paul George’s mid-range game and Kevin Garnett’s rebounding. Wilson has become a p

item.title: You’ve worked with Caleb for a while now. But what has …
item.description: Interview with Caleb Wilson’s Trainer, Robert Swain You’ve worked with Caleb for a while now. But what has the last few weeks of work been like with him as he gets ready for the Draft? Swain: “It’s been amazing. It’s been a lot of hard work. He’s been with me for a week-and-a-half. Then he has gone out to Los Angeles to work with his agency [Klutch Sports]. He’ll be back in about four days. Then we’re going to work out for four straight days prior to the Draft. The preparation has been outstandi

item.title: “You have to take it,” O’Neal told The Post. “If he …
item.description: This postseason Victor Wembanyama has seemingly quieted any talk about who’s the next face of the league, if he hasn’t already snatched that ceremonial title from James, who has held it for two decades. Or has he? “You have to take it,” O’Neal told The Post. “If he wins a championship, yes. If [Jalen] Brunson wins a championship, it’ll be Brunson. Face of the league ain’t something you pass on. You’ve got to go take it.”

item.title: “I’d probably go with [LeBron James] because Bron — …
item.description: Shaquille O’Neal won four championships and an MVP Award over his 19 seasons in the NBA, and has since studied it closely as an analyst. So whom would he select if he were a general manager and could choose any player — past or present — with the No. 1 pick? “Me,” O’Neal told The Post. Excluding himself? “I’d probably go with [LeBron James] because Bron — and I know this is a Kobe [Bryant] comparison — Bron had it when he came in,” O’Neal told The Post. “It took Kobe two or three years to ge

item.title: Like the Mets in 1986, more work remains for the …
item.description: Like the Mets in 1986, more work remains for the Knicks. They are one win away from their first title since 1973, thanks to Anunoby’s stunning putback. “The upside is that people remember it, and they want to talk about it 40 years ago as if it just happened yesterday,” Wilson, 70, said. “The down part about it is that people want to know what you were thinking, and you can’t explain it. “(Anunoby) was just playing basketball and did what he was always told to do: go to the ball. Just like what

item.title: Sam Mitchell: 'Kevin Garnett was very superstitious about everything'
item.description: Sam Mitchell: Kevin Garnett was very superstitious about everything. Everything before every game had to go in a certain order at a certain time. What he ate, what he drank, the way the hot packs were put on his knees. Clayton Wilson, our equipment manager, had to write something on his taped ankles before every game at a certain time. Like, it was routine. So he had this thing about a $2 bill putting in his sock some kind of… and I collected it. What he didn't realize, he didn't know that. So w

item.title: “Flip, I got to get Clayton,” and Flip was just… …
item.description: Sam Mitchell: Clayton would be like ‘KG, Mitch got a bunch of $2 bills! I guarantee you he found yours.’ And KG just going ‘oh…’. So I go in my wallet. I pull out like 15 $2 bills I got folded up. I’m like, "I've been collecting these since the day they came out." And but again, you have to understand, he couldn't play. He would not play. I remember a game Clayton was busy. Couldn't write on his tape. KG called a timeout 2 minutes into the game. Flip like, "What are you…!” “Flip, I got to get Cl

item.title: The finances of sports have gotten to a place where …
item.description: But this takeover phenomenon underscores one of the pronounced dividing lines in America: economics. The finances of sports have gotten to a place where presence is as much about purchasing power as it is about passion. It’s a distorted reality produced by an industry governed by billionaires. Knicks fans’ advantage exists in size and wealth. It’s a numbers game. By sheer virtue of having nine million people in New York City, and the largest collection of millionaires in America, they simply hav

item.title: Knicks fans were heaving half-court shots to reach San …
item.description: Knicks fans were heaving half-court shots to reach San Antonio ahead of New York’s potentially championship-clinching showdown with the Spurs after stormy weather canceled flights Friday. Orange and blue-clad New Yorkers were ready to invade the second-rate Texas city for Saturday night’s Game 5 of the NBA Finals, but travel plans were thrown into chaos thanks to a raging thunderstorm that swept through the tristate area. Brooklyn resident Toby Hyde was supposed to board a 5:33 p.m. flight to Sa

item.title: “But quickly forget that most assume otherwise. Also.. …
item.description: “But quickly forget that most assume otherwise. Also.. it’s a basketball game.” Some believed Selena Gomez was shading Taylor Swift with her “lol” comment on social media after the Knicks rallied from being down by 29 for a 107-106 win over the Spurs. “Mad respect for the game!! Congrats to the peeps that represent! What a come back,” Gomez wrote over a screenshot of an NBA article of the New York’s historic comeback. “So funny how some are all the sudden fans though lol.”

item.title: The New York Police Department is seeking information …
item.description: The New York Police Department is seeking information on a man suspected of beating a 17-year-old into a coma Wednesday after Game 4 of the NBA Finals. An NYPD statement said a group approached the teen outside Madison Square Garden near 237 W 35th St., and an argument ensued. Police posted a picture of a suspect who allegedly punched and kicked the victim, who suffered a seizure before lapsing into a coma. Police did not release an update on the teen’s condition.

item.title: Draymond Green on Victor Wembanyama: European players are a bit dirty
item.description: Draymond Green: Victor Wembanyama is a great player. Victor is a European player. And I've been on record saying European players are dirty and everybody's like Draymond shouldn't say that. And I told y'all no Draymond will [expletive] you up. I'm not dirty. It's a completely different thing. And I don't think anyone that I play against would tell you, man, he dirty. No, I ain't dirty. That's a different thing. I will mess you up though. Victor Wembanyama is a European player. They are a bit dir

item.title: Draymond Green on Victor Wembanyama: He's been shown a …
item.description: Draymond Green on Victor Wembanyama: He's been shown a lot of grace. He probably should be suspended at some point, or have been already. He’s been shown a lot of grace, but I agree with the grace. We want to see Victor Wembanyama and the San Antonio Spurs versus the New York Knicks, not the San Antonio Spurs sans Victor versus New York Knicks. So, I agree. And I wish the NBA would just come out and say, ‘Yeah, in most cases, this probably should be a suspension, but it's not because we want our

item.title: Officials for Saturday’s Knicks-Spurs Game 5: Scott …
item.description: Underdog NBA: Officials for Saturday’s Knicks-Spurs Game 5: Scott Foster James Capers Tyler Ford Mitchell Ervin (alternate)

item.title: Caleb Wilson defended his outside shot multiple times …
item.description: Caleb Wilson defended his outside shot multiple times and, interestingly enough, gave his strongest case when asked what NBA teams were harping on in combine interviews. “People don’t think I can really shoot, which is kind of funny to me. I really work on it and I can shoot. I didn’t have the best numbers this year, but it was an interesting situation because that’s not what I was asked to do at the college I was at. I was just doing what I needed to do to help us win. People that say I can’t s

item.title: Dwyane Wade: New York City is buzzing because of what the Knicks are doing
item.description: Dwyane Wade wouldn’t make a prediction on whether the Knicks will win their first championship in 53 years now that they stand just one win away, but he noticed the effect the team is having on New York City. “I don’t know,” he told Page Six of the Knicks’ chances to win the title. “I’m just a fan watching. I just want to see great basketball. Whoever wins deserves it, both these teams obviously deserve to win. The Knicks, we here in the city, the city is buzzing, it’s hot, not just because of t

item.title: Down 29, to come back versus a team that seemed to be …
item.description: With 1,054 regular-season games and 177 postseason contests, it takes a lot to leave Dwyane Wade feeling “shocked.” And that’s exactly what the Knicks did Wednesday in Game 4 when they set an NBA Finals record by rallying from a 29-point deficit to stun the Spurs and grab a 3-1 series lead. “I was just stunned and shocked like — amazed,” the Basketball Hall of Famer told Page Six in an exclusive interview Friday at the Tribeca Festival in New York City. “I know, I’ve been in the Finals a few tim

item.title: The San Antonio Spurs have slapped a ticket …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs have slapped a ticket restriction on NBA Finals games in a pathetic bid to keep New York fans from flooding the Frost Bank Center. Knicks faithful trying to get into tonight’s Game 5 contest – which could hand New York its first NBA title in 53 years – are being greeted with a warning from Ticketmaster. “Frost Bank Center is located in San Antonio, Texas . Sales to this event will be restricted to customers residing within a 150-mile radius of Frost Bank Center,” the note r

item.title: A Mexican tax official has fouled out of his state …
item.description: A Mexican tax official has fouled out of his state government post. Héctor Contreras Mercader abruptly resigned Thursday as director of the state of Quintana Roo’s Tax Administration Service after a firestorm erupted over how he landed pricey seats near President Trump to watch the Knicks take on the Spurs at Game 3 of the NBA Finals at Madison Square Garden. Photographers caught Contreras, sporting a Knicks star Jalen Brunson jersey, and his son, wearing a Karl-Anthony Towns jersey, in section

item.title: Georgine Ratelband’s $295 “I’m a New York Knicks Girl” …
item.description: A little-known Dutch designer scored big after a friend wore her Knicks T-shirt to Madison Square Garden — and it caught the attention of owner Jim Dolan’s pal. Georgine Ratelband’s $295 “I’m a New York Knicks Girl” tee and five other items from her luxury line are now being sold at the MSG Team Store — debuting just weeks before the team made it to the NBA Finals. “It’s great timing,” Ratelband, 37, told The Post. “I’m ecstatic. I’m over the moon. I’m so proud.” Items from her Knicks collection

item.title: Beatrice Reilly-Jordon, a Staten Island widow and …
item.description: There’s an angel looking out for the New York Knicks. Beatrice Reilly-Jordon, a Staten Island widow and devoted Knicks fan, left a bit of her late husband’s ashes on an empty seat at Madison Square Garden — and her family believes his spirit has been lifting the Knicks as they strive for their first championship in 53 years. “She sprinkled a little on an empty seat next to her at Madison Square Garden recently, so he could enjoy the game with her, watching from heaven,” the couple’s daughter Dom

item.title: James Harden arrested in Houston for unlawfully carrying a weapon
item.description: NBA superstar James Harden was arrested in Houston on Saturday morning. The 36-year-old was taken into custody by the Houston Police Department at 3:41 a.m., according to court records obtained by The California Post, and booked on one misdemeanor charge of unlawful carrying weapons.

item.title: Harden, a former Los Angeles Clippers guard who now …
item.description: Harden, a former Los Angeles Clippers guard who now plays for the Cleveland Cavaliers, was accused of “unlawfully, intentionally and knowingly” having a handgun in his vehicle, according to a copy of the complaint. Authorities alleged in hte document that “the handgun was in plain view” and “was not carried in a holster.”

item.title: James Dolan went on New York sports-talk radio and …
item.description: On Wednesday afternoon, hours before his team’s historic 29-point comeback that gave them a commanding 3–1 series lead, James Dolan went on New York sports-talk radio and laced into Zohran Mamdani and police commissioner Jessica Tisch. “I don’t think they have faith in their own police force,” Dolan told Craig Carton. “We do! We know they know what they’re doing. Honestly, the mayor’s office and the commissioner do not have the experience to do this. They’ve never managed anything like this befo

item.title: Mamdani spokesperson Joe Calvello said Wednesday that …
item.description: The mayor ultimately made the game available on LinkNYC kiosks around the city. The sides released a flurry of statements throughout the week. Mamdani spokesperson Joe Calvello said Wednesday that Dolan “seems hell bent on making sure the city has rancid vibes for the finals run.” And with less than an hour before tip-off, MSG released a statement saying the arena and team “declined to use the permit granted by Mayor Mamdani’s office” because of the small number of fans allowed to gather. “We di

item.title: The Knicks will host watch parties at Radio City Music …
item.description: The Knicks will host watch parties at Radio City Music Hall, Plaza33 outside Madison Square Garden and Wollman Rink in Central Park for Game 5 of the 2026 NBA Finals. The Knicks will face the San Antonio Spurs in Game 5 on Saturday, June 13. Tipoff is scheduled for 8:30 p.m.

item.title: Timberwolves unlikely to trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Chris Hine: My quick $0.02 on yesterday … that Giannis Antetokounmpo update felt like Milwaukee trying to drum up more of a market at the 11th hour before having to make a call. (At least that’s how it was viewed after checking in w/ some folks). Get the sense any Wolves-Giannis deal still very unlikely.

item.title: A Knicks fan is crying foul after he booked a …
item.description: A Knicks fan is crying foul after he booked a penthouse at the Ritz-Carlton for Game 4 of the NBA Finals — but was allegedly kicked out of the $20,000 hotel room because of a demand from San Antonio Spurs superstar Victor Wembanyama. Pennsylvanian Phil Godlewski was with a party of six, who spent a total of $750,000 on tickets to Wednesday’s game — and rented out one of the two penthouse suites on the 41st floor of the Ritz-Carlton on West 28th Street in NoMad.

item.title: “I hear Ronnie say five words. That’s all he said. …
item.description: The Scranton entrepreneur — who was with his 11 and 9-year-old sons and his friend Ron Pavalonis, and his son — wasn’t told that Wemby was staying in the other penthouse suite. When Pavalonis was entering his room around 5 p.m., he said he saw Wemby exiting his own suite — presumably on his way to Madison Square Garden, and greeted the Spurs star player. “I hear Ronnie say five words. That’s all he said. ‘Good luck tonight, big guy,'” Godlewski, 43, told The Post.

item.title: “I said, ‘What? Why?’ And she said, ‘Because we were …
item.description: 10 minutes later, they got a knock on the door from the hotel’s director of rooms operations, Jean Choi — which they did not hear since their room was so big. So Choi — along with three men in black suits — just let themselves in, Godlewski claimed. “They opened the door illegally, and they actually entered the vestibule area. And she’s yelling from within the room, ‘Mr. Godlewski!’ I finally hear her and walk to the door and that’s when she told me, ‘I’m sorry, but you guys are going to have to

item.title: James Harden released from jail, scheduled to appear in court on June 22
item.description: Former Houston Rockets star James Harden was freed from jail Saturday morning after he was arrested on a misdemeanor gun violation hours earlier. According to Harris County records, Harden is accused of carrying an unconcealed weapon in a vehicle. Harden, 36, was driving a Mercedes that was among a group of five vehicles traveling through downtown at about 3:40 a.m. When one of the vehicles was stopped by police near 1600 Jefferson, Harden drove up behind in a Mercedes sedan, according to court

item.title: He was released on a general order bond a short time …
item.description: During an interaction with police, according to court records, the officer saw a gun in the cup holder of the car Harden was driving. Harden told police it was his, according to court records, and was arrested and booked at Harris County Jail. He was released on a general order bond a short time later with the promise that he'll appear in court on June 22.

item.title: Italian big man Luigi Suigo withdraws from draft to play for Villanova
item.description: Dushawn London: NEWS: Villanova has landed a commitment from 7-3 Italian big man Luigi Suigo, source tells @247Sports

item.title: Richie Guerin told The Post this week he doesn’t get …
item.description: The oldest living New York Knick is keeping his cool during the team’s historic — and heart-stopping –championship run. Hall of Famer Richie Guerin told The Post this week he doesn’t get butterflies seeing his former team on the brink of their first championship title since 1973. “Not really. Because, you know, I’ve been there too many times before — to watch a team win a game by effort and lose a game by the other team’s effort,” said the 94-year-old Guerin, who played for the Knicks from 1956

item.title: The Bronx native, who played for Iona College and …
item.description: The Bronx native, who played for Iona College and served as an officer in the Marine Corps, was at Game 3 at Madison Square Garden on Monday with his daughter as a guest of the organization, which announced him on the jumbotron. “It worked out very nice to have an enjoyable evening. Everything nice, but winning the game,” the six-time NBA All-Star said. “Monday, it just happened that the other team did a little bit better down the stretch and was able to come away with the win, just like the Kni

item.title: “Today’s athlete is a lot better and physically …
item.description: “Today’s athlete is a lot better and physically stronger than the athletes of the era that I played in, in any sport. They play a lot more games than we did in the off-season,” he said. He also called the Spurs’ 7′ 4″ powerhouse and now-New York archenemy Victor Wembanyama “a very strong, talented player,” but explained that in his era, a man from France would never play in the NBA. “That’s the other part of the game today, where people are coming into the league from Europe,” he said.

item.title: MSG denies report Spurs are revoking Game 5 tickets to Knicks fans
item.description: Ian Bagley: MSG Sports statement on Game 5 tickets says MSG has confirmed with Spurs ownership that Spurs will not be revoking any Knicks fans’ tickets for tonight.

item.title: First-round pick candidate Sergio De Larrea keeps his name in the 2026 draft
item.description: Sergio de Larrea remains in the 2026 NBA Draft. The Valencia Basket point guard, who has had a great season with Pedro Martínez’s team, has not withdrawn his name from the Draft, which will be held on June 23 and 24, as reported by Jonathan Givony.

item.title: Francesco Ferrari officially withdrawn from the NBA …
item.description: Orazio Cauchi: Francesco Ferrari officially withdrawn from the NBA Draft 2026. He had the opportunity to test the waters and interviewed with Detroit, Atlanta, and Milwaukee. Those teams will continue to monitor him closely as he takes on an important role with Virtus Bologna next season.

item.title: Draymond Green on Victor Wembanyama: He probably should have been suspended at some point already
item.description: NBA Courtside: Draymond Green believes Wemby should’ve been suspended already this finals but the NBA is showing him ‘grace’: “I will say he’s been shown a lot of grace. He probably should be suspended at some point or have been already. But I agree with the Grace” (Via @DraymondShow )

item.title: “I would say, like five assists a game maybe to start …
item.description: Already, Lendeborg can see his fit on the Warriors and how he would make an impact from Day 1 if they were to take him with the No. 11 pick. His answer wasn’t about himself. More so, it was about how he can help everybody else. “I would say, like five assists a game maybe to start off,” Lendeborg said Thursday after his Warriors workout. “A lot of defense, fastbreak opportunities for me. Depending on if I’m here or anywhere else, my role will be a lot different. But if I was here, I’ll be more l

item.title: "That dude is the real deal. We couldn't do anything …
item.description: "I can't say enough about Peter Suder," Tennessee coach Rick Barnes said at the time. "That dude is the real deal. We couldn't do anything with him. He was terrific."

item.title: Being a team captain the last two or three years …
item.description: "I'd also say my communication skills and leadership improved. Being a team captain the last two or three years really helped with that, and this past year I developed a lot more ball-handling and point guard tendencies." Suder said. "My coaching staff has been great these past two years. They got me in the gym and helped change a lot about my game. They came to the combine, checked in on me every day, and have stayed involved throughout the process. It's really a family feel."

item.title: "One of the main things I've learned is that it's your …
item.description: "I've done 17 workouts so far and have five more through June 20," Suder stated. "One of the main things I've learned is that it's your job now. That's how it's treated at the next level. It's not really like recruiting in college. You go in, show what you can do, and then move on to the next workout. The main thing I'm working on right now is movement shooting and different types of catch-and-shoot situations. I'm just trying to perfect that skill for the next level."

item.title: A new name that is very close to signing a contract …
item.description: A new name that is very close to signing a contract with Asvel comes from the NBA. As Meridian Sport has learned, Riley Minix is ​​on the verge of an agreement with the team from Villeurbanne. The American is 25 years old, and was part of San Antonio and Cleveland , as well as their development teams, where he primarily received minutes.

item.title: Carter Bryant on Victor Wembanyama: 'I'm super grateful for Vic'
item.description: "Me and Vic sit next to each other on the plane, so we've had a ton of conversations, whether that was easy conversations, hard conversations, just me checking up on him, him checking up on me," Bryant said. "Sometimes you need a brother to lean on, somebody to hug and somebody to spill out your emotions to and your words to. "We've both been there for each other. I'm super grateful for Vic."

item.title: Bryant did just that, so in Game 7, Wembanyama was …
item.description: Sensing Bryant needed consoling, Wembanyama put his arms on his friend's shoulder's before tapping him on the chest in a "shake it off, stay strong" gesture. Bryant did just that, so in Game 7, Wembanyama was caught on camera telling Bryant "I'm proud of you" while they were on the bench together. "I'm super grateful for Vic," Bryant said. "Vic is an A-1 human being. He's a star on the court, but him as a human being is so much more than he could ever be on the court. And I mean that in the best

item.title: “Very punctual. Very consistent on a day-to-day basis. …
item.description: It'll take plenty of work, but Thomas Scott is willing to put that work in. He has seen hard work all of his life watching his dad. It’s one of the things his father passed down to him. “I’ve always observed my dad being very disciplined and always moving as a professional,” he said. “Very punctual. Very consistent on a day-to-day basis. Somebody that knew how to lock in and stay very focused. He has a lot of personality off the court. But when it came down to working, his work ethic is unmatche

item.title: “My son’s time in New Orleans is when he really …
item.description: “Life has a way of doing that to you and taking you full circle,” Byron Scott said. “My son’s time in New Orleans is when he really started getting the bug about getting involved in basketball. One thing about him is he’s worked his (butt) for years to get in position to do something like this. I’m so proud and happy for him."

item.title: We are in contact with James and his representation …
item.description: Chris Mannix: Cavs say they are "aware of the arrest of James Harden this morning and are in the process of gathering additional information. We are in contact with James and his representation and will continue to monitor developments as they become available."

item.title: Adam Silver on eight different champions in eight years: It's a new era of basketball, we want a league where every team has a chance
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Adam Silver: "It's a new era of NBA basketball. This will be the 8th different team that won a championship over the last 8 years. We've always talked about this parity of opportunity where teams can win multiple championships in a row? Great. But we want a league where every team has an opportunity to compete...it just really speaks to the state of NBA basketball and the state of the future of the game. There's just so much talent everywhere"

item.title: He’s a pleasure to talk to. He’s ever worldly. He’s …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Adam Silver on Wemby: “I’m amazed at Victor. Not just his play on the floor, but he’s such a curious young man. He’s a pleasure to talk to. He’s ever worldly. He’s really dedicated to his craft. He’s got such a bright future ahead of him.”

item.title: I don't know if that's habit, superstition, bathroom, …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Mitch Johnson on Wemby not being on the court for the national anthem in the last 2 games: "I think there's plenty of players that are not on the floor during the anthem. I don't know if that's habit, superstition, bathroom, whatever it may be. I don't foresee anything looking into that at all"

item.title: "It's hard, but you just keep trying to tell your guys …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mike Brown on how to keep the guys locked in after such a crazy Game 4: "It's hard, but you just keep trying to tell your guys to stay present, stay present, stay present, and you know, for us, another thing that we've talked a lot about with our guys is possession over outcome, possession over outcome, just worry about the next possession, lock into the details, play as hard as you can, give everything you can to that next possession, then don't worry about the outcome of the game

item.title: "For me, just stuck to my normal routine, went to …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mike Brown on how he spent last night: "For me, just stuck to my normal routine, went to dinner with my wife and my two step kids, came back, did a little work, watched a little bit of a series called Kings and Conquer, I think it's called Kings and Conquer. I finished the last episode last night, it's a really good series, but my wife told me the main guy in there is from Game of Thrones, and I didn't know that until at the end of the series, and then I recognized him, but finished

item.title: We see them, you know, walking around the area that …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mike Brown on Knicks fans traveling: "Our fans have been fantastic all year, and during the playoffs they've tried to show up and show out wherever we are, not just in the arena. We see them, you know, walking around the area that we're staying in. We see them in the hotel, and just to know that they're, as you know, enthused and rabid as they are. It's second to none. We love having our fans, we love having them in the building, we love having them around, and so hopefully they'll

item.title: “They told us in our meeting it’s basically going to …
item.description: Stefan Bondy: Overheard a security guard at Frost Bank Center: “They told us in our meeting it’s basically going to be a New York Knicks home game.”

item.title: Thunder say Nikola Topić will be ready for the start …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Thunder say Nikola Topić will be ready for the start of training camp after undergoing a minimally invasive lumbar microdiscectomy procedure.

item.title: Franz Wagner on playing in Berlin game: I probably shouldn't have played, but I don't really regret the decision to play
item.description: Injuries hit you hard this past year. How tough was that mentally and physically? Looking back, do you regret playing in the Berlin game, or did it worsen the injury? Franz Wagner: It was very challenging. At the end of the day, if we're not healthy as athletes, we can't do our jobs, so that's the most important thing. And so there's always that pressure that you need to be out there and perform and help the team out. So not being able to do that and not seeing the results in rehab, too, when yo

item.title: And every great team has gone through disappointments …
item.description: After consecutive premature playoff exits, what lessons are you taking from these past few years? Are there specific routines you're building or changing to help get the team to the next level? Franz Wagner: The hope is that you learn these lessons once, and then you've got it down the next year. I can just say, we got a really talented roster. We got guys with high character and dudes that want to work hard and get better. And that's a really good starting point. And every great team has gone t

item.title: Hopefully we can continue to improve in terms of our …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Mitch Johnson on the vibe before the game: "Went through shoot around and our normal prep for the game tonight, and tonight's the most important game of our season, and we're playing the same opponent now for the fifth straight time, and hopefully we can continue to improve in terms of our execution and finishing out games and playing more to our standard for more of the 48 minutes tonight."

item.title: "We know we can do that. We have to win the next 48"
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Wemby on trying to overcome a 3-1 deficit: "We need to take it one game at a time" Haynes: "Not even thinking about the fact that we have to win three?" "We know we can do that. We have to win the next 48"

item.title: "I haven't heard any of them so I don't have to handle …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Wemby on handling criticism: "I haven't heard any of them so I don't have to handle them. You're the first one to tell me about it"

item.description: Michael C. Wright: Luke Kornet will be available tonight, per Mitch Johnson.

item.title: German guard Jack Kayil is keeping his name in the …
item.description: German guard Jack Kayil is keeping his name in the 2026 NBA Draft as an Early Entry Candidate. Kayil is currently playing for Alba Berlin and is in the midst of the German BBL against Bayern Munich.

item.title: Jalen Brunson on some Knicks' fans being disrespectful: 'I love our fans, but we've got to be better'
item.description: Speaking to NBC News' Jesse Kirsch, Brunson urged Knicks fans to be more disciplined and decent, as New York's celebration of making it back to the Finals stage for the first time in 27 years has been marred by violence and several arrests. "You never want to be disrespectful. Throwing stuff and saying things that are not part of the game is considered disrespectful," the Knicks superstar said. "I love our fans, but we've got to be better."

item.title: Whatever Boston is doing, I promise you, we don’t …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: “Boston doesn’t deal with player agents. Boston does deals with teams. Boston keeps all of their business quiet… Boston’s business doesn’t get out there. Whatever Boston is doing, I promise you, we don’t know.” - @WindhorstESPN (h/t @ShotCoverage)

item.title: San Antonio Spurs are the 1st team in NBA Finals …
item.description: Justin Russo: San Antonio Spurs are the 1st team in NBA Finals history to lead by double-digits at the end of the first quarter 3 different times in the same series.

item.title: Spurs are the first team in Finals history to lead by …
item.description: Dan Weiss: Spurs are the first team in Finals history to lead by 10+ points in the first quarter in the first 5 games. 13 points for NY is the fewest since Golden State had 11 in the 4th quarter in Game 3 of the 2022 Finals.

item.title: New York's 13 first-quarter points are tied for the …
item.description: ESPN Insights: Rough start for the Knicks in Game 5 New York's 13 first-quarter points are tied for the second-fewest in an NBA Finals opening quarter of the shot-clock era.

item.title: Those 13 points by the Knicks are the fewest scored by …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Those 13 points by the Knicks are the fewest scored by any team in the 1st quarter of a finals game since Warriors had 11 in Game 6 of the 2016 finals. GSW were down 20 after that 1Q. Before that no team had scored 13 or fewer in the 1Q in the finals since the Bucks in 1974.

item.title: Only Michael Jordan scored a bigger share of his team's points in a Finals game than Jalen Brunson tonight
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Jalen Brunson scored 47.9 percent of his team's points today. It's the second-best mark in NBA Finals history... only behind Michael Jordan the day he won his sixth title.

item.title: To have 45 points in a close out Finals victory …
item.description: Tim Bontemps: From the great @StatsWilliams: To have 45 points in a close out Finals victory all-time: Michael Jordan, Giannis Antetokounmpo, Bob Pettit … And, now, Jalen Brunson. What a performance.

item.title: Jalen Brunson is the second-shortest Finals MVP in NBA …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Not official yet, but Jalen Brunson is the second-shortest Finals MVP in NBA history (behind Isiah Thomas).

item.description: Chris Haynes: The Knicks are flying back to New York tonight to finish celebrating, sources tell me.

item.description: Ian Begley: Behind 45 points from Jalen Brunson, Knicks win in San Antonio to clinch franchise's first NBA title in 53 years. Masterpiece from the NYK captain, who had 15 in fourth to help NYK rebound from 7-pt deficit. Massive offensive rebound from Mitchell Robinson late helped seal game

item.description: HoopsHype: The Knicks win the NBA title 53 years later in the most impressive fashion. They now have the best point differential in NBA playoffs history: +283 to 2017 Warriors' +230 Congratulations.

item.description: Bobby Marks: New York accomplished the rare feat of facing 0 elimination games in these playoffs. 16-3 4-0 in clutch games during the Finals

item.description: Ian Begley: Jalen Brunson NBA Finals MVP by a unanimous vote:

item.title: Brunson: "My confidence comes from my work ethic. …
item.description: NBA Base: Jalen Brunson: "My confidence comes from my work ethic. Every time I had the ball, all I could think about is all the hours in the summer... making this a reality. So, whenever I had the ball, I'm just thinking about just me alone in the gym."

item.title: Kendrick Perkins says Jalen Brunson is already the …
item.description: NBA Base: Kendrick Perkins says Jalen Brunson is already the greatest Knick of ALL-TIME and ranks this squad as a top-5 team in NBA history.

item.title: Mitch Johnson: “We weren’t ready to win an NBA …
item.description: Spurs Nation: Spurs coach Mitch Johnson: “We weren’t ready to win an NBA championship. The better team won. We did a lot of good things and we didn’t finish the job. That's what it is.”

item.title: Mitch keeping perspective after the disappointment of …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Mitch keeping perspective after the disappointment of falling short in quest for title: “I don't think anybody other than the people in that room expected us to be here. So there was a lot of good in that & there's a lot of pain in what just happened and both things can be true.”

item.title: "Thank you mama i appreciate you getting me one" - …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: "Thank you mama i appreciate you getting me one" - Karl-Anthony Towns 🥹

item.title: Spurs can now start negotiating with Wembanyama on …
item.description: Michael C. Wright: It’s last thing on their minds right now, but the Spurs can now start negotiating with Wembanyama on that big rookie extension.

item.title: Brunson scored 45 points. What do you wish your team …
item.description: NBA Base: Q: “Coach, Jalen Brunson scored 45 points. What do you wish your team had done differently defensively against him?” Mitch Johnson: “Make him score less points.”

item.title: Devin Vassell: "First off, to the fans, just thank you …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Devin Vassell: "First off, to the fans, just thank you guys so much for supporting us, especially in my journey, being here six years from where we started, from the 20 wins that we were at to being in the Finals, they've been loyal, and I just want to say thank you to them, because without them, and the noise, and them having our backs, we're not in this situation, we're not in this position. So, just thank you to them."

item.title: Luke Kornet said it was a “joy” to be part of this …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Luke Kornet said it was a “joy” to be part of this team: “We have a lot of great players and a lot of young players and I feel like everyone bought in and were united and about as free and selfless of a group I've ever seen.”

item.title: Josh Hart on Mikal Bridges: "Forget them picks, dawg"
item.description: TSN: Josh Hart on Mikal Bridges: "Forget them picks, dawg" 🤣 #NBAFinals

item.title: Bridges walked off the floor in San Antonio to some …
item.description: Ian Begley: Mikal Bridges walked off the floor in San Antonio to some “—- those picks” chants from Knicks fans

item.title: “Eight years!” Mitchell Robson yelled as he walked …
item.description: Ian Begley: “Eight years!” Mitchell Robson yelled as he walked through the corridor and headed to the locker room. Robinson had a massive offensive rebound to help Knicks down stretch of Game 5

item.title: The previous longest *broken* drought was when Bucks …
item.description: Law Murray: The league isn't that old yet The Knicks set a record for longest title drought *broken* at 53 years. The previous longest *broken* drought was when Bucks won it in 2021, their first title in 50 years

item.title: Jason Kidd: Congratulations to @jalenbrunson1
item.description: Jason Kidd: Congratulations to @jalenbrunson1 and the @nyknicks

item.title: Knicks outscored Spurs by just 12 points total in the …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Knicks outscored Spurs by just 12 points total in the NBA Finals — tied for closest finals ever for a series that lasted 5 or fewer games. Celtics were +12 over the Mavericks in the 2024 Finals but that was skewed by a 38-point loss in Game 4. This series was tight the whole way.

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns on the court: Knicks +44. …
item.description: Henry Abbott: Over the five games of the NBA Finals Karl-Anthony Towns on the court: Knicks +44. Karl-Anthony Towns on the bench: Knicks -32.

item.title: Kwame Mamdani: Parade. Thursday. Manhattan
item.description: Mayor Zohran Kwame Mamdani: Parade. Thursday. Manhattan.

item.title: Victor Wembanyama: I'm going to have to hold that inside of me and slow down and wait
item.description: Ben Golliver: Spurs' Victor Wembanyama after losing to the Knicks in his first NBA Finals: "What I’m pissed about is that there’s probably 100 games before we can get back to the Finals. I’m going to have to hold that inside of me and slow down and wait."

item.title: Personally, in 18 months, it's been, it's been hard …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Victor Wembanyama: "I don't think we could have learned more and gained more experience in one playoff run and in one season. Personally, in 18 months, it's been, it's been hard and full of lessons."

item.title: "This doesn't compare to anything before, this is the …
item.description: Tom Petrini: Wemby: "This doesn't compare to anything before, this is the biggest lesson of my life, the biggest learning moment. I can't tell exactly what the lesson is, but we're learning from that for sure."

item.title: Spurs and Oklahoma City Thunder open as co-favorites …
item.description: The San Antonio Spurs and Oklahoma City Thunder open as co-favorites to win the 2027 NBA Finals at +250, followed by the Boston Celtics at +550. The New York Knicks, despite winning the championship on Saturday, have the fourth best odds at +700. The next block of teams have significantly longer odds beginning with the Indiana Pacers and Denver Nuggets at +2800, and the Minnesota Timbewolves, Los Angeles Lakers, Detroit Pistons and Cleveland Cavaliers at +3000.

item.title: Rochester to Sacramento Kings: 75 seasons w/o title St …
item.description: ‪Law Murray 📘‬: NBA franchises that have waited 54+ years in the NBA for a title entering the 2026-27 season: Rochester to Sacramento Kings: 75 seasons w/o title St Louis to Atlanta Hawks: 68 seasons w/o title Phoenix Suns: 58 seasons w/o title Buffalo to LA Clippers: 56 seasons w/o title

item.title: He was holding up the walkway off the court because he …
item.description: ‪Fred Katz‬: The most Karl-Anthony Towns thing of all time — he was holding up the walkway off the court because he wanted to share the trophy with Knicks fans, so he stopped and leaned into the stands to let a bunch of them touch it. They reacted like it was the best moment of their lives.

item.title: Sixth man Timothée Chalamet embraces the moment
item.description: ‪Dan Devine‬: Sixth man Timothée Chalamet embraces the moment.

item.title: Jalen Brunson: Whenever someone counted us out, we found a way to come back
item.description: Bridges and Hart -- the other two parts of the "Nova Knicks" trio that also includes Jalen Brunson, three players who were NCAA champions at Villanova and teamed up in New York to try to do the same -- combined to score 27 points. Bridges had 14, Hart 13. "I don't know what I'm feeling," Brunson said. "I'm in awe. Whenever someone counted us out, we found a way to come back and do something about it."

item.title: Josh Hart: “Nobody talks about the weight of that …
item.description: Legion Hoops: Josh Hart: “Nobody talks about the weight of that Knicks jersey... Right now, it's the lightest it's ever felt.”

item.title: “Please be safe tonight; don’t get hurt, don’t hurt …
item.description: Ian Begley: NYK owner James Dolan interrupts Josh Hart’s press conference to deliver a message to New Yorkers. “Please be safe tonight; don’t get hurt, don’t hurt anybody.. we’ll have a parade in New York on Thursday … be safe (tonight).”

item.title: If they stopped the games after 46 minutes, the Spurs …
item.description: Dean Oliver: If my count is right, if they stopped the games after 46 minutes, the Spurs would have won the series in 5 games.

item.title: the Spurs led for 69% of the NBA Finals
item.description: Esfandiar Baraheni: Insane stat by the ESPN broadcast: the Spurs led for 69% of the NBA Finals. Closest 5-game series in the Finals I can ever think of.

item.title: Lots of love and support for each other coming out of …
item.description: Kelly Iko: Lots of love and support for each other coming out of that Spurs locker room. Those guys will be around for a while.

item.title: “You did that bro.” Timothée to Jalen Brunson 🔥
item.description: NBA on ESPN: “You did that bro.” Timothée to Jalen Brunson 🔥

item.title: Out of control fans fans smashed up a cop car outside …
item.description: Out of control fans fans smashed up a cop car outside Madison Square Garden, according to shocking video. Two maniacs were seen jumping on top of the car and smashing up its front window in a video posted by Freedomnews.TV.

item.title: Brian Windhorst of ESPN dismissed the idea that the …
item.description: In his appearance on SiriusXM NBA Radio's NBA Today, Brian Windhorst of ESPN dismissed the idea that the Celtics will trade their superstar wing away. “Here’s the thing about Boston. Boston doesn’t deal with player agents. Boston does deals with teams. Boston keeps all of their business quiet," Windhorst claimed. "Boston’s business doesn’t get out there. Whatever Boston is doing, I promise you, we don’t know. The assumption that they’re going to trade Jaylen Brown, I wouldn’t assume that.”

item.title: Victor Wembanyama on losing NBA Finals: 'It's extremely painful, but I'm not running away from that'
item.description: NBA Base: Victor Wembanyama when asked about greats like Michael Jordan and LeBron James taking years to win their first ring: "It’s extremely painful. It’s painful, but I’m not running away from that. I’m using that to fuel me. I'm sure all these guys you named, they're not satisfied with being eliminated in earlier rounds or not making the playoffs, and I'm not satisfied with not winning."

item.title: Dylan Harper falls 27 points short of Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's rookie playoff scoring record
item.description: Jorge Sierra: With Spurs falling in five, Dylan Harper will not be able to beat Kareem's record for most playoff points in a rookie season. He fell 27 points short of Kareem Abdul-Jabbar's 352. By the way, Kareem set the record in just 10 playoff games.

item.title: KAT: "Shoutout to my brother Anthony Edwards talking …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: KAT: "Shoutout to my brother Anthony Edwards talking to him all the time. Those guys made me better, they made me a better leader, they made me a better player, made me the man I am today. Forever grateful for them"

item.title: Clarkson became the first NBA player with Filipino …
item.description: Jordan Clarkson has made Philippine basketball history. Clarkson became the first NBA player with Filipino descent to win a championship in the NBA, after the New York Knicks clinched the coveted title on Sunday (Manila time). The Knicks hacked out a 94-90 win over the San Antonio Spurs in Game 5 of the Finals at the Frost Bank Center in Texas.

item.title: Mitchell Robinson plans to put his truck in the …
item.description: ‪Dan Devine‬: Mitchell Robinson plans to put his truck in the championship parade. He is very excited about that.

item.title: “You know what’s amazing? Yesterday, I caught a …
item.description: ‪Fred Katz‬: Mitchell Robinson with no context: “You know what’s amazing? Yesterday, I caught a snake.”

item.title: Jeremy Sochan celebrating an NBA championship at the …
item.description: ‪Jeff McDonald‬: Jeremy Sochan celebrating an NBA championship at the Frost Bank Center, just as everyone predicted

item.title: Knicks players smoking cigars on the Spurs court after …
item.description: Legion Hoops: Knicks players smoking cigars on the Spurs court after winning an NBA championship. Unreal scenes.

item.title: Mike Brown spends time in his press conference …
item.description: Ian Begley: Mike Brown spends time in his press conference campaigning for associate head coach Chris Jent. “He will help your team at the highest level.” Knicks gravitated toward that selflessness in Brown’s first year here. Some similarities between Knicks/Brown & Yankees/1996 Joe Torre

item.title: "As happy as I've ever felt. It's pretty amazing." Ben …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "As happy as I've ever felt. It's pretty amazing." Ben Stiller is filled with joy after the Knicks become champs for the first time in 53 years ❤️

item.title: Manu Ginobili: Congrats to the @nyknicks on winning …
item.description: Manu Ginobili: Congrats to the @nyknicks on winning the NBA Finals! Coach Mike Brown, Finals MVP Jalen Brunson (an absolute beast!), and a tough, experienced group of players right in their prime got the job done. 👏👏

item.title: Mitch Johnson: 'We weren't ready to win an NBA championship'
item.description: Mitch Johnson: 'We weren't ready to win an NBA championship' Spurs Nation: Spurs coach Mitch Johnson: “We weren’t ready to win an NBA championship. The better team won. We did a lot of good things and we didn’t finish the job. That's what it is.”

item.title: Devin Vassell says Dylan Harper was frustrated with his role with Spurs
item.description: Legion Hoops: Devin Vassell says Dylan Harper was frustrated with his role and playing time with the Spurs the season. “But when we needed him most he stepped up.” (h/t @big_business_)

item.title: Harper, after embracing each of his teammates in the …
item.description: ‪Tom Orsborn‬: Dylan Harper, after embracing each of his teammates in the locker room: "We all learned a lot in this playoff run. A lot of people counted us out and we proved people wrong. It didn't go how we wanted to go, but we definitely learned a lot."

item.title: Stephon Castle on how the Knicks defense made it tough …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Stephon Castle on how the Knicks defense made it tough on him and his teammates: “They crowded the pick and rolls a lot, made us stagnant on the ball. When we tried get to isolation, they took away passing lanes too, made us play one-on-one a lot.”

item.title: Brunson comes to the podium with with the largest …
item.description: Fred Katz‬: Jalen Brunson comes to the podium with with the largest smile and his two trophies and declares: “Do I be myself or do I talk my shit?!”

item.title: Karl-Anthony Towns: "You got to get it out the mud …
item.description: NBA Base: Karl-Anthony Towns: "You got to get it out the mud sometimes. And if you from New York City and you know what it is to get it out the mud, everything ain't going to be pretty, everything ain't going to be the way you want to go. You going to get punched in the face and how you going to keep to your game plan is going to be a testament to who you are."

item.title: Landry Shamet: "It's emotional just considering …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Landry Shamet: "It's emotional just considering everything: Training camp deal 2 years in a row on the verge being out of the league and injuries, ups and downs man. It's crazy. I'm just really, really grateful and proud of myself for not giving up"

item.title: Spurs somehow won the clutch time minutes in this …
item.description: Jace frederick: Fun fact: Spurs somehow won the clutch time minutes in this series 39-38 Shot 38% in the clutch to New York’s 32%

item.title: Jose Alvarado on Jalen Brunson: "He's the best player, …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Jose Alvarado on Jalen Brunson: "He's the best player, he won MVP. It's no debate, it's no question about that"

item.title: Brunson: "I just want to see something to the …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Jalen Brunson: "I just want to see something to the Swifties: She's a really good one. Cut her some slack. It's all good I promise"

item.title: Mike Brown on Jalen Brunson's $113 million paycut: "He …
item.description: Alex Kennedy: Mike Brown on Jalen Brunson's $113 million paycut: "He understands what winning is about. He comes and he probably takes a pay cut that I wouldn't have taken. Every time they would've thrown that number in front of me, I would have said 'no,' and I feel like I'm a good guy. He set the bar before he even stepped on the floor every time it came to renegotiate a deal with him. That set the standard. Now, when you take his play into account, it's off the charts, man... I hope Pat [Ewin

item.title: One Knicks executive arrived prepared for the moment, …
item.description: The bottles had already popped. The floor of the San Antonio Spurs’ visiting locker room was soaked in champagne. One Knicks executive arrived prepared for the moment, carrying with him a brown, wooden box of 53 celebratory cigars, one for each year that had passed since the organization’s last title.
