Can create more room if they move on from Haywood Highsmith and/or Jamaree Bouyea. It'll get tight to avoid the first apron if they re-sign Mark Williams and Jordan Goodwin.

item.title: Collin Gillespie agrees to four-year, $48 million deal to return to Phoenix
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard Collin Gillespie intends to sign a four-year, $48 million deal to return to the Phoenix Suns, sources tell ESPN. Gillespie spent three straight seasons on two-way contracts after going undrafted out of Villanova in 2022 and now earns a lucrative long-term deal.

item.title: Gillespie’s agents at Excel Sports Management, Jordan …
item.description: Shams Charania: Gillespie’s agents at Excel Sports Management, Jordan Gertler and James Dunleavy, negotiated a deal with Suns executives after teams became eligible to talk to their own free agents last weekend. The sides came together on a deal for a guard who has emerged as a significant member and success story of the team's new culture around grit and toughness. Last season, Gillespie had a breakout campaign setting a Suns franchise record for 3-pointers with 232 and averaging career-highs i

item.title: Isaiah Hartenstein expected to remain in OKC
item.description: Starting center Isaiah Hartenstein has a $28.5 million team option for the upcoming season. He’s expected to remain in Oklahoma City. The question among executives around the league is whether the Thunder will exercise their team option or decline it and work out a new multi-year deal at a lower average annual salary.

item.title: Lakers among teams monitoring Lu Dort’s availability
item.description: With Williams expected to return healthy next season, Mitchell, Cason Wallace, Jared McCain, and Alex Caruso in the fold, teams across the league are monitoring fellow starter Lu Dort’s availability. Dort has a team option for $17.22 million for the upcoming season that several front executives who spoke with HoopsHype are projecting the Thunder to exercise, then consider flipping Dort on the trade market. Should such a scenario happen, Dort has been linked with the Lakers and several other team

item.title: Dillon Brooks extension could be worth $25 million per season
item.description: In his first season with Phoenix, Brooks remained a strong defender, helped change the team’s culture, and became a 20-point-per-game scorer for the first time in his career. At 30, Brooks enters the upcoming season on the books for $21 million. Looking ahead, Brooks could earn a potential extension around an average of $25 million per season.

item.title: After being consistent role players for Oklahoma City …
item.description: After being consistent role players for Oklahoma City throughout the past season, league sources say opposing executives have circled the wagon on Isaiah Joe and Aaron Wiggins. Both players saw limited action against the Spurs in the Conference Finals, despite being key contributors during the regular season, and would represent significant depth upgrades for most teams around the league.

item.title: Lastly, the Suns have also done some background on …
item.description: Lastly, the Suns have also done some background on Chicago Bulls guard Josh Giddey, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Brooklyn Nets have attempted to move up into the …
item.description: The Brooklyn Nets have attempted to move up into the top-four range of the NBA Draft by dangling their No. 6 pick with future first-round draft picks, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Bucks would consider retaining Jaylen Brown if they land him in Giannis Antetokounmpo deal
item.description: Despite some executives around the league assuming it would be a foregone conclusion that the Bucks would try to flip Brown to another team to get future first-round draft pick compensation as part of a rebuilding effort, it’s not a certainty. If the Celtics indeed acquire Antetokounmpo and Brown ends up going to the Bucks as part of the trade, the Bucks would consider keeping Brown, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: The Clippers are also said to be interested in Brown …
item.description: The Clippers are also said to be interested in Brown and have future first-round draft picks available to dangle, including the fifth pick in the draft as a potential trade chip, along with a 2029 unprotected first-round draft pick from the Indiana Pacers from the Ivica Zubac trade, should they decide to pursue a trade.

item.title: Portland has discussed trying to acquire …
item.description: The Portland Trail Blazers have been involved in Antetokounmpo trade talks on multiple fronts. Portland has discussed trying to acquire Antetokounmpo, and as a potential facilitator because they have two future first-round Bucks swaps in 2028 and 2030, league sources said.

item.title: It’s worth noting that Nikola Jovic, Kasparas …
item.description: It’s worth noting that Nikola Jovic, Kasparas Jakucionis, and Pelle Larsson have each come up in different iterations of the Antetokounmpo trade talks, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: They are committed to bringing back their own free …
item.description: John Gambadoro: YES the Suns can afford both. They are committed to bringing back their own free agents and willing to go deep into the luxury tax to do that. They won't go into the second apron, but they shouldn't have to.

item.title: WE completed the mission together as brothers, and …
item.description: Kyrie Irving: All for one. One for All WE completed the mission together as brothers, and that’s all that matters to me. Hélà 🤞🏾♾🪶

item.title: After four seasons, I’ve left Sports Illustrated
item.description: Joey Linn: After four seasons, I’ve left Sports Illustrated. Starting now, and hopefully for a long time, I’ll be covering the LA Clippers independently. Thank you for the continued support. It’s made this possible. I hope you’ll join me at my new site.

item.title: Wolves have gauged trade value of Julius Randle, Donte DiVincenzo, and first-rounder
item.description: The Timberwolves have gauged the trade value of Julius Randle, Donte DiVincenzo, and their No. 28 first-round pick in the draft, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Ayo Dosunmu projected to earn $18-20 million per season in free agency
item.description: After trading for Ayo Dosunmu at the deadline, the Minnesota Timberwolves want to re-sign him. According to NBA executives who’ve spoken with HoopsHype, Dosunmu’s range is projected to be somewhere north of $18 million annually and could reach the low $20 million range.

item.title: Other teams have always called on rising swingman …
item.description: Other teams have always called on rising swingman Jaden McDaniels. Minnesota has made him essentially untradable unless it was for a blockbuster trade involving Giannis Antetokounmpo or Nikola Jokic. When the Los Angeles Clippers were struggling earlier this season amid a 6-21 start and gauging the trade market for Kawhi Leonard and James Harden each, the Clippers poked around on McDaniels, but were rebuffed, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: NBA execs believe Magic will eventually choose between Jalen Suggs and Anthony Black
item.description: While the contract of Isaac gives Orlando flexibility to shed salary or use as a potential trade chip, the immediacy of Black’s extension talks will be interesting following Jalen Suggs getting a five-year, $150 million extension, and with Black having comparable statistics to Suggs last season. NBA executives across the league believe Orlando will eventually have to choose between keeping Suggs or Black on the roster long-term due to their incoming salary cap situation.

item.title: RJ Barrett drawing trade interest
item.description: Toronto Raptors swingman RJ Barrett has drawn trade interest in multi-team conversations, league sources told HoopsHype. The Canadian swingman is on an expiring $29.62 million contract this upcoming season. He’s averaged 20.5 points on 49.5 percent shooting from the field and 35.3 percent beyond the arc with 5.9 rebounds and 4.3 assists since joining his hometown Raptors.

item.title: Jazz interested in re-signing Jusuf Nurkic
item.description: The Utah Jazz has expressed interest in re-signing center Jusuf Nurkic, league sources told Hoopshype.

item.title: Sources say other assistant coaching candidates in the …
item.description: Meanwhile, Sean Sweeney’s coaching staff has begun to take shape with HoopsHype reporting the additions of DJ Bakker, Mfon Udofia, and Riley Crean as assistant coaches. Sources say other assistant coaching candidates in the mix for Sweeney’s staff include Joe Prunty, Popeye Jones, Greg Buckner, and Tom Bialaszewski.

item.title: "Definitely helped with [getting] a feel for how this …
item.description: Ivica Zubac won't be flying completely blind in 2026-27, but he'll still be adjusting along the way. His offseason will be important, as will training camp. That's one advantage of getting the trade done in February, Zubac is a member of the Pacers for the entire offseason. Some of the team's assistant coaches, including Jenny Bouckey and Jim Boylen, have been with Zubac in Europe this summer. "Definitely helped with [getting] a feel for how this team wants to play, about the pace, about defensi

item.title: After picks Nos. 5-9, as the NBA Draft order currently …
item.description: Jake Fischer: After picks Nos. 5-9, as the NBA Draft order currently stands, are projected to feature a slew of guards, the next dozen picks have executives around the league preparing for a run on big men.

item.title: Lakers impressed by Braden Smith
item.description: The Lakers spent time in Spain looking at 20-year-old guard Sergio de Larrea, but many NBA scouts see him going later in the first round or even in the second. According to people not authorized to speak publicly, the Lakers were impressed by their workout with Purdue point guard Braden Smith. But he’s on the smaller side (6 feet) and played four years in college, leading scouts to believe his upside is not that high and that he’ll be drafted in the second round.

item.title: Jordan Nwora is staying with Crvena Zvezda despite …
item.description: Jordan Nwora is staying with Crvena Zvezda despite transfer interest from Hapoel Tel Aviv. President Zeljko Drcelic declared that the prolific scorer is not for sale this summer.

item.title: Patrick Mills calls season in Tenerife 'the greatest year of my career'
item.description: Patrick Mills: Accidentally stumbled upon an island in the Atlantic that turned into the greatest year of my career. Thank you to the people of Tenerife for welcoming our family with open arms and sharing the stories, history, and culture that make this island so special. Al golpito. Little by little, this island felt less like a destination and more like a place of belonging. And somewhere in between, there were some pretty special moments on the court too. Ar emir iyen, Tenerife. ❤️ @CB1939Can

item.title: I'm a competitor too, but I also like to stare my …
item.description: In a recent appearance on the Breakfast Club Power 105.1 FM, Jose Alvarado also expressed disappointment with how Victor Wembanyama did not even shake his and the New York Knicks' hands after the 2026 NBA Finals. "Wemby is a great player. ... I mean, I got mixed emotions. I'm a competitor too, but I also like to stare my enemies down. I look forward to them, and I shake their hand. Obviously, it's a game. You don't like the moment you lost your pride, the biggest game of your career, but you're

item.title: Lakers in the mix for Mitchell Robinson, Robert Williams?
item.description: The Lakers have been linked to all sorts of veteran big men in the forthcoming free agent market as well. They could emerge as a suitor for the Knicks' Mitchell Robinson and the Trail Blazers' Robert Williams III, although sources say both players are very open to remaining in their incumbent roles with the Knicks and Blazers. I'm also told that the Lakers have called the Thunder about swingman Lu Dort's availability.

item.title: The Bucks, incidentally, are VERY much aware of how …
item.description: Barry Jackson: The Bucks, incidentally, are VERY much aware of how much Giannis wants to play in Miami. They seem determined to leave the Heat in a compromised roster position so that the Heat also has a hard time doing what the Bucks couldn't do the past 5 years (build a championship team around him). That's the reason I've been saying that it serves Giannis' interest to make clear to the world that he will sign an extension only with Miami, thus dissuading other potential bidders. That could a

item.title: Peterson didn’t hesitate in calling James the GOAT
item.description: Peterson recently spoke about his admiration of James, saying that while he hasn’t yet met him, James commenting on his Instagram posts meant so much and that he hopes to have the career longevity of the all-time great, via NBA Future Now on X: Peterson didn’t hesitate in calling James the GOAT while also adding that his favorite team is the Lakers simply because that is who James plays for. That is the case for many fans these days who sometimes have more loyalty towards individual players than

item.title: The Mavericks and Brayden Burries have mutual interest …
item.description: Dallas, though, has been very open, sources say, to moving down the board. We've been told the Mavericks and Brayden Burries have mutual interest at No. 9. But if Burries is not there — and we've already reported he's under legitimate consideration by the Clippers at No. 5 — that could ultimately lead to even more reason for Dallas to slide back.

item.title: One potential target if Dallas were to trade down: …
item.description: One potential target if Dallas were to trade down: Washington big man Hannes Steinbach. Sources say that Steinbach, whose father Burkhard played alongside Mavericks legend Dirk Nowitzki on the German League team in their shared hometown of Wurzburg some 30 years ago, is among the prospects to have worked out for the Mavericks under consideration for the No. 9 pick.

item.title: Atlanta sees great value in Swain at No. 23,
item.description: I've also heard that Atlanta sees great value in Swain at No. 23, but the Hawks have been signaling for some time that they are prepared (and perhaps even prefer) to trade their second pick in the first round after No. 8 for future draft capital. It sounds as though Atlanta will invite external trade interest for that pick until it is actually on the clock to make the selection Tuesday night.

item.title: Nothing has changed, as I said, I will not play this …
item.description: It was previously known that Luka Doncic would not be part of the Slovenian national team in early July, nor would he help Slovenia in the second part of the 2027 World Cup qualifiers in Qatar at the end of August. " Nothing has changed, as I said, I will not play this summer, " he added. Due to a hamstring injury, the Lakers' first star missed the entire North American league playoffs.

item.title: I won't hide it, if you don't win the title, it's a …
item.description: " I won't hide it, if you don't win the title, it's a bad season. We've been unlucky with injuries, myself included, after we played some great basketball in March. It's going to be a long time before next season. I hope it's better for us ." His club teammate, 2.13 m tall American center Jaxson Hayes , recently received Slovenian citizenship. " I wasn't involved in these talks at all. He's a good guy and that's what he is as a player. Otherwise, we have a young team that gives us hope. I want t

item.title: Even when I'm here, I hardly go anywhere, that's the …
item.description: He has a star status in Los Angeles and around the world, which also has a great impact on his private life, which he did not want to talk about in detail, including the breakup with his fiancée Anamarija Goltes . " Even when I'm here, I hardly go anywhere, that's the price for the position I'm in and what I wanted. It's completely different than in Los Angeles, where I feel good ."

item.title: Of course, I enjoy playing golf. There's nothing …
item.description: But he couldn't hide the excitement he found in the game of golf. " Of course, I enjoy playing golf. There's nothing better than spending four hours in nature. But I also choose to play 36 holes, which isn't exactly the best, and I play and enjoy it for eight hours. That's the most beautiful thing ," he said on the platform with the basketball court at the Stožice hall.

item.title: Nick Young: 'Stephen Curry and Kevin Durant was fighting over Finals MVP'
item.description: Nick Young: "Steph and Kevin Durant was fighting over Finals MVP, that's about it. You could tell they both was trying to get it without saying they trying to get it. Kevin Durant was taking it a little easy, I felt like, and Steph wasn't having that good of a game that game. So Kevin Durant just had to turn it up. And I think that's when he won Finals MVP too, after that game. It was a big night for him."

item.title: Nick Young: "Steph is like the good cop to Draymond's …
item.description: Nick Young: "Steph is like the good cop to Draymond's bad cop. You got to have that kind of balance. Being Draymond's being Draymond, Steph will pull you to the side and be like, 'Don't worry about that, just continue to play, things going to work out.' So he's a good dude. And KD, he don't really he's not really the leader type like the captain type. He leads by just being on the floor, but he's not really too vocal unless he kind of know you."

item.title: Nick Young on JR Smith in Game 1: "At the moment I …
item.description: Nick Young on JR Smith in Game 1: "At the moment I didn't feel bad for him, I was wondering what was he doing? What is he doing? LeBron had 40 something, almost 50. He was cooking. And that was the main thing we was worried about. At the free throw line we thought, 'Oh man, it's over, we let one slip out.' And then when J.R. did that, it gave us hope again."

item.title: Charles Oakley: Patrick Ewing never stood up for nobody
item.description: Charles Oakley: "Patrick (Ewing) always been like that. He never stood up for nobody. Even when they offered him the D-League job, I said something. And the coldest thing out of this all, I took a case for Patrick on an airplane. Put that out there. I took a case from him. Ask any teammate, Chris Childs, Charles, Allen, John, they might not say nothing, but I took a case to better his self. And this happened to me, he don't say nothing."

item.title: "Brunson is a better Knicks than Patrick."
item.description: Charles Oakley: "Brunson is a better Knicks than Patrick."

item.title: "I was supposed to be on the Roommate podcast. I get a …
item.description: Charles Oakley: "I was supposed to be on the Roommate podcast. I get a call the day before. They said, we have to cancel you. And then three weeks later, guess who was on there? Dolan. Dolan. I was shooting a commercial right across from the Garden and Cafe 31 and something else. We shot for one day, the next day somebody said, somebody said we had to shut it down. We had permission and everything. So he bullying me around the city, too."

item.title: The Knicks depth piece was casually seen on the subway …
item.description: Landry Shamet was trying to play it cool. The Knicks depth piece was casually seen on the subway in New York on Wednesday, going incognito with a hat and sunglasses in what seemed to be an attempt to disguise himself. However, some fans did recognize Shamet, 29, as he tried to be like every other New Yorker.

item.title: Love Island USA Season 8 has started, with audiences …
item.description: Love Island USA Season 8 has started, with audiences getting to know fresh faces in the Fiji setting — and the astute might recognize one name. Aniya Harvey is the daughter of retired NBA star Donnell Harvey, who played for the Dallas Mavericks, Orlando Magic, New Jersey Nets, Denver Nuggets and Phoenix Suns. Harvey made his final NBA appearance in 2005 but continued to play professionally internationally until 2014. According to Complex (1), Harvey has largely been out of the spotlight since he

item.title: Most Clippers road games last season had zero beat …
item.description: Joey Linn: The media landscape has changed a lot since then. Especially on the Clippers beat. The LA Times no longer has a Clippers beat writer. Neither does ESPN. Most Clippers road games last season had zero beat reporters covering the team. To fulfill his media duties, Ty Lue would field postgame questions from play-by-play announcer Brian Sieman. No one else was there to ask him anything. Even Kawhi Leonard noticed the lack of media presence on the road. He joked in the locker room after an

item.title: All of that has led me to this point. It's time to bet …
item.description: On many occasions, the cost to attend those events was greater than what I made while there. Obviously, not a sustainable business model. But I picked my spots and worked multiple other jobs to fund my dream job. All of that has led me to this point. It's time to bet on myself and go independent. Local coverage of the Clippers has been dwindling. And many of the outlets who do still publish content about the team, including SI, have leaned heavily into a model that — to put it simply — depriorit

item.title: Jordan Goodwin to return to Phoenix on three-year, $19 million deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard Jordan Goodwin intends to sign a three-year, $19 million deal to return to the Phoenix Suns, with a player option in the third season, sources tell ESPN. Goodwin made the Suns roster out of training camp on a non-guaranteed contract and emerged as a key reserve and culture fit, averaging 8.7 points, 4.9 rebounds, 2.2 assists, and a team-high 106 total steals.

item.title: In consecutive days, the Suns land multiyear …
item.description: Shams Charania: In consecutive days, the Suns land multiyear agreements to keep two young guards in Goodwin and Collin Gillespie whom the franchise developed over the last year. Goodwin's agents at Klutch Sports, Shy Saee and Mike George, landed the deal Sunday, a week after free agents became eligible to negotiate with their own teams. Next order of business for the Suns: retaining restricted free agent Mark Williams.

item.title: Hawks, CJ McCollum agree to a one-year, $21 million contract extension
item.description: Shams Charania: Atlanta Hawks guard CJ McCollum has agreed to a one-year, $21 million contract extension with the franchise, plus a trade kicker, agent Sam Goldfeder of Excel Sports Management tells ESPN. McCollum has been eligible for an extension until June 30 and commits to a new deal that keeps him out of free agency.contract extension with the franchise, plus a trade kicker, agent Sam Goldfeder of Excel Sports Management tells ESPN.

item.title: McCollum has a 7.5% trade kicker in his one-year, $21 …
item.description: Jake Fischer: CJ McCollum has a 7.5% trade kicker in his one-year, $21 million extension to stay with Atlanta, per source.

item.title: Hawks went 19-4 with McCollum as a starter from Feb. …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Hawks went 19-4 with McCollum as a starter from Feb. 22 through end of the regular season -- closing the 11th straight year that the veteran guard has made 150 3s, tied for the 4th-most such campaigns in NBA history, per ESPN Research. McCollum is now signed through next season.

item.title: Dwyane Wade opens up on mental health struggles after retiring
item.description: Dwyane Wade shared that retiring from basketball — a sport he had played his entire life — took an emotional toll. “Once you retire, that's when, you know … you really go through a moment, a time of depression,” he said. “And it's how you decide to handle that depression which is going to determine your next step because, you know — since I was 5 years old — that's what I did, played basketball. And then I was really good at it. So now you're coming out of this world and … now I got to get anoth

item.title: “I was the Black man that was like, ‘Therapy ain't for …
item.description: Wade went on to say that “the first thing” he did when he retired was start therapy, despite the fact that he previously had some resistance to the idea. “I was the Black man that was like, ‘Therapy ain't for me,' ” he explained. However, Wade said he intuitively knew he needed an emotional outlet post-retirement. “Basketball not only was a place where I had so much success … it was also my outlet, you know what I mean? Like, I could scream. I can yell. I can curse. I could, you know — not fight

item.title: Jaylen Brown was also in attendance to watch Game 4 …
item.description: Boston Celtics star Jaylen Brown was also in attendance to watch Game 4 between Monaco and Paris. Brown is currently vacationing in Europe amid uncertainty over his future with the Celtics, as his name has often been mentioned in Giannis Antetokounmpo trade rumors.

item.title: Amsterdam native and Albany Academy alumnus Andre …
item.description: A local NBA star is coming home to host a basketball camp next weekend. Amsterdam native and Albany Academy alumnus Andre Jackson Jr. and Foundation 44 are hosting a basketball clinic on Saturday, June 27th.

item.title: Nets bring in Morez Johnson for workout
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Brooklyn Nets brought in Morez Johnson for a workout today as they figure out what they’ll do with the 6th pick. He helped Michigan win the NCAA title, averaging 13.1 points, 7.3 rebounds, and 1.1 blocks. He was an All-Big Ten Team and Big Ten All-Defensive selection.

item.title: He's been arguably the biggest riser during the draft …
item.description: Keith Smith: I don't know where Morez Johnson ultimately gets selected, but he's been arguably the biggest riser during the draft process. Started out in the latter part of the first round and now could be a lottery pick. The kind of athletic big that teams want at the 4 in today's game.

item.title: He's immediately trade-eligible with no waiting period …
item.description: Keith Smith: One note (that likely will not matter) on CJ McCollum's contract being an extension vs a new deal: He's immediately trade-eligible with no waiting period once he's signed. A new contract signed in July would have come with a December 15 trade restriction.

item.title: If the Hawks choose to stay over the cap with C.J. …
item.description: Keith Smith: If the Hawks choose to stay over the cap with C.J. McCollum on the books, Atlanta should still be able to use the full $15M Non-Taxpayer MLE. That'll be an important tool to help the Hawks round out their depth, especially in the frontcourt.

item.title: Miami 305 stars Michael Beasley and Lance Stephenson …
item.description: Chris Haynes: BIG3 News: Miami 305 stars Michael Beasley and Lance Stephenson will be suspended one game each for their altercations during Saturday’s contest against the LA Riot, sources tell me.

item.title: Lance Stephenson will serve his suspension Week 2 in …
item.description: Chris Haynes: To ensure Miami has four minimum required players available, Lance Stephenson will serve his suspension Week 2 in Detroit and Micheal Beasley will serve his Week 3 in Miami.

item.title: Lakers looking for an upgrade at the center position
item.description: That’s not to say neither Marcus Smart or Deandre Ayton will be back (both have player options), but team sources have repeatedly talked about an upgrade at the center position. They’ve also spoken about a desire to get younger and more athletic. If I had to pick one to be back, I’d lean Smart. I just think the Lakers want a more traditional Luka Dončić-style center.

item.title: Blazers interested in re-signing Robert Williams
item.description: Robert Williams III is an option, though Portland, according to league sources, is expected to show interest in re-signing him after he played 59 games last season — second-most in his injury-hampered career. It’s a bit of bad luck for the Lakers to be looking for a rim-running, rim-protecting center at a time when there aren’t many of them in the league — not to mention ones that are available.

item.title: Lakers were “on the clock” with Dončić right after the …
item.description: Lakers were “on the clock” with Dončić right after the end of last season, and nothing I’ve heard from my sources would change that thought process. Dončić is in the prime of his career and was traded away from a roster built to his specifications to compete for NBA titles. And if the Lakers can’t provide that, maybe he looks elsewhere (if anybody should understand that this is business, it’s Dončić).

item.title: Dwight Howard: We need to do a 2020 lakers vacation …
item.description: Dwight Howard: We need to do a 2020 lakers vacation since we didn’t get a parade 👍🏾

item.title: OG Anunoby's game-winning ball from Finals Game 4 up for auction
item.description: The ball that helped deliver the most significant moment in modern New York basketball history is headed to auction. The ball from OG Anunoby’s game-winning tip-in during Game 4 of the 2026 NBA Finals will be sold by Sotheby’s beginning June 30.

item.title: The basketball is expected to generate enormous …
item.description: The basketball is expected to generate enormous interest because of its connection to a play that helped propel the Knicks to their first championship in 53 years. It may be the single most important play in the history of New York basketball.

item.title: Malique Lewis is completing a late flurry of workouts …
item.description: Malique Lewis is completing a late flurry of workouts with NBA teams, with his stock seemingly gathering real momentum just days out from the 2026 NBA Draft. The Trinidadian wing -- who's coming off two seasons with the South East Melbourne Phoenix as part of the NBL's Next Stars program -- has a workout with the Los Angeles Lakers on Tuesday (AEDT), sources told ESPN. It'll be the sixth and final he completes following a standout showing at EuroCamp in Treviso.

item.title: Lewis also completed workouts this past week with the …
item.description: Lewis also completed workouts this past week with the Phoenix Suns, Dallas Mavericks, Minnesota Timberwolves, Boston Celtics, and Cleveland Cavaliers, sources said, ahead of the NBA Draft, which takes place over June 24 and 25 (AEDT) at the Barclays Centre in Brooklyn, New York.

item.title: Thunder more likely to keep both first-round picks after Aaron Wiggins trade?
item.description: Jeremy Woo: Draft implications of the Aaron Wiggins deal: the Thunder were staring at a roster crunch and now have a simpler pathway to make both of their first-round selections, (12 and 17) if they want to go that route.

item.title: Entering the offseason, Oklahoma City had a projected …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Entering the offseason, Oklahoma City had a projected tax penalty of $213M. The trade reduces that number to $152M. The Thunder enter the Tuesday Draft with two picks in the top-17 (no. 12 and 17).

item.title: Thunder trade Aaron Wiggins to Atlanta
item.description: Shams Charania: The Oklahoma City Thunder are finalizing a trade to send guard Aaron Wiggins to the Atlanta Hawks for two second-round picks (Atlanta's in 2030 and the least favorable of Hawks/Lakers in 2032), sources tell ESPN.

item.title: The Lakers have hired former New Orleans Pelicans …
item.description: Benjamin Royer: The Lakers have hired former New Orleans Pelicans basketball analytics coordinator Dominic Samangy as a basketball analyst.

item.title: “And same thing, the games go on, the games go on, and …
item.description: Guerschon Yabusele on his lack of playing time in New York: “And same thing, the games go on, the games go on, and well, I still do not play. I still do not play. And sometimes, zero minutes, sometimes... And me, there, actually, it hit me, where I say to myself, ‘Ah, but I feel like I am in Boston, actually.’ When I look at Boston, we are in 2026, and it was ten years ago. It hit me. I said to myself, ‘Damn, ten years. A ton of things have happened, you know, since then, and I am still getting

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: "It's been hard being in the limelight, …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa: "It's been hard being in the limelight, but for the rest of my life I'm going to be in it, so it's good that I got it early, to be honest. I didn't like it, but it's good that I got it early."

item.title: "It was solid. A lot of people thought I would just …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa on his academic experience at BYU: "It was solid. A lot of people thought I would just cruise through it. I was in class, like, four in-person classes. I would sit like front row, everything, Book of Mormon class, all that. I had like two classes online, but it was like because there was literally TAs, there was no teacher, but I was in class, I was scootering around campus, golf cart, right to class."

item.title: Dallas and Oklahoma City, amid the Mavericks' myriad …
item.description: Also in that same draft range but not seamlessly connected to the primary topic at hand: The Stein Line has learned that Dallas and Oklahoma City, amid the Mavericks' myriad trade discussions throughout the weekend, have discussed a potential swap of No. 9 to the Thunder for the Nos. 12 and 17 picks.

item.title: The celebrated basketball from British forward OG …
item.description: The celebrated basketball from British forward OG Anunoby’s legendary game-winning tip-in during Game 4 of the NBA Finals 2026 is officially heading to Sotheby’s, with experts projecting the artifact could fetch up to $3 million. This iconic piece of sports memorabilia represents the crowning moment of the New York Knicks’ first championship run in 53 years, sealing one of many dramatic comeback victories against the San Antonio Spurs during their best-of-seven championship series in the 2026 NB

item.title: Darius Garland presented Alireza Beiranvand with the …
item.description: LA Clippers: Darius Garland presented Alireza Beiranvand with the Superior Player of the Match Award after today's Belgium vs. Iran match 🏆

item.title: Aj Dybantsa: 'I'm not a Celtics fan anymore'
item.description: NBA Base: AJ Dybantsa says he is no longer a Celtics fan “I’m a Celtics fan, been to multiple Celtics games... now I’m not a Celtics fan anymore… Cuz I’m about to get drafted so I’m not going to be a fan of them... I told you I’m not a fan anymore so I don’t really care what they do.”

item.title: “I’m just super versatile. 6’9 doing it all. Could …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa on what he brings to the table that other players don’t bring: “I’m just super versatile. 6’9 doing it all. Could play one through five. I did it this year. Small lineups. I was playing four and five. Bigger lineups. I’m bringing the ball up, throwing lobs. I’m an exciting player, so I fill seats. Want the fans, you know, to come see me play. And I’m just super relatable though. Like people just relate to me.” (Via @stephenasmith )

item.title: Buzelis will arrive at the national team camp …
item.description: Chicago Bulls young star Matas Buzelis will have help from his club to accommodate the transition to European basketball. According to Lukas Katilius from BasketNews.lt, Buzelis will arrive at the national team camp accompanied by the Bulls assistant coach Pete Crawford and strength and conditioning coach Justin Irwin.

item.title: Celtics all in on Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Heat Central: The Boston Celtics are going ALL IN on Giannis Antetokounmpo and Jaylen Brown is on the table, per @WindhorstESPN “If Giannis is traded in the next 24 which I think is a good chance of happening, I think it’s going to be Boston or Miami…. Jaylen Brown is on the table”

item.title: If there is a Heat deal, it likely will be completed …
item.description: Ira Winderman: This never was going to be easy. If there is a Heat deal, it likely will be completed later, due to cap/calendar rules. If there is not, then it would seem the time for Pat Riley to talk.

item.title: "It really is something of a dream come true," …
item.description: After his debut NBA season, Miami Heat rookie Kasparas Jakucionis returned to Lithuania to fulfill one of his dreams – making his national team debut. "It really is something of a dream come true," Jakucionis told reporters in Lithuania's national team training camp in Birstonas. "I always watched the national team and always wanted to be part of the national team. I'm really very happy."

item.title: “I visited multiple teams because you never know what …
item.description: NBA Courtside: AJ Dybantsa says he wants and expects to be the number one draft pick: “I visited multiple teams because you never know what can happen on draft night, I was just being smart in my opinion. I want to get drafted number one though.” (Via @stephenasmith )

item.title: The Clippers have been visiting prospects who are …
item.description: Heat Central: The Clippers have been visiting prospects who are projected in the late lottery, per @WindhorstESPN “Miami and Boston have gone all in here. I don’t know if there’s any going back. Whoever doesn’t get Giannis, what happens next? I started talking to agents and executives, the Clippers started to visit prospects in the late lottery. Guys from picks 10-15…. Why are they doing that? We got the Heat sitting there at 13… I’m keeping an eye on the Clippers but I’m also keeping an eye on

item.title: Evans has worked out for the Knicks,
item.description: Isaiah Evans, Wing, Duke: Evans can really, really shoot the ball, both stationary and off the move. That’s about it, though. He needs to get stronger to regularly contribute in the NBA. He doesn’t pass well. He’s not a great defender. However, if he’s able to shoot at an elite level in the NBA and be good enough defensively, that’s a rotation piece all NBA teams look for. Evans has worked out for the Knicks, per league sources.

item.title: Wolves keeping Jaden McDaniels?
item.description: The Wolves have been holding firm on saying no on McDaniels, team sources said. Center Joan Beringer is also a player the Wolves want to keep because of his size, athleticism and feel for the game. What the Wolves do not want to do is liquidate their depth in making a trade to pair the 31-year-old Antetokounmpo with Edwards.

item.title: The list of veterans whom the Wolves have discussed …
item.description: The list of veterans whom the Wolves have discussed this spring includes the Milwaukee Bucks’ Giannis Antetokounmpo, the Dallas Mavericks’ Kyrie Irving, the New Orleans Pelicans’ Trey Murphy III, the Chicago Bulls’ Josh Giddey, the Boston Celtics’ Derrick White and many more. A couple of important caveats are that every team in the league is considering veteran help, and that having interest in a player does not mean any potential trades have ever been close.

item.title: One team that shall remain nameless to protect the …
item.description: For instance, team sources say one team that shall remain nameless to protect the shameless called the Timberwolves and offered a sparingly used bench player averaging single-digit points for Reid.

item.title: source said Philon performed very well in the workout
item.description: A team source said Philon performed very well in the workout on Saturday, but this is the kind of thing that feels almost a little too obvious. Why would Philon come to Minnesota this late in the process if he didn’t think the Wolves would be in a position to draft him? And why would it be out there in big, bold letters for the rest of the NBA to see? It is all very curious.

item.title: The NBA Players Association is launching a new …
item.description: The NBA Players Association is launching a new consumer-facing unit and brand, Plyrs Untd, that will initially focus on events and content. It effectively replaces the NBPA’s current B2B brand, Think450. “Consumers do not know our brand, but consumers obviously know our guys, and our guys, probably more so than any other sport, represent culture in a different kind of way,” David Kelly, NBPA executive director, said in a video call. “In addition to collectively licensing the player rights to com

item.title: Celtics have real shot at Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Marc Stein: UPDATE: One league source, after my story published, told @TheSteinLine that the Celtics emerged from the weekend “with a real shot” to win this race with a Jaylen Brown-centric offer … adding that Milwaukee has considered going ahead even without a third-team facilitator.

item.title: "If you force me to make a choice, yes. But I'm really …
item.description: With Antetokounmpo plagued by injuries and limited to just 36 games this past season, the Milwaukee Bucks appear ready to move on and initiate a rebuild. When asked if the trade will happen in the next 24 hours, Stein said: "If you force me to make a choice, yes. But I'm really taking my cues from every team I talk to, and every agent I talk to, and the answer comes back 'yes' every time".

item.title: "I haven't thought about it yet. I think for now I …
item.description: Doncic was also asked about his plans and will he consider joining Zvezda at some point during his career: "I haven't thought about it yet. I think for now I still have some career left in the NBA, I'm not that old yet. But maybe one day I will come," he concluded.

item.title: Richaun Holmes headed to Israel?
item.description: Maccabi Tel Aviv continues to work towards next season, and the Sports Channel has learned that it is in advanced negotiations with Richaun Holmes. Holmes (32, 2.06 m) signed with Panathinaikos last summer and played in Europe for the first time after good years in the NBA, when he was supposed to be the Greeks' biggest star and won a two-year contract - earning $2.5 million per year

item.title: Trey Lyles has confirmed that he's leaving the Spanish …
item.description: Real Madrid forward Trey Lyles has confirmed that he's leaving the Spanish powerhouse. "Madrid has been great, but it's time," Lyles said in his Instagram post. "Great experience here. Appreciate you all. I love all the Madridistas."

item.title: Nikola Mirotic is also drawing significant interest …
item.description: According to BasketNews sources, Nikola Mirotic is also drawing significant interest across Europe and could become another player to depart Monaco this summer. The veteran forward remains one of the club's highest-paid players and still has a contract for next season.

item.title: "A lot. I think he arrived during my second season. …
item.description: Doncic notably highlighted Nikola Jokic and how he enjoys playing with him: "When I watch Nikola Jokic play and when I play against him I feel great. We joke a bit with each other but the way he's playing is phenomenal. He's playing incredible and does stuff that he sees before anyone else does." Jokic also used a chance to thank Boban Marjanovic for helping him accommodate himself in the US: "A lot. I think he arrived during my second season. Bobi is a great guy, we all know that. We were alway

item.title: "I still stand by Spanoulis. He was a really great …
item.description: Who was your idol growing up? We heard you said it was Vasilis Spanoulis , but was there anyone else whose game you followed and whose moves you liked to copy? "I still stand by Spanoulis. He was a really great player and I loved watching him."

item.title: “I believe if they had Chris Paul on this roster, they …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Isaiah Thomas says if the Spurs kept Chris Paul they would’ve won the NBA Finals: “I believe if they had Chris Paul on this roster, they would’ve won the finals… His experience & who he is would’ve helped De’Aaron Fox, Castle, Harper.”

item.title: Celtics have not given up hope on Giannis Antetokounmpo trade
item.description: League sources say that the Heat remain in full-speed pursuit of Antetokounmpo but also indicate that the Boston Celtics have not yet abandoned hope of completing a trade for The Greek Freak. They are the two teams, sources say, that have engaged in the most advanced trade talks to date with the Bucks.

item.title: “As y’all know I’ve said it multiple times over the …
item.description: Heat Central: Chad Johnson is doubling down on his sources saying Giannis is coming to Miami: “As y’all know I’ve said it multiple times over the months. The sources that I do have, they know what they’re talking about. Which is why I’ve came on here and say things that I’ve said. All the other stuff, with Boston, and him going anywhere, none of that is going to happen. That’s just created dialogue to keep people guessing on where he might go.”

item.title: Bucks interested in Hugo Gonzalez
item.description: The Celtics, like Miami, would naturally be asked to add players and future draft compensation to the ultimate trade construction. Sources say that the Bucks have interest in Boston's prized young Spanish guard Hugo Gonzalez.

item.title: The Timberwolves, who were among the most ardent …
item.description: Is Minnesota still in the hunt? Not as of Friday. That's when The Stein Line revealed in the piece below that the Timberwolves, who were among the most ardent Antetokounmpo trade suitors in February, quietly "circled back to the Bucks this week to see what it would take to make another run at pairing Antetokounmpo with Anthony Edwards." Only for the Wolves to be informed, league sources say, that the Bucks want "even more now than the teams discussed in the winter."

item.title: Marc Stein has dismissed the Lakers' inclusion in the …
item.description: Contrary to recent reports and speculation, the Los Angeles Lakers appear not to be in any potential trade frameworks that would help complete the Giannis Antetokounmpo blockbuster trade. Appearing on ALL NBA Podcast, seasoned NBA insider Marc Stein has dismissed the Lakers' inclusion in the ongoing Giannis trade sweepstakes. "I have to be honest based on what I’ve heard. I haven’t heard that," Stein revealed about LA potentially being involved. "I know there was all kinds of speculation yesterd

item.title: LeBron James has not fully committed to returning to the Lakers next season
item.description: NBA Base: LeBron James has not fully committed to returning to the Lakers next season, per @ramonashelburne “My understanding, had initial conversations with LeBron James' representative, but he has not even fully committed to returning next year in these conversations. The conversations were described to me as 'we're keeping in touch.' Okay, so there's no commitment to even returning or salary figures being discussed.”

item.title: "The Pistons have expressed interest in Kyrie Irving. …
item.description: Marc Stein: "The Pistons have expressed interest in Kyrie Irving. Dallas is telling teams Kyrie Irving is not available. The Pistons have expressed interest in Trey Murphy, but so have a zillion other teams, and New Orleans' threshold for trading Trey Murphy away is really, really high. So the Pistons have looked at other targets. Tyler Herro, if he ends up going to Milwaukee in a Giannis trade, we reported that Thursday night, we revealed that, that the Pistons have interest in Tyler Herro, but

item.title: Rudy Gobert: 'I'm still very far from thinking about retirement'
item.description: Rudy Gobert: "I'm still very far from retirement, I'm still very far from thinking about retirement. I'm still, I visualize a bit of the years that are coming and how I see the future, but but I'm really focused also on the present moment and when it will be the end of my, of my career, whether it's in the French team or in the NBA, I feel super well. I'm coming out of a semi-final of conference."

item.title: There have been rumbles in the last week that he might …
item.description: Marc Stein: "We've been reporting since March that Trae Young is going to stay with the Wizards, that it's probably going to be a three-year deal. I have not heard anything really to change my opinion there. But there have been rumbles in the last week that he might get some external interest, and look, his option deadline is actually Tuesday, the day of the draft is when he officially has to make that decision."

item.title: Greek team has reached out to Bogdan Bogdanovic
item.description: Zeljko Obradovic has contacted Bogdan Bogdanovic for Panathinaikos AKTOR, as revealed by Vasili Karamouza. See what was said in the special broadcast of SPORT24 about the return of the Serbian legend to the greens, as well as the data on the future of the Serbian grandee as analyzed by Haris Stavrou.

item.title: Guerschon Yabusele says his priority in free agency is the NBA
item.description: Guerschon Yabusele: So now you are going to be a free agent. We are waiting for the month of July, but there are still rumors circulating. They are not on the United States side; they are on the France side, since we hear your name circulating around ASVEL. We know that you relaunched yourself there after the Celtics. Let’s also remind people that ASVEL has very big projects. They are announcing a budget of fifty million euros with TP as coach. It is a club you know. Is it an option for you to r

item.title: Sam Amick and I reported that the Lakers were “on the …
item.description: Sam Amick and I reported that the Lakers were “on the clock” with Dončić right after the end of last season, and nothing I’ve heard from my sources would change that thought process. Dončić is in the prime of his career and was traded away from a roster built to his specifications to compete for NBA titles. And if the Lakers can’t provide that, maybe he looks elsewhere (if anybody should understand that this is business, it’s Dončić).

item.title: Thunder reduce total roster expenses from $549 million …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Thunder reduce total roster expenses from $549 million to $476 million just by moving off Aaron Wiggins' $9.2 million salary. Expect a couple more trades to reduce it to a more palatable number.

item.title: The Oklahoma City Thunder will be ~$3.7M under the …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: The Oklahoma City Thunder will be ~$3.7M under the luxury tax, if they decline the team options for Lu Dort, Isaiah Hartenstein and Kenrich Williams. Thunder will be ~$11.7M under the first apron and ~$24.7M under the second apron before re-signing Dort, Hartenstein or Williams.

item.title: “I seen the effect that it had on LeBron when Kyrie …
item.description: Boston Celtics All-Star Jaylen Brown has been the subject of rumors since his season ended, which could split as one half of the 2024 champion All-NBA tandem alongside Jayson Tatum, of which Rich Paul is warning the front office. While looking back on his client LeBron James' final season with the Cleveland Cavs, Paul, James' agent, reflected on how the loss of Kyrie Irving humbled LeBron. For Paul, it was a split between two All-Stars that could have been avoided if the two had spoken to one an

item.title: It's true that these big media in addition tend to …
item.description: Rudy Gobert: "Unfortunately, today the opinion of the majority of fans is based on that in fact. It's based on what when they go on Instagram, they see videos and they see what the big media say, and I, it's true that these big media in addition tend to show more negative things than positive things about me. So necessarily, I am not surprised."

item.title: Shams: If Bucks go with the Celtics offer, Jaylen Brown will be in Milwaukee
item.description: Heat Central: “All I’ll say to you Greeny, if the Milwaukee Bucks go with the Celtics offer, Jaylen Brown will be in Milwaukee” — @ShamsCharania says Jaylen Brown will be staying in MIL after a Giannis trade

item.title: “I want to make this clear, whichever deal occurs, …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Giannis Antetokounmpo WILL BE traded before the draft and is focused on Boston or Miami, per @ShamsCharania “I want to make this clear, whichever deal occurs, it’s going to be a one for one deal. It’s going to consist of MIL and one of these two teams. There’s no 3rd and 4th teams.”

item.title: “I want to tell you that process is already underway. …
item.description: Heat Central: The Miami Heat have ALREADY been trying to identify players to add after a Giannis trade, per @WindhorstESPN “I want to tell you that process is already underway. They have already identified players that they can chase if they get this deal done.”

item.title: Giannis is content with either of these two …
item.description: ‪Tommy Beer‬: Per Shams: Giannis is expected to be traded before tomorrow’s draft Likely headed to either Boston (who have made Jaylen Brown available) or Miami Giannis is content with either of these two destinations (willing to sign extension)

item.title: Jaylen Brown on people wanting him traded: 'You're turning me into a monster'
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Jaylen Brown spoke about his social media feed yesterday and how he’s “grateful” for the negativity on it: “That just gives more fuel to the fire…To all the people that’s doubted me, that want me to do this, or want me gone, you’re turning me into a monster.” 🎥 @FCHWPO on Twitch

item.title: Gary Harris exercises player option with Milwaukee
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: Gary Harris has picked up his $3.8M player option with the Milwaukee Bucks for the 2026-27 season, a league source told @spotrac.com .

item.title: The Detroit Pistons have tendered a qualifying offer …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: The Detroit Pistons have tendered a qualifying offer to Jalen Duren, a league source told @spotrac.com . Duren will now be a restricted free agent this offseason.

item.title: Darryn Peterson recently met with the Utah Jazz before …
item.description: Grant Afseth: Darryn Peterson recently met with the Utah Jazz before arriving in New York City for the NBA Draft, sources told @DALHoopsJournal. Peterson would welcome the opportunity to play in Utah if drafted by the Jazz. Read more on @DALHoopsJournal:

item.title: Darryn Peterson resume right now it’s looking like a …
item.description: Danny Green says Darryn Peterson resume right now it’s looking like a Ben Simmons type of thing: “Like we haven’t seen anything like this since Ben Simmons was in college. Kind of like shutting it down mid-season or like in game type thing. I don’t know want his draft process is like, but like again, this history you’re having right now is not a good start, man, to the career. Like, you want to be known as a guy that’s willing to be out there and lay it on the line. You have to prove yourself li

item.title: “I probably had a basketball in their crib, all three …
item.description: Growing up with his older brother, Carmani, and twin brother, Cayden, when one of them played a sport, all three had to play. The boys grew up playing basketball, baseball, football, soccer and even hockey together. It didn’t take long for Carmani to gravitate to baseball and the twins found their love for basketball. “I probably had a basketball in their crib, all three of my sons,” Carlos said, laughing. “They got serious about basketball around four or five years old. Then they got good, like

item.title: Jaylen Brown to be traded regardless of whether Celtics land Giannis Antetokounmpo?
item.description: Heat Central: Brian Windhorst believes Jaylen Brown will be traded even if the Celtics miss on Giannis and is accepting that reality: “I believe if the Bucks choose the Miami Heat offer, Jaylen Brown will get traded somewhere else. They are going to move on here…. From what I understand, Jaylen Brown is accepting this and realizing it’s a chance to turn the page and may be getting his own team”

item.title: As much as he would love to be in Miami, he would be …
item.description: Barry Jackson: I asked Giannis associate why not say Miami and only Miami. Answer was: As much as he would love to be in Miami, he would be OK with Boston too. It was conveyed to at least one (far) West team that he would not extend there, which dissuaded that team. But Giannis camp ultimately was not going to try to dissuade every team.

item.title: Kyle Kuzma a few minutes ago..
item.description: ‪NBA Stat‬: Kyle Kuzma a few minutes ago... The Giannis trade is getting close. #NBA

item.title: Wolves hot for Derrick White?
item.description: Minnesota has been most aggressive in pursuing White, the Celtics’ defensive mastermind who has the experience and the savvy to help Edwards and the Wolves break through in the West. White turns 32 next month and saw his 3-point shooting percentage drop considerably this season, but so far the Celtics have rebuffed the Wolves’ advances.

item.title: Michigan's Dusty May to coach the Mavericks
item.description: Michigan coach Dusty May is finalizing a deal to take the Dallas Mavericks head coaching job, sources told ESPN, an axis-shifting move that significantly alters the college and NBA landscape. May, 49, led Michigan basketball to the national title in 2026. He'll coach a franchise with Cooper Flagg as a linchpin piece. The Mavericks fired Jason Kidd after a 26-56 season last year.

item.title: Knicks assistant Chris Jent getting real consideration for Blazers' head coaching job
item.description: Marc Stein: Dallas’ hiring of Dusty May leaves Portland as the NBA’s only team without a head coach entering Tuesday’s draft … with sources saying Knicks assistant Chris Jent is getting real consideration for that post alongside finalists Micah Nori (Wolves) and Tyler Lashbrook (Celtics).

item.title: Mavericks are hiring Dusty May as their next head …
item.description: Christian Clark: I can confirm the Mavericks are hiring Dusty May as their next head coach. A lot of talk throughout the process that Dallas was looking closely at the college ranks in its search. Mavs find Jason Kidd's successor more than one month after his firing.

item.title: Dusty May is the first college head coach to take an …
item.description: Dusty May is the first college head coach to take an NBA job since former Michigan coach John Beilein took the Cleveland Cavaliers job in 2019. The last to leave college immediately after winning a national title was Kansas' Larry Brown in 1988. Florida's Billy Donovan was the most recent NCAA title-winning coach to leave for the NBA in 2015.

item.title: During Michigan's championship parade in April, …
item.description: During Michigan's championship parade in April, athletic director Warde Manuel announced May had agreed to a new deal that would keep him at Michigan for "many years to come." That deal wasn't signed at the time of Manuel's proclamation, and no deal announcement emerged in the subsequent months.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa still the favorite for No. 1 draft pick?
item.description: Rival executives continue to speculate that the Washington Wizards will take AJ Dybantsa. And if they do surprise the league, it will be with Darryn Peterson, though sources say Washington has remained very tight-lipped during the process.

item.title: Darryn Peterson's medicals have not raised major …
item.description: Sources say Darryn Peterson's medicals have not raised major concerns with teams, clearing the way for him to be a top-two pick. Although his season at Kansas didn't go according to plan -- he dealt with serious cramping issues -- he has always been viewed by teams as a strong candidate at No. 1.

item.title: Not much has changed for Cameron Boozer as he enters …
item.description: Not much has changed for Cameron Boozer as he enters what should be a straightforward draft night. Sources say he visited Memphis last week and previously visited Chicago and Utah, covering all bases other than Washington. Although he is under consideration in Utah, where his father is part of the front office, rival teams believe the Jazz will have a hard time passing on Dybantsa or Peterson.

item.title: Clippers have interest in Darius Acuff Jr., but his …
item.description: Mikel Brown conducted a workout for the Clippers in LA on Thursday and also has traction as a candidate here. Sources say the Clippers have interest in Darius Acuff Jr., but his fit with a similar-sized guard in Garland is an obstacle.

item.title: While there have been rumors about Yaxel Lendeborg not …
item.description: While there have been rumors about Yaxel Lendeborg not finishing workouts due to either conditioning or the leg injury suffered during the season, sources say the Golden State Warriors are very interested him at No. 11.

item.title: The NBA Players Association is launching a new …
item.description: The NBA Players Association is launching a new consumer-facing unit and brand, Plyrs Untd, that will initially focus on events and content. It effectively replaces the NBPA’s current B2B brand, Think450.

item.title: “Consumers do not know our brand, but consumers …
item.description: “Consumers do not know our brand, but consumers obviously know our guys, and our guys, probably more so than any other sport, represent culture in a different kind of way,” David Kelly, NBPA executive director, said in a video call. “In addition to collectively licensing the player rights to companies, it just makes sense to have a brand that is known by consumers, by customers.”

item.title: Pistons in trade talks regarding Tyler Herro
item.description: The Free Press has confirmed with multiple people within the league not permitted to speak publicly that the Pistons have been involved in discussions regarding Tyler Herro. And though it remains to be seen if the deal will get across the finish line, three of their key role players – Isaiah Stewart, Duncan Robinson and Ron Holland – are candidates to be moved in such a trade.

item.title: Pistons' assets include all of their first-round …
item.description: As the adage goes, it takes money to make money. The Pistons' assets include all of their first-round picks, including No. 21 in Tuesday's draft, a trove of second-round picks and several good players on contracts that can be easily moved.

item.title: While the Grizzlies weigh trade offers for Ja Morant …
item.description: Jake Fischer: While the Grizzlies weigh trade offers for Ja Morant and the decision at No. 3, I’ve also heard several teams have expressed interest in trading for Euroleague star Tarik Biberovic, whose draft rights are held by the Grizzlies. Biberovic, 25, shot 44% from distance for Fenerbahce

item.title: Olympiacos has announced another departure from its …
item.description: Olympiacos has announced another departure from its roster, confirming that French guard Frank Ntilikina has left the club by mutual agreement.

item.title: Mark Cuban on the Mavericks' hiring of Michigan coach …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Mark Cuban on the Mavericks' hiring of Michigan coach Dusty May: "He is an Indiana grad. I met him way back when he worked as a student manager and video coach. I'm sure he will do a great job."

item.title: Bucks looking to remain competitive as they discuss Giannis Antetokounmpo trade
item.description: League sources say the Bucks want to remain competitive next season. They don’t outright own any of their firsts until 2031, and even if they did, the flattened draft odds don’t incentivize teams to bottom out. And the best player on the table is, by far, Jaylen Brown.

item.title: Bucks and Celtics have haggled over what pieces would …
item.description: League sources say the Bucks and Celtics have haggled over what pieces would be attached to Brown: the 27th pick? How many future firsts? Hugo Gonzalez? Payton Pritchard? The Celtics have been reluctant to give up much more than Brown.

item.title: Thunder as a team looking to move up in the draft …
item.description: League sources continue to cite the Thunder as a team looking to move up in the draft using the 12th and 17th picks.

item.title: "I like the energy New York has - I resonate with them …
item.description: SNY Knicks: "I like the energy New York has - I resonate with them well" Isaiah Evans on his workout with the Knicks and his thoughts on New York:

item.title: Meloni, 65, sounded off on Brunson's Law & Order: …
item.description: Jalen Brunson has Christopher Meloni's stamp of approval. Meloni, 65, sounded off on Brunson's Law & Order: Special Victims Unit casting aspirations on Saturday, June 20, telling TMZ that the NBA Finals MVP, 29, "absolutely" has what it takes to appear on the series. "I don't know if you heard, but he's the world champion," Meloni said. "He can do whatever the hell he wants." When asked if that means he has a chance, Meloni added, "He's got the force of will."

item.title: "I've never been like super super like starstruck — …
item.description: The now-NBA champion has opened up about his friendship with the SVU star in the past, sharing on the Roommates Show with Jalen Brunson & Josh Hart last year that he recalled his dad "always watching the show." "I've never been like super super like starstruck — super starstruck," he said of Mariska Hargitay. "I saw her, I was like 'Oh s---.' The first time we met, it was awesome. We talked and everything."

item.title: Dusty May's chance to coach Cooper Flagg among main reasons to take Mavs job
item.description: Jeff Goodman: One source very close to Dusty May on the main reason(s) he left Michigan for the NBA: 1) "The state of college basketball and not knowing what it's going to look like tomorrow, in a month or in 5 years." 2) "The organization and the chance to coach Cooper Flagg."

item.title: Tarris Reed was in for a predraft workout with the …
item.description: John Karalis: Draft News: A league source tells me UConn's Tarris Reed was in for a predraft workout with the Celtics this past weekend in a group that also included Arizona's Jaden Bradley, Illinois' Ben Humrichous, Miami (OH)'s Peter Suder, and UNC's Seth Trimble.

item.title: Chris Cenac Jr. says he met with the Knicks, Nets and …
item.description: Adam Zagoria: Houston’s Chris Cenac Jr. says he met with the Knicks, Nets and 76ers

item.title: Santa Clara F Allen Graves has not worked out for the …
item.description: Adam Zagoria: Santa Clara F Allen Graves has not worked out for the Knicks but did work out for the Sixers ‘The facilities are amazing. The city is beautiful.’ Projected late 1st-round pick.

item.title: Jason Collins will posthumously be honored with the …
item.description: Jason Collins will posthumously be honored with the Arthur Ashe Award for Courage for his impact on LGBTQ+ visibility in pro sports at The ESPYS in July. Collins was the NBA's first openly gay player who went on to become a pioneer for inclusion and an ambassador for the league. The 13-year center died May 12 of a brain tumor at age 47.

item.title: Cameron Boozer says he deserves to be in the conversation for the No. 1 draft pick
item.description: Adam Zagoria: Cam Boozer on whether he should be in the conversation for the No. 1 pick: ‘Yeah, I definitely think I deserve that’

item.title: No. 2 pick candidate Darryn Peterson met with Jazz
item.description: Adam Zagoria: Darryn Peterson said he met with Utah over the weekend ‘All the meetings went well’

item.title: Both Darius Acuff Jr. & Kingston Flemings confirmed …
item.description: Jake Gadon: Both Darius Acuff Jr. & Kingston Flemings confirmed that did work out for the Sacramento Kings two weeks ago Both did individual workouts and met the the entire Kings organization

item.title: Kingston Flemings told me he has not met with or …
item.description: Erik Slater: Kingston Flemings told me he has not met with or worked out for the Nets.

item.title: Clarence Jones: First of all, I always ask them what …
item.description: Clarence Jones: First of all, I always ask them what some of their favorite colors are, you know? Each NBA player has a favorite color that they like. I’ve had some that like red, some want burgundy, some want rarer colors. But what I try to do is get them to understand that we’re going to be on TV in front of millions of people. So let’s kind of think a little bit inside the box, but I’m going to help you take it out of the box. I try to keep them all very conservative, you know, with a little

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo willing to sign extension with Celtics if traded
item.description: Adam Himmelsbach: Regarding Giannis Antetokounmpo…Celtics are in the mix. Giannis’ camp has indicated he’d sign extension with Boston. But it remains unclear how close the Cs and Bucks are to a potential deal.

item.title: Mikel Brown Jr. both met with the #Nets, had them come …
item.description: Brian Lewis: Mikel Brown Jr. both met with the #Nets, had them come visit him and also worked out for them up here in Brooklyn. Says he'd be a good fit that could play on or off the ball. #NBA

item.title: Darius Acuff Jr. said he worked out for the #Nets in …
item.description: Brian Lewis: Darius Acuff Jr. said he worked out for the #Nets in Brooklyn. He said it went well & defense was part of the discussion. "Yeah. Of course. Every team you go to makes you play defense, so you do different defensive drills. You've got to show you can defend a little bit." #NBA

item.title: Keaton Wagler cancelled his workout with the Nets but …
item.description: Erik Slater: Keaton Wagler cancelled his workout with the Nets but has met with them twice: "I feel like [the meetings] went well... They just seem like genuine people that it would be nice to [play for]... They really believe in me a lot.”

item.title: Hawks announce CJ McCollum's extension, president Onsi Saleh calls him 'the ultimate professional'
item.description: The Atlanta Hawks have extended guard CJ McCollum, the team announced today.

item.title: Onsi Saleh talked about bringing CJ McCollum back and …
item.description: Brad Rowland: Onsi Saleh talked about bringing CJ McCollum back and referred to him as "the ultimate professional" and later said "the guy can hoop." Also notes the continuity it provides to have him back and also having a full training camp with him in place as a positive.

item.title: Darryn Peterson said he had no communication with …
item.description: Drew Hill: Darryn Peterson said he had no communication with Memphis following the combine/lottery in Chicago.

item.title: Keaton Wagler sounds confident he won't drop below No. 5
item.description: Brian Lewis: Keaton Wagler on meeting with the #Nets but cancelling a workout for Brooklyn. "After Chicago & then I was going to the #Clippers, that's 4 & 5. I think just hearing from them and kind of knowing where I stood, I just knew I didn't really need to go and work out." #NBA

item.title: Cavs exercise 2026-27 team option on Craig Porter
item.description: Chris Fedor: #Cavs have exercised their fourth-year team option on Craig Porter Jr.

item.title: Darryn Peterson on only working out for the Washington …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Darryn Peterson on only working out for the Washington Wizards with the No. 1 pick: “Just a decision that was made. I’m definitely not dodging any team. Whoever takes me, I’m going to come in and be delighted to be there. Wherever that happens to be, they’ll get the best me.”

item.title: Karim Lopez told me his workout with the Nets that …
item.description: Erik Slater: Karim Lopez told me his workout with the Nets that featured Nate Ament went "great": “It was a great workout. I did really good. [The Nets] were pretty happy. The feedback they gave me is that they love my competitiveness, they love how hard I play, my IQ, stuff like that.”

item.title: Trae Young agrees to a four-year contract with Washington
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: Trae Young intends to sign a four-year, approximately $212 million deal to stay with the Washington Wizards, with a player option in Year 4, sources tell ESPN. The four-time NBA All-Star declined his $49M player option for a long-term commitment ahead of free agency.

item.title: The Wizards and Young’s representatives, CAA’s Co-Head …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Wizards and Young’s representatives, CAA’s Co-Head of Basketball Aaron Mintz, agents Ray Young, Andrew Morrison and Co-Head of Basketball Austin Brown, land on a deal that will be less than the maximum for Young with Washington, but equal to the max he could have signed elsewhere. Young – who wanted a trade to one team only, the Wizards, in January – stamps his desire to be in D.C. and elevate the team’s rising young core.

item.title: Yaxel Lendeborg said he fell down to his knees when he …
item.description: Chancellor Johnson: Yaxel Lendeborg said he fell down to his knees when he saw the Dusty May news. I asked him about his chances of joining his college coach in the pros: “I’m going to tell him he better pick me up, if he doesn’t I’m going to block him.. It would be amazing to stick with him.”

item.title: Trae Young jumps into Top-20 all-time in guaranteed NBA earnings
item.description: Jorge Sierra: With his new contract, Trae Young is now Top 20 all-time in guaranteed NBA earnings.

item.title: Louis Williams on why Kawhi Leonard won't take a pay …
item.description: Louis Williams on why Kawhi Leonard won't take a pay cut: "This is a two-time NBA champion. He's seen it all, he's done it all, he feels accomplished. He's done everything he can do besides win another championship, but he's already done it. So when it comes down to situations of money and you haven't won a championship for an organization, I have a hard time seeing him saying, 'I'm willing to take less so you guys can go make other moves,' especially with the climate and seeing how everybody is

item.title: The Waglers told their son they didn’t think they …
item.description: Back in December, when Keaton Wagler’s name started buzzing in NBA Draft circles and the possibility of him being a one-and-done draft pick became real, the Waglers made a decision. “We had one conversation with (his agents),” Logan Wagler, Keaton’s dad, said. “We don’t wanna have those conversations. We don’t want to know. We don’t want to get excited.” The Waglers told their son they didn’t think they should talk about the NBA. They would if he had questions, but they thought the best approach

item.title: “I wish I could write the manuscript on how this …
item.description: Wagler is 6-foot-5, with vision, instincts, a beautiful jumper and footwork. That didn’t just happen in the last year. But what’s different about his story is that Wagler is the rare one-and-done freshman who didn’t set out with that as the goal, and when it became a reality, he didn’t change. No one around him changed. “I wish I could write the manuscript on how this worked,” Illinois coach Brad Underwood said. “Because to me, it’s the innocence and the parenting 101 lesson. They were very dial

item.title: “I didn’t really expect to be a one-and-done, but I …
item.description: “I didn’t really expect to be a one-and-done, but I kind of knew how good I was, and that I could be able to compete with anyone,” Wagler said. “I didn’t really have a set, ‘I’m going to be here for one year or two years or four or whatever.’ It was just going out there and not thinking about it.”

item.title: Wizards not engaging in trade talks for No. 1 draft pick
item.description: Marc J. Spears: The Washington Wizards are expected to keep the No. 1 pick and aren’t engaging in trade opportunities, a source told @andscape.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: Utah and Washington can automatically be a playoff team if they add me
item.description: AJ Dybantsa: "I think both teams, Utah and Washington, they have a deep enough roster that if they add me, that we can automatically be a playoff team. And that rookie year, obviously."

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: "I've been number one my whole life. So I …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa: "I've been number one my whole life. So I kind of just grew up in the spotlight. So I just been hooping. Expectations are going to be on me, but I mean, I can mess up a couple times, but I'm going to just keep going, I'm going to keep hooping."

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: "My rate of improvement throughout the …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa: "My rate of improvement throughout the season is actually like ridiculous. Like the first game, if you watch my first game reads versus my last game reads, it's night and day, especially the film I'm watching. I was only 35 games, so that's half a season. So imagine if you give me 82 games to just watch film and I have a coach dedicated to watch film with me, unlimited gym access. Yeah, like, you know, those are going to be the smaller wins for me. Like in the beginning of the seaso

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: "I have two immigrant, my mom's from …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa: "I have two immigrant, my mom's from Jamaica, my dad's from Congo. Both came here and was just working to raise three kids. So I think it was kind of inherited in us. All three of them, both my sisters work hard too, so it's kind of just in us to work hard."

item.title: Celtics not actively shopping Jaylen Brown?
item.description: Shams Charania: "Everyone around the league is going to be talking about this. If this deal does not get done for the Celtics, what is the future of Jaylen Brown? The thing the Celtics do have though, and Jaylen Brown knows this, is he's got multiple years left on his deal. To my knowledge, they're not shopping Jaylen Brown. This is a very specific situation. This is now twice in four years where the Celtics have offered Jaylen Brown in a major trade for a future Hall of Famer. In 2022, they off

item.title: "Minnesota has been involved in the Giannis …
item.description: Shams Charania: "Minnesota has been involved in the Giannis Antetokounmpo thing at different points. They're not involved right now. What happens there? They know they need to make an upgrade, they know they need to add something to this team with Anthony Edwards on it. They've made a pitch on a bunch of different guys around the league. We'll see who they can come up with, but there's going to be a lot of tentacles that come out."

item.title: A lot of people are keeping an eye and monitoring the …
item.description: Shams Charania: "You definitely have to look at a couple players in New Orleans, Trey Murphy, Herb Jones. Those are two players that have gotten a ton of interest in the marketplace. A lot of people are keeping an eye and monitoring the Kawhi Leonard situation with the Clippers. He's got one year left on his deal. We don't know how that's going to play out with the Clippers. There's no urgency there, they've been through this with Kawhi before where they're entering the last year of his deal."

item.title: Wizards front office expected Trae Young to command max salary offers
item.description: The NBA rumor mill, which is often inaccurate, predicted months ago that any new deal between Trae Young and the Wizards would average no more than $40 million annually. But in recent weeks, Wizards decision-makers became convinced that, with the NBA’s new anti-tanking measures compelling more teams to compete, Young was going to command maximum-salary contract offers from other franchises through either a straight free-agent signing or a sign-and-trade proposal.

item.title: For Washington, the difference between signing Young …
item.description: Because the Wizards hold Young’s Bird rights, the Wizards had the latitude to offer Young up to a five-year contract with 8 percent annual raises. But Young’s new four-year deal instead features 5 percent year-over-year raises, the maximum year-over-year raise that any other team could have offered Young as a non-Bird free agent.
