For Washington, the difference between signing Young to 5 percent raises instead of 8 percent raises will amount to a total savings of $8.9 million over four years.

item.title: Golden Knights owner Bill Foley launches bid for NBA franchise in Las Vegas
item.description: Bill Foley is looking to add another sports franchise to his portfolio. The founder and majority owner of the Vegas Golden Knights announced that he is pursuing the award of an NBA franchise for Las Vegas. Earlier this year, the NBA voted to formally explore expansion, with one of those potential markets including Las Vegas.

item.title: "Las Vegas has earned its place among the great sports …
item.description: "Las Vegas has earned its place among the great sports cities in America, and an NBA team belongs here," Foley said. "We built the Golden Knights into a championship organization from the ground up, and we are prepared to do it again – with the same standard, the same commitment to this community, and the same insistence on winning. We have the market, a proven world-class arena, and a best-in-class organization in place. Our intention is to be ready the day the NBA is ready."

item.title: Clippers to add another 2026 draft pick as insurance against Aspiration scandal fallout?
item.description: The Clippers have had discussions about trading down, although sources indicate that they do seem set on making an early draft selection this year. Multiple league sources have speculated that if the Clippers look to trade down, don’t be surprised to see them get another pick in this year’s draft — just in case the NBA’s investigation into the Aspiration scandal results in the loss of future picks.

item.title: Keaton Wagler has added muscle to his frame during the …
item.description: Sources have indicated that Keaton Wagler has added muscle to his frame during the pre-draft process. He came to Illinois at 168 pounds and weighed 188 pounds at the combine; his frame should continue adding strength over the next few years.

item.title: Hawks are the most active team in discussing trade …
item.description: League sources say the Hawks are the most active team in discussing trade scenarios. They completed a deal with the Oklahoma City Thunder on Sunday night to acquire Aaron Wiggins into the trade exception they generated when they dealt Luke Kennard to the Los Angeles Lakers at the deadline. Sources have said Atlanta is discussing both trade-up and trade-down scenarios. But it also could just stay here and take the player it wants if he remains on the board.

item.title: Celtics not willing to add multiple firsts for Giannis Antetokounmpo
item.description: Heat Central: The Bucks owners are “split” on which decision to make with a Giannis trade and Boston is NOT willing to add multiple 1st round picks like Miami, per @JakeLFischer “We’re being told there is some type of split faction in MIL right now among the major stakeholders for the Bucks… the Celtics to our understanding, are not willing to offer the same level of multiple years of draft capital that the Miami Heat are” (Via @BleacherReport)

item.title: "Legendary. I feel like will be one of the best, if …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Caleb Wilson on how his draft class will be remembered: "Legendary. I feel like will be one of the best, if not the best draft classes ever"

item.title: Rockets willing to listen to offers excluding Amen Thompson
item.description: Rival teams that have contacted the Rockets recently have been informed that 23-year-old Amen Thompson is the only near untouchable on the roster, league sources told Yahoo Sports. Houston isn't actively shopping its All-Stars or other key members, sources said, but is merely expressing more of a willingness to listen than at previous junctures.

item.title: Charlotte, armed with picks Nos. 14 and 18, is …
item.description: Charlotte, armed with picks Nos. 14 and 18, is believed to be exploring avenues to move up, sources said, along with Chicago at No. 15 (which also owns the No. 4 pick) and Memphis at No. 16 (which also owns the 3rd overall selection).

item.title: "No NBA players have texted me this week"
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: AJ Dybantsa ahead of the draft: "No NBA players have texted me this week"

item.title: AJ Dybantsa on his draft class: I think it's going to be one of the most talked about since LeBron's
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: AJ on his draft class: "I think at least 10 of us will probably be still playing...let's say 8 years down the road. I think that's how deep we are but it could be more. I think it's gonna be probably one of the most talked about draft classes since LeBron's I think"

item.title: While the reigning champion New York Knicks assess …
item.description: Jake Fischer: While the reigning champion New York Knicks assess their trade options for picks No. 24 and 31 in this week’s NBA Draft, sources say reserve guard Jose Alvarado has agreed to move the decision date on his $4.5 million player option for 2026-27 from tomorrow, June 22, back to June 26.

item.title: Adjusting these figures slightly, as Young's new …
item.description: Keith Smith: Adjusting these figures slightly, as Young's new contract is reported to start at the 30% max amount of $49.5M: 26-27: $49.5M 27-28: $51.9M 28-29: $54.5M 29-30: $56.9M (player option) Total: $212.85M (matches what Young could have gotten from another team as a FA)

item.title: Today with the fam we treated ourselves to the huge …
item.description: Manu Ginobili: Today with the fam we treated ourselves to the huge pleasure of watching an ARG match in a World Cup for the first time and on top of that, watching the 10 become the all-time leading scorer in World Cups!! What a privilege!!

item.title: Mohamed Diawara plans to sign multi-year deal to stay with Knicks
item.description: Shams Charania: Restricted free agent Mohamed Diawara intends to sign a multiyear, $10-plus million deal to return to the New York Knicks, sources tell ESPN. Diawara -- the No. 51 pick in last year's NBA draft -- was part of the champion Knicks after spending the season on a standard contract.

item.title: This doesn't preclude them, they say, from taking …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "Speaking to the Wizards today, they are very happy to get Trae Young under contract. This doesn't preclude them, they say, from taking Peterson with the number one pick. Now, whether we believe that or not is a different story."

item.title: And, you know, we kind of talk around it and dance …
item.description: Josh Hart: "No one knows the understand besides y'all that wear the jersey, no one understands the weight that that jersey has sometimes. And, you know, we kind of talk around it and dance around it, but New York is a special place, but there's a lot of pressure when you put that jersey on. So we want to thank you and celebrate you (Carmelo Anthony) a little bit, man, for laying the foundation, but also, you know, talking to us during it."

item.title: @SportsVanessa will serve as ESPN's reporter for …
item.description: Greg Rajan: Rockets sideline reporter @SportsVanessa will serve as ESPN's reporter for Wednesday's second round of the NBA draft. She'll also host the digital "Hoop Streams" show for Tuesday's round 1 on the ESPN app and the network's social platforms.

item.title: Raptors could be interested in Myles Turner?
item.description: A couple of league sources have suggested that the Raptors could be interested in Bucks centre Myles Turner if the Bucks go into fire-sale mode after a potential Antetokounmpo trade.

item.title: It’s expected the Toronto Raptors will announce a …
item.description: It’s expected the Toronto Raptors will announce a multi-year contract extension for head coach Darko Rajakovic early this week, both as a reward for a job well done and a bet that he can continue to develop as a longtime leader of a team that’s trying to transition from "pretty good" to "actually good" and, ideally, much better than that over the life of the deal.

item.title: Per sources, it’s a move that Dick would be open to as …
item.description: The most likely development as the off-season unfolds will be a new address for Gradey Dick, the fourth-year wing who was leapfrogged in the rotation by Ja'Kobe Walter, Jamison Battle and A.J. Lawson in the final weeks of the season. Per sources, it’s a move that Dick would be open to as he looks for a fresh start and a chance to pick up from his promising second season (14.4 points per game with 54 starts) after things went south in Year 3 (six points per game with one start).

item.title: Anthony Davis and his camp have been seeking a max …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Anthony Davis and his camp have been seeking a max extension, and it’s plausible to consider him a potential trade candidate out of Washington, per @JakeLFischer (h/t @TheDunkCentral)

item.title: GOD, I trusted you at the beginning, and I will …
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo: GOD, I trusted you at the beginning, and I will continue to trust you throughout.

item.title: Kyrie Irving is kicking off his latest ANTA Tour this …
item.description: Nick DePaula: Kyrie Irving is kicking off his latest ANTA Tour this week — with a World Tour summer schedule featuring cities throughout Asia and in the US. The Las Vegas and New Jersey dates in July will also include Irving’s “Uncle Drew Circuit” youth basketball tournament games.

item.title: Blazers will be looking to trade for Anthony Davis is he becomes available
item.description: In his latest NBA insider report for Bleacher Report, Jake Fischer announced that the Blazers are open to the possibility of trading for the star big man should the Washington Wizards like to entertain a trade. "Portland, to our understanding, as they have been floating around and poking around in Giannis trade conversations, we've been told that they would look to pivot to Anthony Davis on the trade market," Fischer said. "They obviously have Jaylen Brown interest as well. "We've been told that

item.title: Pelicans have maintained an asking price of MULTIPLE …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: The Pelicans have maintained an asking price of MULTIPLE first-round picks for Trey Murphy or Herb Jones, per @JakeLFischer 6-12 teams have inquired about trading for them

item.title: He (Giannis) is on his way back from the states as of …
item.description: Heat Central: Giannis is on his way back from the states and ready for a trade to Miami or Boston, per @ShamsCharania "At this very hour the MIL Bucks are moving closer and closer to a trade resolution for Giannis.... He (Giannis) is on his way back from the states as of right now. He's ready for a trade whether it's Miami or Boston"

item.title: Those sources were granted anonymity so they could …
item.description: But NCAA and NBA sources who spoke with The Athletic consistently said they believe that May has what it takes to make the leap. Those sources were granted anonymity so they could speak freely, and “modern,” “collaborative” and “adaptable” were all words used to describe May, a Terre Haute, Ind., native. So was the word “kind,” which might seem strange for someone who got his start working as a student manager for the rough-edged Bob Knight at Indiana.

item.title: Duke’s Jon Scheyer was one name the Mavericks heavily …
item.description: The Mavericks interviewed NBA assistant coaches Micah Nori, Royal Ivey and Jama Mahlalelah, league sources said, while also closely examining the college ranks. Duke’s Jon Scheyer was one name the Mavericks heavily considered. In fact, their interest in him pre-dated Ujiri’s arrival, league sources said.

item.title: They have discussed a variety of trade-back scenarios, …
item.description: The Mavericks are expected to go into next season with Flagg and Kyrie Irving as cornerstones. They control the Nos. 9, 30 and 48 selections in this week’s NBA Draft. They have discussed a variety of trade-back scenarios, including one, according to league sources, that would net Dallas the No. 16 pick that Memphis currently controls.

item.title: Carlos Boozer on teams that don't draft his son Cameron: 'They will regret it'
item.description: All these GMs know you. So, when they ask you why should they draft him, and if we don’t, what would you say? Carlos Boozer: If they miss the boat, they will regret it. This kid is a winner. Basically, the one thing about Cam that separates him from everybody else in the draft is that he’s a guy that the rest of the team will galvanize around because he doesn’t cheat the game. He shows up every single day, he studies the game, he puts his work into it every single day. He’s as dedicated as you’r

item.title: We had him with different trainers that were really …
item.description: At what point did you say, ‘What I’m seeing from Cameron is different. What I’m seeing is something that could be on the highest level’? Carlos Boozer: I knew that this one was really special, probably like around like sixth grade. We had him with different trainers that were really good with developing skill sets in the backyard. And he was picking it up faster than everybody else early. And the light bulb went off like seventh, eighth grade, and he just took off from there. And you guys know t

item.title: He’s so fundamentally sound. He has a great focus. …
item.description: When Cam gets to the NBA, what lessons have you instilled in him that you know he’s going to take with him wherever he goes? Carlos Boozer: He’s so fundamentally sound. He has a great focus. He’s not going to get sidetracked. A lot of guys, me included, you get to the NBA and you start to get access to stuff that you didn’t have access to. You start to hang out a little bit. You start to mingle a little bit. He’s so locked in. He may do some of that with his teammates and have a good time. But h

item.title: “But at the end of the day, I’m going to a great …
item.description: “I’m excited just to hear my name get called, walk across that stage,” Cameron Boozer said. “No idea what that feeling is going to be like. You can be nervous, because you just don’t know where you can end up. It’s not really in your hands. “But at the end of the day, I’m going to a great situation, regardless of where I go. And I’m just excited for what the future has in store. It seems to be a lot of fun.”

item.title: Knicks and Diawara are working on eight figure deal to …
item.description: Ian Begley: Mohamed Diawara was certainly going to get significant interest from other teams as a restricted free agent, per league sources. Knicks and Diawara are working on eight figure deal to bring the rookie back to NY. Not done, but all signs pointing to it getting done, sources confirm. Diawara felt welcomed by NY from Day One and was very comfortable with his teammates. So even if another big offer came, NY had an edge. ESPN first reported Diawara deal.

item.title: They can give Mohamed Diawara up to four years, $11.3 …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: New York Knicks updated salary cap situation. They can give Mohamed Diawara up to four years, $11.3 million with his Non Bird rights. His minimum over four years is $10.6 million. They are still free to go above the second apron by re-signing their own free agents.

item.title: According to Eurohoops sources, Real Madrid has signed …
item.description: According to Eurohoops sources, Real Madrid has signed the new 10-year agreement with EuroLeague Basketball and is set to become a franchise alongside the league’s other shareholder clubs.

item.title: Celtics want to keep Hugo Gonzalez
item.description: Bill Simmons: I’m just gonna say…. I would be absolutely shocked if Hugo was/is thrown into any Giannis trade offer. The entire organization is all-in on him - Stevens prob the most. They could not be higher on him. I genuinely don’t believe he’s involved. Guess we’ll see.

item.title: The Celtics, as Bill says, certainly prefer to keep …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Bucks, as we reported last night, have certainly asked for Hugo. The Celtics, as Bill says, certainly prefer to keep him. Not exactly a make-or-break element but one of the variables that factors into this Boston vs. Miami struggle.

item.title: Since when is a 6’9 wing who plays any role you ask, …
item.description: kuz: Since when is a 6’9 wing who plays any role you ask, guards 1–5, shot almost 50% from the field and cares about defense a ‘throw‑in’? lol

item.title: But he says he’s a film junkie and studies opponent …
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: Darryn Peterson says he didn’t watch much basketball when he was a kid, and it was hard to watch full playoff games this year because he just got a new puppy. But he says he’s a film junkie and studies opponent tendencies.

item.title: To that end, the Nets met with Brown at least three …
item.description: To that end, the Nets met with Brown at least three times — including a workout in Brooklyn and visiting his family in Orlando. “It’s definitely unique,” Brown said of the visit. “Obviously there’s definitely been interest there, as there is in almost every single other team. But yeah, definitely, it made an impact. They got to talk to my mom and my dad. It was a great conversation.”

item.title: Julius Randle heads to Brooklyn in three-team trade
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: Minnesota is sending Julius Randle and the No. 28 pick in the NBA Draft to the Brooklyn Nets in a three-team trade that sends Nic Claxton to the Chicago Bulls, sources tell ESPN. The Timberwolves will acquire Brooklyn’s No. 33 pick for Randle and No. 28.

item.title: Timberwolves are trading Julius Randle to Brooklyn,
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Timberwolves are trading Julius Randle to Brooklyn, sources say

item.title: Timberwolves free up significant salary with the …
item.description: Shams Charania: Timberwolves free up significant salary with the remaining two years of Randle's deal, giving them more flexibility to retain Ayo Dosunmu and use exceptions. Nets acquire a three-time All-Star and two-time All-NBA forward in Randle into their cap space -- plus a first-rounder. Chicago uses part of its space on a talented athletic center, a position of need, with $30 million in room left.

item.title: Timberwolves now have the flexibility to re-sign Ayo …
item.description: Bobby Marks: The Timberwolves now have the flexibility to re-sign Ayo Dosunmu and have available the $15M non tax midlevel and $5.5M biannual exception. Minnesota will also create a $33.3M trade exception.

item.title: Chris Haynes reporting that Celtics are the favorites …
item.description: Barry Jackson: Chris Haynes reporting that Celtics are the favorites to acquire Giannis tonight or tomorrow

item.title: Naz Reid will be the starting power forward for Wolves after Julius Randle trade
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Naz Reid is going to be the starting power forward for the Minnesota Timberwolves for the first time in his career. This move is a big statement of belief in Reid and Jaden McDaniels to take the next steps in their development.

item.title: Bulls will use $23.1 million of their cap space to …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Bulls will use $23.1 million of their cap space to acquire Nic Claxton. They go from from $54 million in cap space to $30.9 million in room remaining. They would still have the $9.4 million room mid-level to use after. Great business getting him essentially for free.

item.title: This pending trade is essentially a “free agent …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: This pending trade is essentially a “free agent signing” of starting caliber center who has averaged 69 starts over last 4 seasons. Claxton, 27, is career 62.2% shooter and solid lob threat who also averaged 3.7 assists last season. Typically makes good decisions as roll man.

item.title: This move would still leave Bulls with roughly $30M of …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Nic Claxton has $23M and $21M left on his deal. This move would still leave Bulls with roughly $30M of cap space this summer

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo trade may expand before July 6
item.description: The trade does not include any additional teams -- it is a one-to-one move, sources said -- but both the Heat and Bucks will execute the deal on July 6, which leaves the framework open to see if there are opportunities to expand. Last June, the Phoenix Suns and Houston Rockets agreed to a deal that ultimately increased to a historic seven-team trade.

item.title: The Celtics registered interest in Antetokounmpo at …
item.description: The Bucks seriously negotiated with two finalists in recent weeks: the Heat and Boston Celtics, who were both on Antetokounmpo's list of preferred destinations. The Celtics registered interest in Antetokounmpo at the trade deadline in February, and aggressively pursued him this time around, offering Milwaukee a package featuring 2024 Finals MVP Jaylen Brown and two first-round picks, according to sources.

item.title: Brad Stevens has not actively shopped Brown in trade …
item.description: Celtics president Brad Stevens has not actively shopped Brown in trade talks, sources said, but the franchise has now offered him twice for future Hall of Famers over the past four offseasons. In 2022, Brown was the centerpiece of Boston's proposal to the Brooklyn Nets for Kevin Durant.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo's camp told Bucks he would not sign a long-term extension with the team
item.description: Sources told ESPN that Antetokounmpo and his agent, Alex Saratsis, consistently informed the Bucks from May 2025 through May 2026 that the franchise legend wanted a trade from the organization because he believed it was best for both sides to part ways. Antetokounmpo's reps informed the Bucks several times over the past 13 months -- including before, during and after the season -- that he would not sign another long-term extension with the team, according to sources. The Bucks made the decision

item.title: Kasparas Jakucionis was Miami's version of Boston's …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Kasparas Jakucionis was Miami's version of Boston's Hugo Gonzalez hang-up. Had been hearing all day the Heat did not want to part with the Illinois product on top of their Herro-Ware-Jacquez-No. 13 structure. That's an All-Star guard, three recent first-round picks still on rookie deals, a second lottery pick in tomorrow's loaded draft class, plus two more future firsts and a swap... It's really a sizable haul for Giannis Antetokounmpo. Compared to Jaylen Brown and two first-round

item.title: I can confirm that Heat has been informed by the Bucks …
item.description: Barry Jackson: I can confirm that Heat has been informed by the Bucks that they're getting Giannis. Cautious optimism pays off lol. Miami is exactly where Giannis preferred to be, someone who works for him reiterated again today.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo will be eligible starting on …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Giannis Antetokounmpo will be eligible starting on January 6 to sign an extension for: 4 years/$275M if he opts-out 3 years/$214M if he opts-in The opt-in is slightly more lucrative

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo heads to Miami in six player deal
item.description: Shams Charania: BLOCKBUSTER: The Milwaukee Bucks are trading franchise icon Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis to the Miami Heat for Tyler Herro, Kel'el Ware, Jaime Jaquez Jr., Kasparas Jakucionis, 3 first-round picks (including No. 13), 1 pick swap and 1 second-rounder, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Heat are sending to Milwaukee unprotected …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Heat are sending to Milwaukee unprotected first-rounders in 2031 and 2033 along with No. 13, a pick swap in 2030 and a 2033 second-rounder, according to sources. The trade does not include any additional teams -- it is a one-to-one move, sources said -- but both the Heat and Bucks will execute the deal on July 6, which leaves the framework open to see if there are opportunities to expand.

item.title: Heat will bring Bobby Portis in via the Duncan …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Heat will bring Bobby Portis in via the Duncan Robinson TPE they have. Miami will be hard-capped at the first apron after completing this deal, which will be in July after the league year changes over.

item.title: Ayo Dosunmu will sign five-year deal to remain in Minnesota
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent guard Ayo Dosunmu intends to sign a five-year, $112 million contract to return to the Minnesota Timberwolves, with a player option in the fifth season, sources tell ESPN. Timberwolves made it a major priority to lock in Dosunmu after his tremendous postseason.

item.title: Dosunmu’s agents, Mike Lindeman and Jeff Schwartz of …
item.description: Shams Charania: Dosunmu’s agents, Mike Lindeman and Jeff Schwartz of Excel Sports Management, worked with Timberwolves executives on Monday night to land the long-term commitment. Dosunmu and Anthony Edwards gained a chemistry after his arrival from Chicago in February.

item.title: Wolves not interested in Ja Morant
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Wolves have not been in the Ja Morant mix. Decision was made to look elsewhere for backcourt help

item.title: Bulls are trading Mo Gueye to Minnesota, sources say, …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Bulls are trading Mo Gueye to Minnesota, sources say, to complete the three-team deal that sends Julius Randle and No. 28 in tomorrow’s NBA Draft to Brooklyn for No. 33, and reroutes Nic Claxton into Chicago’s cap space in the new league year. Gueye’s salary is non-guaranteed for 2026-27.

item.title: They got Gueye on an end-of-season, non-guaranteed …
item.description: Joel Lorenzi: Bulls had a few options here to make trade legal (cash, top-55 protected future pick, etc). They got Gueye on an end-of-season, non-guaranteed deal in April, and they get Claxton without digging into assets.

item.title: Bucks didn't want to risk Jaylen Brown wanting out of Milwaukee?
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: Sources say Bucks owner Jimmy Haslem was a driving force in taking the Heat deal for Giannis Antetokounmpo over Boston’s offer. Haslem didn’t want to risk Jaylen Brown wanting out of Milwaukee in a year after dealing with Giannis and Myles Garrett trade demands. Haslem wanted certainty, and the Miami deal provided more of that with more total assets in return to Milwaukee.

item.title: The Bucks clear $20 million in the current two-team …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: The Bucks clear $20 million in the current two-team Giannis Antetokounmpo trade framework. They'd create a massive $25.5 million trade exception, which could grow if they expand the trade. It would be useful for taking on bad salaries with draft picks to add to their surplus.

item.title: The Heat are effectively out of first-round picks they …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: The Heat are effectively out of first-round picks they could trade. They could trade their 2029 first-round pick on the condition that their 2027 first-round pick (top-14 protected) conveys to Charlotte. They could try to regain control of it in a trade with the Hornets.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo was open to signing an extension with Heat, Celtics and Timberwolves before trade
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: The 3 teams Giannis Antetokounmpo was open to signing an extension with: Miami, Boston and Minnesota. The Heat land a foundational piece and now have the attention of top players searching for a new home.

item.title: They can re-sign Norman Powell up to any amount with …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: This Heat roster needs shooting. They can re-sign Norman Powell up to any amount with his Bird rights and even extend him now. They'll need to reduce their payroll to create space to re-sign him. They could also use more flexibility to open up the full $15M mid-level.

item.title: The Heat will bring Bobby Portis in via the Duncan …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: The Heat will bring Bobby Portis in via the Duncan Robinson TPE they have. Miami will be hard-capped at the first apron after completing this deal, which will be in July after the league year changes over.

item.title: If Andrew Wiggins opts into $30 M or opts out to sign …
item.description: Barry Jackson: If Andrew Wiggins opts into $30 M or opts out to sign multiyear deal with Miami instead - and if Norman Powell re-signs - that's a rotation of Giannis, Bam, Mitchell, Powell, Wiggins, with Larsson, Bobby Portis and player/players acquired through 3 different exceptions. Pretty, pretty good.

item.title: Ayo Dosunmu's deal comes in around $22 million per …
item.description: ‪Chris Hine‬: Ayo Dosunmu's deal comes in around $22 million per year, higher than I think a lot of folks thought it might. One thing about the Connelly era -- a few more million than perceived isn't much for him to haggle over if he thinks you're a part of the core long term.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo is the first multi-time MVP …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: FWIW: Giannis Antetokounmpo is the first multi-time MVP winner traded away since Moses Malone.

item.title: The Bucks go from having one of the most dire draft …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: The Bucks go from having one of the most dire draft pick situations to a slight surplus of first-round picks, mostly in the 2030s. The 2030 swap with the Heat could potentially offset the swap they already owe to the Trail Blazers. They could really use more second-round picks.

item.title: Giannis only cares about two things: basketball and …
item.description: As I said, Giannis only cares about two things: basketball and family. That’s it. So if you respect his family, and how private he tries to keep things, and also respect the game of basketball, it feels like you’re going to be in a good spot. Very early in my career, I realized that Antetokounmpo greatly values the opportunity to speak for himself. There is little value in pontificating on what he might be thinking or feeling because he would much prefer to tell you himself. He says what he beli

item.title: With NBA and NHL teams now laser-focused on offseason …
item.description: With NBA and NHL teams now laser-focused on offseason business, Scripps Sports — with local rights to five hockey teams and one basketball team — is in acquisition mode. Scripps already locked up the Pistons last month and was in the running for the Heat. “We have a business model that’s working really well for us ... in markets that we’re already doing business where we have typically an NBC, ABC, CBS or Fox station,” Scripps Sports President Brian Lawlor said. “We’re in 40-something markets. T

item.title: Landing Giannis may force Heat to part ways with Norman Powell
item.description: Bobby Marks: I think it potentially could cost you Norman Powell, your All-Star from last year, who is an unrestricted free agent. Remember, if we deal with these apron rules, right? Miami now becomes hard-capped at the first apron. They're limited as far as what they can go out and offer Norman Powell and the deal. And when you look at their back court, their back court is a little bit depleted here. They need a little bit more front court help. And they've got about five roster spots to go out

item.title: I think the biggest thing is trying to repair …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Jaylen Brown still has three years left on his contract, making roughly $185 million. If Jaylen Brown was going into the last year of his contract, I think basically all leverage would have been lost here. Now, there's some relationship repairing that needs to happen. As Shams said earlier, yes, 2022 he was certainly talked about with Kevin Durant. Now, four years later with Giannis. I think the difference now is that Jaylen Brown is a top 10 player in the NBA and he was basically b

item.title: Jaden McDaniels on Julius Randle trade: 'Crazy'
item.description: Q What's up, Jaden? Did you see the Julius Randle news? Jaden McDaniels: What do you mean? Q. He just got traded. McDaniels: For real? Q. Brooklyn. McDaniels: Oh, that's crazy.

item.title: Kyrie Irving calls reunion speculation 'psychotic' after missing 2016 Cavs gathering in Europe
item.description: Kyrie Irving: Me and my teammates who won a championship together in 2016. We are straight, y'all. All right, it's all love. Real talk. I'm actually walking through the streets of Shanghai right now. So, y'all just flow with me, man. Me and my teammates, me and my brothers who played on the Cavs, we are good. We are more than good. (…) Me and my teammates who won a championship together in 2016. We are straight, y'all. All right, it's all love. And when I get back to the States, I'm going to pop

item.title: I think had Steve Kerr not resigned in Golden State, …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: I think had Steve Kerr not resigned in Golden State, I think Dusty May would have been on the Warrior short list. I think he was on the short list a couple of other places and he was clearly on the short list for the Mavericks.

item.title: Timberwolves not targeting Ja Morant
item.description: The Wolves are not pursuing Memphis point guard Ja Morant, according to team sources, but will continue to canvas the league for other possibilities.

item.title: The NBA vultures are swirling around Ant …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: My question is what's Anthony Edwards thinking? Tim MacMahon: I was going to say we started off we just spent a half hour talking about the Giannis trade that we've been talking about for years, and I'm not declaring anything with certainty except for the fact that the NBA vultures are swirling around Ant in anticipation of him potentially becoming the next superstar who's available in the trade market.

item.title: Anthony Edwards wants to win right now, and the Wolves …
item.description: The Wolves could not afford to run it back again after making minimal changes to the group in the summer of 2025, especially after watching Towns win a championship in New York this season. Anthony Edwards wants to win right now, and the Wolves are banking on addition by subtraction, believing that removing Randle’s ISO-heavy offensive game and redistributing his 15.3 shots per game to McDaniels and Reid will open things up for Minnesota in a different way. McDaniels took a big step forward with

item.title: Naz Reid is now slated to be the primary starter at …
item.description: Naz Reid is now slated to be the primary starter at power forward for the first time in his career, a team source said. He has been the good soldier for the first seven seasons of his career, dutifully playing the role of super sub and winning Sixth Man of the Year in 2024. He has taken a backseat to Towns and Randle, but always quietly pined for the starting spot. Now he has it.

item.title: Especially given the timing, the Celtics’ decision …
item.description: Especially given the timing, the Celtics’ decision could bother Jaylen Brown. Since the last time he went through such trade rumors, he won the 2024 championship and the NBA Finals MVP award. Last season was his best — and not just because he set career highs with 28.7 points, 6.9 rebounds and 5.1 assists per game with the injured Jayson Tatum on the sideline. Teammates believed Brown’s leadership enabled them to exceed expectations. Other All-Stars might have expressed frustration about losing

item.title: LeBron James says he doesn’t know when the most …
item.description: NBA Courtside: LeBron James says he doesn’t know when the most important person in the summertime was the trainer and not the player: “Like the basketball trainer is the most important guy in the summer. I don’t know when that dynamic shifted because you know we used to play and a lot of my friends can attest to this like even when I got in the NBA you know, I built a little basketball court in my backyard in Akron at my house. I would get nine other guys and we would play in the summertime on a

item.title: James on the narrative around Cam Boozer size and …
item.description: NBA Courtside: LeBron James on the narrative around Cam Boozer size and athleticism: “I played with his dad it was kind of the same. They have the same projection. Wasn’t the tallest, you know, under size four. Wasn’t the most athletic, you know, and then we see what he was able to do, you know, I think he’s multiple All-Star, got the max a couple times. Utah gave him the max, you know, so like great career. The narrative of what you or what people are saying you are is never the end of the stor

item.title: Teams preparing for 'bidding war' for Jaylen Brown after Celtics miss on Giannis
item.description: Heat Central: Teams are now preparing to send offers for Jaylen Brown after Celtics strikes out on Giannis, Per @WindhorstESPN “What I expect to happen, is a bidding war for Jaylen Brown. In the most recent days, teams have been preparing for this eventuality… I think it’ll take time to play out, but if there is a draft pick involved, it could happen today” We got Giannis and Blew up Boston all in one

item.title: “The Celtics and Jaylen Brown will have hard …
item.description: Get Up: “The Celtics and Jaylen Brown will have hard conversations moving forward about the fallout from a trade that didn't happen. ... You've gotta get a deal done if you're the Celtics." — @ShamsCharania on what the Giannis trade to Miami means for Jaylen Brown

item.title: The Bucks still have work to do, as league sources say …
item.description: The Bucks still have work to do, as league sources say it remains unclear if Tyler Herro will remain with the team. While there is interest in making the Milwaukee native part of the his hometown’s program, sources say the Bucks will also listen to offers for the 26-year-old former All-Star. Herro is believed to have a robust market, with the Detroit Pistons known to be among the many teams interested in him. Since this trade won’t be finalized until July 6, it remains possible for additional te

item.title: Windhorst says Norman Powell will be GONE, unless he …
item.description: Heat Central: Brian Windhorst says Norman Powell will be GONE, unless he takes a massive pay cut: “They’re gonna lose their All Star, Norman Powell. Unless he takes a crazy discount. He’s gonna be gone… Andy Ellisburg their GM, is superior at his job. Erik Spoelstra, their developmental staff, I’m betting on the Heat. Just not in 2026-2027”

item.title: Heat attempted to include Davion Mitchell in Giannis trade
item.description: Per league sources, the Heat attempted to include point guard Davion Mitchell in the deal instead of Jakucionis. However, because Mitchell is entering the final year of his deal, as are Herro and Jaquez Jr., the Bucks made it clear that they had no interest in three of the four players in the deal having one season remaining. So the Bucks pushed for, and landed, Jakucionis. The 20-year-old, 6-foot-5 guard has three guaranteed seasons left on his rookie deal, including two team options.

item.title: “One guy that I already heard attached to them is …
item.description: Heat Central: Khris Middleton has already been linked to the Miami Heat, per @WindhorstESPN. “One guy that I already heard attached to them is Khris Middleton. Who obviously has a long history with Giannis and Bobby Portis… I don’t think you’re looking at the Heat instantaneously as title contenders”

item.title: Panathinaikos announced on Tuesday that they have …
item.description: Panathinaikos announced on Tuesday that they have officially ended their cooperation with Kenneth Faried, who played for the club last season. Faried joined Panathinaikos at the start of the season when Ergin Ataman’s team had many problems in the center position. Right from the start, he was more than impressive, being named EuroLeague’s MVP for the month of November.

item.title: “Maybe it also comes from some guilt of mine due to …
item.description: Nikola Jokic addressed Serbian journalists in Belgrade, where he initially emphasized feeling guilty about the failures at last year’s EuroBasket, where Serbia was eliminated in the round of 16 by Finland. “Maybe it also comes from some guilt of mine due to the failures we had in the last competition. So I can go home without shame. To get back to where we belong.” Regarding the captain’s armband, Jokic said with a smile, “I won’t tell you how the coach told me“: “I received this honor and privi

item.title: “He’s not afraid to be like, ‘I’m gonna go make a …
item.description: As Kon Knueppel listened to Jalen Brunson’s response, he thought about the times he’s watched him play with that exact demeanor in bigger moments, unafraid of the potential for a mistake. “He’s not afraid to be like, ‘I’m gonna go make a play. I’m gonna make an aggressive play. And if I make a mistake, it’s gonna be an aggressive mistake,’” Knueppel said. “He’s not afraid to lose,” Knueppel added. “And to win you can’t be afraid to lose.”

item.title: “I think superstars, teams are always going to trade …
item.description: The draft is one of the NBA’s busiest transaction periods, rivaling February’s trade deadline and July’s free agency. Will the league’s transactions look noticeably different on draft day in light of what’s coming? “I think superstars, teams are always going to trade first-round picks,” ESPN’s Bobby Marks told Front Office Sports. “I think the protection that has changed, you can’t protect a top-14 pick and the nuances with that. I think there’s a little bit of uncertainty when it comes to trad

item.title: “This is the year to have a lottery pick,” an Eastern …
item.description: Silver implemented the new system after roughly a third of the league tanked to get a high pick in a historically loaded draft. In February, he fined the Jazz and Pacers for resting star players for games or, in Utah’s case, in the middle of one and called it “worse this year than we’ve seen in recent memory.” But executives have previously told FOS that the draft goes beyond the top five, which is why tanking was as widespread as it was. “This is the year to have a lottery pick,” an Eastern C

item.title: I just went through every pick from 2020 through 2024. …
item.description: I just went through every pick from 2020 through 2024. That’s 150 first-round picks. First question: did they make an impact for more than a month or two for the team that drafted them? The obvious choices are Anthony Edwards and LaMelo Ball (2020); Cade Cunningham (2021) and Stephon Castle (2024). Throw in guys like Onyeka Okongwu (2020) and Anthony Black (2023) who clearly are difference makers for the teams that drafted them or traded for them on draft night. How many of that 150 total made t

item.title: Haynes: Tyler Herro thrilled about fresh start
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: Tyler Herro is thrilled about a fresh start and playing for his hometown team the Milwaukee Bucks. Herro always envisioned returning home at some point during his NBA career.

item.title: Jaylen Brown is accepting this and realizes this is a …
item.description: Brown enjoyed his more central role with the Celtics this past season while Jayson Tatum rehabbed his torn Achilles until March. Brown was a second team All-NBA selection and was even in the MVP conversations at various points. "From what I understand, Jaylen Brown is accepting this and realizes this is a chance to turn the page in his career and he may be getting his own team," said ESPN's Brian Windhorst on Monday's episode of First Take. "Which is something he's thought about for some time."

item.title: "If Luka comes up that's a compliment. If Giannis …
item.description: ‪Oh No He Didn't‬: Richard Jefferson on Jaylen Brown's name being mentioned in trade rumors: "If Luka comes up that's a compliment. If Giannis comes up that's a compliment, Jokic, if Shai, if those names come up you are a part of that class.

item.title: The Heat could try to lure impending free agent LeBron …
item.description: The Heat could try to lure impending free agent LeBron James with its full midlevel exception, but a James return is considered a long shot. The Heat also has available a $5.5 million biannual exception as well as a $5.6 million exception from the Haywood Highsmith trade with Brooklyn. That exception expires Aug. 17.

item.title: John Wall on Jaylen Brown: He wants his own team, he needs his own team
item.description: John Wall: "I feel like it is that time. I love him and Jayson Tatum together, they won a championship. You always get these conversations when you have two guys that think they are 1A. It is always difficult and when you let the media get involved or the outside noise get involved, it can mess up a team. I think when he won Finals MVP, it kind of shook a lot of people up. I think his comments from this year when he came out and was like, 'This is my favorite year of all.' Why would you say that

item.title: "The Celtics were not willing to part with enough …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: "The Celtics were not willing to part with enough young players and assets to get a deal done." @ShamsCharania says Boston offered Jaylen Brown and two first-round picks for Giannis Antetokounmpo before he was traded to Miami 👀

item.title: "The Pelicans are trying to trade Trey Murphy. I …
item.description: Marc Spears: "The Pelicans are trying to trade Trey Murphy. I texted him the other day. He was kind of laughing about all these rumors. He's in France right now. But I do think the right, I think the thing is this: it probably will depend on who's sitting there at 11 when that pick comes up. And there's probably a certain amount of players that Joe Dumars and Troy Weaver have on their list that could be attractive to their franchise. If the Warriors can get Trey Murphy, I'm all for trading that

item.title: "Just talking to people in South Beach tonight: …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: "Just talking to people in South Beach tonight: They're very happy that they got Bobby Portis in this trade. They're really excited about how he's gonna fit in with that lineup" - @ramonashelburne

item.title: Malik Beasley: " All 30 of them [ __ ] I'm ready to …
item.description: "What's your ideal team right now? If you had to get a call from any team you could pick right now, who are you picking?" Malik Beasley: " All 30 of them [ __ ] I'm ready to get back in the league. You hear me? Call me.

item.title: Tyler Herro has the highest scoring average of any …
item.description: HoopsHype: New Bucks guard Tyler Herro has the highest scoring average of any Wisconsin-born player in NBA history. Now you know that.

item.title: I will be incredibly surprised if the Wolves ever …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: I will be incredibly surprised if the Wolves ever utilize the $33 million trade exception that was created tonight. Those things are almost always much ado about nothing and it is clear that money was a factor tonight.

item.title: Myles Turner feels like a natural trade candidate now.
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: Myles Turner feels like a natural trade candidate now. But with the way veterans seem more valued this week post-lottery flattening, perhaps the Bucks keep him to help avoid the relegation zone. Most teams would need to send out $21.6 million to acquire him with his trade bonus.

item.title: Also heard right now that there's rising expectation …
item.description: Marc Spears: "I also heard right now that there's rising expectation that the Clippers will keep the pick and take Michael Brown Jr. from Louisville."

item.title: A lot of executives around the NBA believe that Dusty …
item.description: Shams Charania: "Think back to the last eight months. Patrick Dumont, the Mavericks owner, you let go of Nico Harrison and then bring in Masai Ujiri to run this organization, fire Jason Kidd within weeks after bringing in Masai Ujiri. Throughout the onset of the process, it was John Scheyer and Dusty May as the top two targets for the Dallas Mavericks. That's who they really zoned in on. A group of assistants were interviewed as well, but they landed on Dusty May. A lot of executives around the

item.title: "I'm going to tell him he better pick me up. If he …
item.description: Yaxel Lendeborg on Dusty May's hiring and potential reunion: "I'm going to tell him he better pick me up. If he doesn't, I'm going to be mad, I might block him. But it would be amazing being able to stick with him."

item.title: Pistons interested in Norman Powell
item.description: Sources say that the Pistons indeed intend to add Powell, who is coming off his first All-Star season, to their list of potential offseason targets as they look to add shooting and playmaking in support of star guard Cade Cunningham.

item.title: Miami could look to trade Nikola Jovic
item.description: It has also been suggested that Miami could look now to move Nikola Jovic to try to acquire some shooting to help space the floor for Antetokounmpo and his new frontcourt sidekick Bam Adebayo.

item.title: Sources tell @clevelanddotcom the Cavs are looking at …
item.description: Chris Fedor: #Cavs have the No. 29 pick in tonight’s draft — for now. Sources tell @clevelanddotcom the Cavs are looking at the possibility of trading No. 29, either getting out of the draft completely or moving back into Round 2

item.title: “Up until last year I was on the ball pretty much my …
item.description: When Darryn Peterson met with the media at the draft combine in Chicago, he had already interviewed with roughly 10 NBA teams. What surprised him the most was how few questions were asked about the hamstring injury he dealt with at Kansas. Peterson surmised that a recent interview he did with ESPN, where creatine was revealed as a potential cause, provided enough answers to assuage any concerns. While that didn’t come up very often, one frequent talking point was what position he will play at th

item.title: “At the end of the day, whoever wants to pick you is …
item.description: Peterson, who averaged 20.2 points last year at Kansas, measured in at the combine at 6-foot-4 ½ without shoes. That’s the exact same size as Shai-Gilgeous Alexander, who has won consecutive MVP awards while running point in Oklahoma City. It’s also the same height as Devin Booker, one of the best shooting guards in the NBA. Peterson, 19, has the size to play either spot effectively. He would either be a big lead guard or a prototype at the two. And despite his familiarity with one over the othe

item.title: “I think I can fit with any team just because I can …
item.description: Peterson, it would seem, could have the runway to start out off the ball and then develop into a floor general as Young's heir apparent. “I think I can fit with any team just because I can play both on and off the ball and help the team wherever I go,” Peterson said.

item.title: “The timelines, maybe looking back, were a little …
item.description: That plan was more than a decade in the making. He reached the first goal at the age of 32 — a little behind schedule. But when Dallas Mavericks team president Masai Ujiri hired him as the youngest general manager in the NBA on May 8, a few days before his 36th birthday, Schmitz was right on time for his dream job. “The timelines, maybe looking back, were a little arbitrary, round numbers,” Schmitz told The Dallas Morning News. “But I always believe in setting goals for yourself and focusing on

item.title: “Since I was a kid, I couldn’t sit still,” Schmitz …
item.description: This state of constant motion is where Schmitz is most comfortable. “Since I was a kid, I couldn’t sit still,” Schmitz told The News. “I was always running around the neighborhood with friends. I was the type of guy who’d be out in the Arizona heat for 18 hours a day, playing basketball. I always had a lot of energy as a human. I just enjoy doing things. … I’ve just never been a very static person. Obviously, there’s times where you want to slow down and kind of find your inner balance. I always

item.title: “We treated it like it was the NBA,” said Voigt, the …
item.description: Will Voigt was one of the first to witness Schmitz’s work ethic and keen sense for discovering talent. As the former head coach of the Bakersfield Jam, known in today’s G League as the Motor City Cruise, Voigt hired Schmitz fresh out of Arizona in 2012 as a “very low paid” video coordinator intern. While the role often entails creating and editing video for players and coaches to use to scout opponents, Schmitz was thrown into the deep end. “We treated it like it was the NBA,” said Voigt, the as

item.title: “I basically said to him, ‘Hey, I’m going to have 30 …
item.description: Schmitz spent just one year with the Jam from 2012-13, but used what he learned to position himself for the future. In 2012, he sent a cold email to DraftExpress founder Jonathan Givony, offering to make free player evaluation videos. “I basically said to him, ‘Hey, I’m going to have 30 videos on 30 prospects and if you want them, I’ll send them over to you for free.’ ” Schmitz told The News. “I think he heard ‘free’ and jumped all over it, and so that started that relationship.”

item.title: Blazers to hire Timberwolves lead assistant Micah Nori as head coach
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: The Portland Trail Blazers are hiring Minnesota Timberwolves lead assistant Micah Nori as the franchise's next head coach, sources tell ESPN. Nori -- widely regarded as one of the league's top assistants -- has been in the NBA coaching ranks since 2009 and now takes over in Portland on a multiyear deal.

item.title: Bucks were intrigued by idea of making Jaylen Brown franchise centerpiece
item.description: According to league sources, the Bucks were very intrigued with the idea of making Brown their new franchise centerpiece but also had serious concerns about whether history might repeat itself if he came to town. Brown, the five-time All-Star who is coming off the best season of his career and finished sixth in MVP voting, has three seasons and a combined $183 million left on his current deal. But if he didn’t want to be in Milwaukee, as some signs suggested in recent weeks, then the Bucks might

item.title: Pelicans have no plans to pursue Ja Morant
item.description: The Pelicans have been mentioned at various points this offseason as a potential Ja Morant trade suitor, but The Stein Line has been advised that New Orleans has no plans to pursue Memphis' very available guard. The Pelicans, as one source put it, drafted Jeremiah Fears this time last year to be their point guard of the future. Thus they are not, we're told, plotting any sort of run at Morant. New Orleans, however, does continue to receive significant external trade interest in Murphy and Herb J

item.title: While the Grizzlies weigh trade offers for Ja Morant …
item.description: Jake Fischer: While the Grizzlies weigh trade offers for Ja Morant and the decision at No. 3, I’ve also heard several teams have expressed interest in trading for Euroleague star Tarik Biberovic, whose draft rights are held by the Grizzlies. Biberovic, 25, shot 44% from distance for Fenerbahce over the past three seasons and led them to the 2025 Euroleague title.

item.title: So the Miami Heat need a slashing 2 guard 👀
item.description: Jaden Ivey: So the Miami Heat need a slashing 2 guard 👀

item.title: I can remember the accident, but I was too little to …
item.description: Kingston Flemings: I can remember the accident, but I was too little to remember some specific details, so what I’m able to tell you about it mostly comes from what my parents have told me. I was with my family at my aunt’s house, outside playing with my cousin. My aunt lived on a cul-de-sac, and there aren’t too many cars that come through, so we were throwing this little rubber ball to one another across the street. At one point, I ran to get the ball, and there were two cars parked side-by-si

item.title: “No. No! No!! For real!!! Help!!!! Kingston got run …
item.description: Kingston Flemings: I ended up running into the back door of this big Nissan Armada as it was moving, falling on my butt, and then … the back tire ran over my body, basically. Like right at hip level. All the kids playing outside ran in the house screaming and yelling for help. And we have a really big family, so my dad and mom, they were kind of used to hearing kids saying that something was “AN EMERGENCY!!!!!!!!!” and then it being nothing, but this time it was like…. “No. No! No!! For real!!!

item.title: My uncle scooped me up and immediately passed me to my …
item.description: Kingston Flemings: My uncle scooped me up and immediately passed me to my dad. Apparently I wasn’t crying, I was just kind of like, “Ooooooh … Oooooh.” Just moaning, I guess. My parents both say the first thing they remember seeing were these huge tire treads on my pants. And the tracks were actually embedded into my hip, like into my skin, pretty much the entire width of my body. After seeing that, as my mom tells it, “Everyone went into Go Mode.”

item.title: They raced me to the children’s hospital, and when the …
item.description: Kingston Flemings: They raced me to the children’s hospital, and when the doctors initially took a look at me and saw that my belly was stretched out and had gotten really big, they were worried about internal injuries and bleeding. They told my folks that they weren’t equipped to handle the level of trauma they were seeing, so I had to be transferred to a Level 1 trauma hospital, and I guess that whole process took a few hours. I can’t imagine how worried my parents were, and how stressful it a

item.title: Eventually the doctors were able to do a much more …
item.description: Kingston Flemings: Eventually the doctors were able to do a much more comprehensive examination and told my parents that when the tire rolled over my body, because I was so young at the time, and my bones were still so flexible, it actually bent some of the bones rather than breaking them. My bones weren’t hard enough to break yet. So, in that way it was actually a blessing how young I was.

item.title: Celtics listening to trade offers for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Pat McAffee: "The whole league is paying very close attention to the Jaylen Brown and Boston Celtics situation.. My understanding is that the Celtics are listening to interested teams and offers for Jaylen Brown" -- Shams Charania

item.title: "Giannis Antetokounmpo wasn't gonna sign a contract …
item.description: Pat McAffee: "Giannis Antetokounmpo wasn't gonna sign a contract extension in Milwaukee and he wanted out.. The Bucks were open for business and they're moving forward after making this trade with the Miami Heat" ~ @ShamsCharania #PMSLive

item.title: He wanted a trade out of Milwaukee because he felt …
item.description: Pat McAffee: "In May of 2025 Giannis Antetokounmpo and his agent were very direct with the Bucks that it was time to move on.. He wanted a trade out of Milwaukee because he felt like they could no longer compete for a championship" ~ @ShamsCharania #PMSLive

item.title: Nori was lead assistant of the Minnesota Timberwolves …
item.description: Jason Quick: BREAKING: Micah Nori has agreed to become the head coach of the Portland Trail Blazers, sources tell @TheAthletic . Nori was lead assistant of the Minnesota Timberwolves for the past five years. Nori, 52, signed a one-year deal with team options for each of the next two seasons.

item.title: It was kind of just proven when I stepped into this …
item.description: You put a lot of it in your dad’s hands in terms of your basketball career. What built that trust? AJ Dybantsa: It was kind of just proven when I stepped into this space. It was a business. He kind of showed that he can work that route. When people are on my back, I just hand it to them, and you kind of know what to say.

item.title: AJ’s eighth-grade year. First of all, I don’t like …
item.description: When did you know you could do it yourself? Ace Dybantsa: AJ’s eighth-grade year. First of all, I don’t like this narrative of Black parents being stupid, dumb, they don’t know how to do. I’m like, “Really. OK. I’ll show you.” You just learn through stuff. Ask questions. Surround yourself with people that you trust. It’s not too many. But you know, there’s a few. The guy behind [Armato] is one of them. And go from there.

item.title: “I think he’s come late to the (head-coaching) party, …
item.description: “I think he’s come late to the (head-coaching) party, but that doesn’t mean he’s not more than ready and won’t be successful,” Minnesota coach Chris Finch told The Athletic. Added Timberwolves assistant Elston Turner: “A good pick. He’s worked for a lot of teams, been in a lot of positions, been with some big-name players. A good, good pick.”

item.title: “He is elite in what I call the small pieces of the …
item.description: In Minnesota, where the Timberwolves have won five playoff series over the past three years, Finch entrusted Nori with handling the details of the game. “He is elite in what I call the small pieces of the game,” Finch said. “Small not that they are unimportant, but small in that they are often overlooked. He’s elite with lineup combinations. Elite with rotations. Elite with special situations, whether it’s ATOs (after timeouts), end of game or just understanding how to maximize possessions. He’s

item.title: “I will start on the court, I’m a very versatile …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa said his new NBA team will get a player who will make an impact on and off the court. “I will start on the court, I’m a very versatile [player],” Dybantsa said. “[I] can play [positions] 1 through 4. Guard 1 through 4. Playmaker. Scorer. I play the game the right way, but I do give the fans what they want. I put fans in the seats. [I’m a] very exciting player, very explosive, make a lot of highlight plays, but I try to play to win. “But off the court, [I’m] just a good kid. I love gi

item.title: Means a lot. He’s been with me since the jump. [He] …
item.description: What does your father mean to you? AJ Dybantsa: Means a lot. He’s been with me since the jump. [He] literally watched me enter this world. Put a basketball in my hand. Been behind me through it all. That’s on the court. Off the court, all my success was credited to my mom.

item.title: Miami unlikely to be able to re-sign Norman Powell
item.description: Heat Central: We can probably take Norman Powell off this starting 5. The Heat are expecting him not to be there this offseason. Financially it’s going to be very difficult to keep him. They could participate in a sign a trade with Norman Powell…. They understand they have to fill this roster out. My understanding talking to people in Miami, shooting, shooting, and shooting.

item.title: Jonas Valanciunas headed to Zalgiris Kaunas?
item.description: After 14 years in the NBA, Jonas Valanciunas is set to return to Europe and commit to Zalgiris Kaunas, according to BasketNews sources. The Lithuanian center has committed to a two-year deal with Zalgiris if he secures a release from his NBA contract with the Denver Nuggets. If the Valanciunas deal materializes, Zalgiris will complete its roster for the season, with no further additions expected, BasketNews sources confirm.

item.title: Yes, Ant was frustrated by how last season ended. But …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Yes, Ant was frustrated by how last season ended. But he has also already won a lot here and believes in his teammates and the org to keep upgrading. He's under contract for 3 more years. Orgs are always on the clock with star players, but Wolves have time here.

item.title: “LeBron will stay with the Lakers, I think. It’s hard …
item.description: Which team do you think LeBron will be with next season? NBA executive: “LeBron will stay with the Lakers, I think. It’s hard to imagine him leaving. If he wants his money, it’s going to be hard for him to leave. I have to imagine there’s some kind of compromise they can do on, compensation-wise. It’s just easier. I don’t know where else he would be going. I think he would rather play with his son and not have to move.”

item.title: “I think he would be good in Golden State. He’s smart …
item.description: NBA assistant coach: “I think he would be good in Golden State. He’s smart enough to be able to play with those other smart minds and maximize everything. He could use his brain with those guys and not have to use as much athleticism. It’s a perfect spot for him. I don’t know if he can put up numbers. But as far as enjoying the game, using his brain and being effective, it’s a good fit. He can make plays happen instead of reacting to plays. He’d have everything that he needs. He’s a brilliant pa

item.title: “I think LeBron stays with the Lakers. I don’t think …
item.description: NBA scout: “I think LeBron stays with the Lakers. I don’t think he’d leave his son. I could be wrong. But I don’t think LeBron is giving anybody a discount. He wants his respect and wants to do it on his terms. The Lakers may wish they could get off of him, but he still has control over that deal. The Lakers are the best option. But if all three are healthy with Luka, Austin [Reaves] and LeBron, I don’t think the Lakers are a title contender. Luka is amazing. But I don’t see him enough for the L

item.title: Myles Turner and Kyle Kuzma trade candidates for Bucks?
item.description: With Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bobby Portis gone, all eyes turn to holdovers Myles Turner and Kyle Kuzma as trade candidates for the Bucks. Turner has always known that if Antetokounmpo was ever traded, he’d be a strong trade candidate, HoopsHype has learned. During his Pacers tenure, Turner was linked to the Hornets, Pelicans, and Lakers in trade rumors at various points. Those teams could all make sense now, and possibly the Celtics. Turner, 30, is still owed $83.55 million, including a player

item.title: Elsewhere, within league circles, there was palpable …
item.description: Elsewhere, within league circles, there was palpable chatter about the Pistons hoping to get involved in the Antetokounmpo trade to land Tyler Herro, while dangling assets such as Isaiah Stewart, Ron Holland, Duncan Robinson, and draft compensation, league sources told HoopsHype. In addition, the Los Angeles Lakers were linked with trade interest in Kel’el Ware over the past couple of days, sources said.

item.title: If any deal with Detroit ever came to fruition, expect …
item.description: Will the Pistons make another run at Herro, who’s currently set to join his hometown Bucks? Or will Detroit attempt to pivot by making a run at Norman Powell in free agency or via trade for Trey Murphy III, whom they’ve expressed interest in? If any deal with Detroit ever came to fruition, expect Pelicans executive Troy Weaver to insist on reuniting with Stewart, one of his favorite players from his Pistons tenure.

item.title: Julius Randle excited to join Brooklyn Nets
item.description: Julius Randle is excited to join the Nets and wants to be in Brooklyn, league sources told HoopsHype. After coming to New York amid a rebuilding situation, Randle evolved into a three-time All-Star and two-time All-NBA forward who helped the crosstown rival Knicks reach consecutive Eastern Conference Semifinals appearances before being dealt to Minnesota as part of the Karl-Anthony Towns trade. Randle is owed $33.33 million for the upcoming season and has a $35.80 million player option for the 2

item.title: Nets wanted to open up more time for Day’Ron Sharpe after Nic Claxton trade
item.description: In addition to moving up five spots into the late first round of the draft, another factor in the decision to trade Nic Claxton was the opportunity to free up more playing time for center Day’Ron Sharpe, sources told HoopsHype. Sharpe, the longest-tenured Net going into his sixth season at just 24 years old, impressed Nets brass last season and was lauded by coach Jordi Fernandez for his rebounding prowess. Sharpe is currently penciled in as Brooklyn’s starting center.

item.title: Warriors have discussed No. 11 pick to Pelicans for Trey Murphy
item.description: Golden State has also been one of many interested suitors in New Orleans Pelicans forward Trey Murphy III, and the two teams have discussed concepts involving the No. 11 pick, league sources told HoopsHype. As previously reported by HoopsHype, the Pelicans have searched for a trade into the lottery and are targeting a player. Nate Ament has been mentioned as one potential candidate for the Pelicans. Before this past trade deadline, several teams, including the Warriors, Pistons, Pacers, Hawks, T

item.title: Warriors, Hornets discussed picks swap
item.description: The Warriors have talked with the Charlotte Hornets about the possibility of swapping draft picks, with Golden State’s No. 11 pick going to Charlotte for Nos. 14 and 18, contingent on certain players being on the board, league sources told HoopsHype. Charlotte has signaled a willingness to move up in the draft, seemingly to leapfrog Oklahoma City, and executives across the league are wondering which player the Hornets could be targeting.

item.title: Cedric Coward: "Memphis has gotten a bad rap"
item.description: HH: With this young core and picks No. 3 and 16, where do you see this team’s trajectory? Cedric Coward: 'I see a team that’s trying to develop talent where we can compete with the Spurs, the Thunder, and teams of that caliber that are championship-level teams. For me, I can focus on what I do on the court on a day-to-day basis and how I treat my own work in terms of making sure I’m on top of it and then that will trickle down to helping me be a leader and making sure they’re putting in their ow

item.title: Cedric Coward on Ja Morant: "He’s one of my favorite teammates I’ve ever been around"
item.description: HH: How would you describe Ja Morant, and what could be next for his future? Coward: 'He’s one of my favorite teammates I’ve ever been around. He’s just a standup guy. He’s one of them dudes. When we had the ice storm, he offered to pay for my groceries. I declined it, but the offer was just who he is. He always wants to take care of people. He took care of me as a rookie in certain situations. He’s a good person to be around. He’ll joke around with you. Usually, when you go to teams with supers

item.title: Anthony Edwards still committed to Minnesota
item.description: Everyone wants to know what Anthony Edwards thinks. Multiple sources close to Edwards dismissed the chatter as just that. One said there was no truth to any of it. Another reiterated Edwards and his team love Minnesota, and they think being here, in this location with this franchise is the “ideal” situation.

item.title: Edwards wants to remain a part of a highly competitive …
item.description: Yes, Edwards wants to remain a part of a highly competitive team that is capable of winning playoff series and contending for championships. But there is a faith in the team and in President of Basketball Operations Tim Connelly to do what’s necessary to ensure that vision now and into the future.

item.title: Adam Silver: NBA targeting expansion for the 2028-29 season
item.description: NBA commissioner Adam Silver just put a date on when a potential new Seattle Sonics franchise would begin play should the league decide to award an NBA expansion team to the Emerald City. “If we expand, at least, we’re thinking ’28-29 season,” Silver said Tuesday in an interview on The Dan Patrick Show.

item.title: “The only two cities, at the moment, we’re looking at …
item.description: The 2028-29 season has been speculated as the target should the NBA decide to expand. The NBA Board of Governors approved in March to explore expansion in two specific cities: Seattle and Las Vegas. “The only two cities, at the moment, we’re looking at are Las Vegas and Seattle,” Silver continued to Patrick. “We haven’t had a vote on it, it’s not a foregone conclusion, but we’re in the process of talking to groups that are interested in getting an expansion team in those two cities.”

item.title: Silver also said that if the NBA expands by two to 32 …
item.description: Silver maintained that that the NBA could decide to add two, one or no teams at all at this juncture. “We’re currently looking at two teams, but what we’ve said is it’s possible we won’t expand at all, and we could expand to just one market. But we’re officially looking at both those markets right now,” Silver said. Silver also said that if the NBA expands by two to 32 teams, “we’ll want to likely even out the conferences, so there will be some movement then.”

item.title: DeMar DeRozan expected to be waived and stretched if not traded by Kings
item.description: In the midst of a rebuild and over the first apron, barring an unexpected trade, the Sacramento Kings are expected to waive-and-stretch forward DeMar DeRozan, HoopsHype has learned. DeRozan’s $25.74 million expiring contract is only guaranteed for $10 million, which can be stretched over the next three seasons. Should DeRozan enter free agency as expected, the Los Angeles Clippers are expected to have interest in the California native, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Kings unlikely to pursue Ja Morant, plan to draft point guard
item.description: With the Kings projected to take a point guard with the No. 7 pick in the NBA Draft, the chances of the franchise pursuing Ja Morant going forward are unlikely, HoopsHype has learned. This past season, the Kings were willing to acquire Morant if they were incentivized, but Memphis was uninterested in giving up draft compensation to move Morant, a two-time All-Star.

item.title: NBA executives wondering if Norman Powell will leave Heat after Giannis Antetokounmpo trade
item.description: A few executives who spoke with HoopsHype believe Miami’s All-Star guard Norman Powell could now be a candidate to leave the Heat, and the Detroit Pistons are among the teams who’ll be trying to pry him from South Beach, league sources told HoopsHype. Andrew Wiggins has a $30.17 million player option for the upcoming season. Executives are waiting to see if Wiggins will exercise his option or decline it with the intention of signing a new multi-year deal with Miami, or if he’ll consider a new de

item.title: Becky Hammon on Jalen Brunson: 'All he did was prove history wrong'
item.description: Madeline Kenney: Becky Hammon on Jalen Brunson leading the Knicks to a championship: “Jalen, all he did was prove history wrong, he proves he's an outlier, so you can put his name next to Steph Curry and Isiah Thomas, and I thought he played brilliantly, especially down the stretch. I mean, he was that 1A dude, but like, apologize, I'm never gonna apologize for having an opinion, that's what ESPN pays me for.”

item.title: Bucks plan to keep Kel'el Ware for now
item.description: Jake Fischer: I do believe Milwaukee has tried to package Nos. 10 and 13 to move up in tonight's first round, but I don't believe there's going to be any trades in the top-10. Sources say the Bucks, at this point, plan to keep Kel'el Ware, despite interest from rival teams like the Lakers. Milwaukee coveted Ware and Kasparas Jakučionis in their recent drafts. The Bucks also value Tyler Herro, sources say, and don't necessarily plan to move him, while Milwaukee will field offers for the All-Star

item.title: “[The] Brooklyn [Nets] and the [Los Angeles] Clippers …
item.description: Sam Amick: “[The] Brooklyn [Nets] and the [Los Angeles] Clippers were mentioned as teams that were out front in that race, but I think the list is very long. I think that Detroit comes to mind. [With] Boston, actually, Trey is seen as a potential plan B for them if they miss out on Giannis, which they obviously did. So, Trey is going to have a lot of suitors, and I think that could be a draft-night deal. “There was even a scenario floating around with Trey going to the [Atlanta] Hawks in a deal

item.title: Believe that both Washington's Hannes Steinbach and …
item.description: I believe that both Washington's Hannes Steinbach and Michigan's Morez Johnson Jr., in addition to Mara, will all be off the board in the top 15. I've been hearing that the Bulls, who hold No. 15, could well be Johnson's floor for some time.

item.title: Sources say that the Hawks and Mavericks both visited …
item.description: Sources say that the Hawks and Mavericks both visited Ament last weekend in Memphis, where he was completing his pre-draft preparations.

item.title: I believe Labaron Philon Jr. is in play at No. 10 and …
item.description: I believe Labaron Philon Jr. is in play at No. 10 and 13 to the Bucks now that Milwaukee controls that pick by way of the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade. Philon told me that the Bucks were the one team he worked out for multiple times and I've heard from multiple sources with knowledge of the Bucks' thinking that they are quite high on the Alabama point guard.

item.title: Darryn Peterson expected to be drafted at No. 2
item.description: League sources expect Peterson to be the second pick in the draft with Peterson offering the highest upside on the board.

item.title: League sources say the Clippers are weighing Brown, …
item.description: League sources say the Clippers are weighing Brown, Illinois guard Keaton Wagler, and Arkansas guard Darius Acuff with this choice, but they could also trade down and instead target Arizona guard Brayden Burries.

item.title: Hawks want Keaton Wagler if he's available at No. 8
item.description: League sources say the Hawks want Illinois guard Keaton Wagler if he’s still on the board, and there’s always a possibility they could trade up for him.

item.title: #Mavs say new head coach Dusty May will be introduced …
item.description: Mike Curtis: The #Mavs say new head coach Dusty May will be introduced at an introductory press conference at a later date.

item.title: Michigan to receive $5 million as part of Dusty May's buyout
item.description: Jeff Goodman: Per Dusty May's Michigan contract, the school will receive $5 million as part of his buyout. May never signed a new contract after winning the national title, but did change agents - going from Andy Miller to CAA, source told @TheFieldOf68

item.title: LeBron James: "Guys are not taking much time off after …
item.description: LeBron James: "Guys are not taking much time off after the season now to get back on the court because it's too much build up and the game is so much faster now. You can't be, you know, you used to have the ability to kind of use training camp and use like preseason to kind of get into the rhythm, you know, but now with so many, we're playing so much faster, you know, and soft tissue injuries are happening to reoccurring now that you have to start the the the body movement a lot sooner than than

item.title: LeBron James: "I don't know when that changed, that it …
item.description: LeBron James: "I don't know when that changed, that it was more beneficial to do the individual workouts over the five-on-five play through the summer. I don't know when the most important person in the summertime was the trainer and not the player. I know y'all are going to be like, 'Bron, we ain't seen no five-on-five clips of you lately.' Yeah, and you ain't."

item.title: LeBron James to Steve Nash: "The 96 draft, besides you …
item.description: LeBron James to Steve Nash: "The 96 draft, besides you guys being one of the greatest drafts of all time, is that Slam cover shoot that you guys did. I had that in my room when I stayed in Spring Hills, it was on my wall with all you guys there."
