item.title: Listen, a lot of these guys and these prospects are …
item.description: Kendrick Perkins: "I like Memphis. I actually love Memphis. I love the city of Memphis. I don't even want to get into it with the Memphians and all that stuff, man. I got people out there, I was going to school there. I'm just telling you what I'm hearing, so they don't be on my ass like back pockets. Listen, a lot of these guys and these prospects are trying to avoid getting drafted by Memphis. I'm just telling you this, real talk. What y'all thoughts on this? I'm just telling you, this is some

item.title: Myles Turner thought he would be traded to the Celtics and Lakers at different points in his career
item.description: Myles Turner on dealing with constant NBA trade rumors: "I'm so numb to it now. At the very beginning of my career, I was in a trade rumor every single offseason. I had my bags packed twice. I thought at one point in my career I was going to be traded to Boston for Gordon Hayward and I thought I literally was ready to go and then that fell through at the last second. And then I also thought I was going to be traded to the Lakers for a while. So I'm so numb to it now, if it happens it happens and

item.title: Myles Turner: "I've been with my girlfriend for four …
item.description: Myles Turner: "I've been with my girlfriend for four years now and I tell all the time like my life, my career changed when she into my life. It changed my mindset, it changed my mentality. I had the most success I've had in my career with her by my side."

item.title: Myles Turner: "My parents, they're my everything, man. …
item.description: Myles Turner: "My parents, they're my everything, man. They have supported me through and through no matter what decisions I've made in life and they have helped me immensely. They've exemplified what it means like to be parents and to get me to this level. My mom emptied her retirement fund to fund like my AAU. My dad worked the night shift, so I barely saw him during the day, but if I had a game he would sneak out of work, get someone to cover his shift and watch."

item.title: The Dallas Mavericks today announced the appointment …
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks today announced the appointment of Dusty May as the franchise's new head coach. May joins the Mavericks after leading the University of Michigan to the 2026 NCAA National Championship. Widely regarded as one of the sport's premier program builders and player developers, May has earned a reputation for creating winning cultures, maximizing talent, and leading teams to sustained success.

item.title: "We set out to find a leader who embodies the values …
item.description: "We set out to find a leader who embodies the values we want to define our organization," said Dallas Mavericks President of Basketball Operations Masai Ujiri. "Dusty has won at every stage of his career because of his ability to build. He develops players, creates accountability, and brings people together around a shared standard of excellence. His work ethic is extraordinary, and his teams consistently reflect his values. When you study his journey, you see someone who has earned every opport

item.title: “I am honored to join the Dallas Mavericks …
item.description: “I am honored to join the Dallas Mavericks organization," said May. "This is one of the most respected franchises in professional sports, with passionate fans, a talented roster, and a clear commitment to building a championship organization. I am grateful to Patrick Dumont, Masai Ujiri, and the Mavericks organization for this opportunity, and I look forward to helping bring another championship to the city of Dallas."

item.title: Celtics now listening to trade offers for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Shams Charania: "I am told the hard conversations between these two sides have started. This has been a long relationship between Jaylen Brown and the Celtics. It has been mostly fruitful when you think about the success that these two sides have had. But I am told here, as we are hours away from the NBA draft, the Boston Celtics have started to listen, engage and discuss trades around Jaylen Brown. Sources tell me the Celtics are speaking to teams right now about Jaylen Brown. In 2022, the Celt

item.title: So whatever happens tonight, I don't think the draft …
item.description: Shams Charania: "There are multiple teams monitoring Kawhi Leonard right now here over the next few weeks. Kawhi Leonard has one year left on his deal. The Clippers have been in this position before with Kawhi Leonard. When you go into the final year of your deal, you want to know as a veteran player, you are either going to get an extension or not, and Kawhi Leonard is at that point in his career. So whatever happens tonight, I don't think the draft is necessarily dependent on Kawhi Leonard's f

item.title: Caleb Wilson on mock drafts: 'I don't pay attention to it'
item.description: Caleb Wilson on mock draft: “No, I don’t pay attention to it. I feel like whatever God wants me to do, he’s going to do it for me. He’s going to put me where I need to be to succeed. Honestly, it’s all noise. Once I get drafted tomorrow, I get on the flight and it’s time to hoop. I’m not really thinking about where I’m going to be. I’m just thinking about having the opportunity to play at the next level.”

item.title: “Kind of just going in blind. Just wherever, whoever …
item.description: Do you have an idea of your range, or are you going in blind and getting surprised? Allen Graves: “Kind of just going in blind. Just wherever, whoever wants to take a chance on the kid, that's where I'll be. I don't have any preferences or anything like that. Just really grateful to be here, really grateful to be having an opportunity like this and sitting here before y'all, just taking it in one day at a time.”

item.title: Really grateful we get to be here and doing this …
item.description: Allen Graves: “My favorite prospect in this draft, I’m going to go shout out Louisiana, Chris [Cenac]. Louisiana boys. I played against him growing up in high school and everything, so just shout out to him. Really grateful we get to be here and doing this together from Louisiana, just putting on for the boot.”

item.title: “My favorite prospect in this draft, I’m going to say …
item.description: Cameron Carr: “My favorite prospect in this draft, I’m going to say Chris [Cenac]. I played against him this year, and I’ve gotten to see him two times and what he has to bring to the table. I’m excited for everything that guy has in store.”

item.title: If it ain’t me, I like everybody. Everybody has game, …
item.description: Darryn Peterson: “I don’t like that answer. If it ain’t me, I like everybody. Everybody has game, though, and I respect everybody’s game.”

item.title: Crazy wingspan, man. He’s been shooting well. I think …
item.description: Dailyn Swain: “Favorite prospect, probably Cam [Carr]. He’s just my boy, I’ve known him for a while. I think he has immense potential. He’s a freak athlete, he’s been jumping out of the gym since we were kids. Crazy wingspan, man. He’s been shooting well. I think he has potential to be a longtime NBA player, great in the league.”

item.title: Hawks very active on trade talks around No. 8 pick
item.description: Jonathan Wasserman: Few hours before the draft, Atlanta Hawks a team that sounds pretty active in trade talks at #8, per multiple sources

item.title: Here are some names I expect the Phoenix Suns to have …
item.description: John Gambadoro: Ok I know Suns fans have been waiting for this - Here are some names I expect the Phoenix Suns to have interest in if they stay at #47 or move up in the second round. Not sure they can get any of these at 47 so main reason I have been saying I expect they attempt to move up in the second-round. Baba Miller Trevon Brazile Tyler Bilodeau Ja'Kobi Gillespie Joshua Jefferson Dillon Mitchell

item.title: Unrivaled and Project B have similar schedules during …
item.description: The split decision underscores a new reality in women’s basketball: Two winter leagues are battling each other for top WNBA talent. Unrivaled and Project B have similar schedules during the WNBA offseason, making players unlikely to participate in both leagues, though both have worked around players’ WNBA and national team obligations.

item.title: Project B also confirmed to FOS this week that its …
item.description: he league has announced a slew of its own celebrity investors, including WNBA legends Candace Parker and Lauren Jackson, and tennis stars Novak Djokovic and Sloane Stephens. The Financial Times reported in February 2025 that Project B was backed by Saudi Arabia’s sovereign wealth fund, but the company told FOS in November that Saudi Arabia is not a financial backer. Project B also confirmed to FOS this week that its relationship with Saudi events company Sela has ended.

item.title: ESPN NBA reporter Tim Bontemps is in advanced talks to …
item.description: ESPN NBA reporter Tim Bontemps is in advanced talks to join the Atlanta Hawks’ front office, sources told Front Office Sports. A deal has not been finalized, one source said. Spokespeople for the Hawks and ESPN declined to comment.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard says his stoicism comes from playing with the Spurs
item.description: The stoicism, it turns out, has an origin story. “I think it started off just being on the Spurs,” he says, “just seeing the older vets and how they carried themselves and what they wanted their players to act like on the floor.” The Spurs, under Gregg Popovich, famously ran one of the most understated franchises in professional sports — a culture shaped in no small part by Tim Duncan, who won five championships and retired without ever seeming particularly interested in being celebrated for it.

item.title: The understatement makes me laugh. Because a few …
item.description: Instead, he figured it out himself — through observation, repetition, and what turns out to have been a surprisingly intense hatred of losing. “I just cared about winning a lot,” he says. The understatement makes me laugh. Because a few minutes later he casually admits that he used to cry after losses. Not occasionally. Regularly. “Soon as the game over, if we lose, I’ll just start crying,” he says. Then he laughs. “Like, damn, we lost. I thought you supposed to win every game.” For all the myth

item.title: “Health,” he says, without a pause. “You can’t do …
item.description: But when I ask him what he considers the greatest luxury in life, his answer reframes the entire conversation. “Health,” he says, without a pause. “You can’t do nothing without health. As soon as somebody gets sick, they’re just in a bed, or you’re told you can’t do certain things.” Then he goes somewhere more personal: “Whoever it is — your loved ones — you want them to be able to walk, run, talk, spend time with you.”

item.title: “So if somebody was an NBA player or college player …
item.description: When I ask him what he wishes somebody had told him at twelve years old, the answer comes immediately. “I wish I just had a foundation or somebody that was close to some type of college or NBA team,” he says. “It was kind of like learn as I go. You know, being young, being in Moreno Valley… dad’s far away. Not really in basketball like that.” Suddenly the courts make more sense. So do the free leagues he is building in Moreno Valley and the opportunities he is trying to create for kids coming up

item.title: "He's a guy who really cares for your well-being, not …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Michigan forward Yaxel Lendeborg on what Dusty May brings to the Mavs (via ESPN): "He's a guy who really cares for your well-being, not just what you can do for him on the court. He puts away all that stuff aside and steps in like a father-figure role and really cares."

item.title: He's gonna set the standard, but he's also going to …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Michigan forward Morez Johnson Jr. on what Dusty May brings to the Mavs (via ESPN): "He brings consistency. He's going to show up and be the same guy everyday. He's gonna set the standard, but he's also going to follow the standard as well. He's not just gonna say something & do the opposite. He's a great guy. He wants to put his players in the right positions to do well."

item.title: Masai Ujiri on hiring Dusty May: Many things translate from college to the pros
item.description: Mike Curtis: Asked Masai Ujiri about hiring Dusty May as Mavs coach: “Dusty stood out in many ways. I saw him in Chicago in the predraft camp & observed him. Talking to people & it’s a different ball game with NIL & college & how you operate with youth. It’s almost like a professional league in college. Many things translate.”

item.title: “That doesn’t mean a trade will necessarily happen.”
item.description: Daniel Donabedian: Shams Charania repeats that the Celtics are engaged in trade talks about Jaylen Brown, but said nothing may come of it: “That doesn’t mean a trade will necessarily happen.”

item.title: Kevin Durant, the all-time leading scorer in Olympic …
item.description: Kevin Durant, the all-time leading scorer in Olympic basketball history and a four-time gold medalist, will be among the USA Basketball players who will turn to coaching at a 3x3 tournament this summer to support various charities.

item.title: People close to Anthony Edwards dismiss trade rumors
item.description: Multiple sources close to Edwards dismissed the chatter as just that. One said there was no truth to any of it. Another reiterated Edwards and his team love Minnesota, and they think being here, in this location with this franchise, is the “ideal” situation.

item.title: Heat have the full allowable $7.9M to spend on such a …
item.description: Ira Winderman: Worth considering amid Heat's hard-cap crunch is that a second-round pick counts as about half of a standard minimum contract. Heat have the full allowable $7.9M to spend on such a pick or picks, money that does not count against cap, tax or apron. Heat already hold No. 41 pick.

item.title: He reiterates that a decision on Las …
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Adam Silver, in his pre-draft interview on ESPN, says 2028-29 would be the season in which the league expands -- if it expands. He reiterates that a decision on Las Vegas/Seattle/both/either/neither will come by the end of this calendar year.

item.title: "For the front office, this is a 365 days a year …
item.description: Farbod Esnaashari: Jeff Van Gundy on Draft Night: "For the front office, this is a 365 days a year evaluation."

item.title: Downtown Milwaukee business district announces more …
item.description: A.J. Bayatpour: Downtown Milwaukee business district announces more than a dozen landmarks and buildings will light up in green tonight to commemorate the time Giannis and Bobby Portis spent in the city. Landmarks include the Hoan Bridge, Mitchell Park Domes and Discovery World

item.title: Jazz take Darryn Peterson with the No. 2 pick
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Utah Jazz will select Darryn Peterson with the No. 2 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: "As a New Yorker, and a lifelong New Yorker, I don’t …
item.description: NBA Base: Adam Silver on the New York Knicks winning the NBA Championship: "As a New Yorker, and a lifelong New Yorker, I don’t remember excitement around anything like this... It was just a unique moment."

item.title: Originally signed a NIL shoe deal with Nike in early …
item.description: Nick DePaula: AJ Dybantsa is the #1 pick. AJ originally signed a NIL shoe deal with Nike in early 2024, and is now on a new multi-year extension as he begins his NBA career. Deal will include featuring AJ across upcoming GT models and already includes his own Player Exclusive logo.

item.title: Wizards select AJ Dybantsa at No. 1
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Washington Wizards are selecting AJ Dybantsa with the No. 1 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa will spearhead the Wizards youth movement …
item.description: Cyro Asseo: AJ Dybantsa will spearhead the Wizards youth movement alongside Kyshawn George, Alex Sarr and the duo of Trae Young-Anthony Davis. His ability to get to the rim and draw fouls will be huge for this young team.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa is wearing a TC5 pin in honor of the late …
item.description: Yahoo Sports: AJ Dybantsa is wearing a TC5 pin in honor of the late Terrence Clarke at the NBA Draft ❤️

item.title: "For me, not being in that conversation, I don’t …
item.description: NBA Base: Cameron Boozer says he doesn't understand why he isn't in the conversation for the No. 1 pick: "For me, not being in that conversation, I don’t really understand it. And I think many years down the line, people are going to look back and say that I should've been [the number one pick.]"

item.title: I’ve been thinking a lot about how I can show kids who …
item.description: Jalen Brunson: A lot of people have asked me over the years what drives my work ethic. The truth is that ever since I’ve been a little kid, I’ve loved to compete and have been willing to try anything. I’ve been thinking a lot about how I can show kids who might not be the tallest or the fastest, that they, too, have greatness in them. And that they should always keep trying. I’m happy to share now that JALEN PLAYS IT ALL, written by me and illustrated by the incredible Neely Daggett, will be rel

item.title: Bulls will select Caleb Wilson at No. 4
item.description: Jake Fischer: We've got all chalk so far. Caleb Wilson is going to be the selection for the Chicago Bulls at No. 4, sources say.

item.title: "It just means a lot. ... Obviously I have a lot more …

item.title: League sources say Golden State and Atlanta are …
item.description: A subplot I'm watching tonight: Will Trey Murphy III finally get traded out of New Orleans? There has been all kinds of chatter about the 26-year-old small forward being on the move of late, with a laundry list of teams interested. League sources say Golden State and Atlanta are currently vetting scenarios in which they'd trade their pick (No. 8 and No. 11, respectively) to land him. He's been tied to Brooklyn (pre-Julius Randle trade), the Clippers (No. 5 pick) and Boston (No. 27) as well.

item.title: From conversations I’ve had, people in the building …

Really unique prospect. From conversations I’ve had, people in the building seem to believe the jumpshot will come along.

item.title: Adam Silver on tanking: 'We'll never go back to where we were'
item.description: The NBA’s new anti-tanking rules will be in place through the 2029 draft. After that, the league’s board of governors will decide whether to keep it in place or find another system. Yet commissioner Adam Silver said Tuesday night one thing is certain: “We’ll never go back to where we were.” In an interview with ESPN before the draft, Silver said the age of teams fighting to see who could finish at the bottom of the league standings — and thus improve their chances of a better lottery spot — was

item.title: “If we were to expand, most likely in the 2028-29 …
item.description: Silver was also asked about the potential expansion of the league to 32 teams. In March, the NBA’s board of governors approved a vote to explore bids and applicants for expansion teams exclusively in Las Vegas and Seattle. Silver said zeroing in on those cities was a show of intent and focus from the league. However, nothing has been decided. “If we were to expand, most likely in the 2028-29 season, I bet that’s when they would come into the league, but no decisions have been made yet,” Silver s

item.title: He’s got to develop a consistent perimeter shot, and …
item.description: One college assistant coach identified a couple areas Morez Johnson Jr. must improve to make it as a pro. At the end of the year, he needed to finish his shots better rolling to the rim. Whether it was the length of people in the tournament or the competition being better, I don’t know. He’s got to develop a consistent perimeter shot, and they wanted him to take it. But the one thing this kid does is, he plays extremely hard. Runs the floor, is the point on their press. Guards multiple positions

OKC is getting a center who measured 7'3 barefoot at the combine with a 7'6 wingspan and a 9'9 standing reach. He's a big body down low who also has an impressive level of skill, especially as a passer.

item.title: Chris Finch on Terrence Shannon and Jaylen Clark: We got to figure out how to get these guys a more consistent role
item.description: ‪Ryan Eichten‬: Chris Finch on what he sees for Terrence Shannon and Jaylen Clark: "It's about creating pathways for those guys to be able to play. You might say like, isn't that the coach's job? But sometimes, you know, there's a gridlock. So we've gotta look at how we can either kind of change things around or get to a point where these guys can get a more consistent role." (2/2)

item.title: An absolute wow: With Morez Johnson Jr., Yaxel …

I just love the simplicity with which he sees the game."

item.title: “I thought it was a shame that a lot of the story …
item.description: Nori’s former boss, Minnesota Timberwolves head coach Chris Finch, said Tuesday that he wished more attention had been paid to Nori’s accomplishment than to the terms of his contract. “I thought it was a shame that a lot of the story about Micah’s great opportunity was overshadowed by the nature of the deal that he signed,” Finch told reporters before the NBA Draft. “That’s a personal decision. That’s a business decision. That’s between him, the team and the agent.”

item.title: I know he wasn’t focused on certainly money, but he …
item.description: “I know this: There’s 30 of these jobs,” Finch said, “They’re hard to get. They’re incredibly rewarding in a lot of different ways. I know he wasn’t focused on certainly money, but he was first focused on opportunity. That’s what we preach to all of our guys here. You should chase opportunity, not money. “Then, I also know that you’ve got to bet on yourself in this world,” Finch said. “Especially when you’re an outsider. … We consider ourselves a little bit of outsiders. We weren’t raised in the

item.title: Brooklyn selects Joshua Jefferson at No. 28 via Minnesota trade
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Brooklyn Nets will select Joshua Jefferson with the No. 28 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft. Brooklyn acquired this pick from the Minnesota Timberwolves as part of a 3-team deal, which also landed Julius Randle in Brooklyn and sent Nic Claxton to the Chicago Bulls.

item.title: “Now that it’s done, it’s a blessing.”

item.title: "Obviously, it's not a bad thing to hear. I think it's …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Aday Mara on being viewed as a potential Victor Wembanyama stopper: "Obviously, it's not a bad thing to hear. I think it's going to take time for adjustment, but I'm excited. I'm excited to play against him (and) obviously to play in the NBA. But I feel like I'm going to play against him a lot. If it's not in the NBA, it's going to be on the national team. So I'm excited to get ready to play against him."

item.title: It's an honor to represent my country. Obviously, you …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Aday Mara on being the highest-drafted player from Spain since Ricky Rubio (No. 5 in 2009): "It means a lot. It's an honor to represent my country. Obviously, you get into a list with really good names. I'm happy for that, too. I think it's great for Spanish basketball. I'm just super happy that I got to the list of being with those players and getting drafted high. Obviously, it's important. But I don't think it's how high you can go or not. It's just the team and the program,

I'm never going to predict the future, but every indication, everything that I think about over the past few years has been building around those guys. So obviously, you never know. "But at the same time, the one thing I want to make very clear is how valued he's always been. He's been amazing…. And whether that run ends 10 years from now when he retires, or before, there's a lot to celebrate. We have a great relationship, an open relationship wh

item.title: Brian Wright on De’Aaron Fox: “The confidence level is …
item.description: Jared Weiss: San Antonio Spurs GM Brian Wright on De’Aaron Fox: “The confidence level is very high.” Wright went on to get into Fox not making excuses before saying, “We have the ultimate faith in De’Aaron and who he is as a player, what he’s been for us, and what he means to this team. And there’s no wavering in that at all.”

item.title: We'll continue to put them in situations for them to …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: More from Brian Wright on Dylan Harper and De'Aaron Fox: "They're are a tough matchup. We'll continue to put them in situations for them to be at their best...They'll learn from this year on how they continue to play off one another and support one another and go from there."

item.title: I think this has been a great opportunity for us to …
item.description: Eric Nehm: While he couldn't specifically talk about the trade of Giannis Antetokounmpo, Bucks general manager Jon Horst was asked about his vision for the team moving forward and how their two picks tonight fit into that vision. Horst: I think we're always building. We're always looking towards adding positional size. I think that the character component of these two young men we were able to draft matters a ton. I think we talked a lot at Taylor's press conference about style of play and ident

item.title: Connelly when asked what his conversations with …
item.description: Dane Moore: Tim Connelly when asked what his conversations with ownership have been like about potentially paying the luxury tax for a third season in a row. "It's about winning. If the moves can exponentially increase our chance to win at the highest level, then we're all in. We can't be irresponsible fiscally. We can't ignore the real realities and the increasing punishment as you get deeper and deeper into the tax. But we're here to win. We're to try to get over the hump."

item.title: I'm told he did get a pretty hard workout in when he …
item.description: Oh No He Didn’t: Shams: "When AJ Dybantsa visited the Wizards earlier this month in DC I know AJ Dybantsa said he didn't work out for anyone. I'm told he did get a pretty hard workout in when he was on that visit, had dinner with them, hung out with all of them"

item.title: Knicks now hold picks 31, 47 (via Suns) and 55 in …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Knicks now hold picks 31, 47 (via Suns) and 55 in Wednesday's Round 2 and will add four more future second-rounders when the moves made tonight are official. Dallas and Phoenix say they can't discuss No. 25 Sergio De Larrea and No. 30 Koa Peat until the moves are finalized.

item.title: Clippers expected to trade Kawhi Leonard if extension negotiations fail
item.description: ClutchPoints: "If an extension is not worked out...I expect the Clippers look to move Kawhi. I don't expect that he will stay and play on an expiring deal" Chris Haynes speaks on the future of Kawhi Leonard and the Clippers 😳 (via @NBATV)

item.title: On the conversations with Kawhi Leonard regarding …
item.description: Joey Linn: Lawrence Frank has made it clear several times tonight the Clippers are not championship contenders right now. "When we get back to being a contender, we feel [Wagler] is gonna be the player with the most impact out of the group... We understand we're not a contender. We're competitive." On the conversations with Kawhi Leonard regarding this, he says there's always ongoing communication between the two sides, but didn't say much more than that.

item.title: Brian Wright said the Spurs have "ultimate faith in …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Brian Wright said the Spurs have "ultimate faith in De'Aaron Fox and who he is as a player, what he's been for us, and what he means to this team, and there's no wavering in that at all."

item.title: AJ Dybantsa on what his dad means to him: "So many …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: AJ Dybantsa on what his dad means to him: "So many sacrifices that he's made. He quit his job just to retire to come move in with me at Utah Prep so I'd be safe. Sending me over to California by myself to go get a better basketball experience. All the little stuff that I didn't notice he was doing at a young age obviously it's paying off now. But he's my dad. I mean, he acts as my manager. He does a whole bunch of stuff that he doesn't have to do. But I just give him the utmost

item.title: Darryn Peterson: "The celebration stops tonight. I got …
item.description: Alex Kennedy: Darryn Peterson: "The celebration stops tonight. I got drafted today, but tomorrow, I'm now an NBA player. I'm going to get home and work out until I've got to report to Utah... Kobe is my guy, and I'm inspired by him. So I just try to think, what would Kobe do right now? He'd get drafted and move on and try to go be the best possible, so that's what I'm going to go do. I see a guy that went No. 1 on the screen there right now (AJ Dybantsa), so I'm extra motivated... It will always

item.title: Peterson on his rookie-year goals: "Play all 82 games. …
item.description: Alex Kennedy: Darryn Peterson on his rookie-year goals: "Play all 82 games. Coming off the year I had at Kansas, I didn't get to play all the games I wanted to. My first goal is to play all 82. Make the playoffs. Try to get a ring. I saw some rookies get deep in the playoffs, and they kind of inspired me to get there. They came up short, but I want to go all the way and get a ring."

item.title: Aday Mara on being called the "Wemby stopper". “Im …
item.description: NBA Base: Aday Mara on being called the "Wemby stopper". “Im ready yeah, I don't know how yet. But I will do it” (Via @SInow)

item.title: “The reason we picked Keaton [Wagler] ... It’s about …
item.description: Justin Russo: Asked Lawrence Frank if this represents a second chance for the Clippers: “The reason we picked Keaton [Wagler] ... It’s about when we get to being back to contender, we really feel he’s going to be the best player with the most impact of the group.”

item.title: Mike Schmitz was asked if Dusty May had any say in the …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Mike Schmitz was asked if Dusty May had any say in the selection of Morez Johnson Jr., who May coached last season at Michigan: “Dusty gave us a great rundown on several prospects in the draft that he’s familiar with. Just to have that insight from his perspective is obviously very unique. He spoke the world of Morez, referenced him as an ‘everyday guy.’

item.title: That's always been our philosophy ... Sometimes you …
item.description: William Lou: “It's always been best-available, two-way players. That's always been our philosophy ... Sometimes you get into a trap if you try to take for fit in the first round." Bobby Webster on the Raptors drafting so many big two-way forwards

item.title: Myles Turner an option for Raptors?
item.description: NBA sources I’ve spoken with have connected the Raptors to Bucks centre Myles Turner, with the possibility that Raptors centre Jakob Poeltl, third-year wing Gradey Dick and potentially draft compensation being the core of a deal that would net Toronto the veteran big with credentials as a rim protector and three-point threat.

item.title: Asked Zach Kleiman about Ja Morant. He said he's …
item.description: Jonah Dylan: Asked Zach Kleiman about Ja Morant. He said he's focused on the draft and "we're not going to speculate on hypothetical transactions tonight."

item.title: Cameron Carr on whom he’d rather guard, SGA or Anthony …
item.description: Lakers draft pick Cameron Carr on whom he’d rather guard, SGA or Anthony Edwards: "Yeah, I ain't dealing with the flopping, Anthony Edwards"

item.title: Darryn Peterson: “I think me and Keyonte are going to …
item.description: [KUTV2news] Darryn Peterson: “I think me and Keyonte are going to be one of the best backcourts in the NBA…and what fans should know about me? I’m a cool guy, approachable. Want a pic or autograph or anything just come say what’s up!”

item.title: Dunleavy joked that he and Lacob simply disagreed over …
item.description: In his post-draft press conference, Dunleavy downplayed the conversation around the No. 11 overall selection, which turned out to be Michigan forward Yaxel Lendeborg. Initially, Dunleavy joked that he and Lacob simply disagreed over which was the best golf course in San Francisco.

item.title: “As far as the discussion with Joe and I, I think at …
item.description: “As far as the discussion with Joe and I, I think at that point there was probably some talks about trades and things like that, but ultimately we were in all in agreement to land on taking Yaxel," Dunleavy told reporters. "From our standpoint, we knew we were going to pick Yaxel at 11, he was the guy. But you just want to flush him out, make sure you're not missing anything that falls in your lap or makes a ton of sense. So that's what we were doing. And I think Joe was like, 'Come on, just let

item.title: “Lakers Collective” is one of the biggest Los …
item.description: There are quite a few podcasts dedicated to covering the Los Angeles Lakers, which makes sense since they are one of the most popular franchises in professional sports. “Lakers Collective” is one of the biggest Los Angeles-themed shows, but it is unfortunately losing one of its hosts, Jason Timpf. Sadly, his departure is due to some serious health issues that he announced on Tuesday.

item.title: Timpf revealed that he has been battling a virus and …
item.description: Timpf revealed that he has been battling a virus and myocarditis, and he was bedridden for six days around the NBA Finals. “I got just an absolutely gnarly viral illness. Like, the worst viral illness I’ve ever had in my life. It was absolutely brutal,” Timpf said. “During that process, I ended up getting a condition called myocarditis, which is essentially inflammation of the heart muscle. It’s caused, like, some palpitations and other crap that I have been dealing with. “It’s made it so I can’

item.title: Celtics interested in Evan Mobley?
item.description: Chris Mannix: Teams are looking to see if Jaylen Brown is truly available and what the asking price for him is going to be. But the Celtics, I believe, are listening to those calls and are thinking up their own trade matches of their own. I spent some time this afternoon talking to different teams about what might the Celtics be interested in. And the name in the team that came back to me a lot was Evan Mobley in the Cleveland Cavaliers. Evan Mobley, young, big, just entering the prime of his ca

item.title: Kendrick Perkins: Teams were asking Memphis to attach …
item.description: Kendrick Perkins: Teams were asking Memphis to attach picks in order to take Ja Morant, not the other way around. They were asking Memphis, "Hey man, attach picks with Ja and we'll take him."

item.title: One of the reasons the Wolves were unable to complete …
item.description: One of the reasons the Wolves were unable to complete a trade for Antetokounmpo is that they resisted the Bucks’ demands that Jaden McDaniels be included in the deal, a team source said. “I think the whole room is excited,” Connelly said. “I think we’re ready to continue to try to take the next step. But Ant’s just such a competitive guy. He wants to make sure we’re moving in the right direction and we keep getting better and better.”

item.title: “Giannis will never win a championship with him being …
item.description: NBACentral: “Giannis will never win a championship with him being a number 1 option. I repeat. Giannis Antetokounmpo will never win another championship with him being the number 1 option” - Kendrick Perkins (h/t @HeatCulture13)

item.title: However, Khris Middleton was considered the “Giannis …
item.description: However, Khris Middleton was considered the “Giannis whisperer,” a player who had the star’s utmost respect and could deliver hard criticism or honest thoughts about his game. And while Middleton (and the coaching staff) struggled to create cohesion alongside Antetokounmpo and Lillard, Middleton had inherently understood the on-court hierarchy. He may not have liked it, but he long ago decided he would not be the problem. Following his trade, Middleton, bitter, rented a car to drive silently fro

item.title: Danny Green: Celtics trade talk around Jaylen Brown is 'disrespectful'
item.description: Danny Green on Celtics trying to trade Jaylen Brown for Giannis: This is disrespectful. And I'm not even that type of person. But y'all could kick rocks, bro. I’m out of here. I'm gone. I'm cool. Y'all don't want me, I'm cool. Like, not only did it not fall through, but they're still talking about we're still entertaining idea. Like right now, they're still entertaining trades.

item.title: One of those players, team sources who have been …
item.description: Connelly said on Tuesday night that the Timberwolves were trying to get back into the first round when a few players they liked started to fall, but they were unable to complete a deal. One of those players, team sources who have been granted anonymity to discuss nonpublic information said, was Alabama guard Labaron Philon, who went to Philadelphia at No. 22.

item.title: Jay Willams says OKC and the Spurs won the draft: …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Jay Willams says OKC and the Spurs won the draft: “They continue to put their chokehold on the NBA. What did we talk about a lot with OKC? How did Chet Holmgren play in the NBA finals? What’s going on with Isaiah Hartenstein when he had go against Wemby? That got a guy named Aday Mara who has a 7’4 wingspan, super talented can give you depth off the bench and give you an extra body to go against Wemby. How about for San Antonio for Wembanyama we talked a lot about who’s the enforc

item.title: It doesn’t matter that, according to multiple people …
item.description: It doesn’t matter that Edwards is under contract for three more years. It doesn’t matter that he has made the most money a player his age could possibly make in the NBA. It doesn’t matter that, according to multiple people with knowledge of Edwards’ thinking, the Timberwolves star is not flinching at the challenge ahead and believes the team has a bright future ahead of it. It doesn’t matter that the Timberwolves have put a veteran team around him with good leaders that has won five playoff seri

item.title: Edwards is happy in Minnesota.
item.description: Edwards is happy in Minnesota. Despite his global reach as one of the most popular young players in the league, a signature pitchman for Adidas, Sprite and more, and his supporting role in Adam Sandler’s move “Hustle,” Edwards enjoys relative solitude here. He likes to go to work and then return to his home with his family and friends unbothered, and that is exactly what he is allowed to do.

item.title: As soon as the Timberwolves were eliminated by San …
item.description: It isn’t just media talking heads who love to play this card. Executives across the NBA are constantly combing the rosters to see where the next vulnerability will surface, where the next star player can be swiped. As soon as the Timberwolves were eliminated by San Antonio in the second round of the playoffs last month, front office personnel from other teams started to check in with me, wondering about Edwards’ patience with the Timberwolves’ direction. The game is the game.

item.title: A Shanghai fan whose social media account takes the …
item.description: On Tuesday, a Shanghai fan whose social media account takes the name of the Dallas Mavericks star as well as his jersey number posted a video on a Chinese platform that sparked widespread envy in the comments section. In the clip, the 18-year-old fan introduces himself and claims that Irving is currently inside his home, before panning the camera to reveal the NBA star waving to the audience. Inside the home, Irving chatted with the fan as if he were a friend, comparing hand sizes and gifting hi

item.title: Victor Wembanyama arrives at the Louis Vuitton Men’s …
item.description: ?NBA Stat?: Summer plans: Victor Wembanyama arrives at the Louis Vuitton Men’s Show in Paris. #NBA

item.title: Brad Stevens was asked if he still believes Tatum and …
item.description: Justin Turpin: Brad Stevens was asked if he still believes Tatum and Brown can bring a championship: “Yes,” he said.

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo reacted to Wes Edens' ultimatum with resignation: 'They're gonna trade me'
item.description: Memories of Game 5 of the 2021 NBA Finals, a win that put the Milwaukee Bucks one game from their championship, coursed through him like the intravenous fluids he needed that night four years earlier. But when a senior team official wandered into view, his mood immediately shifted. Antetokounmpo derisively referenced aloud, without prompt, a comment by Bucks co-owner Wes Edens a day earlier, saying the franchise legend would either sign a contract extension or be traded. Antetokounmpo felt it co

item.title: But multiple high-ranking Bucks officials said …
item.description: Antetokounmpo and Lillard had spoken long before, recruiting one another to their respective cities, and Antetokounmpo famously picked Lillard first for his team in the 2023 All-Star game over Holiday. That's not to say Antetokounmpo demanded the trade for Lillard, or signed off on moving Holiday. But multiple high-ranking Bucks officials said decision-makers would talk with the star, bounce ideas off him. They knew what he wanted, or would be OK with, when it came to coaching or personnel decis

item.title: “Giannis is Milwaukee,” a former teammate said. “When …
item.description: The team was foundering, and they faced more questions about energy and effort. Antetokounmpo and Connaughton openly wondered where the joy had gone. One player said his teammates were still arguing about schemes as they felt the urgency to turn it around. “Giannis is Milwaukee,” a former teammate said. “When you have a guy like Giannis it’s never going to leave your mind of wanting to win a championship. It’s not a thing. There is no such thing of having a good year and figuring it out. It was

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo challenged the veracity of …
item.description: Then on the team’s media day on Sept. 30, on a Zoom call from Greece because he and the team said he contracted COVID-19, Giannis Antetokounmpo challenged the veracity of Edens’ statement that they had the on-court “meeting” at the practice facility in early May. Edens was annoyed, but two high-level team sources said he did not carry a grudge. Rivers held another remote training camp in 2025, this time in Miami. Even with a roster that had been completely turned over from the one he took over i

item.title: Antetokounmpo let it be known he did not like locker …
item.description: Antetokounmpo let it be known he did not like locker room leaks, but his mental state was chronicled nearly all season by anonymous sources. He pleaded for accountability but then tried to pass off those reports as someone else’s doing. He would call his teammates selfish but then stand on a visiting team’s court with a former coach and yell about how that person would make sure he got the ball.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: 'I’ve been dreaming about this since I was about 12 years old'
item.description: Dybantsa had imagined that exact moment, becoming the first pick in the draft, for years. So, how did the moment compare to what he had envisioned in his dreams? “The reality is way better,” Dybantsa told The Athletic, a Wizards cap still atop his head. “I’ve been dreaming about this since I was about 12 years old. And seven years later, I’m accomplishing my dream, and it just feels super surreal, and I’m just happy I got to spend it with my family.”

item.title: AJ asked league officials to use his given name when …
item.description: His joy shone through in the seconds after Silver spoke. He hugged his mom, Chelsea; his godparents; his older sister, Samarra; his younger sister, Jasmyn; and, finally, his dad, Anicet Sr., nicknamed Ace. AJ asked league officials to use his given name when he was drafted as a way of honoring his father. On his custom-made Kenzo suit, near his left lapel, he wore a pin of the flag of the Republic of the Congo, where his dad is from, and a pin of the flag of Jamaica, where his mom is from. He wo

item.title: Kyle Kuzma: Had an amazing time with @Meta speaking …
item.description: Kyle Kuzma: Had an amazing time with @Meta speaking about the future of AI, what I look for in companies, leadership, and how to best serve this new modern world for good through philanthropy. Thanks, @Meta !!!!

item.title: A woman caught on video emptying a public trash can on …
item.description: A woman caught on video emptying a public trash can on the street then stealing it during New York City’s Knicks championship parade was a director at JPMorgan Chase who was fired Tuesday over the incident, The Post has learned. Angie Báez, 40, was promoted to Executive Director of Community and Industry Engagement for Card and Connected Commerce at JPMorgan Chase more than a year ago, according to her LinkedIn profile. She previously served as Executive Director of Diversity, Equity, and Inclu

item.title: Grizzlies actively fielding offers to trade the No. 32 pick in the draft
item.description: Shams Charania: Memphis, like the Knicks at No. 31, is actively fielding offers to move the No. 32 pick for Night 2 of the draft, per sources. So picks 31 and 32 are up for auction in trades around the NBA -- with future assets being proposed.

item.title: The Suns did NOT workout Koa Peat because of where …
item.description: John Gambadoro: Interesting nugget here. The Suns did NOT workout Koa Peat because of where they were picking in the second round. But once they felt like they had a chance - albeit a small one to get into the first round - they were able to get him to come into the building on Sunday for a visit.

item.title: Austin Reaves intends to re-sign with Lakers on four-year max extension
item.description: Shams Charania: Los Angeles Lakers star Austin Reaves intends to sign a four-year, $185 million maximum contract to return to the franchise, with a player option for the final season in 2029-30, sources tell ESPN. Reaves declined his $14.9M player option for the new max deal.

item.title: Obviously, LeBron would have to want to sign with the …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: Obviously, LeBron would have to want to sign with the Cavs, but if your pathway to paying LeBron the money is to trade Jarrett Allen for him, the Lakers would KILL for Jarrett Allen. KILL for him. All right? They would do that deal in 17-tenths of a second.

item.title: Austin Reaves intends to sign four-year max contract to return to Lakers
item.description: Shams Charania: Los Angeles Lakers star Austin Reaves intends to sign a four-year, $185 million maximum contract to return to the franchise, with a player option for the final season in 2029-30, sources tell ESPN. Reaves declined his $14.9M player option for the new max deal.

item.title: The Lakers and Reaves' agents, Aaron Reilly and Reggie …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Lakers and Reaves' agents, Aaron Reilly and Reggie Berry of AMR Agency, worked through discussions over the last 10 days after teams were able to negotiate with their own free agents. Reaves lands the richest contract for an undrafted player in NBA history.

item.title: Detroit continues to express trade interest in Tyler Herro
item.description: Marc Stein: With Reaves now sure to stay in LA, as was long believed to be his clear preference, league sources say Detroit continues to express trade interest in Milwaukee's soon-to-be acquired Tyler Herro. The Bucks are expected to listen to offers but could also elect to keep Herro.

item.title: Buddy Hield a potential target for Miami
item.description: Also, keep an eye on Buddy Hield. Only $3M of his $9.6M salary is guaranteed. The Hawks must make a decision by Thursday, per his contract. Hield, who has had interest in Heat in the past, averaged 7.6 points and shot 34.9 in 51 games for Golden State and Atlanta.

item.title: "Obviously I really enjoyed working with Daryl. I …
item.description: Austin Krell: Nick Nurse on Daryl Morey's departure: "Obviously I really enjoyed working with Daryl. I really did day-to-day and all that kind of stuff was very enjoyable. But it's a decision that's been made and now we got to move on from it. I wish him the best. Always have a fondness for Daryl. But Mike's here and he's in charge now and my job is to get this team better every day and that's what we're going to do."

item.title: Caleb Wilson will wear No. 8
item.description: Chicago Bulls: OFFICIAL: Caleb Wilson will wear No. 8.

item.title: “They’re getting a dog. It’s that simple. I’m going to …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Caleb Wilson on what the Bulls are getting: “They’re getting a dog. It’s that simple. I’m going to do whatever it takes to win. I’m going to play as hard as I can and create opportunities for myself and others more than anyone else who’s playing in the NBA.. I’m going to do whatever it takes to win. It’s just that simple”

item.title: Morez Johnson Jr. will wear No. 14 for the Dallas …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Morez Johnson Jr. will wear No. 14 for the Dallas Mavericks, last worn by former two-way guard Miles Kelly last season.

item.title: "'It's far in the future, but would you want to be a …
item.description: Ayo Dosunmu: "'It's far in the future, but would you want to be a coach or work in a front office after playing?' I would definitely want to be a probably a GM."

item.title: "When I first came here, I had to sacrifice. I was the …
item.description: Shaquille O'Neal: "When I first came here, I had to sacrifice. I was the man everywhere I went. But I was telling them because I had some a question. I said because I don't remember playing against them. You know who he was. Because they all know I don't look at no tape, I don't pay attention. I just come, I just show up and play. So during the playoffs, I'm watching this motherfucker (Dwyane Wade) kill BD. I'm like, who's this motherfucker? And I know, I can see it because I seen him before. I

item.title: Reaves was expected to receive free-agent interest …
item.description: Shams Charania: Reaves was expected to receive free-agent interest from multiple teams -- such as Detroit -- on maximum-level salary, so the Lakers stepped up on Wednesday to lock in their homegrown talent. Reaves went from undrafted to a two-way player -- to making the Lakers roster and now multiple lucrative deals in L.A.

item.title: The Lakers and Austin Reaves agreed to a four-year …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Lakers and Austin Reaves agreed to a four-year maximum contract his new $185M contract. Here's how that deal will look: 26-27: $41.3M 27-28: $44.6M 28-29: $47.9M 29-30: $51.2M (player option) Reaves' deal will get signed after the Lakers use their cap space, should they choose that route. LA still has lots of flexibility for building the roster this summer.

item.title: Anthony Edwards the next star to watch potentially hit the trade block?
item.description: Windhorst: "If you say to me Giannis is now been traded, who would be the next NBA star that you would watch? The answer would be Anthony Edwards. Already, Anthony Edwards has watched Karl-Anthony Towns, a player he totally fit with on the court, be traded to the Knicks and win a championship. Last summer, they let Nickeill Alexander-Walker go, he goes and wins most improved player down in Atlanta."

item.title: If Memphis doesn’t trade tonight’s No. 32 pick, I’ve …
item.description: Jake Fischer: If Memphis doesn’t trade tonight’s No. 32 pick, I’ve heard they hold some interest in Aaron Nkrumah. But the Grizzlies are indeed seeking more future second-round draft capital. Memphis already netted five second-round picks by maneuvering down the board last night. The Grizzlies, sources say, also received a 2029 most favorable second of Detroit/New York/Milwaukee, a 2031 Dallas second, and a 2032 Detroit second, for sliding from No. 16 to No. 21.

item.title: "(Miami Heat) are going to lose Norm Powell in free …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "(Miami Heat) are going to lose Norm Powell in free agency because of the trade and because they took a lot of more money back and some of the restrictions that come with it. So you basically have five roster spots to give to players on the veteran minimum exception. You need some shooting, you need a little bit more depth in your front court here. It is a matter of what players are willing to take a little bit of a discount to go down to South Florida."

item.title: "I like the pick for OKC getting Mara from the …
item.description: Paul Pierce: "I like the pick for OKC getting Mara from the 7-footer from Spain, the other three. Yeah, because he's a mature, he's a winner, and he gives them big man depth. You know, like I feel like OKC never misses. And I thought this was a pick, it shocked me that they they picked him, but I was just like, oh wow, he fits perfectly as a backup big. He's 7'3, he's going to play 14, 15 minutes a night, and he can give them something, uh, you know, this year coming up.

item.title: I’m told that the many moving parts that landed Cam …
item.description: Marc Stein: I’m told that the many moving parts that landed Cam Carr with the Lakers at No. 24, Sergio De Larrea with the Mavericks at No. 25, Koa Peat with the Suns at No. 30 and netted the Knicks five second-round picks is tracking to take the form of a four-team TRADE once made official.

item.title: The Detroit Pistons traded the No. 21 pick (Karim …
item.description: Michael Scotto: FULL TRADE: The Detroit Pistons traded the No. 21 pick (Karim Lopez) and three-second round picks (a 2029 second that’s the second-best of Pistons, Bucks, Knicks, a 2031 Mavericks second, and a 2032 Pistons second) to the Memphis Grizzlies for pick 17 (Ebuka Okorie), per sources.

item.title: “We’ve encountered extremely high interest from …
item.description: Noting that they have made significant progress on the NBA Europe project, George Aivazoglou said, “We are making significant progress ahead of the launch, which we aim to hold in October 2027. We are aware that this is an ambitious goal, but my colleagues in London, Madrid, and New York, together with our partners at FIBA, are making progress on various fronts to turn this timeline into a reality. Furthermore, the interest we’ve seen from potential clubs, investor groups, and business partners

item.title: “We’ve seen an extremely high level of interest from …
item.description: “We’ve seen an extremely high level of interest from Turkey, and especially from Istanbul. We believe Istanbul could be one of the cornerstones of this new league. Istanbul is a world-class city. It is a place with an incredible passion for sports, a very strong cultural influence, and home to one of the largest and most dedicated fan bases in Europe. If European basketball is to undergo a transformation, we believe Turkey must be one of the countries shaping that future.”

item.title: "We are looking for partners to build the league of …
item.description: "We are looking for partners to build the league of the next 100 years"Emphasizing that they have been in talks with stakeholders ready to invest billions of dollars in European basketball, George Aivazoglou shared the following views: “We are seeing unprecedented interest from numerous potential clubs and investors ready to invest billions of dollars in European basketball alongside the NBA and FIBA. This includes Turkey. However, the financial aspect is just one element of the proposals we are

item.title: “That’s a big thing,” Dolan said last week, when the …
item.description: But did it change how he views his other professional sports franchise, the New York Rangers, and what it will take for them to end their 32-year-and-counting Stanley Cup drought? “That’s a big thing,” Dolan said last week, when the subject of winning a title with the Rangers was broached during a radio appearance on WFAN. “I have many of the same people who are helping me with the Knicks are focusing on the Rangers, too — particularly my son Quentin, who does all the conditioning and wellness f

item.title: “That’s the temptation in New York,” Dolan said. “If …
item.description: He noted previous instances in which the Knicks “flipped up our strategy in order to get the shiny object.” The inference was that they got caught chasing executives and coaches who brought star power and reputations as great basketball minds, with host Craig Carton pointing to Phil Jackson’s dispiriting tenure as Knicks president from 2014-17 as one example. “That’s the temptation in New York,” Dolan said. “If you’re running a team and you’re under the gun in New York, you want to produce. Beca

item.title: “I’m not going to worry about a GM who’s really good …
item.description: It was around then, Dolan said, that he had an epiphany. “I’m not going to worry about a GM who’s really good at strategy,” he recalled deciding. “What I need is somebody who’s the best collector of talent.” That led him to Rose, who fit the bill as one of the most powerful agents in sports and had spent decades cultivating relationships around the NBA. It was an outside-of-the-box hire, to be sure, but it gave the Knicks a proven dealmaker who could lure winning players to the Garden.

item.title: Celtics willing to move Jaylen Brown for a center
item.description: Boston continued to discuss Brown trades on Tuesday, two sources familiar with the talks tell Sports Illustrated. Many of the conversations were initiated by rival teams, sources say. Some were brokered by the Celtics. “They are hunting a big man,” says a rival executive. “And they are willing to trade [Brown] to get it.”

item.title: Brown was upset when his name came up in trade talks …
item.description: Brown was upset when his name came up in trade talks in 2022, sources said back then. It would be unsurprising if he was seething now. Stevens made the point that he has stayed in regular contact with Brown’s representatives the last few days—“We certainly wanted to be as proactive and upfront with that as possible,” Stevens said—while reiterating his value to the team.

item.title: “That bothered the heck out of me. Because it had …
item.description: Ian Begley: “That bothered the heck out of me. Because it had nothing to do with Mikal (Bridges). That was the price I was willing to pay.” Leon Rose on criticisms of Mikal Bridges based on the 5 first round picks Knicks sent to BKN to acquire him:

item.title: Ebuka Okorie’s introductory press conference will take …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Ebuka Okorie’s introductory press conference will take place tomorrow, July 25 at 11 am. Trajan Langdon will also attend.

item.title: Sergio De Larrea's 2026-27 plans with Dallas still uncertain
item.description: Marc Stein: The pre-draft expectation, sources tell @TheSteinLine & @DLLS_Sports, was that Sergio De Larrea would likely spend next season in Europe again. Told that 2026-27 plans now that he's Dallas-bound -- including whether De Larrea plays summer league -- are all being worked through.

item.title: Hannes Steinbach and Christian Anderson are meeting …
item.description: Rod Boone: Newest members of the #Hornets have arrived in town. Hannes Steinbach and Christian Anderson are meeting with some Charlotte youth at a local church.

item.title: Multiple NBA sources confirmed to the Sun Sentinel …
item.description: Multiple NBA sources confirmed to the Sun Sentinel that an earlier construct of the Heat’s proposal for Giannis Antetokounmpo included Davion Mitchell instead of Kasparas Jakucionis. The Bucks, however, insisted on the youthful promise and rookie-scale contract of Jakucionis.

item.title: Xavier Tillman signs with Turkish team
item.description: Xavier Tillman is continuing his basketball career overseas. The former Boston Celtics, Memphis Grizzlies, and Charlotte Hornets center has signed with Turkish club Trabzonspor, the organization announced on Wednesday.

item.title: Adam Silver reiterated his desire for the NBA to …
item.description: Speaking on The Dan Patrick Show, Adam Silver reiterated his desire for the NBA to become “more NFL-like” and said the Spurs-Knicks Finals this spring was the ultimate indicator that the small-market vs. big-market “storyline” is in decline. “The goal over time has been, and I’ve said it, to be more NFL-like. Where you believe your team has a shot,” Silver said.

item.title: Adam Silver: “Maybe not every single season, but your …
item.description: Adam Silver: “Maybe not every single season, but your view is, regardless of the market size, if your team is well-managed, if you have a little bit of luck in there, you have a good chance to be in the playoffs and potentially be the champion. And so I think what we’ve seen is societal changes; I think that nobody’s going to say that Victor Wembanyama in San Antonio can’t get global attention because it’s a smaller market.”

item.title: A woman caught on video emptying a public trash can on …
item.description: A woman caught on video emptying a public trash can on the street, then stealing it during New York City’s Knicks championship parade, was a director at JPMorgan Chase — who was fired Tuesday over the incident, The Post has learned. Angie Báez, 40, was promoted to executive director of community and industry engagement for card and connected commerce at JPMorgan Chase more than a year ago, according to her LinkedIn profile.

item.title: JB Bickerstaff on Blazers' contract with Micah Nori: It creates an environment where players look at you as being easily replaced
item.description: "It changes the math on where a coach stands, and it creates an environment where how do you hold players accountable when it looks like you are easily replaced and removed if things don't go the way that players may see it going," Bickerstaff said. "It's almost, which is disappointing, a mindset of the substitute teacher being there with no guarantee or support [regarding] what it looks like long term or in the future. "I've talked to a lot of coaches -- head coaches, assistant coaches -- who a

item.title: “From our perspective, it's like someone's taking …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: NBA coaches association president J.B. Bickerstaff on Micah Nori’s year-to-year contract with Portland: “From our perspective, it's like someone's taking advantage of your dream and devaluing what we feel like coaches have earned over the years.”

item.title: The deal has a below-market base salary and includes …
item.description: According to league sources, Nori's contract with the Trail Blazers includes only one guaranteed year, with team options on the second and third year on the deal. The deal has a below-market base salary and includes incentives based on team success, sources told ESPN.

item.title: A source with knowledge of the discussions told ESPN …
item.description: A source with knowledge of the discussions told ESPN that Blazers management was negotiating a potential contract with multiple candidates simultaneously as a method of maximizing leverage.

item.title: Heat exercise team option on Pelle Larsson
item.description: Ira Winderman: Heat announce they have exercised the 2026-27 team option on guard Pelle Larsson, which will make him a restricted free agent next summer unless extended in the interim.

item.title: Austin Reaves felt “great relief” when his reps and …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: Austin Reaves felt “great relief” when his reps and the Lakers agreed to a four-year, $185 million extension, a source close to Reaves told ESPN. “He wants to be a Laker for life and there's nothing more important to him than trying to win one for that city,” the source said.

item.title: "They called me at 6 in the morning, but I was asleep. …
item.description: DAZN Espana: "They called me at 6 in the morning, but I was asleep. In a few hours, we'll talk and see what decision we make" 👀 Will Sergio de Larrea make it to Dallas? Is his immediate future in the NBA? He responds on DAZN after winning the Liga Endesa 👑 #LigaEndesaxDAZN 🏀

item.title: Bulls trading No. 56 pick to Lakers for cash
item.description: Shams Charania: Sources: The Los Angeles Lakers are acquiring No. 56 from the Chicago Bulls in the second round of the NBA draft tonight at 8 pm ET on ESPN. Lakers sent cash to the Bulls to get a pick tonight.

item.title: Cash being sent is from Lakers is from their cash …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The cash being sent is from Lakers is from their cash limit from the current 2025-26 salary cap year. They have $4.7 million remaining to distribute to the Knicks in yesterday's trade and in this one. Cash limit to be sent/received will reset to $8.5 million on July 1.

item.title: The maximum allotment this season was $7.96M
item.description: Bobby Marks: The amount cash allowed to send or receive in a trade resets on July 1. Teams are at their own discretion to use cash received in a trade as they please. The maximum allotment this season was $7.96M. A team is hard capped at the second apron if cash is sent.

item.title: Keaton Wagler mentioned that Darius Garland was one of …
item.description: Law Murray: Keaton Wagler mentioned that Darius Garland was one of the first people to reach out to him and wish him well

item.title: "He's got incredible body balance. And I think these …
item.description: Farbod Esnaashari: Lawrence Frank on Keaton Wagler's skillset: "He's got incredible body balance. And I think these are lessons you learn when scouting, when athleticism is a very, very broad term. And what we've learned is functional athleticism is different than athleticism. And Keaton very much embodies that."

item.title: “And it kind of meets the common goal of why they gave …
item.description: “It’s going to be fun. And like I said, winning a championship is the main goal,” Reaves said. “And I feel like, you know, with me and Luka, that’s a great starting piece to compete. And when we put a roster together that can go compete, I feel like winning a championship will basically solidify everything that I want and everything that the Lakers want. “And it kind of meets the common goal of why they gave me the money they did.”

item.title: “I mean, it was a breath of fresh air because I hadn’t …
item.description: “I mean, it was a breath of fresh air because I hadn’t stopped thinking about where I was going to be. And it’s just like a sigh of relief,” he said in a phone interview Wednesday. “I mean, regardless of whatever played out, I’m obviously grateful and in a good position to take care of the people I love. My heart’s been in L.A. Every morning I wake up, I’m just like, ‘I hope we can get this done today.’ “And even if I was frustrated through the process when we (weren’t) getting the numbers we wa

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo on getting back to Finals: It doesn't matter if I'm a role player, I want to get there again
item.description: “Some way, somehow, I have to get there again,” Antetokounmpo told the Journal Sentinel. “It doesn’t necessarily have to be with me being the main guy and all that. If it is me, great. But I want to be there again. If that’s going to be me being there as a role player, if that’s going to me being there as the fifth option, if that’s going to be me being there as the No. 1 guy that takes them there, I don’t give two (expletive). I want to get there again."

item.title: The whole second half of the season, he was a guy we …
item.description: Pistons president of basketball operations Trajan Langdon was straightforward when asked about being aggressive in getting Okorie. “We wanted to go get the guy we wanted,” Langdon said. “He’s a guy we targeted in this process, watched a lot of film on him. The whole second half of the season, he was a guy we liked, a guy who would fit what we’re trying to do here. In terms of playing faster and having another guy who can be on the floor, handling the ball, not only scoring but creating for guys

item.title: “Alongside Cade (Cunningham), I’ll just be another …
item.description: “Alongside Cade (Cunningham), I’ll just be another playmaker on the floor,” Okorie said via speakerphone at the Pistons’ practice facility. “Another shot creator, able to create for myself and others. On the defensive end, bringing intensity, using my quickness, getting in passing lanes and getting a lot of deflections.”

item.title: Warriors wanted Brayden Burries at No. 11
item.description: The expectation: The Bucks would take Nate Ament from Tennessee at 10. So when Atlanta took Houston guard Kingston Flemings at No. 8, and Dallas selected Michigan forward Morez Johnson Jr. at No. 9, the Warriors thought they had their guy. But having acquired a second lottery pick one day earlier, Milwaukee could be greedy. And it was. The Bucks selected the best player on the board at No. 10 — Arizona guard Brayden Burries — knowing they could get Ament at No. 13. And they did. That’s how close

item.title: One team source indicated that the Warriors had …
item.description: One team source indicated that the Warriors had Lendeborg rated about even with Burries and only had Duke’s Cam Boozer clearly placed on a higher level in this draft.

item.title: The lively draft-room scene broadcast nationally while …
item.description: The lively draft-room scene broadcast nationally while the Warriors were on the clock was, according to multiple sources, actually Dunleavy and Joe Lacob brushing off a moderate offer to move out of the slot.

item.title: Keaton Wagler said that he went to dinner with LA …
item.description: Justin Russo: Keaton Wagler said that he went to dinner with LA Clippers coach Tyronn Lue on his first night in Los Angeles, and Wagler mentioned that the film work he did with Lue opened his eyes to how smart of a basketball mind Lue is and he’s excited to play under him.

item.title: "I watch a lot of, you know, good NBA guards like …
item.description: Law Murray: Keaton Wagler on who he watches: "I watch a lot of, you know, good NBA guards like Haliburton and SGA and a couple others. So I try to just steal little things out of, you know, each of those players, but I think the closest person is probably Haliburton, just off of size and the way we play, being able to have a really good feel for the game and use wall screens to create advantages."

item.title: "I learned a lot throughout this whole process, …
item.description: Law Murray: Asked Keaton Wagler about what he learned about the Clippers in this draft process and if he told them he wanted to be here "I learned a lot throughout this whole process, talking with them at the combine. And then when I came and worked out here, I got to spend, you know, a good 3 days here, and I just saw just how genuine they were: front office, coaches, players. They're all around and they were just super genuine. And I just love the culture that they have built. And, you know, t

item.title: Nori had been in the mix for several years and felt he …
item.description: Chris Mannix: Nori had been in the mix for several years and felt he wasn’t getting closer to a gig, sources close to him say. He sees this as his best shot. Thinks he can win enough to earn those options. But, as Tim writes, other coaches are not happy about the precedent.

item.description: Shams Charania: Sources: The New York Knicks are trading Nos. 31 and 55 to the Houston Rockets for Nos. 39 and 53 and a 2029 Kings second-rounder. The moves begin for night two of the NBA Draft.

item.title: He'll contribute now, but I think he obviously has to …
item.description: Law Murray: Frank: "We really think Keaton's best basketball is way ahead of him. He'll contribute now, but I think he obviously has to get stronger. He's going to continue to put a ton of work in. There's a lot of growth to be made."

item.title: Rockets were looking to trade into the last few picks …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Rockets were looking to trade into the last few picks of last night's first round, sources say. Not much of a surprise they're the team to jump into the 31st pick, where Houston plans to take Bruce Thornton.

item.description: Jake Fischer: At No. 34, I'm hearing Meleek Thomas will be the selection for the Cleveland Cavaliers.

item.title: I believe Meleek Thomas would have been Cleveland's …
item.description: Jake Fischer: I believe Meleek Thomas would have been Cleveland's pick last night, if the Cavaliers didn't trade No. 29 to the Kings.

item.title: By trading out of first pick in second round of draft, …
item.description: Ian Begley: By trading out of first pick in second round of draft, Knicks have positioned themselves to find players more likely to accept rookie minimum deal via second round pick exception. Every dollar counts as NYK operates as team that will not exceed 2nd apron in team salary. This approach makes it nearly impossible to find a realistic scenario where they can retain both Mitchell Robinson and Landry Shamet in free agency. ESPN reported that Knicks traded No. 31 and No. 55 to Houston for No

item.title: Next year, the top 16 picks of the 2nd round will be …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: The NBA isn't just instituting draft lottery reform in 2027, it is also putting in 2nd round reform. Next year, the top 16 picks of the 2nd round will be in reverse order of the lottery result. So if a team draws the 16th pick in the 2027 draft, it gets to pick 1st in 2nd round.

item.title: Isaiah Evans heads to Minnesota at No. 33 via trade
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Brooklyn Nets will select Isaiah Evans with the No. 33 pick in the 2026 NBA Draft. Evans is headed to the Minnesota Timberwolves as part of a 3-team trade, which also landed Julius Randle in Brooklyn and sent Nic Claxton to the Bulls.

There are other waivers and salary-shedding moves Detroit could do to get up over $30M in cap space. They'd also have the $9.4M Room Exception to use if they go with cap space.

item.title: They can get to $33 million if they waive Duncan …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Pistons cap situation is fluid. They could have $20.6 million in cap space while retaining Jalen Duren's $19.4 million cap hold. They can get to $33 million if they waive Duncan Robinson ($2 million guaranteed). They could pursue an upgrade in free agency or trade with it.

item.title: “You just felt like, ‘I’m going to keep coming and I’m …
item.description: The former commander in chief, however, didn’t keep his praise to just Anunoby, however, as he listed all the key attributes of Brunson that led him to became NBA Finals MVP. “It’s the toughness and endurance and mental fortitude of that guy,” Obama said. “You just felt like, ‘I’m going to keep coming and I’m not going to doubt myself and not going to let my team doubt myself.’ And for a second-round draft pick, who was a champion, who was a winner, you didn’t see it coming.”

item.description: Michael Scotto: TRADE: The Los Angeles Clippers will select Henri Veesaar with the 52nd pick in the 2026 NBA Draft and are trading him to the Atlanta Hawks for the 57th pick in the draft and cash consideration, league sources say.

item.description: Ian Begley: Jack Kayil says he expects to be with Knicks next season as opposed to being a draft-and-stash prospect.

item.description: Shams Charania: Sources: The Rockets are trading No. 55 to the Clippers for cash. A Wildcat is on the way.

item.description: Nick Friedell: The Warriors take Lajae Jones from Florida State with the 54th pick. His coach at FSU was former Warriors assistant Luke Loucks.

item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Milwaukee Bucks will select Malique Lewis with the 60th pick in the 2026 NBA Draft.
