item.title: Multiple teams strongly pursuing LaMelo Ball
item.description: Shams Charania: Reporting live for the NBA Draft Show -- the Charlotte Hornets are engaged in LaMelo Ball trade talks as multiple teams strongly pursue the star guard: Multiple teams are strongly pursuing LaMelo Ball right now. The Hornets are engaged, I'm told, and Ball had a strong finish to the season. It mirrored the Hornets rise over the course of the last calendar year. Both sides have been in a good place. Ball does want to be in Charlotte, I'm told, but this is an organization fielding s

item.title: Wolves, Raptors emerge as teams interested in LaMelo Ball
item.description: Marc Stein: The Timberwolves and Raptors have emerged as two of the teams with trade interest in Charlotte’s LaMelo Ball, league sources tell @JakeLFischer and me. More to come …

item.title: Lamelo Ball, sources say, has been looking forward to …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Lamelo Ball, sources say, has been looking forward to this upcoming Hornets season to build off of Charlotte’s momentum from 2025-26, but the Hornets are indeed engaging the trade market for their franchise guard.

item.title: "You can look at other deals around the league and see …
item.description: Ryan Blackburn: Ben Tenzer on the Nuggets adding some second round picks by trading down: "You can look at other deals around the league and see what two 2nds get you at the trade deadline...it's tough to not have as many [picks] as other teams." "Throughout the years, we've given assets for other options at the time, so this is what it is, and we're comfortable with that."

item.title: And with AD, he wants to be here, we want him here. …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Will Dawkins: "Can't comment on the Trae one just yet but we feel pretty good where that's headed. And with AD, he wants to be here, we want him here. We'll have that conversation in the middle of August when we can officially have that"

item.title: Undrafted Creighton guard Josh Dix has agreed to a …
item.description: Shams Charania: Undrafted Creighton guard Josh Dix has agreed to a two-way NBA deal with the Oklahoma City Thunder, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: DeMar DeRozan on the move?
item.description: SiriusXM NBA Radio: "The Kings are looking to trade DeMar DeRozan" @ChrisBHaynes tells @talkhoops why Sacramento would like to move on from one of their starting guards Hear Deals and Dunks weekdays at 1 PM ET sxm.app.link/DealsAndDunksL

item.title: LaMelo Ball has drawn trade interest from several …
item.description: ‪Michael Scotto‬: LaMelo Ball has drawn trade interest from several teams, including the Toronto Raptors and Minnesota Timberwolves, sources confirm. Toronto originally expressed trade interest in Ball during the summer of 2025 and renewed talks with the Charlotte Hornets over the past few days.

item.title: Minnesota Timberwolves have expressed a strong desire …
item.description: Chris Haynes: Minnesota Timberwolves have expressed a strong desire to acquire Charlotte Hornets star LaMelo Ball, league sources tell me. Hornets only listening to offers at the moment.

item.title: The Wolves can expand the Julius Randle trade to …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Wolves can expand the Julius Randle trade to acquire LaMelo Ball. They'd want to do a 100 percent salary match so they can exceed the first apron, which can be done by including Donte DiVincenzo. Then it's about adding draft picks & young players to incentivize Charlotte.

item.title: If the Minnesota Timberwolves trade for LaMelo Ball, …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: If the Minnesota Timberwolves trade for LaMelo Ball, keep an eye on the three-team trade that the Wolves agreed to with the Brooklyn Nets and Chicago Bulls becoming an expanded trade to include the Charlotte Hornets. That would allow Minnesota to use Julius Randle and the outgoing salary-match (he'd still end up Brooklyn). Would need other pieces involved too but could work for the teams involved.

item.title: "Maybe his former teams." One of the most fascinating …
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: "Maybe his former teams." One of the most fascinating comments of the night by @BobbyMarks42 regarding which teams could be interested in trading for Kawhi Leonard. Now, I can't imagine it's the Spurs. But the Raptors? Yeah. Sign me up for Ingram + whatever it takes for Kawhi.

item.title: Quadir Copeland intends to sign a two-way contract …
item.description: Jake Fischer: NC State guard Quadir Copeland intends to sign a two-way contract with the Houston Rockets, sources say.

item.title: The Los Angeles Lakers have agreed to a two-way …
item.description: Michael Scotto‬: Sources: The Los Angeles Lakers have agreed to a two-way contract with MAC Player of the Year Peter Suder. He led Miami (OH) to a 31-0 regular-season record and shot 42% from 3-point range this season. Keith Kreiter and Sam Cipriano of Edge Sports completed the deal.

item.title: Leon Rose: I got killed about Jalen Brunson, and now everyone thinks I’m a genius
item.description: Leon Rose on the criticism, at the time, of aggressively pursuing Brunson: “I got killed about Jalen, and now everyone thinks I’m a genius. It’s about what we did in order to get you. Clearing space, at the time people thinking that it was so much money that we were paying you. … This was the first major move and I had all the conviction in the world. I’ve known him since he was born, I know what he’s all about, I know what a winner he is, I know that I was willing to ride with him.”

item.title: “It was tough,” Rose said on the show. “Thibs is a …
item.description: There is little left to debate about the decision to fire Tom Thibodeau last year and hire Mike Brown — it resulted in a championship. Since Rose never talks to the media, though, he never spoke publicly about the move. But he fulfilled a promise to Jalen Brunson and Josh Hart — that he’d go on their “Roommates Show” podcast if the Knicks won the championship. Rose was asked about the coaching change. “It was tough,” Rose said on the show. “Thibs is a great coach. Did so much for the organizatio

item.title: “First of all, just his openness and his willingness …
item.description: One of the most important criteria was a coach who was collaborative — more so than Thibodeau. That is something that stood out to Rose and the front office about Brown. “First of all, just his openness and his willingness to share ideas,” Rose said. “And share ideas with the front office, his staff and the inclusion of everyone. I think you guys probably saw that. He really was open to things, open to people’s suggestions, open to ideas. I believe that’s what led to some of the changes that wer

item.title: “He’s one of the rare athletes, rare humans with a …
item.description: NBA on ESPN: “He’s one of the rare athletes, rare humans with a combination to be a very special player in the future.” Wizards GM Will Dawkins on the decision to draft AJ Dybantsa with the No. 1 pick 🙌

item.title: Bucks also expressed interest in LaMelo Ball
item.description: Jake Fischer: Sources say the Milwaukee Bucks have also registered trade interest recently in LaMelo Ball, but that has dated back to when the Bucks looked at him as a potential addition to Giannis Antetokounmpo. Story here: tinyurl.com/4hxz8593

item.title: Sources: East Carolina’s Jordan Riley will sign an NBA …
item.description: Billy Reinhardt: Sources: East Carolina’s Jordan Riley will sign an NBA contract and play in Summer League with the New Orleans Pelicans. Agent Jason Rich of TCA Next negotiated the deal.

item.title: Ben Humrichous has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Illinois’ Ben Humrichous has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract with the Brooklyn Nets, sources tell DraftExpress. The 6’9” hybrid forward played a key role off the bench for the Illini throughout their Final Four run. Showed well throughout his predraft workout circuit.

item.title: Lamar Wilkerson has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Indiana’s Lamar Wilkerson has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract with the Oklahoma City Thunder, sources tell DraftExpress. The 6’4+ sharp-shooting guard averaged 20+ PPG each of the past two seasons and has drilled 39.2% of his 780 career three-point attempts.

item.title: Kowacie Reeves agreed to an Exhibit 10 deal with the …
item.description: ‪Scott Agness‬: Kowacie Reeves agreed to an Exhibit 10 deal with the Pacers, league source told Fieldhouse Files. He's a 6-foot-7 forward from Georgia Tech.

item.title: The Blazers have agreed to an Exhibit 10 deal with …
item.description: ‪Sean Highkin‬: The Blazers have agreed to an Exhibit 10 deal with undrafted Pittsburgh wing Barry Dunning, I'm told. He'll be with the Blazers next month at Summer League in Las Vegas and in training camp this fall.

item.title: The 6-foot-3 Lee agreed to a free-agent contract with …
item.description: From the time he first arrived on Princeton University’s campus as a lightly recruited point guard, Xaivian Lee believed he has what it takes to play in the NBA. Now he’s got a golden opportunity to find out. The 6-foot-3 Lee agreed to a free-agent contract with the Cleveland Cavaliers shortly after the NBA Draft. He’ll get a chance to prove himself in the NBA’s summer league and, most likely, in the G League during the 2026-27 season.

item.title: Hornets listening to offers for LaMelo Ball despite his interest in staying
item.description: Sources say LaMelo Ball has been looking forward to this upcoming season with the team that drafted him No. 3 overall in 2020 to try to build off of Charlotte's momentum from 2025-26. The Hornets went a surprising 44-38 this season, only to fall at the final Play-In Tournament hurdle in Orlando and miss the playoffs for a league-high 10th consecutive season. We're told that the Hornets are nonetheless listening to the offers they're getting despite their recent success under second-year coach Ch

item.title: Jaden McDaniels off limits in trade conversations
item.description: Minnesota has maintained that perimeter defensive ace Jaden McDaniels is off limits in trade conversations. Sources say that the Wolves also prefer to "do right" by injured guard Donte DiVincenzo and keep the Villanova product out of their offseason business after he sustained a devastating torn Achilles during Minnesota's first-round playoff victory over Denver. The Wolves, sources say, would also prefer not to part with 2024-25 Sixth Man of the Year Naz Reid, but it is difficult to see how the

item.title: Keba Keita has agreed to an Exhibit 10 contract with …
item.description: ‪Tony East‬: Keba Keita has agreed to an Exhibit 10 contract with the Pacers, per league source. The plan is for him to be with the team for summer league. Played at BYU the last two seasons.

item.title: Nuggets VP Ben Tenzer on Nikola Jokic's extension: 'We feel really comfortable with that relationship'
item.description: Bennett Durando: Ben Tenzer: "We just can't comment on (extension progress with Nikola Jokic) because of the league rules, but you've heard what he's said about his happiness here. We feel really comfortable with that relationship."

item.title: Sam Hoiberg intends to sign with the Phoenix Suns,
item.description: Jake Fischer: Nebraska’s Big-Ten All-Defensive guard Sam Hoiberg intends to sign with the Phoenix Suns, per source.

item.title: Valanciunas described a routine built around constant …
item.description: Valanciunas described a routine built around constant improvement and preparation. "He's at the facility early in the morning, getting shots up, putting in extra work. After every game, we'd hit the weight room. Every single game. He plays 40 minutes, I play my eight, and we'd still do exactly the same workout afterward. His work ethic is something else." "Sure, maybe he has a beer in the summer, maybe you see the videos with his horses and everything else. That's his business," JV explained. "B

item.title: “Giannis said so many things,” a former coach said, …
item.description: These deals bothered Doc Rivers and other coaches, as they felt the organization had bowed too much to appease their star player. To them, Giannis Antetokounmpo wanted things both ways. “Giannis said so many things,” a former coach said, “It stems from your actions, which is, 'My brothers have to be on this team.' Well then, are you about a championship? 'Cause they’re not only not helping us win a championship, it’s creating dissension.” Jon Horst, meanwhile, appeared to try to appease Rivers w

item.title: Austin Reaves collapsed on the grass at The Course at …
item.description: Austin Reaves collapsed on the grass at The Course at Eagle Mountain. Talks with the Los Angeles Lakers about his free agency lasted only a week, but Reaves was fried. Tuesday needed to be the last of it — for his sanity. “I didn’t like it,” Reaves told The Athletic. “It was weird. It just was, I mean, it’s just a lot of ‘What ifs.’ I just like to know where I’m going to be.”

item.title: Jonas Valanciunas, who has spent 14 years playing at …
item.description: Jonas Valanciunas, who has spent 14 years playing at the highest level, said no player has impressed him more with their dedication to the game (than Nikola Jokic). "Yeah, people see one photo, some appearance somewhere, maybe they spot a guy having a beer or doing something else, and it's very easy to form an opinion. But then there's the work that goes on behind the scenes. Jokic is a perfect example of that," he said on BasketNews Off the Record podcast. "Everyone says he has a beer here and

item.title: The Dallas Mavericks are still mapping out when …
item.description: The Dallas Mavericks are still mapping out when Spanish guard Sergio De Larrea will join the roster, general manager Mike Schmitz said, leaving open whether the 20-year-old reports for the 2026-27 season or remains overseas a year longer. Dallas acquired De Larrea’s draft rights in a draft-night trade with the New York Knicks, who selected him at No. 25 before routing the pick to the Mavericks. He is widely projected as a draft-and-stash prospect expected to stay with Valencia in the near term.

item.title: Orlando used the No. 51 pick to select Nelson, the …
item.description: After remaining out of the first round of the NBA draft on Tuesday night, the Magic made multiple trades throughout the second round on Wednesday before ultimately landing on South Florida forward Izaiyah Nelson. Entering the night with only the No. 46 pick, Orlando sent that pick, which was used to select Tennessee forward Felix Okpara, to the Washington Wizards in exchange for the Nos. 51 and 60 draft picks, a league source confirmed to the Orlando Sentinel. Shortly after, the Magic then sent

item.title: AJ Dybantsa on taking Trae Young's #3 jersey number: …
item.description: [Finlay] AJ Dybantsa on taking Trae Young's #3 jersey number: "I was trolling about but... if he wants to give me the number I'll take the number"

item.title: A drumbeat of laudatory speeches praised state …
item.description: A drumbeat of laudatory speeches praised state lawmakers’ bipartisan sprint this spring to pledge $365 million toward a revamp of the now publicly owned arena and keep the Trail Blazers in Portland, after a group led by Texas billionaire Tom Dundon purchased the team for $4.25 billion. A phalanx of business and elected leaders, including Mayor Keith Wilson and Multnomah County Chair Jessica Vega Pederson, touted the civic and economic benefits that the estimated $600 million project — to be fund

item.title: Tobe Awaka has agreed to a two-way NBA deal with the …
item.description: Shams Charania: Undrafted Arizona forward Tobe Awaka has agreed to a two-way NBA deal with the Chicago Bulls, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Nate Johnson has agreed to an Exhibit 10 contract with …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Undrafted Kansas State guard Nate Johnson has agreed to an Exhibit 10 contract with the OKC Thunder, per source. Johnson is a 6-3 guard who averaged 12.8 points and 4.9 rebounds as a senior last season on 40.8% shooting from deep. Represented by Edge Sports International.

item.title: Lamar Wilkerson has agreed to an Exhibit 10 deal with …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Undrafted Indiana guard Lamar Wilkerson has agreed to an Exhibit 10 deal with the OKC Thunder. His teammate, Sam Alexis, has also agreed to play Summer League with the Thunder.

item.title: The Blazers are poised to land an architecture firm …
item.description: The Blazers are poised to land an architecture firm this week, according to the team. It could be another two months before a general contractor is chosen and early December by the time design plans are finalized, according to a copy of the project timeline reviewed by The Oregonian/OregonLive.

item.title: Oscar Cluff will play for the Houston Rockets in the …
item.description: Olgun Uluc: Oscar Cluff will play for the Houston Rockets in the 2026 NBA Summer League, sources told ESPN. The Australian big-man started all 39 games as a senior at Purdue, averaging 10.6 ppg (68.3% FG), 7.5 rpg, and 1.8 apg.

item.title: Max Mackinnon has agreed to an Exhibit 10 deal with …
item.description: Olgun Uluc: Max Mackinnon has agreed to an Exhibit 10 deal with the Charlotte Hornets, sources told ESPN. The 6’6 Australian guard averaged 15.7 ppg, 2.6 rpg, and 2.5 apg as a senior at LSU.

item.title: Jaylen Brown: 'ESPN is unethical, and Stephen A. Smith is the head face of that'
item.description: Jaylen Brown: "ESPN is unethical, and Stephen A. Smith is the head face of that. The organization, the players, they were all in agreeaance. They all knew what I meant by that. Our team was basically salary dumped. We lost a lot of players. For us to emerge and still be in the same position..."

item.title: Texas billionaire Tom Dundon is scheduled to headline …
item.description: Texas billionaire Tom Dundon is scheduled to headline the annual meeting of the Portland Metro Chamber at the Moda Center on Wednesday. The $350-per-ticket event will also feature state and local elected leaders who’ve championed an overhaul of the city-owned arena, including Portland Mayor Keith Wilson, Multnomah County Chair Jessica Vega Pederson, Oregon Senate President Rob Wagner and state Sen. Kate Lieber. Proponents of the complex public finance deal, estimated to cost about $600 million,

item.title: “We’re not going to be held hostage, we’re not going …
item.description: City and county lawmakers are pumping the brakes, saying it’s better to take the time to get it right than to sign away huge sums of public money under pressure from Portland’s professional basketball franchise, which has called the Rose City home since 1970. “We’re not going to be held hostage, we’re not going to sign a bad deal,” Portland Council President Jamie Dunphy told The Oregonian/OregonLive, echoing a position expressed by many of his colleagues. “We’re not going to be a blank check fo

item.title: Another Portland councilor recently called the idea …
item.description: Another Portland councilor recently called the idea that the Blazers would relocate to another city “a massive bluff,” a sentiment that some on the county commission privately share. Local leaders also have a list of desires they hope to extract from the dealmaking, at least one of which doesn’t even involve the Moda Center but rather the Veterans Memorial Coliseum next door.

item.title: Mike Zarren on how the NBA possibly expanding to add 2 …
item.description: Bobby Krivitsky: Mike Zarren on how the NBA possibly expanding to add 2 new teams impacts the Celtics' current planning: "We don't know if the rules for an expansion draft will be the same as they were last time...The more good contracts you have, the more good contracts you'll have left after an expansion draft... The more good players you can have...the more that will be left." He also notes expansion isn't guaranteed.

item.title: Re: the video going around from the Nuggets draft room …
item.description: Vinny Benedetto: Re: the video going around from the Nuggets draft room last night, it sounds there was some miscommunication between Denver and San Antonio as the Nuggets' clock was on the verge of expiring.

item.title: Nike offered AJ Dybantsa his first sneaker deal at age …
item.description: Nike offered AJ Dybantsa his first sneaker deal at age 16, and he said yes (even though his friend Shaquille O’Neal wanted him at Reebok) because “Nike’s the best marketing sports brand out there. So when they offered me, that was like a no brainer. Being in the same conversation as LeBron, Kobe, Kevin Durant, Devin Booker. Like I said, kind of a no-brainer.” Nike wanted to know all about him. He told them the taxi driver story, told them he was Starboy, told him he already had his own logo, too

item.title: Leon Rose is not Jalen Brunson’s godfather. But, he …
item.description: Roommates Show: The truth has been revealed. Leon Rose is not Jalen Brunson’s godfather. But, he did meet Jalen before Rick did @BetMGM

item.title: Although the comments may have been meant as playful …
item.description: The quips were references to a motorcycle accident that prematurely ended Jay Williams’ playing career. The Duke phenom enjoyed a promising rookie year, but played only one season before the accident derailed his career. Although the comments may have been meant as playful jabs at Williams, they created an awkward moment that the internet quickly seized on. “For me, it was extremely uncomfortable,” Williams tells Front Office Sports. He says there was “no real tension,” but it was “awkward.”

item.title: Williams takes pride in the second career he has built …
item.description: Williams takes pride in the second career he has built in broadcast media over the last two decades as an ESPN analyst who is a regular panelist on shows like Get Up and First Take. “It’s about what you do after something like that happens,” he says. He and Richard Jefferson go way back, having played against each other in the 2001 NCAA national championship game. Williams chose not to get into a back-and-forth with his fellow analysts in real time, but there was no stopping social media account

item.title: He’s been living with his accident for more than 20 …
item.description: He’s been living with his accident for more than 20 years now, and says “people typically use it to try to diminish what I’ve been able to accomplish.” “For me, frankly, I think it speaks volumes about what I have been able to accomplish,” he tells FOS. As for how he, Jefferson, Smith, and Negandhi will move forward, Williams isn’t concerned. “We’ll talk about it, we’ll learn from it, we’ll move on. Welcome to TV.”

item.title: Al Horford staying with Warriors
item.description: Shams Charania: Golden State's Al Horford is declining his player option and intends to sign a new two-year, $14 million deal to stay with the Warriors, sources tell me and @anthonyVslater. Horford, 40, becomes only the 13th player in NBA history to reach 20 seasons.

item.title: Signs seemed good for Golden State of late as Al …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: Signs seemed good for Golden State of late as Al Horford has been working out in practice facility.

item.title: Boston has apparently decided that Jaylen Brown …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "The most important thing in the league right now for what is going to happen in the 2026-27 season is that Boston has apparently decided that Jaylen Brown probably needs to be traded for them to be competitive. Because that is now going to be the next storyline in the NBA. Obviously, the Heat getting Giannis is a huge story for them. From the moment they traded Jimmy Butler, they have been on the hunt for the next star. That is a huge story for them."

item.title: "Well, the team that you're hearing about the most …
item.description: The Minnesota Timberwolves have reportedly emerged as the strongest candidate to pursue the Charlotte Hornets star, with league sources continuing to connect Ball to a potential partnership with Anthony Edwards. Speaking about the situation, MacMahon explained that Minnesota is the team being discussed most frequently among league executives and insiders. "Well, the team that you're hearing about the most from people around the league is the Minnesota Timberwolves. And it's no secret," he said p

item.title: "If the cost is Gobert, I can't see how they go …
item.description: Brian Windhorst on the Timberwolves potentially trading Rudy Gobert for LaMelo Ball: "If the cost is Gobert, I can't see how they go forward. They won the series against the Nuggets because of Gobert. There is a height and size race in the Western Conference right now. So I'm not sure what the pathway is. I guess it's possible that they could expand the Julius Randle trade to a multiple team trade to open this door, but I do think Minnesota feels like they have to look at this, and I think Charl

item.title: Buddy Hield's contract with the Atlanta Hawks will …
item.description: Keith Smith: Barring an amendment to push the deadline back, Buddy Hield's contract with the Atlanta Hawks will become fully guaranteed at $9.6M if he's not waived today. Hield's deal is already partially guaranteed for $3M.

item.title: Hornets waiving Tosan Evbuomwan
item.description: Marc Stein: The Hornets are waiving forward Tosan Evbuomwan, league sources say. Evbuomwan has averaged 7.4 points, 3.6 rebounds, and 1.4 assists in 50 career NBA games and is coming off a G League Finals MVP performance this past season.

item.title: Kevin Durant became the oldest player in @NBA history …
item.description: Crazy Stats: This season, Kevin Durant became the oldest player in @NBA history to score 2,000 points in a single season. At 37 years and 195 days old, he surpassed the previous mark of 36 years and 270 days held by Karl Malone (2,095 points during the 1999–00 season). Moreover, the Rockets are the fourth team with which Durant has recorded a 2,000-point season. No other player in league history has accomplished this with more than two teams.

item.title: Ja Morant super available in trade talks
item.description: NBA Base: Ja Morant is “flagrantly available” in trade talks, but his 2-year contract is making teams hesitant to take on the risk, per @WindhorstESPN "I just can't see him playing for the Grizzlies again." (Via @UnSportsESPN)

item.title: Lowe: 'I don't think Jaylen Brown starts the season with the Boston Celtics next year'
item.description: CelticsBlog: Zach Lowe on his podcast: "It feels like the the toothpaste is too far out of the tube with Jaylen Brown." "If you ask me right now... I don't think Jaylen Brown starts the season with the Boston Celtics next year. That could end up being wrong. I'm not saying that definitively, I'm saying if you made me bet, yes or no, is Jaylen Brown a Celtic next year, I'm betting no"

item.title: Been authorized to discuss the matter publicly, the …
item.description: Earlier this week, according to league sources who requested anonymity because they haven’t been authorized to discuss the matter publicly, the Knicks and reserve guard Jose Alvarado agreed to postpone the decision date for his $4.5 million player option from June 22 to June 26, allowing New York to get through the draft before making any more significant financial commitments this offseason.

item.title: NBA executives also have concerns about Stokes' …
item.description: NBA executives also have concerns about Stokes' maturity and demeanor, to the point that those questions are almost impossible to extract from his evaluation. Scouts have frequently noted his on-court temper and moodiness. He is described by those who know him as competitive and intelligent, and learning to better channel that on the floor will go a long way.

item.title: “After he retired, he let that guard down, and …
item.description: Whether it was sticking his tongue out during a drive, mimicking specific movements on the court, relying on a deadly fadeaway jumper, or displaying a ruthless attitude toward his teammates, Kobe was truly the closest thing we will likely ever see to Michael. One of the people who idolized him is former NBA player Damjan Rudez, who recalled Kobe’s on-court aura. “Kobe had this sort of shield around him. During his career, he was genuinely impenetrable. You had no idea what he was like in private

The sense that I get around the league is that the Celtics are having these discussions with a level of purpose. All the Jaylen Brown trade rumors in Boston. It was Kawhi Leonard in 2018. It was Anthony Davis in 19 and 20. It was Kevin Durant in 2022. But this to me feels different. item.title: He LA Clippers will be adding Purdue shooting guard …

item.title: At the start of the 2024-25 season, Rivers’ first full …
item.description: Beyond a pair of double doors inside the lower-level bowl of Fiserv Forum, a hallway leads to the Bucks locker room. Following the signing of his maximum contract extension in the winter of 2020, it was adorned with Antetokounmpo’s Nike logo, an interlocking “GA." At the start of the 2024-25 season, Rivers’ first full campaign as head coach, the “GA” was removed. Instead, the team emblazoned the word “together” in different languages. The English version of the word was misspelled.

item.title: Having a $90,000 golf simulator for the head coach …
item.description: On its own, a graphic error is not much. Having a $90,000 golf simulator for the head coach take over a communal space in the practice facility is not much. Calling some players into a meeting and not others is not much. Bucks players realized these were not isolated incidents, but a pattern with Rivers, and it diminished their comfort and trust. “Guys had trouble with his delivery,” one player said. “The whole ‘I’ve done this before, this is the way’ approach that was preached to us from Doc wa

item.description: The Spanish national team will face Denmark on July 2 in Madrid before traveling to Tbilisi to take on Georgia on July 5. Spain enters the final window with a perfect 4-0 record and has already secured a place in the next qualification round. Veteran center Willy Hernangomez will captain a roster that combines experienced internationals with some of the country's most promising young players. Among the young names included are Izan Almansa, Mario Saint-Supery, Alvaro Cardenas, and Boston Celtics

item.description: After ESPN reported Wednesday night that the Charlotte Hornets were fielding offers for Ball, the Timberwolves were one of several teams to express interest in trading for the star point guard, team and league sources told The Athletic. The people spoke on the condition of anonymity because discussions were still in the very early stages.

item.title: The Randle trade is not expected to become official …
item.description: The Randle trade is not expected to become official for several weeks because both the Nets and Wolves have to work through other moves first to maximize their dealmaking abilities under the rules of the collective bargaining agreement. “We’ll always kind of invent a way to transact even if, at times, on paper, it doesn’t look as though we can,” Lloyd said. “Something will happen. I don’t know how. We’ll have mechanisms to utilize here in the next couple weeks, too.”

So, when you're playing a team like OKC or San Antonio, who can spread you out with shooters. They can't cover that ground.” Vince: “You need more of an athletic big for that pick. item.title: Tracy: "The Chicago Bulls were trying to make a trade. …
item.description: Tracy: "The Chicago Bulls were trying to make a trade. If anyone watched ‘The Last Dance,’ they were talking about trading Scottie Pippen around that time in 1997. I was going to be a part of that deal. They called me the night before the draft. I had to go to the clinic, take a physical, and it was about to go down the next night. Jerry Reinsdorf and Michael Jordan axed that deal, and the trade didn't go through…Sitting in the green room as an 18-year-old and there's a possibility I might be pl

item.title: Andrew Bogut has been fined $9,500 following an …
item.description: NBL Communications: Sydney Kings Assistant Coach and co-owner Andrew Bogut has been fined $9,500 following an investigation under the NBL Code of Conduct and Social Media Policy. The sanction was determined following a review of Mr Bogut’s conduct, including a series of social media posts and an incident following a game on 27 March 2026. The club has been formally notified and Mr Bogut retains the right to appeal.

He's got to put on some weight, he's got to work on his conditioning. Because he kept, you could tell he got tired, partly because it just looks to me like playing defense today is so much harder because you have to

item.title: “For me, it was extremely uncomfortable,” Williams …
item.description: The quips were references to a motorcycle accident that prematurely ended Williams’ playing career. The Duke phenom enjoyed a promising rookie year, but played only one season before the accident derailed his career. Although the comments may have been meant as playful jabs at Williams, they created an awkward moment that the internet quickly seized on. “For me, it was extremely uncomfortable,” Williams tells Front Office Sports. He says there was “no real tension,” but it was “awkward.”

item.title: Williams takes pride in the second career he has built …
item.description: Williams takes pride in the second career he has built in broadcast media over the last two decades as an ESPN analyst who is a regular panelist on shows like Get Up and First Take. “It’s about what you do after something like that happens,” he says. He and Jefferson go way back, having played against each other in the 2001 NCAA national championship game. Williams chose not to get into a back-and-forth with his fellow analysts in real time, but there was no stopping social media accounts from c

item.title: Timberwolves trade big haul, including Naz Reid, to Charlotte for star point guard LaMelo Ball
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The Charlotte Hornets are trading star guard LaMelo Ball and Josh Green to the Minnesota Timberwolves for Naz Reid, a 2033 unprotected first-round pick, three first-round pick swaps (2028, 2029, 2030) and three second-round picks (2029, 2032, 2033), sources tell ESPN.

item.title: The Timberwolves send a haul to the Hornets for a …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Timberwolves send a haul to the Hornets for a star-studded new backcourt in Anthony Edwards and LaMelo Ball -- the Nos. 1 and 3 picks in 2020. Charlotte didn't shop Ball in the market, but listened and received multiple rich offers before filling a significant need in the frontcourt with Reid. Timberwolves co-owners Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez enter the luxury tax for the third straight year -- and the franchise makes a big move to fill the point guard spot.

item.title: The Minnesota Timberwolves are acquiring Charlotte …
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: The Minnesota Timberwolves are acquiring Charlotte Hornets star LaMelo Ball in exchange for for Naz Reid, a first round pick, three first round pick swaps and three second round picks.

item.title: Charlotte is indeed now trading LaMelo Ball to …
item.description: Marc Stein: Charlotte is indeed now trading LaMelo Ball to Minnesota, league sources say, after @TheSteinLine revealed Minnesota’s interest last night. The Hornets have successfully pried Naz Reid away from the Wolves and will now try to retain Pistons target Coby White in free agency.

item.title: The easiest path could be to fold this deal into the …
item.description: Keith Smith: Multiple ways the LaMelo Ball trade could be completed, but unless other parts of this emerge later, the easiest path could be to fold this deal into the previously agreed to trade with the Nets, Bulls and Timberwolves. That would solve salary-matching issues for the Wolves.

"I knew a few off the top of my head, but I hadn't seen the list," Al Horford told ESPN. "It's hard to believe. It's something that, for me, I'm very grateful. It's just so hard to be in this league for that amount of years. There's a lot of c

item.description: "I want to see it through," Al Horford said. "That [injury] kind of put a damper on things. Jimmy is a very special player, and he was doing so much for us. I feel like things were starting to turn."

Julius Randle & Naz Reid for LaMelo Ball & Josh Green is a 100 percent salary match. They're now hard capped to second apron for aggregation.

item.title: Hornets have extracted an undeniable haul from …
item.description: Marc Stein: The Hornets have extracted an undeniable haul from Minnesota to land Naz Reid plus an unprotected first-round pick in 2033 as well as three first-round pick swaps and three second-round picks. It is expected this trade will be folded into the three-team Julius Randle deal.

item.title: Hornets reduce their payroll by $21 million by trading …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Hornets reduce their payroll by $21 million by trading LaMelo Ball and Josh Green for Naz Reid. Josh Green for Naz Reid is a clean salary match, allowing them to create a $40.8 million trade exception for Ball. They also have 12 future first-round picks through 2033.

item.title: Ball was somebody the Wolves long thought could be a …
item.description: ‪‪Chris Hine‬: LaMelo Ball was somebody the Wolves long thought could be a great backcourt mate with Anthony Edwards and think he can be a winning player, despite public perception. The question was if he’d ever become available. He did, and here we are.

item.title: LaMelo Ball and Josh Green are going to the Wolves for …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov‬: LaMelo Ball and Josh Green are going to the Wolves for Naz Reid, a 2033 unprotected 1st round pick, swaps in 2028, 2029, and 2030, and 3 second round picks, a league source confirmed. Shams was first on it. A huge swing for both teams.
