item.title: Blazers owner Tom Dundon wants Portland arena renovations to be fully publicly funded
item.description: The battle lines are rapidly hardening in the Portland arena renovation funding right, and the message from Trail Blazers owner Tom Dundon is stark and clear: Don’t expect me to help pay for it. A day after Portland Mayor Keith Wilson publicly pleaded with the city council to approve up to $120 million in municipal funding to renovate the Moda Center, Dundon appeared at a Portland Metro Chamber event late Wednesday and said private financing is not part of his vision to upgrade the 31-year-old,

item.title: “When you charge an incremental fee on the ticket, …
item.description: While not a specific and overt threat to leave town if the renovation funding bill fails, Dundon did little at the session to dismiss existing fears that he would do so. “I think everybody can characterize things however they want. I don’t see it the same way, but I’m not trying to get people to agree or disagree with me,” he said. More specifically, Dundon also argued he’s still contributing indirectly to the renovation funding by passing along ticket taxes that are part of the proposed structu

item.title: Am going to have a hard time agreeing to give public …
item.description: As Dundon made his remarks, several members of Portland’s 12-member city council remained strongly opposed to providing funding. The city’s share of a nearly $600 million arena renovation project would add to up to $365 million in Oregon state funds and $88 million in Multnomah County money that already have been approved. The pathway for the city approval, however, is increasingly uncertain. “I am going to have a hard time agreeing to give public money if I’m not seeing a private investment,” s

item.title: AJ Dybantsa looking forward to facing his favorite player, Kevin Durant
item.description: Chase Hughes: AJ Dybantsa said his favorite player has always been Kevin Durant, "so me sharing the court with him will be ridiculous." He said the arena he most looks forward to playing in is TD Garden near his hometown.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa on John Wall's advice: "Really tap into …
item.description: Chase Hughes: AJ Dybantsa on John Wall's advice: "Really tap into the community, they love you here.... I saw the video of [fans] at the Anthem."

item.title: "I fill seats," said he started to notice the crowds …
item.description: Chase Hughes: AJ Dybantsa on his famous line "I fill seats," said he started to notice the crowds change when he was in the 8th grade & he showed up to his HS and they had yellow tape on the sidelines to help contain the fans. Then he played at TD Garden with BYU and saw the seats all packed.

item.title: "He got to his middy and I take huge pride getting to …
item.description: Chase Hughes: AJ Dybantsa on his viral video comparing himself to Len Bias: "He got to his middy and I take huge pride getting to my middy... I started watching his film probably a couple months ago. I started pulling up his film on TikTok and I didn't know who he was."

item.title: Official: @AJ_Dybantsa will wear No. 4 🔥
item.description: Washington Wizards: Official: @AJ_Dybantsa will wear No. 4 🔥

item.title: "DP was definitely #1 pick for most of the year... we …
item.description: Run It Back: Jazz owner Ryan Smith is excited that Darryn Peterson is headed to Utah 🔥 "DP was definitely #1 pick for most of the year... we were in a pretty good spot... he slides in perfectly next to Keyonte George." 💯

item.title: Kawhi Leonard would be willing to sign extensions with Raptors, Spurs
item.description: While the LA Clippers are undeniably determined to keep hold of Kawhi Leonard — and with Leonard's clear first choice known to be staying with them in his beloved Southern California — league sources say that Toronto has registered interest in trying to re-acquire the engine of the lone championship team in franchise history should Leonard become truly available.

item.title: Sources say he has a similar view regarding a …
item.description: And sources say that Leonard would not have interest in signing an extension with Detroit in the event that the Pistons traded for him this summer. Sources say he has a similar view regarding a long-term partnership with the Heat if Miami had any designs on pursuing Leonard to slot alongside Antetokounmpo and Bam Adebayo.

item.title: Toronto and San Antonio
item.description: Sources close to the situation tell The Stein Line that, when it comes to potential trade talks, there are only two other teams that Leonard would consider signing an extension with: The two teams he played for before becoming a Clipper. Toronto and San Antonio.

item.title: Jazz owner Ryan Smith on Walker Kessler: We love him, he's a big piece of where we're going
item.description: Run It Back: Utah Jazz owner Ryan Smith on re-signing Walker Kessler: "We love Walk. He's a big piece of where we're going."

item.title: "We met with Darryn at the combine. Darryn was awesome …
item.description: Jazz owner Ryan Smith: "We met with Darryn at the combine. Darryn was awesome in the interview. Darryn wanted to go number one. Everyone wants to go number one. But by the way, we had three different top guys come in. I don't even call it a workout. I call it dinner. I don't even go. I'm like, okay, I'll go to dinner with you because there's no sweat, there's no nothing. They're sitting down with Will watching film. They put on their jersey to check out the size and everything. They take a coupl

item.title: "There's reasons why those narratives take off and the …
item.description: Jazz owner Ryan Smith: "There's reasons why those narratives take off and the one thing in the NBA is a lot of times I think we all have to do this, we all have to sit back and be like, okay, that's out there. I've never been in a business where so much is untrue that's out there. It's just completely untrue. And then you've got like your mother-in-law reading, can you believe this? And I'm like, oh my word. Like it's just -- and I try to explain to other consumers of regular news out there in t

item.title: "I don't want to set expectations, but like we need to …
item.description: Jazz owner Ryan Smith: "I don't want to set expectations, but like we need to start winning. I think that's that's that's kind of where we're at. When we took over, I took over what was the number one team in the regular season and we got, I mean, it was interesting. My first day was like post-COVID. We were kind of into COVID and I had like two weeks to get a deal done with Rudy Gobert and Donovan Mitchell. It was really complicated because normally you have a long time to do this, but that tea

item.title: OKC has agreed to an Exhibit 10 deal with Ohio State’s …
item.description: Justin Martinez: OKC has agreed to an Exhibit 10 deal with Ohio State’s Christoph Tilly. The 7-foot center from Germany averaged 11 points and 4.7 rebounds as a senior last season. He spent the first three years of his college career at Santa Clara.

item.title: For those who have questioned Heat willingness to …
item.description: Ira Winderman: For those who have questioned Heat willingness to spend, they paid $1.5 million to move up four spots in Wednesday night's second round. Adam Simon, "Ownership gave us the opportunity to move up. So we appreciate them doing that."

item.title: Bucks center Jericho Sims to exercise player option for 2026-27
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: Milwaukee Bucks center Jericho Sims plans to exercise his $2.8 million player option for the 2026-27 season, league sources told @hoopshype . The 27-year-old center started 19 of 67 games for the Bucks, averaging 5.0 points and 5.5 rebounds per game last season.

item.title: Raptors exercise 2026-27 team option on Jamal Shead
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: The Toronto Raptors have exercised the $2.30 million team option on Jamal Shead for the 2026-27 season, league sources told @hoopshype . Shead played all 82 games, averaging 6.6 points and 5.4 assist per game for Toronto last season.

item.title: Jimmy Butler says he's a month and a half away from running again
item.description: Nick Friedell: Jimmy Butler says he’s doing well in his rehab. He’s about a month and a half away from running again. He’s hoping to have the team back down to his San Diego area home before training camp starts again.

item.title: Timebrwolves not trading Donte DiVincenzo
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Wolves do still have other moves in the works. They need a power forward and they need more shooting. I am told that they are not trading Donte DiVincenzo. The work continues.

item.title: Louisville’s Isaac McKneely has agreed to an …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Louisville’s Isaac McKneely has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract with the Atlanta Hawks, sources tell DraftExpress. The bona fide sniper is a 41.4% three point shooter on nearly 800 career attempts.

item.title: Santa Clara forward Elijah Mahi has signed a summer …
item.description: Dushawn London: Santa Clara forward Elijah Mahi has signed a summer league deal with the Oklahoma City Thunder, source tells @247Sports

item.title: Tennessee State guard Aaron Nkrumah will sign an …
item.description: Tennessee State guard Aaron Nkrumah will sign an Exhibit 10 contract with the Nuggets and is expected to compete for one of the team’s two-way roster openings, according to Brett Siegel of ClutchPoints (Twitter link). A 6’6″ senior, Nkrumah ranked 52nd on ESPN’s big board and was considered to have a good chance to get drafted heading into Wednesday night. His age may have worked against him, as he’ll turn 25 in November. Nkrumah was named Player of the Year in the Ohio Valley Conference this se

item.title: @Tucker_DeVries has signed an Exhibit-10 contract with …
item.description: Indiana Basketball: Boston bound. ☘️ @Tucker_DeVries has signed an Exhibit-10 contract with the @celtics !

item.title: Speaking at an event announcing a new jersey patch for …
item.description: Nick Friedell: Speaking at an event announcing a new jersey patch for the Warriors — Joe Lacob is very happy to have Yaxel Lendeborg with the team. “A good day in Warriors history.”

item.title: "The best part of my job, my favorite thing that I do, …
item.description: Sean Highkin: Joe Cronin: "The best part of my job, my favorite thing that I do, is I have a front-row seat to watch people live their dreams. It's such a cool and unique thing to watch people do what they love and go on their paths. To be next to Micah through this, couldn't be more excited." Micah Nori starts out by thanking the Timberwolves organization. "Without them and the success we've had the last five years, I'm not here."

item.title: "As a first-time head coach, you very rarely get an …
item.description: Sean Highkin: Micah Nori: "As a first-time head coach, you very rarely get an opportunity to coach a team that's been in the playoffs. Usually, you're taking over a bad situation." Micah Nori on his vision: "Our identity is, when they come in here, we want to be a physical team. The great thing about this roster is, we have defenders all across the board. We have a team that should be able to create turnovers and play with pace."

item.title: "The way I look at this is, opportunity. For 28 years, …
item.description: Sean Highkin: Micah Nori on why he took the contract: "The way I look at this is, opportunity. For 28 years, the first 25 years I never had an agent. I never look at money or years. I know that if I'm successful, the rest of these things will take care of themselves."

item.title: JJ Barea will coach the Nuggets’ Summer League squad, …
item.description: Vinny Benedetto: JJ Barea will coach the Nuggets’ Summer League squad, I’m told.

item.title: Two-way player Chucky Hepburn to sign qualifying offer with Raptors
item.description: Michael Scotto: Toronto Raptors guard Chucky Hepburn will sign his two-way qualifying offer and return to the team next season, league sources told @hoopshype. He averaged 12.4 points and shot 36.8% from 3-point range with 8.6 assists and 2.4 steals for game with the Raptors 905 in the G League.

item.title: Iowa’s Brendan Hausen will join the Memphis Grizzlies …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Iowa’s Brendan Hausen will join the Memphis Grizzlies for NBA Summer League, sources tell DraftExpress. The 6’4” long-range shooter hit 200 threes in his NCAA career while maintaining a 38.3 3P% and 84.6 FT%. Left a lasting impression at his pre-draft workout in Memphis.

item.title: Kobe Stewart from Presbyterian will be playing NBA …
item.description: Dushawn London: 6’7” Guard Kobe Stewart from Presbyterian will be playing NBA Summer League with the Milwaukee Bucks. Stewart started over 20 games last season in the G-League and shot 36% from 3. Represented by Keith Kreiter and Sam Cipriano at Edge Sports.

item.title: Buchwald is adding to its footprint in the sports …
item.description: Buchwald is adding to its footprint in the sports space with the signing of former NBA star, producer, and media personality Baron Davis for representation in all areas. The agency will rep Davis in film, TV, branding, business development, publishing, media rights, voiceover, and non-fiction.

item.title: Brandon Roy returns to coaching
item.description: Garfield High School will formally welcome alumnus and former NBA All-Star Brandon Roy as its new head boys basketball coach during a press conference on Thursday. Roy, a Garfield graduate whose basketball career began at the Seattle school, returns to lead the Bulldogs after remaining involved with the program for years as a coach, mentor, and supporter of student-athletes, according to Seattle Public Schools.

item.title: Tre White and Melvin Council Jr. have signed free …
item.description: Former University of Kansas men’s basketball players Tre White and Melvin Council Jr. have signed free agent deals with NBA teams after not being selected in the two-round NBA Draft, KU confirmed in a pair of posts on social media site X on Thursday morning. White, a 6-foot-7 wing from Dallas, has signed a deal with the Miami Heat. He initially reported the news with 247Sports via a social media post. Council, a 6-4 guard from Rochester, New York, has signed with the New Orleans Pelicans. Counci

item.title: Tracy McGrady is bringing back a piece of basketball …
item.description: Tracy McGrady is bringing back a piece of basketball history. The seven-time All-Star is reviving the legendary ABCD camp with Sonny Vaccaro, the sneaker executive and founder of the camp. McGrady, who will own 80% of the camp, and Vaccaro, who will retain the rest, spoke to Front Office Sports about the revival in recent interviews. “It was only because of Tracy McGrady that I’m doing this,” Vaccaro told FOS. “He had the most impact on what I did because he was probably the biggest underdog. He

item.title: “Looking at the landscape of summer basketball I felt …
item.description: “Looking at the landscape of summer basketball I felt like we were getting away from traditionally how camps need to be ran,” McGrady told FOS. “You had to earn your right to be at ABCD. And nowadays it’s a lot of relationship-based, a lot is just handed to these kids instead of making them work…This property is missed from what is going on in summer AAU basketball. Had some conversations with Sonny, he agreed to that and I was just like, ‘let me take it over.’”

item.title: McGrady could have a new home as an analyst next …
item.description: McGrady could have a new home as an analyst next season. He told FOS that his deal with NBC was only for one year and it’s currently unknown if he will return to the network next season. McGrady was part of NBC’s pre and post-game team with Maria Taylor, Vince Carter and Carmelo Anthony, which drew mixed reviews from critics. Before joining NBC, McGrady worked as an analyst for ESPN. NBC did not immediately respond to a request for comment. “I give ourselves a B+,” McGrady said of the group’s pe

item.title: Hawks pick up Mouhamed Gueye's team option for 2026-27
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Atlanta Hawks have exercised their fourth-year, $2.41 million team option on Mouhamed Gueye for the 2026-27 season, league sources told @hoopshype. Gueye played in a career-high 77 games for Atlanta, averaging 4.4 points and 3.6 rebounds in 15.3 minutes per game last season.

item.title: Malik Reneau has signed a deal with the Orlando Magic,
item.description: Vince Wolfram: Miami’s Malik Reneau has signed a deal with the Orlando Magic, per ADS Sports Management. The 2025–26 All-ACC First Team selection, who led the Hurricanes in scoring at 18.9 PPG last season, is headed to Orlando.

item.title: Shawn Phillips Jr. has signed a Summer League deal …
item.description: Dushawn London: Missouri big man Shawn Phillips Jr. has signed a Summer League deal with the New Orleans Pelicans, source tells @247Sports

item.title: Ja’Kobi Gillespie says he’s already envisioned feeding …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Undersized guard Ja’Kobi Gillespie says he’s already envisioned feeding Victor Wembanyama for lob dunks: “Yeah, for sure. Just throw it up there. That’s what it looks like.”

item.title: ESPN and ABC’s presentation of the first round of the …
item.description: ESPN and ABC’s presentation of the first round of the 2026 NBA Draft on Tuesday, June 23, averaged 4.2 million viewers, up 10% year over year. The audience peaked with 5.5 million viewers during the 8:15 p.m. ET quarter hour.

item.title: Kylan Boswell agrees to a two-way contract with Charlotte
item.description: Illinois Men’s Basketball: From Champaign to Charlotte. Kylan Boswell signs a two-way contract with the Hornets.

item.title: Zach Cleveland has signed an Exhibit 10 contract with …
item.description: Liberty Men’s Basketball: Zach Cleveland has signed an Exhibit 10 contract with the @Memphis Grizzlies!

item.title: Anthony Dell’Orso will play for the Sacramento Kings …
item.description: Olgun Uluc: Anthony Dell’Orso will play for the Sacramento Kings in the 2026 NBA Summer League, sources told ESPN. The 6'6 wing out of Melbourne averaged 8.5 ppg, 2.0 rpg, and 1.8 apg as a senior at Arizona.

item.title: Jayden Quaintance said Spurs continue to stress “their …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Jayden Quaintance said Spurs continue to stress “their priority is to make me 100 percent healthy.” He also said he doesn’t know yet whether he’ll be able to participate in Summer League.

item.title: Tarris Reed Jr. called losing the national title …
item.description: Tom Orsborn: Tarris Reed Jr. called losing the national title “probably the toughest moment of my life” and that both he and his new teammates will have chips on their shoulders next season. “I feel like that's going to be huge for us next year,” he said.

item.title: Hawks, Buddy Hield agree to push back his guarantee deadline
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: Atlanta Hawks and sharpshooter Buddy Hield have agreed to push back the guaranteed deadline date on his contract so both sides have more time to flush out options. Of his $9.7M salary, $3M is fully guaranteed. Deadline was today.

item.title: Robert McCray V has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Florida State’s Robert McCray V has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract with the Los Angeles Lakers, sources tell DraftExpress. The 6’4” guard led the Seminoles in scoring and assists en route to All-ACC honors. Super bouncy athlete with a 43” max vert.

item.title: Cam Boozer said he chose number 27 because his mom …
item.description: Damichael Cole: Cam Boozer said he chose number 27 because his mom wore it in college when she played college volleyball

item.title: GG Jackson is here at the introductory press …
item.description: Drew Hill: GG Jackson is here at the introductory press conference for the newest Grizzlies.

item.title: Dusty May left Michigan after two seasons and a …
item.description: Dusty May left Michigan after two seasons and a national championship for the NBA, in part, because of the uncertainties of NIL support and the transfer portal, Michigan president Domenico Grasso said Thursday at the university’s Regents meeting. Grasso addressed the Board of Regents and those gathered at the meeting after budgets were presented by various departments.

item.title: Coach May told me that among his reasons for leaving …
item.description: “Moving to the NBA is an opportunity for Dusty to further elevate his career. We wish him all best,” Grasso said. “His departure, however, is a bellwether of college athletics. Our current system is in dire need of clarity and equitable reform. Coach May told me that among his reasons for leaving were uncertainties and pressures involving the transfer portal and NIL support for student-athletes. He and I agree that the future of college sports is headed in the wrong direction.”

item.title: Pelicans exercised 2026-27 team option on Karlo Matkovic
item.description: Michael Scotto: The New Orleans Pelicans have exercised their third-year, $2.30 million team option for the 2026-27 season on forward Karlo Matković. He averaged 5.7 points on 60.4% shooting from the field and 42.2% from 3-point range with 3.7 rebounds in 62 games played last season.

item.title: Chucky Hepburn has made good progress in his recovery …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Chucky Hepburn has made good progress in his recovery from season-ending knee surgery & is somebody the Raptors are excited to have back. Hepburn had a strong camp/preseason last year & would've seen some run when Immanuel Quickley got hurt late in the season. Another tough, defensive-minded PG that can play

item.title: Cameron Matthews will play for the Charlotte Hornets …
item.description: Owen O’Connor: Source: Cameron Matthews will play for the Charlotte Hornets at NBA Summer League in Las Vegas. Matthews is also strongly in the conversation for one of Charlotte’s two-way contracts as an elite versatile defender, according to a league source.

item.title: Tyon Grant-Foster will join the San Antonio Spurs for …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Gonzaga’s Tyon Grant-Foster will join the San Antonio Spurs for NBA Summer League, sources tell DraftExpress. The rangy, defensively disruptive 6’7” swingman was recently green lit by the NBA’s fitness-to-play panel. Finally cleared to pursue his NBA dream.

item.title: St. Bernard Parish will hold a parade today to …
item.description: St. Bernard Parish will hold a parade today to celebrate NBA Champion Mitchell Robinson, a Chalmette High School graduate. The parade begins at 6:30 p.m. and will travel eastbound on Judge Perez Drive from Old Hickory Avenue to Palmisano Boulevard, ending near Chalmette High School. The route is approximately 1.2 miles.

item.title: Big man Mark Williams returns to Phoenix on a three-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Restricted free agent Mark Williams intends to sign a three-year, $38 million contract to return to the Phoenix Suns, sources tell ESPN. The deal is fully guaranteed for Williams, who averaged 11 points, 8 rebounds and 1 block in 23.6 minutes per game as the Suns starting center.

item.title: First young guards Collin Gillespie and Jordan Goodwin …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Suns lock in the last of their own priority free agents, finishing negotiations with Williams' Excel Sports Management agents Jeff Schwartz and Jordan Gertler on Thursday night. First young guards Collin Gillespie and Jordan Goodwin over the weekend, now Williams.

item.title: Prior to the agreement, Phoenix was $5.4M above apron …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Prior to the agreement, Phoenix was $5.4M above apron 1 and $18.4M below the second apron.

item.title: Kevin Porter exercises his player option to remain in Milwaukee
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: Milwaukee Bucks guard Kevin Porter Jr. is exercising his $5.39 million player option for the 2026-27 season. The 25-year-old guard will remain a key piece for the Bucks in the post Giannis Antetokounmpo era.

item.title: Mitchell Robinson unlikely to re-sign with Knicks
item.description: Stefan Bondy: Heard it’s unlikely Mitchell Robinson will be back with Knicks next season. He’s longest tenured Knick but also an unrestricted free agent. Owner James Dolan said he’s unwilling to pay into second apron so running it back with bench is not feasible.

item.title: Jacari White has signed a Summer League deal with the …
item.description: Dushawn London: Virginia guard Jacari White has signed a Summer League deal with the Los Angeles Lakers, source tells @247Sports

item.title: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar puts four of his NBA championship rings up for auction
item.description: Hall of Fame center Kareem Abdul-Jabbar has put four of his NBA championship rings up for auction. Abdul-Jabbar has hundreds of items from his memorabilia collection available in the auction, being conducted by Goldin Auctions, with "much of the proceeds" going to his Skyhook Foundation charity to help benefit kids learn about science, technology, engineering and math. Besides the 1980, 1985, 1987 and 1988 championship rings he earned as a member of the Los Angeles Lakers, Abdul-Jabbar is auctio

item.title: "Looking back on what I have done with my life, …
item.description: When it comes to choosing between storing a championship ring or trophy in a room, or providing kids with an opportunity to change their lives, the choice is pretty simple. Sell it all," Abdul-Jabbar wrote on his blog about why he's auctioning off valuables from his sports history. "Looking back on what I have done with my life, instead of gazing at the sparkle of jewels or gold plating celebrating something I did a long time ago, I'd rather look into the delighted face of a child holding their

item.title: "We do this by sending children from economically …
item.description: Some of the championship rings start with a minimum bid of $60,000. Also available on the website are game-worn jerseys, uniforms, trophies and even various keys to cities that he received. "Since my life is still happening and ever-evolving, I am less personally attached to those items than I am to my desire to create new history for myself -- and futures for others," Abdul-Jabbar said. "Much of the proceeds from my auction will go to support my charity, the Skyhook Foundation, whose mission is

item.title: A car belonging to the Minnesota Timberwolves' Jaden …
item.description: A car belonging to the Minnesota Timberwolves' Jaden McDaniels was stolen at the 25-year-old's Los Angeles home, the LAPD told NBC4 Investigates. McDaniels walked outside his residence Wednesday morning to find his 2022 Dodge Charger missing from his parking space. McDaniels, whose team was at the center of NBA trade talk Thursday morning, visited a LAPD station, where officers took at stolen car report. Law enforcement sources told NBC4 Investigates they do not believe the former Washington Hus

item.title: Hornets plan to keep Naz Reid
item.description: According to ESPN's Shams Charania, the Hornets plan to keep Reid on their roster despite already receiving trade inquiries for the versatile big man. "They already got some trade calls in on [Naz Reid], but the plan is right now to keep him. This is a team that could still shift the roster around a little bit if they want," Charania reported on ESPN's SportsCenter.

item.title: The player in question is Jeremiah Robinson-Earl, a …
item.description: Aris has already confirmed several important additions, including Nenad Dimitrijevic, E. J. Liddell, and Khem Birch, and according to multiple Greek media reports, the club has now targeted a new reinforcement from the United States. The player in question is Jeremiah Robinson-Earl, a member of the Dallas Mavericks, who spent most of the season in the G League playing for the Texas Legends.

item.title: Dan Gilbert on reuniting with LeBron James: 'If you're both mutually on the same mission, it can work out'
item.description: Interviewer: How did two such strong personalities as Dan Gilbert—owner of the Cleveland Cavs, mega-billionaire, business owner, operator—and LeBron James have that kind of falling out and then make it work again? Dan Gilbert: Well, I flew to Miami, where he was with two or three of his advisers, and we had a couple-hour meeting. I think, at the end of the day, it was in both of our interests to do this because he wanted to come back. He wanted to come back home, and we’re the only place he coul

item.title: Dan Gilbert admits his infamous letter after LeBron James' departure was 'a stupid thing'
item.description: Did you prep for going into that meeting? Did you have a sense of what you were going to say, or did you just play it? Dan Gilbert: I’m sure I prepped it a little bit. Interviewer: Did you apologize early on, or before the meeting? Dan Gilbert: About the letter? Interviewer: Yeah. Dan Gilbert: Yeah. I mean, I admitted that the letter was a stupid thing. And for me personally, at that time, if you Googled my name—I mean, I think I’ve done some other stuff in my career—and that would be the only t

item.title: "This season I had this year was probably one of the …
item.description: After signing a two-year deal with New York last offseason, Yabusele struggled to carve out a consistent role with the eventual NBA champions before later being traded to the Chicago Bulls. On his new YouTube series Summer With the Bear, Yabusele opened up about the frustration of the season while making it clear he views the experience as motivation to come back stronger. "This season I had this year was probably one of the toughest seasons I've had because things didn't really go the way I hop

item.title: "I got a call from my agent telling me first that we …
item.description: His path could have looked very different. Before choosing the Knicks, Yabusele said he had a similar offer from the Denver Nuggets following his stint with the Philadelphia 76ers. "I got a call from my agent telling me first that we had an offer from Denver and that it could be cool. It was a two-year deal, so I started thinking about that option. Right after that, my agent calls me and says, 'I just got off the phone with New York, and New York is also interested in pretty much the same deal,'

item.title: Butler expressed gratitude after being asked about his …
item.description: Butler expressed gratitude after being asked about his thoughts on the Golden State franchise after playing with different teams. “It’s not good, it’s great. It legitimately is the best. You don’t realize it until you’ve been in other places, and I’m not talking down on anybody that are quite lesser than… You’re talking about the way that y’all run everything, the people that you have around, obviously the guys that get to rep the uniform. They just do everything the right way, the winning way,

item.title: Dan Gilbert on fellow NBA owners: "I like the majority …
item.description: Dan Gilbert on fellow NBA owners: "I like the majority of them. There really are very few that I would say that I don't like. Most of them are pretty good guys, nice people."

item.title: He too, he used to pick on Mark Cuban, he used to pick …
item.description: Dan Gilbert: "David Stern was a phenomenal commissioner for 30 years. He built that league. But he too, he used to pick on Mark Cuban, he used to pick on me, he picked on the new guys."

item.title: Dan Gilbert on Adam Silver: "I give him an A-minus. I …
item.description: Dan Gilbert on Adam Silver: "I give him an A-minus. I think he's really good. You always got to have something to go for."

item.title: Dan Gilbert: Yeah. And it was stupid overall. If I had …
item.description: Interviewer: You wrote a very strongly worded letter. Dan Gilbert: Yes. Interviewer: I’m not going to make you read it. Dan Gilbert: No. Interviewer: But it talked about betrayal. Dan Gilbert: Well, what happened was, remember, he went on national television and did a show about where he was going to go play next. Interviewer: Yeah. Is that where you found out, though? Dan Gilbert: Yes. Interviewer: You found out when he announced it? Dan Gilbert: Yes. They never told us in advance. Interviewer:

item.title: So two people read it. Were you counseled not to put …
item.description: Interviewer: You gave him some heat. What were you thinking? Was that you doing it? Did some intern draft that letter? Dan Gilbert: I actually—you should have seen the first version. No, I’m kidding. Interviewer: Wait, did you have other people read this letter? Dan Gilbert: Yeah, I had one or two people, but they didn’t tell me what they should have told me. Interviewer: Who were the one or two people? Your brother? Dan Gilbert: No, one of the PR people. And I think maybe my ex-wife read it. In

item.title: Dan Gilbert reveals Comic Sans in his LeBron James letter was a joke by a team member weeks earlier
item.description: Well, the whole conversation around it was also around the font choice of the letter. Dan Gilbert: That was another thing. Interviewer: Comic Sans. Dan Gilbert: What was weird is that somebody on my team, just as a joke, I think the week or two before, changed my font to Comic Sans. Interviewer: What was your original font going to be? Dan Gilbert: Probably Times New Roman or something. So it wasn’t changed just for that letter. It was changed weeks ahead of that. Interviewer: So everything was

item.title: How’s your relationship now with LeBron? You guys …
item.description: How’s your relationship now with LeBron? You guys still— Dan Gilbert: I think it’s fine. We don’t see each other that much. He works in California. I work in Detroit and Cleveland. Interviewer: Yeah. Dan Gilbert: He came to a game or two last year. Interviewer: Was it hard for you to watch? In the game before the Eastern Conference Finals, it was— Dan Gilbert: Game seven. Interviewer: It was game seven, and it was the Cleveland Cavaliers— Dan Gilbert: And the Detroit Pistons. Interviewer: And yo

item.title: Scottie Pippen, NBA champion Dwyane Wade and actress …
item.description: The sports world wasn’t about to miss the party either. Los Angeles Rams stars Matthew Stafford and Puka Nacua made the trip to support the Americans, while Los Angeles Angels shortstop Zach Neto and Dodgers pitchers Blake Snell and Tyler Glasnow swapped the baseball diamond for the world’s biggest soccer stage. Basketball Hall of Famer Scottie Pippen, NBA champion Dwyane Wade and actress Gabrielle Union were also among those soaking in the World Cup atmosphere.

item.title: (Jaden McDaniels, who rival teams have sternly been …
item.description: (Jaden McDaniels, who rival teams have sternly been sent in the opposite direction when inquiring, sources say, is considered part of Minnesota's new big three.)

item.title: "I've heard Sandro Mamukelashvili from Toronto as …
item.description: "I've heard Sandro Mamukelashvili from Toronto as potentially an option there (for Minnesota). Now, I think that's going to depend on what his free agent market place ends up being."

item.title: White, who was critical in Charlotte's overtime win …
item.description: White, who was critical in Charlotte's overtime win against Miami and played just 17 minutes in a 121-90 blowout elimination loss to Orlando, had also communicated a strong desire ahead of free agency to be a starter in the league whether in Charlotte or elsewhere, sources say.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa on his NBA player comparisons: "Some …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa on his NBA player comparisons: "Some people say younger Paul George. I have heard a bigger Shai, I have heard a bigger Jalen Brunson. But the most accurate one I have ever got was Tracy McGrady. I think he was just super uber-talented, being 6'9, jumping over people to shoot, being a windmill, and being super athletic. I think that is kind of me."

item.title: "I’ve interviewed for so many jobs, I think it was a …
item.description: For Nori, this is the realization of a dream almost three decades in the making as a lifer assistant coach with five different organizations across 28 years in the NBA, most recently with the Minnesota Timberwolves for the last five seasons. He's interviewed for numerous head coaching jobs in that time, including the Lakers and Cavaliers openings in 2024 and the Knicks job that went to Mike Brown last summer, perpetually finishing second or third in those searches. "I’ve interviewed for so many

item.title: State Warriors sign richest sponsorship deal in North American team sports history
item.description: Kurt Badenhausen: NEWS: The Warriors signed a new jersey patch deal with AI cloud firm Iren. The multi-year agreement is the richest sponsorship in the history of North American team sports at an average of more than $50 million per year.

item.title: Hornets in the mix for Jaylen Brown?
item.description: Mike Lacett: I’m hearing the @hornets are one of these teams

item.title: “He’s going to get traded,” the analyst said on ESPN’s …
item.description: According to Brian Windhorst, Boston is actively discussing Brown with multiple teams and expects those conversations to continue as the organization considers reshaping its roster. “He’s going to get traded,” the analyst said on ESPN’s Unsportsmanlike. Windhorst also noted that while Celtics president Brad Stevens praised Brown during his recent media availability, he never ruled out the possibility of a trade. “He obviously told him that there was a good chance they’re going to trade him,” Win

item.title: I think that the Celtics were asking more for Brown …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: "They were in on Jaylen Brown as well. It was made clear by the Celtics that he was going to be available and the Wolves were trying to have conversations about what it would take to get Jaylen Brown. I think that they looked at those two, Jaylen Brown and LaMelo Ball, and Brown, just because he's won a championship, he's a little older, he was top-six MVP this year, I think that the Celtics were asking more for Brown than what the Hornets wanted for Ball."

item.title: Lakers have not made LeBron James an offer yet and communication has been limited
item.description: The Lakers can create up to $50 million in space and James may be willing to be flexible on his contract depending on how the team uses the space to upgrade its roster as Luka Doncic begins his second full season with the team. "There was a check-in call made, to my understanding, early in free agency once teams were able to start negotiating with their own free agents after the end of the NBA Finals," said ESPN's Shams Charania on NBA Today. "Since then though there hasn't been much communicati

item.title: “Right now, the Cavs can only offer LeBron four …
item.description: Windhorst discussed James’ rumored reunion with the Cleveland Cavaliers on “The Pat McAfee Show,” and said he believes it’s highly unlikely to occur. “Right now, the Cavs can only offer LeBron four million bucks,” Windhorst said. “One of the things I’ve learned about LeBron is you never count his money and never spend his money. So, making assumptions that LeBron would take a lot less money, I see people making that assumption, and I just don’t think it’s smart.”

item.title: The only real way that I can see that the Cavs could …
item.description: Windhorst added, “He is still a max-level player. … The only real way that I can see that the Cavs could get LeBron the money would be to do a trade with Jarrett Allen. … But I don’t think that would improve the Cavs. … I don’t see the pathway.”

item.title: It's a mutual thing where his agent Rich Paul and the …
item.description: Sam Amick: LaVine, I don't have clarity on where he might have some promise, but as we've talked about before, it's he's not leaving that money on the table. So if he can't find a situation, again it's a mutual thing where his agent Rich Paul and the Kings are working together trying to look at this. If he can't find somewhere to do, one concept was just a sign and trade for a lower number where Zach could get his next spot, his next chapter, but the Kings would need to like what was coming back

item.title: LaVar Ball to Minnesota: 'You got to get the other two Ball boys'
item.description: LaVar Ball: "Minnesota Timberwolves, y'all did the coldest move in the game. You went and got that Melo. Yes, but here is the thing, let me give you the recipe. You got to get the other two Ball boys. You get the other two Ball boys, it will be the most watched team ever in the NBA. Trust me when I tell you. You don't think I'm just talking? No, I'm not."

item.title: Lakers adding Robbie Avila
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Saint Louis’ Robbie Avila has agreed to an Exhibit-10 contract with the Los Angeles Lakers, sources tell DraftExpress. The A10 Player of the Year is one of the nation’s most skilled bigs. Shot 40% from three and averaged 4+ assists per game as a frontcourt playmaking hub.

item.title: The Heat has added 7-1 Arnas Sakenis to its summer …
item.description: Barry Jackson: The Heat has added 7-1 Arnas Sakenis to its summer league program. Was 11th in the nation in blocked shots per game (2.34) for Southern Illinois University Edwardsville.

item.title: Timberwolves explored James Harden, Ja Morant as point guard options
item.description: At the same time, teams like Minnesota and Toronto — franchises that had been extremely aggressive in pursuing upgrades — found themselves with an opportunity to improve their fortunes. The Wolves, after sending Julius Randle to Brooklyn, were left with a $33 million trade exception and a cap book $66 million leaner, while continuing to signal intentions to add a quality point guard, sources say (a pursuit that at one time included Cleveland's James Harden and Memphis' Ja Morant). The Raptors, w

item.title: The Raptors were another rumored suitor for the point …
item.description: Ball, 24, is under contract for the next three seasons and is set to make $40.8 million next year as a running mate for Anthony Edwards in Minnesota. The Raptors were another rumored suitor for the point guard and league sources confirmed to MassLive that the Celtics did not pursue a deal for the former Hornets guard.

item.title: Tim MacMahon: "I texted several general managers a …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: "I texted several general managers a simple question, what do you think of the LaMelo deal? And one replied one sentence, Tim is a riverboat gambler. And this is big-time, high-stakes gambling that he's doing right here. And you understand the swing that he's taking, you understand the hole in the roster it's creating. Chris Finch is also one of the most creative coaches in the league and he's going to earn his money figuring out how to make this work."

item.title: Jon Krawczynski: "I think Tim Connelly and Chris Finch …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: "I think Tim Connelly and Chris Finch have long believed that LaMelo Ball is a perfect fit for Anthony Edwards in that backcourt. And I know that over the last several years, they have checked in on LaMelo and Charlotte a couple of times. Each time they were turned away, and so something has happened in the last couple of days or weeks to get the Charlotte Hornets to a point where they were comfortable parting with LaMelo Ball."

item.title: Charlotte also quickly resumed negotiations with …
item.description: Charlotte also quickly resumed negotiations with White, who sources say initially turned down an offer following the Finals in search of a starting role, now with definite clarity on his potential future under Lee. (White's three-year, $74 million deal, as Yahoo reported earlier, is fully guaranteed with no player or team options.)

item.title: The Mark Williams three-year contract is very …
item.description: John Gambadoro: A couple of things here. The Mark Williams three-year contract is very tradeable especially if he plays well. He will start and when Maluach is ready whenever that may be Mark can either be a backup or be traded. Second, I understand many fans wanted a big move not to run it back with a team that got swept in the first-round by OKC. What I would say is that they are going to rely on a second year of Jordan Ott and his system, health of Jalen Green and Grayson Allen, development o

item.title: Austin Reaves is already No. 1 in guaranteed career …
item.description: Jorge Sierra: Austin Reaves is already No. 1 in guaranteed career earnings among undrafted players.

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: 'I am definitely grateful that the Wizards picked up Trae and picked up AD'
item.description: AJ Dybantsa: "I am definitely grateful that the Wizards picked up Trae and picked up AD. I am going to have vets, I am going to have a guy that wants a championship, I am going to have a guy that is a multiple-time All-Star that I can ask questions to."

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: "I seen him on TikTok, there was a dude …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa: "I seen him on TikTok, there was a dude that put me better than Michael Jordan. I believe Michael Jordan is the GOAT, so I decided to watch the video and see he was 6'6, 220 with a 40-inch vertical, and I was like that kind of sounds like me. So I decided to watch some film, I seen him get into his mid-range a lot and I take pride in my mid-range, so I kind of seen the comparison a little bit."

item.title: "As a first time head coach, you very rarely get an …
item.description: "As a first time head coach, you very rarely get an opportunity to coach a roster that has already been in the playoffs," Nori said. "Usually, you’re taking over bad situations and tough situations where you’re going to have some bumps and bruises. Very fortunate to have the roster that we have moving forward."

item.title: Richard Jefferson on thhe group chat dynamics of the …
item.description: Richard Jefferson on thhe group chat dynamics of the 2016 Cavs: "When we talk about the group chat from the team, there's like six of us, and it's been the same six. There was the full team group chat, then there was the guys that were J.R., Swish, Tristan, Kevin, Channing, and Bron. So that's the group of us. And then we start doing, hey guys, we should get together, do some golf and go do this, and then a conversation kind of got started is, and like, hey, it would be wrong if we didn't invite

item.title: The Cleveland Cavaliers are nearing a deal to sell a …
item.description: The Cleveland Cavaliers are nearing a deal to sell a 5% to 10% stake in the NBA franchise to private equity firm Blue Owl. The deal values the NBA team and Cleveland WNBA team set to start play in 2028 at a combined $5.5 billion, according to someone familiar with the agreement. Blue Owl’s sports strategy fund buys limited partner stakes in NBA franchises, a fund originally named Dyal HomeCourt. The fund already has stakes in the Atlanta Hawks, Charlotte Hornets, Minnesota Timberwolves and Sacra

item.title: Dan Gilbert hired Allen & Company to explore selling …
item.description: Last year, Cavaliers owner Dan Gilbert hired Allen & Company to explore selling up to 15% of his NBA franchise. Gilbert will retain his majority stake in the team he bought in 2005 for $375 million, which was a record at the time for a standalone NBA franchise. In December, Sportico valued the Cavs at $4.86 billion in its NBA team valuations, which ranks 16th in the league.

item.title: ESPN and ABC's broadcast of the 2026 NBA Draft first …
item.description: ESPN and ABC's broadcast of the 2026 NBA Draft first round averaged 4.2 million viewers Tuesday night, a 10% increase from the prior year, the network announced. The audience peaked at 5.5 million viewers during the 8:15 p.m. ET quarter hour. AJ Dybantsa went first overall to the Washington Wizards, followed by Darryn Peterson to the Utah Jazz at No. 2, Cameron Boozer to the Memphis Grizzlies at No. 3, Caleb Wilson to the Chicago Bulls at No. 4 and Keaton Wagler to the Los Angeles Clippers at No

item.title: Thunder trade Isaiah Joe to Detroit
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: The Oklahoma City Thunder are trading sharpshooter Isaiah Joe to the Detroit Pistons for two future second-round picks, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: So my understanding is there hasn't been an offer out …
item.description: Shams Charania: So my understanding is there hasn't been an offer out to LeBron James yet as far as his free agency. We'll see how that develops over the next four or five days when free agency starts. But a lot of it is like how much can we upgrade the roster and then potentially go back to LeBron or do you take care of LeBron first and then figure out any type of potential roster move?

item.title: However, the Wolves can no longer function as a …
item.description: This trade, which can’t be official until July 6 since the Bulls required cap space to acquire Nic Claxton, will be expanded to include the Wolves’ acquisition of Ball for several reasons. The first is that Reid’s $23.3 million salary isn’t enough to match Ball’s $41.25 million individually. Randle’s $33.3 million salary is ($9,098,000 trade exception). Then they could simply trade Reid for Green and create a trade exception worth the difference in their salaries. However, the Wolves can no long

item.title: The once supersized Wolves are now looking very thin …
item.description: The once supersized Wolves are now looking very thin in the frontline. They will have a difficult time finding an upgrade with no draft picks left to trade. They might need to part with one of Terrence Shannon Jr. or Joan Beringer to get a team interested. Otherwise, they’re looking at starting a lineup featuring LaMelo Ball, Ayo Dosunmu, Anthony Edwards, Jaden McDaniels, and Rudy Gobert. The Wolves would have limited options for upgrading the frontcourt. They will no longer create a $33 million

item.title: Celtics seeking at least four first-round picks for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Shams Charania on Jaylen Brown: My understanding is in some cases the Celtics have asked teams for at least four first-round picks. The part that makes it tricky here is Jaylen Brown's got three years left on his deal at a super max clip. Which team meets that valuation so far that the Celtics have set in some regard with four first-round picks? And I think throughout all of this, the question is going to be which side will influence the urgency of a trade? Because if he's got three years left,

item.title: Kawhi Leonard would not sign extension if traded to Pistons
item.description: The Kawhi Leonard story featured above revealed Toronto's interest in exploring trades to try to reacquire the best player they've ever had to team with Scottie Barnes … with sources saying that Leonard would indeed have the Raptors on his short list of teams that he's open to signing a contract extension with in the event that his seven-season relationship with the Clippers came to an end. One of the most notable details from that reporting: Leonard would not be amenable to extending his contra

item.title: Nets interested in Mitchell Robinson
item.description: The Stein Line has learned that the Nets have legitimate interest in trying to sign big man Mitchell Robinson away from the team across the East River that just won its first NBA championship in more than 50 years.

item.title: Lakers an option for Mitchell Robinson?
item.description: The Lakers, amid a growing pessimism that the Knicks will be able to hang onto Mitchell Robinson, are also regarded as a likely suitor for the New York big man once free agency officially begins.

item.title: Nikola Vucevic: I want to keep playing in the NBA
item.description: Nikola Vucevic: ‘My focus is truly on the NBA; my desire is to stay there. As I’ve said before, it is the best league. I’ve been there for 15 years and I would like to stay there until the end; I believe I still have a place there and the quality to play at a high level. Regarding Red Star (Zvezda), I’ve said before that if I ever decide to return to Europe, Zvezda would be one of the favorites—everyone knows that. But for now, that really isn't the focus. I don't want to create unrealistic desi

item.title: Nikola Vucevic says adjusting to Celtics bench role was 'the hardest thing'
item.description: Nikola Vucevic on Celtics: ‘The hardest thing for me was that I had been a starter for such a long number of years and seasons, where I had my set minutes, thirty-plus minutes, a certain number of shots, and a defined role. When you play like that, you know what is expected of you and you are used to your own rhythm. Coming to Boston, I was prepared for the possibility that I would very likely be coming off the bench if a trade occurred. Regarding me personally, it wasn’t a problem to adapt from

item.title: Damjan Rudez on Andrew Wiggins: He doesn't have that high-energy drive that separates the superstars from the great players
item.description: Damjan Rudez: 'I cannot accurately describe how immense Andrew Wiggins' potential truly was. Having been on the court with him, I believe he had more raw potential than KAT, Zach LaVine, or 99% of the players I ever played with—even including guys like Paul George. There was a dunk he had against Atlanta where I swear I was looking at Michael Jordan. He would just rise and keep rising over three defenders. However, he didn't always have the 'motor'—that high-energy drive that separates the super

item.title: Damjan Rudez: 'Nobody had a nice word to say about Kobe Bryant as a teammate'
item.description: Damjan Rudez: 'Kobe Bryant had a magnetic aura that is hard to describe; he possessed a gravitational force that made everyone in his vicinity react differently. For my generation, Kobe was our Michael Jordan. I watched every move he made, from his 81-point game to all his records, and dreamt of playing against him. During his playing days, he was impenetrable; he didn't allow the public into his private life or show a soft side. He was a monster—a serious psychopath and a killer on the court wh

item.title: Damjan Rudez: Kevin Garnett 'destroyed' Anthony Bennett with relentless trash talk
item.description: Damjan Rudez: 'Kevin Garnett didn't just want to beat you; he wanted to destroy you and find that 1% edge by getting inside your head. He demanded that everyone be on his irrational level of intensity, or he had no use for you. I saw him do this to Anthony Bennett in a way that was hard to watch. It wasn't just 'trash talk'; it was a level of verbal aggression involving things you wouldn't even say to an enemy. But that was his power. He would admit that before a game, he would imagine the oppon

item.title: Nikola Vucevic blasts Europeans for calling the NBA 'just a show'
item.description: Nikola Vucevic: 'What irritates me is that people from Europe say the NBA is just a show. If people had watched Boston or Oklahoma City or San Antonio all year, they would realize that NBA teams play defense. It looks different because the players are so good and so powerful and talented that everything they do looks so easy and beautiful. It’s like watching a Formula 1 driver; they take corners and brake without a problem, but if you tried to do it, it wouldn't go so easily. In the NBA, they le

item.title: Jalen Duren eyeing sign-and-trade after being 'underwhelmed' by Pistons' offer
item.description: All-NBA forward Jalen Duren was underwhelmed by the Detroit Pistons’ initial offer in restricted free agency and is planning to explore sign-and-trade scenarios when they are permitted on Tuesday, league sources told The Athletic.

item.title: According to the sources, who spoke on condition of …
item.description: According to the sources, who spoke on condition of anonymity because the negotiation is ongoing, the gap between the sides is large enough that Duren is considering ways to land elsewhere, with the sign-and-trade route deemed preferable to signing an offer sheet with another team that the Pistons could, and likely would, match.

item.title: Dipping below the second apron would lead to much of …
item.description: The Knicks’ front office knows this. And yet, it also understands that dipping below the second apron would lead to much of its depth flocking elsewhere, which is why, despite Dolan’s desires, it wants to go over the second apron, according to league sources, who were granted anonymity to speak freely. But so far, Dolan has not changed his mind.

item.title: The Board confirmed the 20-team lineup for the 2026-27 …
item.description: The Board of Directors of Euroleague Commercial Assets (ECA) has approved a series of key decisions regarding the future of European basketball, including the participating teams for the 2026-27 season, the competition calendar, expansion plans and the ongoing transition to a franchise model. All decisions will be submitted for ratification at the ECA General Assembly on July 7. The Board confirmed the 20-team lineup for the 2026-27 EuroLeague season, with Paris Basketball and Beşiktaş Istanbu

item.title: Pistons conveying to teams that Jalen Duren will not be traded
item.description: Chris Haynes: Detroit Pistons are conveying that Jalen Duren will not be moved and are solely focused on coming to terms on an agreement to keep their All-Star center, league sources tell me.

item.title: Detroit will likely acquire Joe into the Isaiah …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Detroit will likely acquire Joe into the Isaiah Stewart $15M trade exception or signing exception. Pistons are operating as an over the cap team because of the Jalen Duren FA cap hold.

item.title: OKC will create 2 trade exceptions from the Wiggins …
item.description: Bobby Marks: OKC will create 2 trade exceptions from the Wiggins and Joe trade. Because they are will be created post July 1, both are available despite OKC a second apron team.

item.title: Celtics still engaging in trade discussions for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Ultimately, though the Celtics were willing to part with Brown, they weren't willing to also include young prospects Hugo Gonzalez and Baylor Scheierman to land Antetokounmpo, a decision that has put them in an awkward situation with Brown. Multiple sources say the team is still engaging in trade discussions for the five-time All-Star. Says one league source: "It'd be insane for Boston to bring him back after dangling him so publicly." Says another: "I wouldn't do a damn thing. Those two guys ar

item.title: Giannis Antetokounmpo was 'genuinely torn' between wanting to be traded or stay in Milwaukee
item.description: Giannis Antetokounmpo was genuinely torn, multiple sources close to him say. Between loyalty and love of a city that had embraced him as a skinny kid from Greece 13 years ago, and his desire to compete for more championships with a franchise that had exhausted all its resources to try to do that for him. Those contradictory forces had swirled for years.

item.title: Horst had canvassed the league for months, fielding …
item.description: Horst had canvassed the league for months, fielding offers and presenting them to his front office and ownership group, then getting feedback from Antetokounmpo's camp as to whether he'd be willing to extend his contract with those teams. At least four strong bids had died on the vine after Antetokounmpo rejected them, multiple sources with knowledge of them say.

item.title: But by then, the Bucks had their answer, multiple …
item.description: THE BUCKS WERE pressing up against the deadline co-owner Jimmy Haslam had set a month earlier. During a news conference to introduce new coach Taylor Jenkins and sitting alongside Horst, Haslam had made a declaration: The Bucks would have Antetokounmpo's future settled by draft night. Either he would agree to sign an extension to remain with Milwaukee -- or he'd be traded. But by then, the Bucks had their answer, multiple sources with knowledge of the situation say. Antetokounmpo was not going t

item.title: Several team sources voiced to ESPN a guiding ethos of …
item.description: For half a decade, the Bucks were one of the most successful franchises in the league, leading the NBA in winning percentage (.686) across the regular season and playoffs from 2018 to 2023. Several team sources voiced to ESPN a guiding ethos of the organization through the years: Teams don't willingly trade superstars, especially in a small market such as Milwaukee. It's why the Bucks had made every attempt, through multiple roster iterations, to build championship-level teams with Antetokounmpo

item.title: Antetokounmpo and his reps expressed to Bucks …
item.description: Later that summer, Antetokounmpo and his reps expressed to Bucks management their concerns over the roster, and asked the team to explore a trade to the New York Knicks. Talks stalled.

item.title: In Miami and Boston, however, sleep was hard to come …
item.description: HORST SLEPT WELL Sunday night and longer than he usually does, relieved to be on the other side of the most consequential decision of his professional life. In Miami and Boston, however, sleep was hard to come by. Both franchises believed they had a great chance of landing Antetokounmpo, according to team sources, but they were paralyzed until a final decision had been made.

item.title: Sparking a series of organizational discussions that …
item.description: The Celtics had lost in the first round to the Philadelphia 76ers, sparking a series of organizational discussions that led to the conclusion that the current roster simply "wasn't good enough," as one Celtics source put it.

item.title: Horst had always liked the concept of the Heat's deal
item.description: THROUGHOUT THE DAY on Monday, the Bucks had gone back and forth over the two offers. There were "spirited debates" and differences of opinion, as one Bucks source put it. But in the end, they preferred the optionality that Miami's package gave them in building something new. Horst had always liked the concept of the Heat's deal.

item.title: Last fall, when Antetokounmpo moved his family from …
item.description: Last fall, when Antetokounmpo moved his family from Milwaukee back to his native Greece, it seemed to be a signal that the emotional cord between the city and the man who had become a star in it had been severed. Antetokounmpo could never bring himself to publicly ask for a trade, but moving his family to Greece and enrolling his children in Greek schools seemed to be his way of doing so. The move didn't last long, though. Antetokounmpo's family was back in Milwaukee within a few months, multipl

item.title: "Looking back on it, the one thing we did that changed …
item.description: The second was the inclusion of Holiday in the trade that brought superstar Lillard from Portland. "Looking back on it, the one thing we did that changed everything was put Jrue in that trade," a Bucks source said. "Jrue was such a leader on and off the floor. He would do things like if Giannis was holding the ball too much, Jrue would just bring it up and play with everybody else without making it a thing. He was a pro at it. "But Jrue was in the deal [for Lillard] because we thought we needed

item.title: Celtics wanted Rudy Gobert, Naz Reid, first-round pick and pick swaps from Timberwolves for Jaylen Brown
item.description: The Timberwolves explored multiple avenues to add another star next to Edwards in the days leading up to landing Ball. There were discussions with the Chicago Bulls about Josh Giddey. They had talks with the Boston Celtics about Jaylen Brown, a team source said, but the Celtics were looking for a package including Gobert, Reid and similar pick compensation that went to Charlotte for Ball, team sources said. That was just too much for the Wolves to pay for a soon-to-be 30-year-old forward who, wh

item.title: Word had started to circulate on Wednesday night that …
item.description: Word had started to circulate on Wednesday night that the Hornets were seriously entertaining offers on Ball, and the Timberwolves wasted no time. The final offer for Ball was prepared and sent to owners Marc Lore and Alex Rodriguez for review and approval early Thursday morning, according to multiple team sources that spoke on the condition of anonymity because the trade has not been officially announced.

item.title: And immediately gave the green light, team sources …
item.description: Lore and Rodriguez looked at the offer — former Sixth Man of the Year and Timberwolves cult hero Naz Reid, an unprotected first-round pick in 2033 and three first-round pick swaps (2028, 2029, 2030) and three second-round picks (2029, 2032, 2033) to Charlotte for Ball and swingman Josh Green — and immediately gave the green light, team sources said.

item.title: “We needed a guy like LaMelo to make everybody …
item.description: Ball averaged 20.1 points, 7.1 assists and 4.8 rebounds last season. There are questions about his decision-making and the seriousness with which he takes the game, but the Wolves love his size, shot creation and playmaking. Maybe even more importantly, the Hornets were 11.6 points per 100 possessions better on offense with Ball on the floor last season, per Cleaning The Glass. Kon Knueppel, Charlotte’s terrific rookie, made 46 percent of his 3-pointers when Ball was on the court. That number di

item.title: Anthony Edwards has long admired LaMelo Ball's game
item.description: Ball’s ability to initiate offense, shoot 3-pointers at a high volume and see over the top of the defense with his 6-foot-7 frame to find open shooters is exactly what the Wolves were missing. Ball, who was the No. 3 pick in the same 2020 draft class that saw Edwards chosen No. 1, is also just 24. He fits with the timeline of a young core that is tip-toeing into its prime, and he arrives with the tacit approval of Edwards, who has long admired Ball’s game, sources said.

item.title: “He makes Ant better, Jaden (McDaniels) better, Rudy …
item.description: “He makes Ant better, Jaden (McDaniels) better, Rudy and Joan (Beringer) better,” one team source said. “How many other players in the league would make your three best players better without getting in their way?”

item.title: Reid was preparing to become the full-time starter at …
item.description: Just a few days earlier, Reid was preparing to become the full-time starter at power forward for the first time in his career when the Timberwolves traded Randle to Brooklyn. But the Hornets insisted on Reid being in the trade, sources said.

item.title: They do not have a traditional power forward on the …
item.description: They do not have a traditional power forward on the roster, and league sources say the Wolves are looking at several options for help at the four. They are also in the market to add more shooting around Ball and Edwards. The work continues, but the magnitude of the Ball trade means that the major deals are done now.

item.title: “I can see him [LeBron James] coming back. It’ll …
item.description: The Lakers made a check-in call to LeBron James early in free agency and haven’t followed up with an offer since. Now the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade has rallied him to suggest that LeBron James and his son, Bronny James, could make a shocking move to the Heat. item.title: “You could actually get Bronny some real minutes at PG …
item.description: Chalmers implied that Bronny would be part of this potential move. He went on to highlight how the infrastructure in Miami would serve as the perfect environment to nurture Bronny’s development. “You could actually get Bronny some real minutes at PG and keep developing him and let him learn from other guys, so I think it’s a great move.”

item.title: Miami to decline $2.4 million team option on Jahmir Young
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Miami Heat will decline the $2.41 million team option on Jahmir Young, sources told @hoopshype . Young made All-NBA G League Second Team, averaging 25.9 points, 8.5 assists, 4.9 rebounds, and 1.8 steals last season. He’s played 20 NBA games for the Bulls and Heat in two years.

I had some people say I didn't like the Rudy Gobert deal, and that worked out great for the Timberwolves. So, with those caveats, the almost consensus opinion of the people I talked to around the league was LaMelo Ball had next to no value a year ago. item.description: Justin Martinez: Aday Mara says he didn’t do a pre-draft workout with OKC: “I just did an interview at the Combine, and I think it went really, really well. That’s why it was surprising. My agents were telling me sometimes you do workouts and then you get drafted by a team you haven’t done anything with, so that’s what happened. But just these couple of days that I’ve been here have been great. I’ve been getting to know everyone and talking with everyone. Everyone is great, so I’m happy.”

item.title: VanVleet said he had so thoroughly convinced himself …
item.description: “I said, 'No, that ain’t mine. No. I didn’t (tear my ACL).' They’re like, 'You did, bruh. You tore your ACL,'” VanVleet said. “I said no, so I’m arguing. I did that for like three days.” VanVleet said he had so thoroughly convinced himself that he didn’t actually tear his ACL that when he was supposed to meet the doctor at the gym the next morning, he actually showed up at 2 a.m. to run and shoot and ended up spending the night at the facility before his doctor’s appointment. “I’m f— up, crying

item.title: Kenny Atkinson: When you're one of the final four teams in the NBA, I think you're doing something right
item.description: What are you most proud of this season? Kenny Atkinson: I think we were 17-16 at one point. It looked like the wheels were falling off. Just how we kept it together, with expectations being so high, and we started playing a lot of young guys. We got through that with great leadership, starting with Donovan Mitchell, then Evan Mobley, even before De'Andre Hunter came. Those guys kind of kept the ship afloat. As we got healthier, we really went on some good runs. Listen, success is relative, right

item.title: The thing that changed that equation was James …
item.description: Was the equation harder to figure out, or did it become easier once James Harden came into the fold? Kenny Atkinson: It's a great point, and I was worried about that. How do we get this thing going in two and a half months? The thing that changed that equation was James Harden's IQ and ability. And I always know James is a system, just being by himself. But it was more like he's played in so many different scenarios in the NBA and his career that he adapted. It's probably the fastest adaptation

item.title: "You can't predicate your game on scoring. You've got …

item.title: “I can’t even say that. I’m just waiting to see that …
item.description: Mike Curtis: Dereck Lively II was asked if there was any target date for his return: “I can’t even say that. I’m just waiting to see that my foot’s good. Even when it’s good, I gotta wait even more to make sure I don’t have to do this again…If I gotta take the things that the fans & the public gotta say about me, “I’m brittle. I’m all that.” Cool. When I come back, don’t forget who I am.”

item.title: Dereck Lively on return: 'Whenever I come back, don't forget who I am'
item.description: “I’m just waiting to see that my foot’s good,” Lively said. “Even when it’s good, I’ve got to wait even more to make sure I don’t have to deal with this again. I’m never going to rush back. I’m never going to worry about that.” He has heard the durability questions and waved them off. “If I’ve got to take what the fans or the public have to say about me, that I’m brittle or all that, cool,” Lively said. “But whenever I come back, don’t forget who I am.”

item.title: Luke Travers will play for the Milwaukee Bucks in the …

item.title: “Cam is used to being around winning programs, winning …
item.description: “Cam is used to being around winning programs, winning cultures, and he’s got winning DNA,” Scott Drew said. “He can score four points and be happy the team won. He can defend and rebound and pass and be happy. He can be on the bench and cheer on his teammates, so he cares about winning. And I know at the end of the day, a great organization like the Lakers, that’s what they care most about. It’s a great fit for him.”

item.title: Zaire Wade, the son of NBA Hall of Famer Dwayne Wade, …
item.description: Zaire Wade, the son of NBA Hall of Famer Dwayne Wade, was arrested on suspicion of domestic violence in Burbank, law enforcement sources told NBC4 Investigates. Burbank police were called to a home at about 5:30 a.m. Sunday after someone called 911 because a woman was screaming, according to the sources. When police arrived, they found former reality show star and former basketball player Zaire Wade and a female who had on her lacerations on her face and body, the sources said.

item.title: Isaiah Hartenstein getting a mutual option likely …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Isaiah Hartenstein getting a mutual option likely means the third year is a player option with a partial guarantee, like James Harden’s current contract. The ~$3M in payroll savings reduces the Thunder’s tax penalty by $21M and leaves them about $15M above the second apron.

item.title: Isaiah Hartenstein signs three-year contract to stay with Thunder
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: Oklahoma City's Isaiah Hartenstein intends to sign a new three-year contract to return to the Thunder through 2028-29 that brings his total earnings with the franchise at five years and $134 million guaranteed, sources tell ESPN. A key signing in 2024 leading to the 2025 championship, Thunder lock in the defensive anchor.

item.title: Plus a maximum 15% trade kicker and a unique mutual …
item.description: Shams Charania: Hartenstein and his reps, Andrew Morrison and Aaron Mintz of CAA Sports, worked with the Thunder on an agreement that brings $75 million in new money over the next three years -- plus a maximum 15% trade kicker and a unique mutual option that allows both parties to rework the agreement again in 2028 prior to the final season.

item.title: The EuroLeague has taken steps to protect itself …
item.description: The EuroLeague has taken steps to protect itself against the threat posed by the NBA's planned arrival in Europe for the 2027-28 season. This Friday, it approved an ambitious package of strategic measures that strengthen its medium- and long-term structure, including the renewal of Real Madrid 's license for ten years , the implementation of a permanent franchise system , and the expansion to 24 teams starting in 2027-28. The goal is to consolidate its position just as the NBA and FIBA ​​acceler

item.title: NBA coaching community irate at circumstances of Micah Nori's hiring
item.description: Tim MacMahon: "Everybody in the coaching community loves Micah Nori and respects Micah Nori and thinks that he is ready and deserving of a head coaching job. I talked to several coaches who were absolutely irate about the circumstances, specifically the structure of this contract. This is Tom Dundon's work. This is that Stanley Cup winning, Dallas-based billionaire who, in his brief time in the NBA, has been notoriously cheap, frugal, doesn't travel two-way guys for the playoffs, has had layoffs

item.title: I heard from a whole lot of coaches, a few were ready …
item.description: Tim MacMahon: "Micah Nori accepted a contract as a renter coach. It's a one plus one plus one contract. It's a three-year deal with team options for the second and third year. I heard from a whole lot of coaches, a few were ready to go on the record and backed out. Props to JB Bickerstaff, who is the president of the NBCA, the National Basketball Coaches Association. He felt like it was his duty as the president of that organization to take a firm stance on this. He felt like this contract was a

item.title: "The most annoying part was Delly and Bron waking up …
item.description: Richard Jefferson: "The most annoying part was Delly and Bron waking up to work out every day. That was the most annoying part. It's like, really, you're going to go work out?"

item.title: "When we saw Delly, we thought he grew two inches. …
item.description: Channing Frye: "When we saw Delly, we thought he grew two inches. This dude is built like a WWE wrestler. He is the most yoked I have ever seen him. He said a few years ago he completely changed his whole routine and how he is approaching the game. Delly did work out. He was up with us at night and he was up by himself in the morning. This dude is dedicated to his craft. We love seeing him. You got a glimpse of his personality when he lets loose, but he is always thinking about basketball."

item.title: The NBA is expanding its Launchpad startup program to …
item.description: The NBA is expanding its Launchpad startup program to Asia as part of a multiyear commitment to source new innovations from that continent. The announcement was made at the NBA x Citi Investor Summit 2026 in Singapore. Launchpad is currently supporting its fifth cohort of startups that engage in pilots with NBA properties. That program annually attracts hundreds of applicants for what’s typically five or six slots. To date, all the accepted companies have been headquartered in North America or E

item.title: Dwyane Wade's son has been arrested for felony …
item.description: Dwyane Wade's son has been arrested for felony domestic violence ... TMZ has learned. Zaire Wade -- the NBA Hall of Famer's eldest child -- was arrested and booked by the Burbank Police Department on Sunday. He was booked on three charges ... felony domestic violence, criminal threats, and false imprisonment and an emergency protective order has been issued. We've reached out to cops for more information.

item.title: Marcus Smart an option for Houston?
item.description: The Rockets do, however, loom as a possible free agent destination for another one of Udoka's former players in Boston: Marcus Smart. League sources say that Smart, who is expected to draw interest from Houston and possibly other teams in free agency, is very much giving consideration to declining his $5.4 million player option for next season with the Lakers before Monday's deadline to do so and then proceed to the open market.

item.title: One league source I spoke with said that the Clippers …
item.description: There are mixed signals around the NBA on the subject of his availability and the possibility of him being moved, with reports circulating that the two-time Finals MVP would be open to reuniting with his former teams, San Antonio and Toronto, though the Houston Rockets could also be part of that mix, per one source. One league source I spoke with said that the Clippers are absolutely not moving him, preferring to let the last year of his contract play out to preserve future flexibility, but stil

item.title: Blazers actively pursuing Jaylen Brown
item.description: There is one team leaguewide, sources say, most frequently described as actively pursuing Boston's Jaylen Brown. The Portland Trail Blazers. The Blazers have been known to covet Brown dating back to Damian Lillard's first tenure with the franchise. And they still do.

item.title: "Trust me, those guys asked LeBron what he was …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "Trust me, those guys asked LeBron what he was planning on doing and LeBron did not tell them," - @WindhorstESPN on LeBron James' future plans.

item.description: ESPN Cleveland: “It might even be more likely than not that he leaves Los Angeles than stays with the Lakers,” - @JakeLFischer on the latest rumblings around LeBron James 👀👀👀
