item.description: ESPN Cleveland: “They got soul issues. They got I don’t like you issues and I don’t want to be here with you issues,” - @channingfrye on why he doesn’t think LeBron should go back to the Cavs 😳😳😳

item.title: Lowe floated the possibility of the Clippers signing …
item.description: The Los Angeles Clippers have the capacity to open up some level of cap space this offseason if they decline team options on Brook Lopez, Bogdan Bogdanovic and Nicolas Batum. "I don't know if this has been decided yet, but someone who would know told me [Wednesday] that the Clippers are telling or behaving I guess as though they're going to have cap space in the offseason," said Zach Lowe. "They can't open much. They can open about $20 million but they have to cut some options and renounce some

item.title: The Blazers could also pursue a trade for Anthony …
item.description: Portland previously attempted to trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo this offseason, but he communicated that he didn't have interest in signing long-term with the franchise. The Blazers could also pursue a trade for Anthony Davis this offseason as the Washington Wizards consider their options. The Blazers could offer a combination of draft capital, Jerami Grant's expiring contract as salary ballast, and young players such as Scoot Henderson and Sharron Sharpe.

item.title: Myler Turner drawing trade interst
item.description: Sources say that the Bucks have indeed received some trade interest in veteran center Myles Turner in the wake of the Giannis Antetokounmpo blockbuster, but one league source asserted Friday night that Milwaukee is not actively engaged in moving him.

item.title: League sources confirmed the Post’s report to Front …
item.description: On Thursday, the New York Post reported it was unlikely the team would retain Mitchell Robinson, its longest-tenured player, who is an unrestricted free agent and perhaps the best backup center in the NBA. Robinson just finished the final year of a four-year, $60 million contract that started at $17 million annually and finished at roughly $13 million. League sources confirmed the Post’s report to Front Office Sports, and cited Robinson’s extensive injury history and free throw issues as reasons

item.title: “I think he's getting multiple years guaranteed at …
item.description: Internally, the Raptors have budgeted a deal in the $10-million-per-year range, a number that they can get to if they find a taker for the last year of Dick’s contract. That would be a considerable raise for Mamukelashvili, who is expected to decline the $2.8-million player option on his contract by Monday’s deadline. The problem is that bigs with his profile are highly valued, with multiple league sources expecting Mamukelashvili to have a robust market in free agency. “I think he's getting mul

item.title: The Nuggets have been signaling for months that they …
item.description: The same most certainly goes for Denver and Peyton Watson. The Nuggets have been signaling for months that they plan to match any offer sheet for Watson … to the point that sources say it would take an offer sheet in the $30 million range in order for Denver to balk at re-signing him. The Lakers and Bulls are two teams that have been mentioned as potential Watson suitors going back to February's trade deadline. Sources with knowledge of the situation have this week added that the Clippers are al

item.title: Pelicans to decline team option on Kevon Looney
item.description: Chris Haynes: New Orleans Pelicans are expected to decline the $8M team option on veteran center Kevon Looney, making the 3-time NBA champ an unrestricted free agent, league sources tell me.

item.title: Boyd is off to The Bay ✍️ Congrats Nick on joining the …
item.description: Wisconsin Basketball: Boyd is off to The Bay ✍️ Congrats Nick on joining the @warriors !

item.title: Lonnie Walker IV eyeing NBA comeback
item.description: Marc Stein: Lonnie Walker IV is a candidate to return to the NBA this summer, league sources say, after the scoring guard played in the EuroLeague last season. The seven-year NBA vet (10.0 PPG) has an NBA out in his Maccabi Tel Aviv contract until July 15 and is expected to draw interest.

item.title: Wilson does not run on resentment, but he’ll take any …
item.description: The implication of Caleb Wilson’s widely anticipated availability at the Bulls’ selection is that no matter the shuffle of the picks preceding him, Wilson was always staring upward at AJ Dybantsa, Darryn Peterson and Cameron Boozer. That, in a draft of four-star bets, Wilson was always fourth. Wilson does not run on resentment, but he’ll take any chance he gets to make a statement. “I mean, I played all of them, though,” Wilson said. “You know what happened when I played them, so it doesn’t real

item.title: “I know it’s a different game, and I know development …
item.description: The expected dose of naïveté is absent, though. He’s measured and thoughtful. He’s thought through the pathways toward his goals. He understands the process necessary for a rookie about to undergo the throes of a rebuild: the monotony, the midseason walls. “I know it’s a different game, and I know development isn’t linear, but I got everything I need to be great, honestly,” Wilson said. “I’m extremely athletic. I have a game that translates to the NBA well. Everything that I need to work on is

item.title: Alex Antetokounmpo Greece bound
item.description: Alex Antetokounmpo has officially signed with Promitheas Vikos Cola for the 2026–27 season, the Greek club announced Saturday. This high-profile addition injects significant size and athleticism into the Patras roster for their upcoming campaign. Standing 2.03 meters tall, the versatile 24-year-old forward brings a wealth of global experience to the squad. Born on August 27, 2001, the Greek-Nigerian prospect is the youngest brother of NBA superstar Giannis Antetokounmpo, alongside Thanasis, Kost

item.title: The Warriors have reached an agreement to add …
item.description: The Warriors have reached an agreement to add undrafted rookie forward Graham Ike, the Gonzaga men’s basketball national team announced (via Twitter). Ike will sign an Exhibit 10 contract with Golden State, Jon Chepkvich of DraftExpress and RookieScale.com confirms to Hoops Rumors.

item.title: Timofey Mozgov on 2016 Cavs reunion: 'No one called me'
item.description: Hosted by LeBron James, the luxury multi-country vacation featured core members of the historic 2015–16 roster, including Kevin Love, J.R. Smith, Richard Jefferson, Channing Frye, and Tristan Thompson, with Iman Shumpert and Matthew Dellavedova joining the group later. Despite playing in 13 games during that legendary postseason run, the 39-year-old Russian big was completely omitted from the planning. Speaking with the Russian outlet Sport Express on Friday, Timofey Mozgov revealed that nobody

There are some people out there that look at that a little more deeper than the eye test says. I had, not an executive, but an analytics guy, saying to me ‘we view Jaylen Brown as the seventh-best player on the team.'😳 @BobbyMarks42 tells @BGeltzNBA and @SamMitchellNBA the market for Jaylen Brown isn’t as robust as it might seem. item.title: Celtics to re-sign Ron Harper Jr. on a three-year, $9 million deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Boston Celtics G/F Ron Harper Jr. intends to sign a new three-year, $9 million deal to return to the franchise, sources tell ESPN. The Celtics are declining Harper's $2.6M team option, clearing the way for the sides to commit on a longer deal.

It goes too far.

item.title: “This is a time that I don’t think New Yorkers or the …
item.description: Kevin Durant urged the Knicks and Jalen Brunson to make the most of the organization’s historic championship win while they still can. The Rockets star addressed the Knicks during an interview with Wall Street Journal Live in Cannes earlier this week — and the advice he would give Brunson in the face of new advertising and promotional opportunities. “I would tell him to strike while the iron’s hot,” Durant said during the panel alongside Boardroom CEO Rich Kleiman and OBB and Bolded founder and

item.title: Deandre Ayton expected to pick up his player option
item.description: It’s no secret the Lakers are looking to upgrade at center (it should be noted Deandre Ayton is expected to pick up his player option and be back with the team). Robinson is the kind of big man they could use, but his health concerns — particularly his chronic ankle issues — will give the Lakers and every team some pause.

item.title: Nuggets to join Jaylen Brown's sweepstakes?
item.description: Sources say that the Nuggets have at least internally discussed whether they have the asset wherewithal to join the trade chase for Boston's Jaylen Brown, but the Nuggets have yet to emerge as a bonafide landing spot for the Celtics' MVP candidate.

item.title: Nets, Spurs interested in signing Rui Hachimura
item.description: Brooklyn keeps coming up as a likely suitor, too, for the Lakers' Rui Hachimura, league sources say. The Nets and Spurs are two teams we've heard have interest in adding Hachimura's services, while the Spurs are also faced with resolving Harrison Barnes' future as the veteran forward heads to unrestricted free agency.

item.title: Moritz Wagner projects to have multiple suitors in …
item.description: Sources say Moritz Wagner projects to have multiple suitors in free agency and a willingness to play elsewhere even though his brother Franz obviously remains a Magic cornerstone.

item.title: Bogdan Bogdanovic openly talked about his love to …
item.description: In an interview with Vogue Adria, Bogdan Bogdanovic openly talked about his love to basketball and Serbia NT: "Love to it exists since I was little and of course I wanted to be the best version of myself," Bogdanovic started. "When I play with the national team that's a special emotion playing for Serbia. Those are my proudest moments and I want to make every moment special."

item.title: "In Europe we spend a lot of time on tactics and …
item.description: "In Europe we spend a lot of time on tactics and analysis and we have much more time for practice to prepare for the games. NBA pace is much more intense and it's more focused on your team and individual responsibility," he said. "Mentally in NBA, I always have to be ready to adjust on the fly. Basketball there allows more freedom, it's faster, EuroLeague basketball is tactically more disciplined, expectations and pressure strongly influence on your performances."

item.title: Ben Wallace is seeking a buyer for his …
item.description: NBA legend Ben Wallace is seeking a buyer for his 22,000-square-foot mansion and 35-acre estate in Manakin-Sabot, just months after unloading his longtime basketball gym near the city.

item.title: Nikola Jokic to hold off extension talks with Denver?
item.description: Nikola Jokić informed the Nuggets around this time last year that he preferred to delay contract extension talks for another year. The Stein Line has learned that it is under consideration again this summer: Electing to take the same step and holding off on extension talks.

item.title: Tyrese Haliburton on injury recovery: 'I've never felt this strong'
item.description: Tyrese Haliburton can already sense that it helps him play through contact on both ends of the floor. "My body feels great," Haliburton said. "I feel great moving. I feel great playing. My body just feels great in all honesty. I've never felt this strong. I still gotta get in a little bit of shape but I feel great. It's exciting and it's only what, the end of June here. I still have three more months. I feel like I'm in good shape, NBA shape, but there's a difference between NBA shape and Tyrese

item.title: His shingles diagnosis in February knocked what to …
item.description: After an Achilles tendon tear in his right leg in the first quarter of Game 7 of the 2025 NBA Finals last June stole from Tyrese Haliburton a chance to give the Pacers their first NBA title and kept him out the entire following season, his shingles diagnosis in February knocked what to that point had been a steady recovery process off course. The illness -- a product of the re-activation of the virus that causes chickenpox -- attacked his face and his right eye in particular. He suffered from ne

item.title: "I look back at videos of me and I can see how swollen …
item.description: "I look back at videos of me and I can see how swollen my face is," Haliburton said. "I'm glad that's beyond me and past me now. I feel great. My body feels great. I'm able to do everything. I feel like I'm operating like I'm a healthy NBA player for the first time in a long time, which is exciting. The fact that I'm able to come here without having to answer questions from anybody in our organization about my health, that I'm just able to jump in and do some things with the kids, that means the

item.title: Jimmy Butler: 'I'm still going to be a top player whenever I get back'
item.description: Nearing the four-month mark since ACL surgery on his right knee, Golden State Warriors wing Jimmy Butler predicted a loud return to the court whenever he is ultimately cleared this upcoming season. "I'm right back to who I've always been," Butler told ESPN. "Probably better if we was to really talk about it. I know I'm still going to be a top player whenever I get back. I have a couple good years left in me, and we're going to ride it until the wheels fall off."

item.title: Butler spoke with ESPN about his future with the …
item.description: Butler spoke with ESPN about his future with the franchise and his desire to retire in a Warriors uniform. "End is a bad word, but this is where I want to be done," Butler said. "Retire here. This organization is top-tier. It's the best. Keep it a buck. It's the best. You're playing with one of the greatest players ever. I'm grateful to be able to witness this. I want to be able to do my part, get this organization back on top where we belong."

item.title: "You talking about being traded?" Butler said. "It …
item.description: Should a large-scale trade materialize, Butler's expiring contract could be the vehicle to make it happen. "You talking about being traded?" Butler said. "It wouldn't be the first time. [But] it's good to know that I'm wanted here. If I get traded, I get traded. Their job is to win. Can I help them do that? Yes. If they feel like somebody else can help them do that on a quicker timetable than whenever I come back, then they got to go and do that, but as of right now, I'm here. I don't take that

item.title: Serbia secured a convincing 90-76 victory over Czechia …
item.description: Serbia secured a convincing 90-76 victory over Czechia in a closed-door friendly at Aleksandar Nikolić Hall in Belgrade as part of its preparations for the upcoming FIBA window.

item.title: Nikola Jović led Serbia with 20 points, adding five …
item.description: Nikola Jović led Serbia with 20 points, adding five rebounds and three assists. Nikola Jokić finished with 16 points, 14 rebounds and eight assists, narrowly missing a triple-double, while Dejan Davidovac contributed 14 points. Vít Krejčí paced Czechia with 16 points and four assists.

item.title: Jonathan Isaac waived by Magic
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: The Orlando Magic has waived Jonathan Isaac, league sources told @hoopshype. The former sixth overall pick of the 2017 NBA Draft averaged 6.8 points and 4.5 rebounds in seven seasons with the Magic.

item.title: Magic to save $6.5 million in 2026-27 salaries after waiving Jonathan Isaac
item.description: Michael Scotto: Jonathan Isaac was guaranteed $8 million of his $14.5 million salary for the 2026-27 season. The following two seasons on his deal were non-guaranteed. The 28-year-old, 6-foot-10 forward now becomes an unrestricted free agent.

item.title: While Isaac could still re-sign with Orlando, he will …
item.description: With the move, the Magic give themselves some financial flexibility by creating more space below the second apron and lowering their projected luxury payment from roughly $16.5 million down to around $10 million for the upcoming season. The franchise is expected to pay luxury tax for the first time in over a decade. While Isaac could still re-sign with Orlando, he will also be allowed to negotiate with all other clubs as a free agent starting at 6 p.m. on Tuesday. Teams may begin signing free ag

item.title: Kyle Mangas, who was briefly on a two-way contract …
item.description: Adam Himmelsbach: Per source, veteran guard Kyle Mangas, who was briefly on a two-way contract with the Spurs last season, is expected to be added to the Celtics’ summer league roster.

item.title: Kyle Filipowski will wear #2 for the Jazz after giving …
item.description: Sarah Todd: Kyle Filipowski will wear #2 for the Jazz after giving up #22 to Darryn Peterson.

item.title: Jimmy Butler is at Miami Stadium,
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Jimmy Butler is at Miami Stadium, cheering for Colombia tonight.

item.title: Magic Johnson-led group among candidates to land Las Vegas NBA franchise
item.description: There are multiple groups looking at landing the new franchise and potentially building a new arena in Las Vegas, a person with knowledge of the situation told the Review-Journal. One group, the MAGI, led by NBA legend turned businessman Earvin “Magic” Johnson, expressed interest in Las Vegas in February, before the vote to explore Las Vegas and Seattle for expansion occurred. Johnson and members of his group met with Gov. Joe Lombardo and other civic leaders in relation to expansion.

item.title: NBA superstar and part-time Las Vegas resident …
item.description: Other notable people involved in groups include Golden Knights owner Bill Foley, who announced his bid officially on Monday, and former Milwaukee Bucks co-owner Marc Lasry. NBA superstar and part-time Las Vegas resident Shaquille O’Neal is also interested in being part of an ownership group, according to his Las Vegas-based business manager Perry Rogers. Rogers said they’ve had conversations with various ownership groups.

item.title: Michael Jordan’s name has been mentioned by members of …
item.description: One source also said that Michael Jordan’s name has been mentioned by members of ownership groups, noting the billionaire six-time NBA champion was potentially interested in being part of a group. There haven’t been direct talks with Jordan and area officials, a source said.

item.title: Bobby Marks explained that Jaylen Brown’s trade value …
item.description: Bobby Marks explained that Jaylen Brown’s trade value has gone down and reported what an analyst told him. He said, “I had one, not an executive, but an analytics guy saying, ‘Yeah, we view them as like the seventh best player on a team.”

item.title: Brown started crashing out after reading Marks’ …
item.description: Brown started crashing out after reading Marks’ comment. He said, “Analytics nowadays used to discredit and control narratives – Roll the ball out none of these guys better than me on both ends who does he work for.” Marks then pointed out that he would want the Celtics star on his team, and that he doesn’t prefer analytics. He also added that Brown would surely help any team in the league win a championship, citing his impactful winning record in the last four years ((191-80 in the last 4 years

item.title: Brown then posted on X, “Nobody has won more combined …
item.description: Brown then posted on X, “Nobody has won more combined regular-season and playoff games since I entered the league 10 years ago,” before claiming that analytics are ‘ruining’ hoops right now. He also asked Marks to state his source and identify the analyst who made the comment.

item.title: Deni Avdija off the table in trade talks?
item.description: The question for Boston with the Blazers will be what type of package they would demand from Portland. The Blazers have plenty of intriguing young talent across their roster but they would certainly want to retain the majority of it to compete in the Western Conference even after a Brown acquisition. All-Star Deni Avdija would not be on the table in any trade talks, league sources tell MassLive and it would be tough for Boston to pry away Donovan Clingan as well. The Blazers do not have strong c

item.title: Seth Trimble is joining the Wizards after going …
item.description: Former UNC guard Seth Trimble is joining the Wizards after going undrafted, according to an announcement from the Tar Heels’ men’s basketball team (Twitter link).

item.title: With Isaiah Hartenstein back in the fold, the Thunder …
item.description: Keith Smith: With Isaiah Hartenstein back in the fold, the Thunder now project to be: 🏀$10.2M over the tax 🏀$2.2M over the first apron 🏀$10.8M under the second apron This is without Lu Dort or Kenrich Williams but does count both first-round draft picks.

item.title: LeBron was in a bad mood about ESPN. He thought ESPN …
item.description: Heat Central: LeBron James felt like ESPN took advantage of him in 2010 when he decided to join the Heat, per @WindhorstESPN “The Heat did not like us there. LeBron was in a bad mood about ESPN. He thought ESPN had turned his back on him and taken advantage of him in the wake of the decision. He was annoyed by all the coverage. Looking back on it, it was annoying.”

item.title: Jaylen Brown shared this video on his Instagram that …
item.description: Fullcourtpass: Jaylen Brown shared this video on his Instagram that talks about… - How he was “the best player” on a championship team - How he got “blackballed” by the Olympic team - How the Celtics tried to trade him multiple times (h/t @Savageboston)

item.title: “Listen, I’m more of an eye test guy,” he said. “I …
item.description: “The original clip that was posted this morning did not have the full context of Bobby’s exact answer,” Geltzeiler wrote. “And in this case, it’s VERY important to hear everything he said.” The fuller quotes provide a bit more context around what Marks was saying and how he disagreed with the analytics take on Brown. “Listen, I’m more of an eye test guy,” he said. “I like impact winning, 29 years old, has played in 60 more games, 4 to 5 last year. They’re 191-80 the last 4 years he plays. Okay?

item.title: Marks also tried to assuage Brown, responding to his …
item.description: Marks also tried to assuage Brown, responding to his post to say he didn’t agree with the analytics guys’ assessment. “But what wasn’t posted was the 2m of saying I’m not an analytics person but an eye test guy,” Marks wrote to Brown. “That I would want Jaylen Brown on my team. That you impact winning (191-80 in the last 4 years), is a top-10 player and would help any team looking to win a championship. That part of course didn’t make it. But that’s the media in 2026.”

item.title: Labaron Philon Jr. says Alperen Sengun will be the …
item.description: [NBA2KL] Sixers rookie Labaron Philon Jr. says Alperen Sengun will be the first star whose ankles he breaks: “I be on TikTok a lot… they be saying he play like he got a refrigerator on his back.”

item.title: Mikal Bridges: "It's been great. I don't think I can …
item.description: Mikal Bridges: "It's been great. I don't think I can really step outside my home, walk up a block without a lot of fans, but it's cool because they deserve it. They've been here for so long, so I'm happy for them. Even for me stopping by and taking pictures of stuff, I know it means so much to them as well, so it's been pretty cool."

item.title: Kevin Durant: "I think Jalen has done such a great job …
item.description: Kevin Durant: "I think Jalen has done such a great job of being him every single day and stepping up and his identity is just about grinding from the bottom and being somebody in New York City who made history in New York City. Right now it's an important time for him and the Knicks and I feel like he's going to take full advantage of it."

item.title: Fred VanVleet: I wasn’t anywhere near coming back during season
item.description: Even when the doctors assured VanVleet that the images were accurate and they had been sent around to other experts for confirmation, VanVleet said he told them, “What I feel right now, I feel like I could play in 10 days, I’m telling you. I’ve played through s— like this. They’re like, 'Nah. You need to get surgery.'” Throughout the season there was some speculation — and perhaps sheerly overly optimistic hope — that VanVleet could return by the end of the regular season and contribute in the p

item.title: Nets extend Day'ron Sharpe's team option deadline by one day
item.description: Brian Lewis: The #Nets extended Day’Ron Sharpe’s option date by one day (now June 29), league sources told the Post. This gives the Brooklyn more optionality with his contract.

item.title: Mavs interested in adding Willie Green to Dusty May's staff
item.description: Marc Stein: Former Pelicans coach Willie Green, who has been pursued by Golden State to rejoin Steve Kerr's staff as an assistant, also has interest from Dallas as new Mavericks coach Dusty May assembles his first NBA staff, league sources say. Former Bucks coach Adrian Griffin and Heat consultant Noah LaRoche have likewise been mentioned as expected candidates for May's bench in Dallas. As @TheSteinLine previously reported, LaRoche was part of the monthlong interview process that eventually led

item.title: Several teams are expected to show interest in signing …
item.description: Several teams are expected to show interest in signing Williams as an unrestricted free agent, including the Phoenix Suns, Golden State Warriors, Boston Celtics, and the Mavericks, who want to retain him, league sources told HoopsHype. Dallas has Early Bird Rights on Williams entering free agency this summer.

item.title: Thomas, who was selected 34th overall in the second …
item.description: With that in mind, after finding Jaylon Tyson in the first round of the 2024 NBA Draft to give themselves a young, cheaper rotation option, the Cavaliers appear to feel equally bullish about rookie Meleek Thomas, who turns 20 on August 6th. Thomas, who was selected 34th overall in the second round of the draft, is expected to agree to a standard NBA contract with the second-round exception, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Tyler Herro drew trade interest from Lakers and Nets before Bucks trade
item.description: Before Herro joined Milwaukee in the Antetokounmpo trade and the Los Angeles Lakers agreed to a four-year, $185 million max deal with Austin Reaves, the Lakers had trade discussions surrounding Herro and Kel’el Ware, which would’ve involved multiple first-round draft picks, league sources told HoopsHype. In addition, the Brooklyn Nets also did due diligence and gauged the price tag for Herro, HoopsHype has learned.

item.title: Cam carr’s dad chris carr confirmed that luka doncic …
item.description: Coach Rome: cam carr’s dad chris carr confirmed that luka doncic and austin reaves have already texted cam welcoming him to the lakers

item.title: Mike James: I think that I realized a long time ago …
item.description: I feel like you know you're good enough to be in the NBA. Was it more a matter of not being in the right place at the right time? Mike James: I think that I realized a long time ago that just because you are good enough to be in the NBA doesn't mean you're supposed to be. I think it's a lot of people that are not in the NBA that probably could be, have had the chance to be, but you know, certain things don't work out. Even though you could be better than a person, maybe he's just a better fit, a

item.title: While the Pelicans hope to retain Bey going forward, …
item.description: While the Pelicans hope to retain Bey going forward, the same can’t be said for assistant coach Jodie Meeks, who won’t return to New Orleans next season, league sources told HoopsHype. Meeks was with the Birmingham Squadron, the G League affiliate for New Orleans, from 2022-24 before moving up as an assistant coach on former head coach Willie Green’s staff.

item.title: Who's your GOAT? Mike James: Michael Jordan. He's the …
item.description: Who's your GOAT? Mike James: Michael Jordan. He's the best. How old were you when he was playing? Mike James: In 1984, I was negative six; in 1998, I was eight. I got the craziest memory. They had a game when they played the Jazz, I think the last year he played. And they won 96-54 in a Finals game. And I just remember watching like, 'Damn, Michael Jordan this good? This game boring.' And I just went outside and started hooping. It was Game 3, his last title. They beat them badly. And I was just

item.title: Even without extension talks, Nikola Jokic has reiterated his desire to stay long-term in Denver
item.description: Despite the possibility of Nikola Jokic holding off on extension talks for now, per The Stein Line, Jokic has reiterated a desire to stay long-term in Denver in recent talks, league sources told HoopsHype. If Jokic waits until next summer, he’s eligible for an additional year on an extension, which should be noted.

item.title: Jaylen Brown trade suitors: Blazers, Raptors, Nuggets, Nets, Hornets, Hawks, Clippers
item.description: Brown has drawn trade interest from various teams across the league, including the Portland Trail Blazers, Toronto Raptors, Denver Nuggets, Brooklyn Nets, Charlotte Hornets, Atlanta Hawks, and Los Angeles Clippers, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Pelicans, Rockets, Magic not in the mix for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Other teams, such as the New Orleans Pelicans, Houston Rockets, and Orlando Magic, who’ve been loosely linked to Brown, have been described as not in the mix.

item.title: Deni Avdija was not on the table in trade talks for …
item.description: Trail Blazers All-Star forward Deni Avdija was not on the table in trade talks for Antetokounmpo, and he remains off the table in Brown discussions as of now, league sources told HoopsHype. Portland’s other young players, such as Scoot Henderson and Shaedon Sharpe, are considered available. With Boston considering center upgrades and needing to replace Brown’s position if he’s traded, Portland’s Donovan Clingan and 2025 All-Defensive forward Toumani Camara would both be coveted by Boston. Howeve

item.title: The Nuggets have considered a major roster shakeup, …
item.description: The Nuggets have considered a major roster shakeup, with the franchise willing to listen to offers for anyone other than Nikola Jokic. We have a full outline in the next section of this story on all the Denver trade chatter surrounding its core.

item.title: Celtics still interested in Naz Reid after trade to Hornets
item.description: Reid once again was brought up in talks for Brown after joining the Hornets, HoopsHype has learned. Charlotte has also gauged the trade market value of forward Miles Bridges, who’s on an expiring $22.83 million deal. His salary would be needed to make any Brown trade work with Charlotte. With that said, exploratory talks with the Hornets have cooled.

item.title: The Pistons were linked with interest in free agent …
item.description: Regarding other potential acquisitions, the Pistons were linked with interest in free agent Norman Powell and Kawhi Leonard as a trade suitor. However, that chatter has lessened over the past few days following the Joe acquisition and rumblings that Leonard wouldn’t want to extend with Detroit within league circles.

item.title: Bulls to pursue Norman Powell?
item.description: The Chicago Bulls are among the teams expected to have interest in Powell as free agency nears, league sources told HoopsHype. The 33-year-old guard is a career 39.6 percent three-point shooter. The Detroit Pistons have also been linked to Powell as a potential suitor. Yet, it remains to be seen how the acquisition of Isaiah Joe could impact Detroit’s pursuit of Powell.

item.title: Herro is on an expiring $33 million deal and is …
item.description: Heading into the upcoming season, Herro is on an expiring $33 million deal and is expected to continue to draw trade interest, with many executives around the league curious if Milwaukee will eventually trend more towards a full rebuild or maintain a competitive team with the players acquired for Antetokounmpo.

item.title: Jalen Duren seeking close to $40 million per year?
item.description: With that said, the Pistons have maintained a strong desire to keep their 22-year-old, All-NBA center. Fellow All-NBA selection, Cade Cunningham, also wants Duren back, according to league sources. Within league circles, there’s a belief that Duren is seeking close to $40 million annually, or roughly 25 percent of the NBA’s salary cap. Ultimately, multiple NBA executives who spoke with HoopsHype project Duren to remain with the Pistons and land somewhere around the mid-30s annually once the smo

item.title: Aaron Gordon has drawn trade interest from Blazers; Celtics have kept tabs
item.description: Nuggets forward Aaron Gordon was considered among the contingency plans for the Heat if the franchise didn’t land Giannis Antetokounmpo via trade, league sources told HoopsHype. Similarly, the Portland Trail Blazers have expressed trade interest in Gordon should they not land Jaylen Brown, their preferred target, HoopsHype has learned. Ironically, the Celtics have also kept tabs on Gordon from afar.

item.title: Cam Johnson has drawn trade interest from Lakers, Celtics, Heat, Clippers, Magic
item.description: Meanwhile, as the Nuggets navigate getting below the second apron, sharpshooting forward Cam Johnson has drawn trade interest from several teams, including the Los Angeles Lakers, Boston Celtics, Miami Heat, Los Angeles Clippers, and Orlando Magic, league sources told HoopsHype. The 30-year-old forward shot career-highs from the field (.480) and beyond the arc (.430), and is on an expiring $23.06 million contract for the upcoming season.

item.title: Cavs, 76eres, Lakers, Spurs, Suns, Pistons, Blazers among teams interested in Dean Wade?
item.description: Cleveland Cavaliers forward Dean Wade is expected to have various suitors in free agency, including the Cavaliers, Philadelphia 76ers, Los Angeles Lakers, San Antonio Spurs, Phoenix Suns, Detroit Pistons, and Portland Trail Blazers, league sources told HoopsHype.

item.title: Max Strus and Dennis Schroeder have been mentioned in …
item.description: Meanwhile, as the Cavaliers navigate James Harden’s return and Wade’s free agency, Cleveland’s Max Strus and Dennis Schroeder have been mentioned in trade talks with the franchise currently projected over the second apron.

item.title: Marvin Bagley expected to earn at least the taxpayer MLE
item.description: Bagley, 27, is expected to draw interest from several teams and command an average annual salary in the taxpayer mid-level exception range or slightly higher.

item.title: Saddiq Bey, Pelicans interested in an extension
item.description: Saddiq Bey is coming off the best season of his career with the New Orleans Pelicans and now both sides are interested in an extension, league sources told HoopsHype. He is eligible to sign one as early as July 11. After a torn ACL that sidelined him for the entire 2024-25 season, the 27-year-old forward averaged career highs in points (17.7) and field goal percentage (.451), while starting 64 of 72 games played. Bey was drafted in Detroit by current Pelicans executive Troy Weaver, who also trad

item.title: Landale, 30, is expected to have a strong market, …
item.description: Landale, 30, is expected to have a strong market, including interest from the Hawks, Chicago Bulls, Los Angeles Lakers, Los Angeles Clippers, Philadelphia 76ers, and Cleveland Cavaliers, league sources told HoopsHype. Expect the Australian center to command offers north of the bi-annual exception in free agency.

item.title: NBA execs: Marcus Sasser available on trade market
item.description: Furthermore, Pistons guard Marcus Sasser is said to be available on the trade market, according to NBA executives around the league who’ve spoken with HoopsHype. Sasser, a career 38.6 percent 3-point shooter, has a $5.20 million salary for the upcoming season and will then be eligible for restricted free agency the following year.

item.title: Cedric Maxwell on beef between Jaylen Brown, Stephen A. Smith: 'Why is an elephant right now battling with an ant?'
item.description: Cedric Maxwell: "But the other side of that, they gave him $300 million. If you're that mad with me and you give me $300 million, you can dangle me around like a carrot. I could care less, but in this case, I do understand that players have egos and their egos could be bruised. We've seen him go back and forth with Stephen A. Smith over and over again. I always say why is an elephant right now battling with an ant? Jaylen Brown should be beyond that."

item.title: Cedric Maxwell: "I am a huge Jaylen Brown fan. I would …
item.description: Cedric Maxwell: "I am a huge Jaylen Brown fan. I would hate to see you lose him, but I understand that there's some business aspects that come into play. If I was the Celtics right now, I'd want a hell of a package for him because Jaylen Brown was one of the top 10 players, one of the top seven players in the NBA last year. He became All-NBA, All-World essentially, and one thing he said, 'I am right now the best two-way player in the NBA other than that the alien down in San Antonio.' He has a g

item.title: Austin Reaves was going to get paid and he's got …
item.description: Law Murray: "Free agency is really difficult and these teams talk so much. That's why we have the trash rumor mill that we have now. Austin Reaves was going to get paid and he's got really good representation. These dudes have a feel and the pulse of the league and he offers an ability that, if he would have left, he would have been a difference-maker wherever he would go. I've got a lot of respect for Austin Reaves' game and so do a lot of teams in the league."

item.title: "Joel wanted (LeBron James) on the Sixers, okay, back …
item.description: Law Murray: "Joel wanted (LeBron James) on the Sixers, okay, back in 2018. Like, really, really bad. And when LeBron left Cleveland for the Lakers, Joel had one of my favorite of his troll tweets when he was like, I'm paraphrasing, but he was like, they're not going to like you in Los Angeles. That's Kobe's town. And that dude LeBron, it's been that's how it's felt. Like he's been polarizing in a Los Angeles uniform because you got Bron fans, you got Lakers fans, you got Kobe fans. It's a whole

item.title: “When it comes to prospects I’ve evaluated in my 10 …
item.description: Most often, AJ himself speaks in short, direct sentences, and if he doesn’t know you, it’s unlikely that you’ll receive a smile. But on the court he emanates confidence, barking instructions and encouragement to his teammates while shredding defenses. In the final minute of the first quarter, he drills a 3-pointer at one end before swatting one away at the other, sending a jolt of electricity through the arena. “When it comes to prospects I’ve evaluated in my 10 years, AJ is easily one of the be

item.title: “He’d come home crying because the bigger kids were …
item.description: AJ showed little interest in sports as a toddler but loved superheroes. When AJ was 5, his father bought a Spider-Man mini hoop, attached it to his door, and taught him to take shots from his bed. AJ was rapt. Ace later installed a 25-by-25-foot blacktop court in the backyard, and whenever someone was looking for AJ, that was the place to start. He carried his basketball everywhere and slept with it tucked under his arm. In sixth grade, AJ started working with trainer Brandon Ball, whose session

item.title: Mitchell Robinson addresses free agency
item.description: Now, we’ve heard from Mitchell Robinson himself. In an interview with WGNO (h/t New York Basketball, Robinson was asked about the possibility of returning to New York and building on the franchise’s first title in 53 years. “We can see what happens. It would be great, try to do it, try to run it back again, try to go back-to-back… Defending champs, so we’ll, that’s very a possibility.” — Mitchell Robinson. Robinson’s comments arrive as multiple reports continued to emerge throughout Saturday, al

item.title: Lakers have tendered a two-way qualifying offer to …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Los Angeles Lakers have tendered a two-way qualifying offer to Chris Manon, a league source told @spotrac. Manon will now be a restricted free agent this summer.

item.title: Timberwolves have tendered a qualifying offer to …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Minnesota Timberwolves have tendered a qualifying offer to Jaylen Clark and a two-way qualifying offer to Enrique Freeman, a league source told @spotrac. Clark and Freeman will now be restricted free agents this offseason. Clark has reportedly agreed to a new deal already.

item.title: Dru Smith's $2.6 million salary for next season …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: Dru Smith's $2.6 million salary for next season becomes fully guaranteed if he's still on the Heat's roster past 11:59 p.m. today.

item.title: Karlo Matkovic: ‘The role of a veteran is very …
item.description: Karlo Matkovic: ‘The role of a veteran is very important, and DeAndre Jordan really helped me a lot this year. It helps to have a team like that where someone doesn't just come to me and say 'Cheers' and then ignore me. He learned from Jokić, he learned from Dončić. (…) He really turned out to be an 'extra' veteran. I go with him after games; he calls me, we joke around, it's great. But you can also see how he maintains his body—it’s unreal.’

item.title: Whether Bronny is a professional at that level yet is …
item.description: Karlo Matkovic on LeBron James: ‘He literally made a son to be his teammate. People talk about him and say he’s not good, but at the very least, he brought his son into the league. LeBron said he can play, he said '100% believe me.' And Bronny is not bad in the G League; he is strong, even though he is small. You have people like Brunson saying from the side that he thinks he can't make it, but all those haters... everyone has their own opinion. It's a unique situation in the league. You have to

item.title: Vladimir Radmanovic after forcing Lakers trade to Bobcats: 'Be careful what you wish for'
item.description: Vladimir Radmanovic on being traded to Hornets: ‘After being kept on the bench for nearly two months with only 'teaspoon' minutes, I couldn't establish any continuity as a player. It was one of the most frustrating periods of my career. I eventually went to Mitch Kupchak and told him, 'If this is going to continue, please count me out and look for a trade.' It’s a classic case of 'be careful what you wish for.' I was traded to the Charlotte Bobcats, which was a franchise in total disarray at the

item.title: Vladimir Radmanovic: Kobe Bryant was like a shark, if he sensed a bit of blood, it was over
item.description: Vladimir Radmanovic: 'Kobe Bryant was like a shark; if he sensed even a little bit of blood, it was over. He tested every single teammate to see what he could expect from them in terms of mental toughness. This involved provocations, playing dirty during practice, and even throwing 'cheap shots.' To an outsider, it might look like something was wrong with him or that he wasn't balanced, but from the inside, you realized it was a constant test. I saw players like Smush Parker fail that test. Ther

item.title: I knew that joining the Lakers might not be the best …
item.description: Vladimir Radmanovic on being recruited by the Lakers: ‘When I was a free agent deciding where to sign, my manager told me that three people were going to contact me: Kobe Bryant, Magic Johnson, and Phil Jackson. No matter how humble you try to be, it is incredibly flattering when those three individuals call you personally and tell you they want you in their organization. Vlade Divac acted as a mediator, likely sharing my phone number. I knew that joining the Lakers might not be the best option

item.title: Vladimir Radmanovic on the Influence of Phil Jackson’s …
item.description: Vladimir Radmanovic on the Influence of Phil Jackson’s methods: ‘Phil Jackson was an incredibly interesting type of coach, unlike anyone else in the league. He had these methods—meditation, yoga, and turning off the lights in the locker room. He would tell us to close our eyes and 'think of nothing.' At the time, I was just starting to explore my faith more seriously, and I felt like meditating didn't really fit with my beliefs, so I’d just use the time to take a quick nap while he talked. But h

item.title: Tony Parker officially became the head coach of …
item.description: Tony Parker officially became the head coach of LDLC-ASVEL Villeurbanne last night. French Hall of Fame member will start his coaching career with the simultaneous role of being a coach and a president of the club at the same time. In an exclusive interview with L'Equipe, Parker talked in detail about his arrival to the sidelines: "This choice became logical over time. When I started the process of becoming a coach, I was more focused on the NBA. But between my attachment to my country, the fact

item.title: Nikola Jovic says Tyler Herro was in 'every single' Heat trade rumor
item.description: Nikola Jovic: ‘Ever since I arrived in Miami, I have been in trade rumors for Kevin Durant twice. Yes, twice for KD. For Kyrie Irving twice. For Damian Lillard, it was already a done deal because Lillard publicly stated that he wanted to come to Miami. It was like, 'I want Miami,' and that was it. For Lillard! And now most recently, I was in there for Giannis. I have been in seven or eight trade rumors already. Me and my brother Tyler Herro. I mean, my brother Tyler Herro, what he has endured, i

item.title: Cedric Maxwell: If I was the Celtics right now, I'd want a hell of a package for Jaylen Brown
item.description: Cedric Maxwell: I’m a huge Jaylen Brown fan. Let's start there. I would that you would lose him, but I understand that there's some business aspects that come into play. If I was the Celtics right now, I'd want a hell of a package for him because Jaylen Brown was one of the top 10 players, one of the top seven players in the NBA last year. He became All-NBA. And one thing he said, I am right now the best two-way player in the NBA other than, that the alien down in San Antonio.

item.title: Patrick Beverley: James Harden is better than Dwyane Wade
item.description: Patrick Beverley: I can never disrespect Dwyane Wade, but if you ask me an honest question of who's better, I'm not just going to be on some like ignorant sh*t. Like I'mma go like, "Oh, James Harden is better." Like he's a better scorer. He's a better passer. He shoots the three better. He gets to the free throw line more. He has more triple-doubles. He has scored the more opponents. He has more MVPs. He's never missed the playoffs, more playoffs experience. And the more… I mean, Wade has a ring

item.title: Patrick Beverley recalls asking out of the Clippers, says Kawhi Leonard tried to keep him
item.description: Patrick Beverley when he asked out of the Clippers: ‘I text Kawhi Leonard like, "Yo, I'm just letting you know I asked for a trade, you one of my closest dudes on the team. I text Kawhi. I text Paul George. I text Marcus Morris. I'm just letting you know. I ask for, it's time for extension. Kawhi say, "I call you back." All right. I go up to my room. Never forget this. We in like I think we in the Wynn hotel, like in a meeting area. I go up to my room. Come back. My agent called me like, "Yo, co

item.title: Miles Bridges to Phoenix
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The Charlotte Hornets are trading Miles Bridges, a 2029 first-round pick and a 2027 second-round pick to the Phoenix Suns for Grayson Allen, Royce O'Neale and a 2033 first-round pick, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Rockets are likely to extend a qualifying offer to …
item.description: Kelly Iko: The Houston Rockets are likely to extend a qualifying offer to wing Tari Eason, sources tell @YahooSports.

item.title: Heat targeting Tim Hardaway Jr. and Mike Conley
item.description: League sources say that the Heat will have strong interest in the veteran duo of Tim Hardaway Jr. and Mike Conley Jr.

item.title: Warriors expected to pursue Jock Landale
item.description: League sources say that the Warriors, though, are planning to register interest in various other free agents while waiting to see how James' highly anticipated free agency plays out. Sources say that Golden State is among the teams expected to pursue veteran big man Jock Landale in addition to scoring guards such as Anfernee Simons and Collin Sexton.

item.title: Spurs, Magic and Sixers interested in John Collins
item.description: League sources say John Collins certainly has fans in Minnesota, but it appears unlikely that the Timberwolves will have sufficient spending power to pursue the scoring forward, who spent last season with the Clippers. Collins is also expected to draw interest from Orlando, San Antonio and Philadelphia once free agency begins. The 76ers and Western Conference champion Spurs continue to get linked in free agency projections to the top forwards in the marketplace.

item.title: Jay Triano, who has coached both the Raptors and the …
item.description: Sources say Jay Triano, who has coached both the Raptors and the Suns in addition to numerous stops as an assistant and a lengthy stint as head coach of Canada's national team, is a leading candidate to join Micah Nori’s first coaching staff in Portland after working on Jason Kidd’s staff in Dallas last season. Nori’s first coaching job in the NBA came after working as an advance scout for more than a decade when Triano made him part of his Raptors coaching staff.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard back to Toronto?
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Raptors and Clippers have held real trade conversations this weekend on sending Kawhi Leonard back to Toronto, according to the league sources.

item.title: Toronto has expressed a preference in dealing Brandon …
item.description: Jake Fischer: In discussions with the Clippers, sources say Toronto has expressed a preference in dealing Brandon Ingram to Los Angeles, as opposed to RJ Barrett.

item.title: LeBron James has not expressed interest in returning to Heat yet
item.description: Speculation continues to swirl about whether LeBron James could have interest in a return to the Heat. Nothing should be ruled out, but this is not something that has been actively pursued by James to this point, according to a source. Miami very likely would explore if James decides to pursue it.

item.title: Heat linked to Khris Middleton
item.description: Other potential targets have not been confirmed, but Miami has been strongly linked to veteran NBA forward Khris Middleton, who played with soon-to-be-new-Heat-forward Giannis Antetekounmpo in Milwaukee.

item.title: The Heat is expected to try to add at least two …
item.description: The Heat is expected to try to add at least two skilled three-point shooters in free agency. Other options, listed here, include Landry Shamet, Anfernee Simons and Quentin Grimes, among others.

item.title: I can confirm there will be Heat interest in Tim …
item.description: Barry Jackson: With regard to the Heat report by the very hard-working @TheSteinLine and @JakeLFischer , I can confirm there will be Heat interest in Tim Hardaway Jr. when free agency begins Tuesday night, per a source. This would be a very sensible marriage if it happens... With regard to Mike Conley, he has received some consideration internally. But there are some internally who would prefer going a younger route.

item.title: During his rookie season, Cooper Flagg became the …
item.description: Crazy Stats: During his rookie season, Cooper Flagg became the youngest player in @NBA history to record games of 35, 40, 45, and 50 points, as well as the youngest player to record at least 10 assists in a game.

item.title: Nets decline option on Ziaire Williams
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Brooklyn Nets will decline their $6.25 million team option on Ziaire Williams, league sources told @hoopshype. The 24-year-old forward averaged 10.2 points in 56 games played for Brooklyn last season. Williams will become an unrestricted free agent.

item.title: Nuggets looking for another ballhandler
item.description: The Nuggets are not allowed to discuss the extension with Jokić until July 6 and, per team sources, don’t yet have clarity about how he will handle it. In the interim, front-office co-heads Jon Wallace and Ben Tenzer are focused on improving a team that fell to the Minnesota Timberwolves in the first round in late April. More specifically, league sources say, the Nuggets are on the lookout for another creative ballhandler.

item.title: However, team sources say that there has been no …
item.description: But there is a fascinating financial factor to consider, too, as Denver wants to re-sign restricted free agent forward Peyton Watson and has been widely expected to shed salary in order to make that move more feasible. Denver also wants to re-sign 25-year-old small forward Spencer Jones. However, team sources say that there has been no mandate from Nuggets ownership to cut costs. As such, it remains to be seen how aggressively — or perhaps delicately — the Nuggets move forward.

item.title: Mavs also interested in Cam Johnson
item.description: Rival teams consistently point to Nuggets forward Cam Johnson, acquired last summer in a deal that sent away Michael Porter Jr., as the most likely candidate to be dealt, in large part because the 30-year-old has just one season left on his deal at $23 million. Miami and Dallas are believed to be among the many interested teams.

item.title: To this point, however, league sources say that while …
item.description: Gordon remains a beloved member of Denver’s group. He has a two-way skill set that is coveted and rare around the league, but league sources say the Nuggets are listening to offers for the 30-year-old who has missed significant time the past two seasons with injuries. To this point, however, league sources say that while there are a lot of teams interested in acquiring Gordon, that interest remains underwhelming to the Nuggets when compared to how they value him.

item.title: For those asking about Anfernee Simons in the wake of …
item.description: Barry Jackson: For those asking about Anfernee Simons in the wake of Bobby Marks saying he would be perfect fit at the minimum for Heat, feedback I've received is that he will enter free agency looking for more than that, understandably (averaged 14.9 ppg, 38.1 on 3s in career). There are always players who fall through cracks and take minimums (like Hardaway with Denver last year, Oubre with 76ers two years ago, etc. etc.). Heat will be poised to pounce if the right player falls through the cra

item.title: “I would like to say I can be a lockdown defender. …
item.description: “I would like to say I can be a lockdown defender. Obviously, I haven’t guarded the best in the world yet, but I’m willing to take this challenge. [A] 6-10 wingspan, that can help me a bit,” Peterson said. “Just my effort. I might not be the fastest guy, or quickest guy, but my effort, I will be able to take out some of that.”

item.title: Mavs discussed a trade package with Clippers for Kawhi Leonard
item.description: Mavericks president Masai Ujiri has interest in reuniting with Kawhi Leonard, multiple league sources with knowledge of Dallas’ thinking told The Athletic. The Mavericks and LA Clippers have discussed a deal that would send the seven-time All-Star Leonard to Dallas for a package that would include P.J. Washington, Klay Thompson and draft picks, league sources said.

item.title: Warriors trying to trade for Anthony Davis to entice LeBron James to join
item.description: The Warriors are attempting to trade for Wizards big Anthony Davis and then sign Lakers free agent forward LeBron James, according to multiple league sources.

item.title: Sources say the Warriors hope adding Davis would help …
item.description: Sources say the Warriors hope adding Davis would help lure LeBron from Los Angeles to the Bay Area once free agency officially opens on June 30. The pitch would be simple: reunite with AD, team up with Steph Curry and Draymond Green, play for Steve Kerr, and chase one more championship with a roster of legends.

item.title: Ja Morant expected to be traded this week
item.description: Sources say the Heat had interest in Grizzlies point guard Ja Morant before the Giannis deal, but there is not much expectation that he will get bought out. In fact, league sources expect Morant to be traded this week.

item.title: League sources say the Heat have shopped Nikola Jović …
item.description: League sources say the Heat have shopped Nikola Jović in an attempt to cut salary to open flexibility for Powell. But Jović is entering the first year of a four-year, $62 million contract that already looks like an albatross by role player standards given his lack of development at 23 years old. Miami would have to attach draft capital to Jović’s contract to dump him, but the front office only has a 2029 first and swaps in 2028 and 2032 to dangle.

item.title: League sources say that interest on the part of the …
item.description: League sources say that interest on the part of the Mavericks and Raptors is genuine, though. It’s just a matter of what Clippers owner Steve Ballmer wants to do.

item.title: Pelicans general manager Joe Dumars has been pushing …
item.description: League sources say multiple teams have put two first-round picks on the table for Murphy. Pelicans general manager Joe Dumars has been pushing for three firsts in discussions so far, and there is still a chance he gets there. But for now, teams appear to be holding firm at two.

item.title: Hawks picking up option for Buddy Hield
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Atlanta Hawks will fully guarantee the $9.66 million salary for Buddy Hield, league sources told @hoopshype. Hield is a career 39.5 percent 3-point shooter.

item.title: Definitely hearing the Hawks are involved in several …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Definitely hearing the Hawks are involved in several trade scenarios that could end up utilizing Buddy Hield’s salary for 2026-27.

item.title: With Buddy Hield on the books at his full $9.6M …
item.description: Keith Smith: With Buddy Hield on the books at his full $9.6M salary, the Atlanta Hawks are roughly $7.1M under the luxury tax and $15.1M under the first apron. The Hawks have one open roster spot. This factors in the Aaron Wiggins trade, plus both first-round draft picks.

item.title: As far as I can tell, it's still unclear whether …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: As far as I can tell, it's still unclear whether Clippers are really open to moving Kawhi & whether Kawhi is really open to a Toronto reunion or if they're just posturing. Those are obviously key factors, in addition to what other teams are offering (tho Kawhi market is limited)

item.title: Should also note, as I have before: in 13 years under …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Should also note, as I have before: in 13 years under this Masai Ujiri and now Bobby Webster-led front office, the Raptors have never moved multiple 1st-round picks in a trade. Don't expect them to do it for a 35-year-old Kawhi Leonard.

item.title: Warriors head coach Steve Kerr met with Robinson after …
item.description: Now as he enters his 11th season with the Warriors, Robinson is about to step into a new role as the team’s NBA Summer League head coach. Warriors head coach Steve Kerr met with Robinson after the season ended and told him the news, and that he thought manning the sidelines throughout summer league would be a great opportunity for Robinson's personal and professional career development as the next challenge. Naming Khalid Robinson as the Warriors’ summer league head coach falls in line with how

item.title: Jordan Clarkson could return to Knicks?
item.description: Don’t rule out a Jordan Clarkson reunion. Despite James Dolan’s mandate to stay under the second apron, Clarkson’s re-signing is “still a possibility,” a source told The Post. Clarkson, 34, will become an unrestricted free agent Tuesday after one eventful season with the Knicks, which ended with a championship but included erratic playing time.

item.title: The Raptors' interest in Kawhi was described to me as …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: The Raptors' interest in Kawhi was described to me as "real but conditional." Contingent on price, obviously. Hard to see them paying more than last time, so think similar framework: secondary star (Ingram), former lottery pick (Dick), 1st-rounder in the following year's draft.

item.title: Portland Trail Blazers are adding Mike Williams as an …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Portland Trail Blazers are adding Mike Williams as an assistant coach. Williams moves to the front of the bench in Portland on coach Micah Nori’s staff. He’s been an assistant coach with the Jazz, a G League head coach, and Wizards player development assistant coach.

item.title: On Friday, June 26, "Captain Clutch" Brunson made a …
item.description: Mariska Hargitay is usually the one cheering for Jalen Brunson. But the New York Knicks captain and MVP definitely returned the favor in a big way. On Friday, June 26, "Captain Clutch" Brunson made a surprise appearance during Hargitay's performance in Every Brilliant Thing, the hit solo play she's starring in on Broadway through Sunday, July 5.

item.title: David West: 'The nerd influence is too much'
item.description: David West: I am neither insecure nor lack the intelligence to understand data. Im a fan of the game, a lifer if you will, but the nerd influence is too much. Style, nuance, individualism, mid range, post play, all gone. Dweeb based basketball is predictable. 3s or 2s, nothing else.

item.title: League revenue, however, will flow overwhelmingly to …
item.description: Sportico spoke recently with multiple people involved in building NBA Europe. Under the condition of anonymity, they broke down the economic structure of the league, which is planning to launch in October 2027. At the outset, league equity will be split 50/50 between the NBA and the owners of the 12 initial permanent franchises. League revenue, however, will flow overwhelmingly to the teams themselves, largely in the form of fixed distributions and performance payments, the people said.

item.title: League is committed to funding all of the NBA Europe's losses as it gets off the ground
item.description: The NBA is still finalizing how exactly to allocate the billions raised in the initial team sales, but much of it will be placed on the NBA Europe balance sheet for initial operating capital and reserves. As a result, the NBA is committed to funding all of the new league’s losses as it gets off the ground, the people said, insulating franchise owners from capital calls as the new entity tries to scale into a profitable, standalone operation. The NBA will take a cut of league revenue once there i

item.title: The current plan is to launch NBA Europe next year …
item.description: The current plan is to launch NBA Europe next year with 12 permanent franchises and four clubs that secure rotating slots via competition in other FIBA circuits. For those first dozen teams, the group is speaking with bidders in Athens, Barcelona, Berlin, Istanbul, London, Lyon, Madrid, Manchester, Milan, Munich, Paris and Rome. That’s a footprint that intentionally indexes heavily on Western Europe, which has wealthier markets even if it might be further removed from the continent’s more ardent

item.title: The permanent franchise sales will produce billions in …
item.description: The permanent franchise sales will produce billions in capital, with some bids involving London and Paris already topping $1 billion, sources said. While the NBA hasn’t finalized exactly how that money will be divvied up, the majority of it will be used to fund the league’s upfront costs and early operations, the people said. That includes team distributions, reserves, marketing capital and staffing. A portion could also be distributed to existing NBA owners as a dividend of sorts.

item.title: NBA Europe franchise bids for London and Paris already topping $1 billion
item.description: NBA Europe’s central revenue will include bigger media packages, league-wide sponsorships, merchandise and licensing opportunities, and perhaps some infrastructure projects. Those revenues will be largely transferred back to the teams, essentially via a preferred distribution, the sources said. Initially, more than 80% will go to teams via a fixed payment made to all clubs (similar to the NFL’s annual distributions) and a performance pool, which will filter more money to teams based on their on-

item.title: In the first six years the league is planning more …
item.description: That’s an example of league economics modeled more on traditional European soccer than the NBA or other U.S. circuits. In the first six years the league is planning more than $1.5 billion in fixed and performance distributions to its clubs, one source said. The rotating clubs will share equally in those payments, per sources. The NBA and its bankers have told prospective buyers that they expect the league to be profitable within its first decade, sources said. Once there is positive net income,

item.title: The league is expecting to announce winning groups in …
item.description: There’s also the possibility that some bidders will look to purchase existing teams as part of their push for NBA Europe inclusion. Given the fact that bid complexity (and number of bidders) will vary widely by market, the league is expecting to announce winning groups in a staggered manner as they’re finalized, the source said.

item.title: "The priorities for the Pistons this summer is to …
item.description: Marc Stein: "The priorities for the Pistons this summer is to re-sign Duren and to do an extension with Ausar Thompson. And we haven't even talked about restricted free agency that there are some really good restricted free agents. Duren, your guy Peyton Watson, Walker Kessler, who of course we know the Lakers would love to try to acquire if possible."

item.title: Tim Hardaway Jr., whose father Tim Sr. partnered with …
item.description: Impending free agent guard Tim Hardaway Jr., whose father Tim Sr. partnered with Alonzo Mourning as linchpins on the Heat’s best teams of the 20th century, has emerged as an expected Heat target in the coming days. The Heat will have interest in Hardaway when free agency begins at 6 p.m. on Tuesday, according to a source. He’s among a select group of skilled shooters that Miami is expected to call. Other potential targets have not been confirmed, but Miami has been strongly linked to veteran NBA

item.title: Cameron Boozer: Memphis is going to really surprise people this year
item.description: Cameron Boozer on the Grizzlies' potential for the upcoming season: "We are a team who is going to really surprise people this year. We are going to be shockingly good to people. I think people don't really understand that just because obviously it was kind of a rebuild, but you look at the players we have, the roster who we are bringing in this year, and I think we are going to have something pretty special."

item.title: Cameron Boozer: "I don't cheat the game, I show up at …
item.description: Cameron Boozer: "I don't cheat the game, I show up at work, I have high energy and I think it starts with great coaching. I have always been surrounded by great coaches and players who really want to buy in and win. For me, I was always the best player on my team doing the right things, and it just trickles down and everyone starts doing the right things. People talk about my winning, but you can't do that without a team."

item.title: "I confirmed tonight that Dave Joerger has interviewed …
item.description: Marc Stein: "I confirmed tonight that Dave Joerger has interviewed for the (Nuggets assistant) post, but here's the thing, Dave Joerger is also being pursued very heavily by Paris Basketball, which is the team that is run by former Timberwolves front office lead decision maker David Kahn."

item.title: On Sunday, I went to the Liberty-Sparks game on the …
item.description: The WNBA’s biggest problem is the gulf between how good it feels to be a WNBA fan and how toxic the league seems from the outside looking in. The environment inside a WNBA arena is palpably life-affirming. The fans are more friendly than those of other sports. The players are more accessible. On Sunday, I went to the Liberty-Sparks game on the 30th anniversary of their first meeting, the inaugural game of the WNBA. I felt the gravitas and gratitude of Lisa Leslie, who played in that inaugural cl

item.title: The WNBA has changed my brain chemistry in ways I …
item.description: This week, I had a conversation on my WNBA podcast with USA Today’s Callie Fin about how the league empowered her during a medically dangerous pregnancy. The WNBA has changed my brain chemistry in ways I couldn’t have imagined. I show up to the arena less self-conscious, more authentic. I’ve had conversations about identity, community, and ambition around this league that simply do not happen in many other corners of professional sports. This is a league that’s anchored by vital fan communities

item.title: Knicks to pursue Kevon Looney?
item.description: If Robinson does, in fact, depart New York amid the Knicks' well-chronicled financial constraints as the dreaded second apron closes in, sources say that the newly crowned champions are expected to try to bring on a veteran center with plenty of playoff experience: Former Warriors big man Kevon Looney.

item.title: The Nuggets still have a great deal of confidence that …
item.description: While chatter will likely continue until there’s another long-term commitment from Nikola Jokic to the Denver Nuggets, team sources tell The Athletic the 31-year-old is still signaling a desire to remain in the Mile High City past his current contract. Those sources, granted anonymity to speak candidly on the matter, say the Nuggets still have a great deal of confidence that Jokic’s stated plan to be “a Nugget forever” that was shared in late April remains intact.

item.title: Simmons: Jaylen Brown acting like he's done with Celtics
item.description: According to The Ringer’s Bill Simmons, folks around him during the French festival noted that Brown appears to have mentally moved on from Boston already. “The word on the street on Jaylen in France, and I know multiple people who were either in his vicinity or whatever, is that he was acting like he’s done in Boston, and that this is it, and I don’t think he’s very happy with this,” Simmons said during Sunday’s episode of his podcast.

item.title: The Clippers naturally trying to extract more than has …
item.description: Dialogue between the teams continued Sunday, league sources say, with the Clippers naturally trying to extract more than has been offered if they indeed are parting with their Face of the Franchise, while the Raptors remain adamant about trying to build a deal — which also would naturally include a level of draft capital — around scoring forward Brandon Ingram and Gradey Dick rather than Canadian-born swingman RJ Barrett.

item.title: The Raptors have discussed the possibility of a trade …
item.description: There are plenty of moving parts and some contradictory information kicking around, but two sources close to the situation that I spoke with on Sunday confirmed that the Raptors have discussed the possibility of a trade with the Los Angeles Clippers that would bring the 2026 second-team all-NBA selection back to Toronto. The Raptors' interest in Leonard was first reported by Marc Stein and Jake Fischer.

item.title: The Clippers, furthermore, would have interest in …
item.description: Sources say that Leonard's willingness to sign an extension with the Raptors were he to be traded back to Toronto, as The Stein Line also reported Thursday, has opened the door for a trade to progress to completion. If the Clippers, that is, can ultimately bear to move on from The Kawhi Era. It's believed that the Clippers, furthermore, would have interest in Toronto's offer because adding Ingram — who earned All-Star status last season — should be able to help keep LA sufficiently competitive i

item.title: The most likely construction of the deal would centre …
item.description: The most likely construction of the deal would centre on a package including 2026 all-star Brandon Ingram and third-year wing Gradey Dick, which would work in terms of salary matching, per one source. The Raptors would likely have to add some draft picks as additional compensation.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard trying to strong-arm his way out of Clippers?
item.description: Leonard has one year and $50.3 million left on his contract. According to sources, the 35-year-old is unlikely to get an extension from the Clippers and is open to finding a team that would extend his deal beyond the upcoming season. “The Clippers aren’t going to commit to anything with him, so it looks like he’s trying to strong-arm his way out of there,” said one source.

item.title: Said another: “The Clippers are willing to move him … …
item.description: Said another: “The Clippers are willing to move him … and I think a lot of teams are wary of acquiring Kawhi because you just don't know if he's going to play … there’s risk there, obviously. Toronto had him, and so maybe feels more comfortable that he would play for them, could play for them.”

item.title: Clippers exploring re-signing John Collins amid interest from Spurs, Magic, and 76ers
item.description: We're also hearing that the Clippers might elect to try to re-sign forward John Collins, who as The Stein Line previously reported is expected to draw interest from the likes of San Antonio, Orlando and Philadelphia once free agency formally begins Tuesday night at 6 PM ET.

item.title: Suns to extend Miles Bridged contract eventually
item.description: John Gambadoro: This Suns trade for Miles Bridges came about late last night. The plan is that Bridges will not just be here for one year - at some point not sure when they would like to extend him. To replace Grayson Allen the Suns are looking at 7 to 8 free agent shooting guards. So, another move coming here in free agency soon.

item.title: Sources are telling me that he’s expected to attract …
item.description: The chatter is building momentum as the NBA heads into the opening of its free agency period on Tuesday night. The Raptors have some business to focus on there, too, with power forward Sandro Mamukelashvili expected to opt out of the final year of his $2.8-million deal on Monday. Sources are telling me that he’s expected to attract multiple offers starting at more than $10 million a year, which could make it challenging for the Raptors to keep him.

item.title: Clippers interested in Goga Bitadze
item.description: Word is that the Clippers are also among the teams that have expressed trade interest in Orlando's Goga Bitadze. The Stein Line has since learned, though, that the Magic have rebuffed inquires for the Georgian big man to this point. It appears that Orlando wants to retain Bitadze in part because it is bracing for the loss of Moritz Wagner, brother of Magic star Franz Wagner, in unrestricted free agency.

item.title: Lakers eyeing Ziaire Williams
item.description: According to a league source, one player the Lakers could take a look at in free agency is Brooklyn small forward Zaire Williams, who had his team option declined by the Nets on Sunday. The Lakers have desires to get younger and more athletic on the wing, and Williams is coming off back-to-back seasons in Brooklyn where he averaged more than 10 points and shot better than 34 percent from 3-point range.

item.title: Brooklyn declining Ziaire Williams' $6.25 million team …
item.description: Brooklyn declining Ziaire Williams' $6.25 million team option, as reported by HoopsHype and the New York Post, could be a prelude to the Nets re-signing him to a longer deal using their room exception once they're done using all their cap space. For now, the Nets have $36 million in functional cap room.

item.title: Team sources say that there has been no mandate from …
item.description: Denver wants to re-sign restricted free agent forward Peyton Watson and has been widely expected to shed salary in order to make that move more feasible. Denver also wants to re-sign 25-year-old small forward Spencer Jones. However, team sources say that there has been no mandate from Nuggets ownership to cut costs. As such, it remains to be seen how aggressively — or perhaps delicately — the Nuggets move forward.

item.title: Heat to guarantee Dru Smith's salary
item.description: The Heat will guarantee Smith’s full $2.6 million salary for next season, according to a league source. Miami had a Sunday deadline of 11:59 p.m. to waive Smith and shed his $2.6 million salary cap hit for this upcoming season.

item.title: No player in the NBA lowers the points that his …
item.description: Jeremias Engelmann: No player in the NBA lowers the points that his teammates score, while on the court with him, more than Jaylen Brown He lops off 4 points, per 100, from each of the 4 teammates that share the court with him

item.title: In contrast, a jump in Jaylen Brown's scoring output …
item.description: David Lee: stars tend to suppress their teammates' PPG, but they pair that scoring usage with hyper-efficiency (Giannis/prime Joel), influence high quality shots (Wemby), and/or suppress TOs (Luka). in contrast, a jump in Jaylen Brown's scoring output leads to diminishing returns for BOS👇

item.title: Nobody has won more combined regular season and …
item.description: Jaylen Brown: Nobody has won more combined regular season and playoff games since I entered the league 10 years ago

item.title: Analytics have / are ruining the game we playing AI …
item.description: Jaylen Brown: Analytics have / are ruining the game we playing AI hoops

item.title: Raptors, Clippers seriously engaged in Kawhi Leonard trade talks
item.description: The LA Clippers and Toronto Raptors are seriously engaged in trade talks for All-NBA forward Kawhi Leonard, sources told ESPN. LA and Toronto discussed a deal over the past couple of weeks, as Leonard's reps informed other teams he only wanted to sign an extension with the Raptors if the Clippers were not going to keep him, sources said. He had preferred to stay with the Clippers, but the franchise made no long-term commitment to him this offseason, sources said. Leonard has only the 2026-27 sea

item.title: Byron Scott says everybody thinks it’s LeBron James …
item.description: NBA Courtside: Byron Scott says everybody thinks it’s LeBron James decision to stay at the Lakers, but the organization should say we’re moving on: “We’re gonna start looking at other options to bring in to make us a better organization.” (Via Byron Scott’s Fast Break)

item.title: Byron Scott says LeBron James is not a top 5 All-Time …
item.description: Byron Scott says LeBron James is not a top 5 All-Time Lakers player, he might be top 10: “I’m going by if you put that purple and gold there’s a different standard.
