So, I’m going to elaborate on a little bit. There’s a different standard and you look at the top guys, the Kareem, the Magic’s, the Kobe’s, the Shaq’s, you know, guys like that. Those guys got multiple championships with this organization.”

item.title: The Denver Nuggets have tendered qualifying offers to …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: The Denver Nuggets have tendered qualifying offers to Spencer Jones and Peyton Watson, a league source told @spotrac.com Both Jones and Watson will now be restricted free agents this offseason.

item.title: LeBron James has played with or against 37.1% of all …
item.description: Crazy Stats: LeBron James has played with or against 37.1% of all @NBA players in history, that includes 200+ opponents from 4 different decades of draft classes (pre-2000, 2001-10, 2011-20 and 2021-current). Moreover, James appeared in 28.8% of all NBA seasons.

item.title: Windhorst: LeBron James has not made himself available to meet with the Lakers yet
item.description: Heat Central: LeBron James HAS NOT made himself available to meet with the Lakers, per @WindhorstESPN 👀 “They reached out to LeBron representative Rich Paul and said ‘we’d like to have LeBron Back.’ But there have been no further talks. From what I understand, the Lakers have not made an offer. But LeBron, from what I understand as of today, when I got this update, LeBron has not, you know, made himself available to meet with the Lakers yet.”

item.title: "Kawhi Leonard's preference is to stay with the …
item.description: Marc Stein: "Kawhi Leonard's preference is to stay with the Clippers. And the Clippers would prefer to keep him, but this is the last year of his deal and they got to come to an extension and it's not clear if they can come to an agreement on an extension."

item.title: Celtics were already shopping Jaylen Brown before Giannis talks heated up?
item.description: Brown hasn't said how he feels about the Celtics openly shopping him in trade scenarios, which sources have said took place with other teams even before the talks for Antetokounmpo heated up. The Celtics have three choices: (1) trade Brown to another team, which so far hasn't yielded strong offers; (2) extend Brown, hoping the financial commitment will assuage any hard feelings; or (3) bring him back without an extension and try to make him comfortable continuing on in this state of limbo.

item.title: “I think the Cavs might have been involved a little …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: “I think the Cavs might have been involved a little bit, I heard in the Miles Bridges discussions,” - @WindhorstESPN on the Cavs. Windy notes that the Cavs could trade Max Strus for an upgrade.

item.title: If an analytic dude said that and he told you, why …
item.description: Paul Pierce: Paul Pierce on the comments saying Jaylen Brown analytics wise he’s the 7th best player on the Celtics: “I mean this is getting ridiculous. It’s gotten to the point where I’m no longer going to start watching any of the networks about sports. Like seriously this is just come on. I just feel like it’s clickbait. It doesn’t make sense. Like don’t even make that public. If an analytic dude said that and he told you, why would you make that public? Because it sounds silly. Jaylen Brown

item.title: James Harden opts out of deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Cleveland Cavaliers' James Harden is declining his $42.3 million player option for 2026-27 and the sides are working through a new multiyear deal together, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: There's a small pocket of execs and agents who believe …
item.description: There's a small pocket of execs and agents who believe the Celtics are bluffing with Brown, but the prevailing thought is they want to move on this -- soon.

item.title: Teams want draft compensation in order to pull the trigger on Ja Morant trade
item.description: The Memphis Grizzlies have been open to dealing Morant since the trade deadline, and in the past few days their asking price has dropped considerably from draft capital before last week's draft to pick swaps. Now they're now trying to match player for player in deals. Teams have asked the Grizzlies for draft compensation in order to take on Morant, so the gulf has been considerable, especially with so many point guards going early in Round 1 of the draft.

item.title: Team sources said the Bucks would be OK keeping Herro …
item.description: Team sources said the Bucks would be OK keeping Herro on the roster, but execs around the league have told ESPN that Milwaukee has been scouring the trade market to see what it could get in return for him. Flipping Herro for more draft picks or young players could potentially expand the Bucks' return for Antetokounmpo and further their rebuild.

item.title: Despite the Clippers' insisting they did nothing …
item.description: Despite the Clippers' insisting they did nothing wrong, team officials seem less sure about the outcome of the high-profile investigation and whether the NBA's richest owner will face punishment, according to sources with knowledge of their thinking. So far, no public evidence has emerged that conclusively proves the primary allegation that the Clippers' circumvented the cap to compensate Leonard, and it's unclear whether any such evidence has emerged in the investigation, either. In September 2

item.title: Denver does have some wiggle room with the $10m …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Denver does have some wiggle room with the $10m contract or Jonas Valanciunas. $2m is guaranteed with the balance protected if not waived by 7/8

item.title: Bob Iger and venture capitalist Joshua Kushner—the …
item.description: Former Disney CEO Bob Iger and venture capitalist Joshua Kushner—the younger brother of President Donald Trump’s son-in-law Jared—are in talks to make a bid for a new NBA team based in Las Vegas, according to Bloomberg, just months after the league announced plans to explore an expansion.

item.title: If traded, Kawhi Leonard would only sign extension with Raptors
item.description: NBA Courtside: JUST IN: The Toronto Raptors are the only team Kawhi Leonard will extend with if he’s traded from the Clippers, per @ShamsCharania

item.title: Fred VanVleet will not hit free agency
item.description: Shams Charania: Houston Rockets guard Fred VanVleet is opting into his $25 million player option for the 2026-27 season, Klutch Sports CEO Rich Paul tells ESPN.

item.title: The Golden State Warriors have tendered a qualifying …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: The Golden State Warriors have tendered a qualifying offer to Quinten Post and two-way qualifying offers to Taran Armstrong and Nico Mannion, a league source told @spotrac.com . All three players will now be restricted free agents this offseason.

item.title: Two of the most valuable rookie cards for Shohei …
item.description: Two of the most valuable rookie cards for Shohei Ohtani and LeBron James sold through Goldin Auctions on Sunday night, as money continues to flood into the sports card market. Ohtani’s 2018 Topps Chrome #150 SuperFractor, the one-of-a-kind parallel of one of his top rookie cards, sold for $2.56 million, including the buyer’s premium. This exact card last sold in 2022 for $139,200. It is now the second highest publicly known sale of an Ohtani trading card behind his one-of-one 2025 Topps Chrome G

item.title: Malik Beasley, Ed Davis indicted on sports gambling charges
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Malik Beasley, a veteran NBA guard, and former lottery pick Ed Davis have been indicted on sports gambling charges by federal prosecutors in the US Attorney's Office for the Eastern District of New York.

item.title: The government is coordinating a voluntary surrender …
item.description: Shams Charania: Nine-year NBA veteran Malik Beasley has been indicted on federal charges for gambling related to a sports betting scheme, such as point shaving and prop bets, his attorney Steve Haney tells ESPN. The government is coordinating a voluntary surrender of Beasley this week.

item.title: “The notion of them trying to trade for AD first as a …
item.description: To sweeten the deal, journalist Kevin O’Connor reported that Davis could also be lured to Dub Nation. However, NBA insider Marc Stein was quick to pump the brakes on those reports, explaining why such a blockbuster move is far from materializing. “Based on everything I was told tonight, I don’t think there is anything active there,” Marc Stein stated on The All-NBA Podcast. “The notion of them trying to trade for AD first as a lure for LeBron, hard to see that happening in that sequence… I think

item.title: Andrew Nembhard has been named to Canada’s Senior …
item.description: ‪Tony East‬: Pacers guard Andrew Nembhard has been named to Canada’s Senior Men’s National Team training camp roster ahead of upcoming FIBA Men’s World Cup Qualifiers.

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander on watching Canada in the FIFA …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander on watching Canada in the FIFA World Cup: "Watching their games & their success, it's been inspiring. To see the growth in that program & where it's come in a short amount of time, it's been inspiring, it's been fun, and it makes you proud to be Canadian."

item.title: "I think he's very unrattled and that's why he's the …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Dillon Brooks on guarding SGA in the 1st round of the playoffs: "I wanted to tackle him." 😂 "I think he's very unrattled and that's why he's the captain of our team. Super composed, knows the game."

item.title: Zach LaVine opts into player option
item.description: Shams Charania: Sacramento Kings guard Zach LaVine is opting into his $49 million player option for the 2026-27 season, Klutch Sports CEO Rich Paul tells ESPN.

item.title: Lou Williams on rumors of the Clippers trading Kawhi …
item.description: Run It Back: Lou Williams on rumors of the Clippers trading Kawhi to the Raptors: "I didn't see this on my bingo card, but it makes sense to me." 👀 @MichelleDBeadle | @ChandlerParsons | @TeamLou23

item.title: They can re-sign Tari Eason and offer Marcus Smart a …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: Houston Rockets salary cap situation with Fred VanVleet opting in. They can re-sign Tari Eason and offer Marcus Smart a significant portion of their non-tax mid-level exception while remaining under the first apron if they clear money first, such as Dorian Finney-Smith.

item.title: Federal prosecutors say Beasley had millions of …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Federal prosecutors say Beasley had millions of dollars in gambling losses and Davis loaned him money. Beasley, prosecutors say, worked off that debt as part of this betting scheme. The scheme included 4 games during the 2023-24 season while Beasley was on the Bucks, feds allege

item.title: Beasley is accused of also working with bettors to hit …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Beasley is accused of also working with bettors to hit the over on some of his stats. They bet on him to hit the over on rebounds in a March 10 Bucks-Clippers game. He did that in the last few seconds. The group celebrated afterward.

item.title: Draymond Green's opt-out gives Warriors room to pursue Anthony Davis and recruit LeBron James
item.description: Shams Charania: The opt-out comes days after the Warriors said they expect Green to pick up the $27.7M option. Instead Green delivers Golden State time and space to pursue Davis with the Wizards and convince James to join Stephen Curry, Green and his old Lakers championship teammate in the Bay.

item.title: “This is potentially a very big domino in this NBA …
item.description: Brian Windhorst on what Draymond opting out means for LeBron: “This is potentially a very big domino in this NBA offseason… This opens the door for the Warriors to use their MLE with $15M… The Lakers want to meet with him and give him their plan but the way it was explained to me, LeBron has been unavailable for a meeting with the Lakers… I would just say this, stay tuned.”

item.title: They've got $48 million below the first apron to …
item.description: ‪Yossi Gozlan‬: Golden State Warriors salary cap situation after Draymond Green opted out, clearing $27.7 million off their payroll. They've got $48 million below the first apron to budget re-signing him, Kristaps Porzingis, and LeBron James with the $15 million non-tax mid-level exception.

item.title: We know Atlanta isn’t going after Goga Bitadze, Hawks …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Two trades that we’re monitoring today… The Clippers and Raptors’continuing conversations on Kawhi Leonard, plus the Hawks working on a big addition. We know Atlanta isn’t going after Goga Bitadze, Hawks fans, as the Magic aren’t talking trade for their reserve center, sources say, while anticipating Mo Wagner will leave Orlando in free agency.

item.title: Draymond Green declines player option
item.description: Shams Charania: Golden State Warriors forward Draymond Green is declining his $27.7 million player option to become a free agent, sources tell ESPN. This move gives the Warriors flexibility to pursue LeBron James in free agency and Anthony Davis via trade to form a Big 4.

item.title: The Warriors have A LOT more flexibility now for …
item.description: Kevin O'Connor: This is a huge development. The Warriors have A LOT more flexibility now for pursuing LeBron James AND Anthony Davis, which as I reported last night is their plan.

item.title: The move will likely open up the full $15M non tax …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Golden State was $20M below the first apron prior to Green declining his option. The move will likely open up the full $15M non tax midlevel exception to use and still have enough flexibility to build the bench.

item.title: "We’ll review the indictment and vigorously the …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: "We’ll review the indictment and vigorously the charges and maintain Malik’s innocence,” Steve Haney, Beasley's lawyer, told The Athletic.

item.title: Federal prosecutors say Davis and Beasley got close …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: Federal prosecutors say Davis and Beasley got close after being teammates on the Timberwolves Davis texted Beasley: "Only way you can beat Vegas is sports betting. Everything else they got the edge" Beasley agreed to manipulate his play during 4 games during 2023-24 season.

item.title: Sandro Mamukelashvili declining player option to hit free agency
item.description: Shams Charania: Toronto Raptors big man Sandro Mamukelashvili is declining his $2.8 million player option for 2026-27 to become a free agent, George Roussakis and Mark Bartelstein of @PrioritySports tell ESPN. Mamukelashvili will have a strong developing market in free agency.

item.title: Nets to sign Day'Ron Sharpe to two-year deal
item.description: Michael Scotto: Breaking: The Brooklyn Nets plan to sign Day’Ron Sharpe to a two-year deal worth roughly $20 million, sources told @hoopshype . Sharpe has impressed Brooklyn and is expected to have a bigger role this season. Agents Aaron Reilly and Reggie Berry of @AMRAgency finalized the deal.

item.title: Nets to sign Josh Minott to a new contract
item.description: Shams Charania: Brooklyn Nets forward Josh Minott intends to sign a new two-year, $9 million to return to the franchise, with a team option in second season, sources tell ESPN. The Nets declined Minott’s team option to clear the way for a new contract.

item.title: The Brooklyn Nets plan to sign Josh Minott to a …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Brooklyn Nets plan to sign Josh Minott to a two-year, $9 million deal. Team option Year 2. He averaged 10.8 points on 49% shooting from the field and 40% from 3-point range in 19.3 minutes for Brooklyn last season. Agent Aaron Turner and Nets officials did the deal.

item.title: Deandre Ayton to opt into player option for 2026-27 season to return to Lakers
item.description: Shams Charania: Los Angeles Lakers center Deandre Ayton is opting into his $8.1 million player option for the 2026-27 season, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: LeBron James, Lakers have yet to discuss future, but that's expected to change following Warriors reports
item.description: Chris Haynes: LeBron James and Los Angeles Lakers still have yet to have a conversation regarding his future but that’s expected to change in light of Golden State Warriors’ strategic maneuvering to position themselves as a landing spot, sources tell me.

item.title: Raptors want Mamu back and he wants to come back but, …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Raptors want Mamu back and he wants to come back but, with their tax crunch & (cough) aspirations in the trade market, they’ll need to dump salary to make a competitive offer. He may have priced himself out of a return with his strong season (similar to Biyombo situation in 2016)

item.title: Nets could still have around $40M in cap space to use …
item.description: Keith Smith: Nets could still have around $40M in cap space to use in free agency, pending how they time out acquiring Julius Randle. The signing of Day'Ron Sharpe will come after Nets have used their cap space, either through the Room Exception or remaining cap space, similiar to last year.

item.title: Sharpe had a $6.25m team option
item.description: Bobby Marks: Sharpe had a $6.25m team option. To maximize spending power, Brooklyn can use the $9.4M room midlevel after cap space is exhausted.

item.title: I can confirm, via his lawyer Steve Haney, that Malik …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: I can confirm, via his lawyer Steve Haney, that Malik Beasley has been indicted on five counts including sports betting, money laundering, and wire fraud. They “look forward to defending all charges,” he says.

item.title: Raptors exercising player option on Trayce Jackson-Davis
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Toronto Raptors are exercising the $2.41 million team option on Trayce Jackson-Davis, league sources told @hoopshype . The 26-year-old forward has averaged 6.2 points on 63.1% shooting from the field and 4.4 rebounds in 14.2 minutes in 3 seasons with the Warriors and Raptors.

item.title: The Toronto Raptors are opting into Trayce …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Toronto Raptors are opting into Trayce Jackson-Davis's $2.4 million team option for the 2026-27 season, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Nets to exercise team option on Malachi Smith
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Brooklyn Nets will exercise the $2.15 million team option on Malachi Smith, which is fully non-guaranteed for the 2026-27 season, league sources told @hoopshype . Smith averaged 8.3 points, 3.4 rebounds and 3.3 assists per game for Brooklyn last season.

item.title: The Heat's newest addition spent last season playing …
item.description: Barry Jackson: The Heat's newest addition spent last season playing for Clemson's football team. Ian Schieffelin, a 6-8 forward, accepted an offer to play on the Heat's summer league team. He started 99 games and appeared in 134 for Clemson's basketball team over 4 years (averaged 12.4 points, 9.4 rebounds in 2024-25), then played football last season after using up his hoops eligibility. He caught 2 passes for 10 yards, playing 20 snaps for Dabo Swinney's team.

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Dillon Brooks have been …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander and Dillon Brooks have been named Team Canada co-captains, according to head coach Gordie Herbert.

item.title: Andrew Wiggins opts into player option for 2026-27 with Miami, signs three-year, $64 million contract with Heat
item.description: Jake Fischer: Miami Heat swingman Andrew Wiggins has opted in for his $30.2 million salary this 2026-27 season, sources say.

item.title: Miami Heat forward Andrew Wiggins intends to a sign a …
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: Miami Heat forward Andrew Wiggins intends to a sign a three-year, $64 million contract to stay with the franchise, with a player option in the third season, sources tell ESPN. Heat executives and Wiggins' agents at CAA sports Steven Heumann and Andrew Morrison, as well as Richard Clarke, came to terms on a critical commitment.

item.title: Wiggins will serve as a key starter alongside Heat …
item.description: Shams Charania: Wiggins will serve as a key starter alongside Heat stars Giannis Antetokounmpo and Bam Adebayo -- and Miami received his commitment on a $64M deal through 2028-29 (player option in 2028). He opts in for $30M in 2026-27 as part of the deal.

item.title: It'll be much harder to re-sign Norman Powell without …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Miami Heat salary cap situation with Andrew Wiggins opting in, Pelle Larsson opted in, Dru Smith guaranteed, and Jahmir Young opted out. They have $20.5 million under the hard cap to fill five roster spots. It'll be much harder to re-sign Norman Powell without additional moves.

item.title: Mavericks plan to bring back Ryan Nembhard next season by exercising team option
item.description: Marc Stein: The Mavericks plan to exercise guard Ryan Nembhard's $2.2 million team option for next season before today's 5 PM ET deadline, league sources say.

item.title: Pacers to bring back Micah Potter by picking up team option
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Indiana Pacers plan to exercise the $2.80 million team option on Micah Potter, which is fully non-guaranteed for the 2026-27 season and have extended a two-way qualifying offer to Jalen Slawson, league sources told @hoopshype .

item.title: The Sixers will exercise the $3.4 million team option …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Sixers will exercise the $3.4 million team option for Dominick Barlow, league sources tell @TheSteinLine .

item.title: GG Jackson, Olivier-Maxence Prosper returning to Memphis as team picks up their player options
item.description: Jake Fischer: The same goes for Memphis and GG Jackson’s $2.4 million club option.

item.title: The Grizzlies are picking up the $2.5 million team …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Grizzlies are picking up the $2.5 million team option for Olivier-Maxence Prosper, according to league sources.

item.title: 76ers to decline team option on Trendon Watford, allowing him to hit unrestricted free agency
item.description: Tony Jones: The Philadelphia 76ers have declined the team option on forward Trendon Watford, league sources tell The Athletic. Watford now becomes an unrestricted free agent

item.title: Marcus Smart declines player option and will enter unrestricted free agency
item.description: Chris Haynes: Los Angeles Lakers guard Marcus Smart is declining his $5.4M player option to become an unrestricted free agent, league sources tell me.

item.title: Los Angeles Lakers guard Marcus Smart will decline his …
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: Los Angeles Lakers guard Marcus Smart will decline his $5.39 million player option for the 2026-27 season and enter unrestricted free agency, league sources told @hoopshype . Smart started 54 of 62 games played last season for the Lakers. He’ll be coveted in free agency.

item.title: Ben Simmons planning NBA comeback, mentions Miami and Philadelphia as potential landing spots
item.description: The plan is to stay in L.A. for a few days and then return to Miami, where he’ll ramp up his training: two and a half to three hours of lifting and body work, as well as hour-and-a-half sessions on the court, with a day or two off every week. He hopes to be back to full strength by his 30th birthday, July 20. He’s now an unrestricted free agent, and I ask him explicitly if he’s planning to play in the NBA next year. “I plan on getting as strong as I can physically, getting my ass on the court, a

item.title: “You can’t be in a state where you’re in a high-stress …
item.description: Ben Simmons says it took him a long time to realize that it was also a nervous system issue. “You can’t be in a state where you’re in a high-stress situation all the time, especially trying to recover from a back injury,” he says. “I would’ve rather gone through a broken foot again, ’cause it would have been clear and easy.”

item.title: When I ask Simmons why he didn’t just tell people how …
item.description: This is why Simmons finds it particularly frustrating when people question his love of the game or his competitiveness, or constantly evaluate his game through the lens of how he once played. “The joy that I play with comes from being healthy,” he says. “How do you love something when you’re fucked up? How do you love something that you do when you’re incapable of doing it?” When I ask Simmons why he didn’t just tell people how much pain he was in, he says that he didn’t want it to sound like an

item.title: “Nah, there’s no pressure,” he says. “I had pressure …
item.description: Simmons seems to be in a good place mentally now, but with all the scrutiny that comes from being Ben Simmons, I wanted to know if there’s added pressure for this comeback. “Nah, there’s no pressure,” he says. “I had pressure when I was injured. People are gonna say the same thing every single time. It’s like, All right, well, if that’s what you think, then that’s what you think.” He thinks people are quick to forget the impact he could have on the court in his best moments, the way he contribut

item.title: So, Wiggins will make $30.2 million this upcoming …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: So, Wiggins will make $30.2 million this upcoming season. Then he agreed a two-year, $34 million extension through the 2028-29 season.

item.title: This gets him under contract for $17 M range next …
item.description: Barry Jackson: OK, clarity on all this, from @Anthony_Chiang and me: Wiggins will get $30 M this season; he opted in (as @JakeFischer said). This gets him under contract for $17 M range next season.

item.title: So Heat can use about $12 M of its $15.1 M midlevel …
item.description: Barry Jackson: So Heat can use about $12 M of its $15.1 M midlevel exception this season and remain under $209 M hard cap - presuming the other players they sign are on minimum deals. Wiggins' 3 year, $64 M deal includes an opt out for 2028-29. So Heat gets Wiggins guaranteed for 2 years in front-loaded deal.

item.title: According to NBA source the major giveback from …
item.description: Ira Winderman: According to NBA source the major giveback from Wiggins is in the first and second years of that two year-extension, taking a reduction to $16.5 million in the first year and then $17.5 million in second year. So the saving vs. cap/tax not immediate, but rather down the road.

item.title: LeBron James prioritizing happiness over money in free agency?
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "It's been communicated to me that the number one thing for LeBron (James) next season is happiness, not money. So this is obviously we knew that he's going to have to take a pay cut from 50 million. So that has been communicated to me. And what does that mean? I don't know. Does that mean he's willing to take the mid-level? Does that mean he's willing to take the taxpayer mid-level? The mid-level is about 15, the taxpayer mid-level is like six and a half or seven or something l

item.title: AJ Dybantsa: "The Wizards haven't been to the playoffs …
item.description: AJ Dybantsa: "The Wizards haven't been to the playoffs in some time, so I just want to make the playoffs year one. I think we have the team to do it, getting Anthony Davis, getting Trae Young and having our young core. But ultimate goal is I'll start getting D-Wade, I always wanted to be a Hall of Famer, but he says the goal I should have is to be a top 100 player. So I think 20 years from now, I answer the league 20 years from now, it will be the 100-year anniversary of the NBA. And you know, l

item.title: The Las Vegas Diamond Arena, one of the proposed sites …
item.description: The Las Vegas Diamond Arena, one of the proposed sites for a possible NBA franchise, announced Monday that it has purchased the last piece of land needed for the project. According to a release, the Diamond Arena has completed the purchase of an additional 3.03 acres, giving the project full control of its development site at the northeast corner of Las Vegas Boulevard and Four Seasons Drive, directly across from Mandalay Bay.

item.title: The completed purchase cements the project’s position …
item.description: The completed purchase cements the project’s position as a fully entitled and development-ready site. The proposed arena features 21,212 seats and is designed for NBA basketball, concerts, conventions, and major events. “By closing escrow on the final parcel, we now own the entire arena site and have solidified our position as the premier venue for a future NBA franchise in Las Vegas,” said Tom Letizia, spokesman for the Diamond Arena.

item.title: A memorial service was held Monday morning for Chicago …
item.description: A memorial service was held Monday morning for Chicago Bulls broadcaster and three-time NBA champion Stacey King. The service was held at the Advocate Center, a training facility close to the United Center.

item.title: Sam Amick: Celtics are asking for the sun and the moon in Jaylen Brown trade talks
item.description: Sam Amick on Jaylen Brown: "This one is, if I had to handicap it, it just feels like somebody has got to put enough on the table to get this thing done, but Boston is asking for the sun and the moon. I keep looking at Portland and because people keep bringing Portland up when I talk to them, they wanted to go big on Giannis Antetokounmpo and that did not work. Even without a commitment, they were willing to try to do a Giannis trade. And when you see that kind of boldness, then you kind of go, a

item.title: Kings had, before they drafted Darius Acuff, they had …
item.description: Sam Amick: I hate always talking, we talk about players like assets and that is just part of the economy of the game, but you constantly hear with Ja that he remains a negative value asset, that that is the way he is perceived. And so that means when teams are calling, and I have shared this before, like in my backyard, the Kings had, before they drafted Darius Acuff, they had legitimate interest in Ja (Morant), but it was always with the disclaimer that they wanted draft assets attached to him

item.title: Sixth Avenue and West 3 rd Street is being co-named …
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s Department of Transportation (DOT) is affixing signs in the Knicks’ royal blue and orange colors with each player’s name and jersey number to street poles up-and-down Manhattan’s Sixth and Seventh Avenues. For instance, Sixth Avenue and West 3 rd Street is being co-named for starting shooting guard Josh Hart, who wears number three, and Seventh Avenue South and West 11th Street is being co-named for team captain and point guard Jalen Brunson, who sports number 11.

item.title: Gov. Jenniffer González Colón welcomed NBA champion …
item.description: Gov. Jenniffer González Colón welcomed NBA champion José Alvarado of the New York Knicks on Saturday, joined by Puerto Rico Basketball Federation President Yum Ramos and Puerto Rico National Basketball Team General Manager Carlos Arroyo. Alvarado arrived on the island Thursday with his family and the NBA championship trophy.

item.title: José Alvarado is the third Puerto Rican to win an NBA …
item.description: “Today, Puerto Rico is not only welcoming an NBA champion but also a Puerto Rican who carries the island in his heart wherever he goes. José Alvarado is the third Puerto Rican to win an NBA championship, a feat made possible through his unique tenacity,” the governor said as she presented him with the Governor’s Medal and a proclamation.

item.title: Rich Paul met with Wizards to discuss Anthony Davis' future with the franchise
item.description: Anthony Davis' agent, Rich Paul, met with the Washington Wizards on Sunday to discuss his future with the franchise. Davis is eligible this offseason for a four-year extension worth up to $275 million this offseason with the Wizards. As the Wizards recently signed Trae Young to a four-year, $212 million contract, Davis is clearly gauging whether he could receive a similar commitment from the franchise. Davis has also been the subject of trade rumors involving the Golden State Warriors with a pot

item.title: "I'm sure that Anthony Davis could be given a contract …
item.description: "Anthony Davis just saw Trae Young get a $200 million new contract with the Wizards," said ESPN's Brian Windhorst on Monday's episode of Get Up. "Yesterday, Rich Paul, who represents Draymond Green and LeBron and Anthony Davis met with the Wizards to discuss the future. "I'm sure that Anthony Davis could be given a contract that would make him very happy to be in Washington. Maybe the Wizards are prepared to do that. The Wizards want to win immediately. "If not, the Warriors could potentially co

item.title: The Utah Jazz have officially issued the qualifying …
item.description: Tony Jones: The Utah Jazz have officially issued the qualifying offer to center Walker Kessler, league sources tell The Athletic. This is an expected move that allows Kessler to be a restricted free agent

item.title: Marcus Smart is now officially declining his $5.4 …
item.description: Marc Stein: Marcus Smart is now officially declining his $5.4 million player option with the Lakers for next season amid a growing expectation Houston is eager to reunite Smart and Ime Udoka once free agency begins Tuesday at 6 PM ET.

item.title: While the Heat's intention is to keep Andrew Wiggins, …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: While the Heat's intention is to keep Andrew Wiggins, he is still immediately eligible to be traded even after agreeing and eventually signing his two-year extension.

item.title: The deadline for the Heat to extend a qualifying offer …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: The deadline for the Heat to extend a qualifying offer to Keshad Johnson is today. But it looks like the Heat will bypass that qualifying offer and Johnson will become an unrestricted free agent.

item.title: Indications are Johnson will spend summer-league time …
item.description: Ira Winderman: Heat have one personnel decision remaining today, with it now looking as if there will not be a qualifying offer to Keshad Johnson, making him an unrestricted free agent Tuesday. Indications are Johnson will spend summer-league time with the Heat.

item.title: “He was completely upfront and honest with me,” said …
item.description: As he evaluated who would represent him, Jrue Holiday could have prioritized a sports agency that conducted an elaborate sales pitch. One that promised a lucrative contract regardless of whether Holiday matched his All-Star potential. One that promised hefty endorsements regardless of his marketability. Instead, Jason Glushon convinced Holiday to join his new independent sports agency in 2016 by avoiding any self-promotion or salesmanship. “He was completely upfront and honest with me,” said Hol

item.title: “It’s about being honest and fighting for our players …
item.description: Glushon conceded he lacks a “perfect answer” to explain how his independent sports agency has thrived in a competitive landscape. Glushon credited a former mentor (former longtime agent and current Detroit Pistons vice chairman Arn Tellem). He had extensive experience at Wasserman as an intern (2006-2009) and as an agent (2009-16). He praised his company’s head of sales and marketing (David Braun) and agent (Ryan Leff) for ensuring enough bandwidth to support their clients and families. But Holi

item.title: “It doesn’t have to be glamorous if it works,” Coward …
item.description: Shortly afterwards, the Grizzlies acquired Coward in a pre-draft trade with Portland as last year’s No. 11 pick. Coward then made the NBA’s All-Rookie First team for his positional versatility, team-oriented play and efficiency. That journey validated why Coward became intrigued with joining an independent sports agency. “It doesn’t have to be glamorous if it works,” Coward said. “As an agency, I think we’re growing each and every other day. [Ramasar’s] putting as much into this as he puts into

item.title: “We are in the process of reviewing the federal …
item.description: Tim Reynolds: NBA spokesman Mike Bass: “We are in the process of reviewing the federal indictment unsealed today involving Malik Beasley and Ed Davis. Beasley last played in the NBA during the 2024-25 season and Davis last played in the league during the 2021-22 season. (cont.)

item.title: “We will continue to investigate this matter and …
item.description: Tim Reynolds: “We will continue to investigate this matter and cooperate with the relevant authorities. We take these allegations with the utmost seriousness, and the integrity of our game remains our top priority.”

item.title: Clippers decline team options on Jordan Miller, Kobe Sanders
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Los Angeles Clippers have declined team options on Jordan Miller and Kobe Sanders for the 2026-27 season, league sources told @hoopshype. Instead, the Clippers have extended Miller and Sanders qualifying offers heading into free agency.

item.title: The Los Angeles Clippers have tendered Bennedict …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Los Angeles Clippers have tendered Bennedict Mathurin a qualifying offer, making him a restricted free agent, league sources told @hoopshype. The 24-year-old swingman averaged a career-high 17.6 points last season.

item.title: Keith Palek signed a summer league contract with the …
item.description: Keith Palek signed a summer league contract with the Knicks on June 25, 12 days after the Knicks defeated the San Antonio Spurs in the NBA Finals to end a 53-year Larry O’Brien trophy drought in Manhattan.

item.title: New Mavericks coach Dusty May expects to lean on Kyrie Irving in NBA coaching debut
item.description: Ron Harrod Jr: Mavs coach Dusty May said that he will lean on Kyrie Irving as a first-time NBA head coach. May said Irving is like a Jazz musician.

item.title: Dusty May said that his coaching staff will have a …
item.description: Ron Harrod Jr: Dusty May said that his coaching staff will have a college flair, but he will hire coaches with NBA experience too.

item.title: Hey, gotta love Dusty May’s candor, at the end of his …
item.description: Brad Townsend: Hey, gotta love Dusty May’s candor, at the end of his lengthy list of reasons why Dallas “checked all the boxes” in deciding to jump to the NBA, he said with a smile: “No-tax state doesn’t hurt.”

item.title: According to Alex Karaban, he chose No. 33 because …
item.description: James Ham: According to Alex Karaban, he chose No. 33 because Danny Hurley called him Larry Bird all the time at UConn.

item.title: Grizzlies keep GG Jackson, Olivier-Maxence Prosper for next season
item.description: Grizzlies PR: The @Memphis Grizzlies today announced the team exercised 2026-27 contract options on forwards GG Jackson II and Olivier-Maxence Prosper.

item.title: Rockets exercise 2026-27 team option on JD Davison
item.description: Kelly Iko: The Houston Rockets are picking up the $2.6 million team option on guard J.D. Davison, sources tell @YahooSports .

item.title: Houston is also extending a qualifying offer to Tari …
item.description: Kelly Iko: Houston is also extending a qualifying offer to Tari Eason this afternoon, sources say, making him a restricted free agent.

item.title: “More than anything, I want to win.” -Darius Acuff Jr
item.description: James Ham: “More than anything, I want to win.” -Darius Acuff Jr.

item.title: According to Alex Karaban, Domantas Sabonis, Nique …
item.description: James Ham: According to Alex Karaban, Domantas Sabonis, Nique Clifford and Precious Achiuwa all reached out after he was drafted.

item.title: Jalen Pickett to hit free agency after Nuggets decline team option
item.description: The Nuggets are declining Jalen Pickett’s fourth-year team option for the 2026-27 season, releasing him from his contract, league sources told The Denver Post.

item.title: His $2.41 million salary next season would’ve offered …
item.description: His $2.41 million salary next season would’ve offered the Nuggets a sliver of cap relief, but they chose instead to move on from the former Penn State star. Monday was the deadline to make a decision on his option.

item.title: The Los Angeles Clippers hope to come to multi-year …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Update: The Los Angeles Clippers hope to come to multi-year agreements with Jordan Miller and Kobe Sanders after offering both players qualifying offers, league sources said.

item.title: Thomas Bryant returns to Cavs on a one-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent center Thomas Bryant intends to sign a one-year deal to return to the Cleveland Cavaliers, sources tell ESPN. Bryant averaged 12.2 minutes and 6.2 points over 60 games for the Cavs, who worked with Mark Bartelstein and Zach Kurtin of @PrioritySports on the new contract.

item.title: Hornets exercised 2026-27 team option on Pat Connaughton
item.description: Rod Boone: #Hornets are picking up the $3.8 million team option on Pat Connaughton's contract, league sources told @theobserver. The veteran was a huge voice in the locker room this past season and Charlotte even brought him back after he got caught in a roster crunch at trade deadline.

item.title: #Hornets have been active this past week and are …
item.description: Rod Boone: #Hornets have been active this past week and are continuing to work through their free agency plan and offseason roster construction.

item.title: Two-way forward Chaney Johnson re-signs with Nets
item.description: Michael Scotto: Brooklyn Nets forward Chaney Johnson is signing his two-way qualifying offer and will return next season, league sources told @hoopshype. Johnson averaged 8.2 points on 54.3% shooting from the field with 4.6 rebounds in 20.5 minutes during 17 games played for Brooklyn last season

item.title: Plenty of noise around Kawhi Leonard/Clippers/Raptors …
item.description: Michael Grange: Plenty of noise around Kawhi Leonard/Clippers/Raptors *potential* deal, but my understanding is second-year wing Ja’Kobe Walter would NOT be part of any deal the Raptors might make, per sources. It almost goes without saying prize rookie Colin Murray-Boyles is off limits also:

item.title: 2026 First-round pick Bennett Stirtz: 'I thought I was going to be Division II my whole career'
item.description: During the pre-NBA Draft process, Bennett Stirtz worked out with the Chicago Bulls, Oklahoma City Thunder, Golden State Warriors, Memphis Grizzlies, Toronto Raptors and others. Now he’s in New York, getting ready for his name to be called at the 2026 NBA Draft. A few years ago, not even Stirtz thought this was possible. “Not at all,” Stirtz said. “I thought I was going to be Division II my whole career.”

item.title: Stirtz’s story might be worth a film of its own one …
item.description: Stirtz’s story might be worth a film of its own one day. In fact, a mini documentary is being made about his NBA Draft experience. But if an actual movie were to be produced, Stirtz picks Matthew McConaughey as the actor to play him. As for Ben McCollum, the energetic 45-year-old head coach, Stirtz takes a little while longer to choose who would be best for the role. “Timothée Chalamet,” Stirtz says with a smile.

item.title: Bennett Stirtz grabs a Grizzlies hat as he walks onto …
item.description: Bennett Stirtz grabs a Grizzlies hat as he walks onto the stage to shake Silver’s hand and pose for a photo. But there’s a twist. Stirtz was alerted shortly before the pick that the Grizzlies would be trading him to his actual landing spot, the Oklahoma City Thunder. “My agent — it was probably like a minute before Memphis picked — he's like, ‘You're going to OKC,’” Stirtz said. “Before anyone else knew, I knew.” McCollum called Stirtz and the Thunder a “perfect fit.”

item.title: Kings trade Devin Carter to Atlanta
item.description: Shams Charania: The Sacramento Kings are trading guard Devin Carter and a future second-round pick to the Atlanta Hawks, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Bradley Beal declines player option with Clippers
item.description: Chris Haynes: Los Angeles Clippers guard Bradley Beal will decline his $5.6M player option and become an unrestricted free agent, league sources tell me.

item.title: Pistons pick up team option on Dannis Jenkins
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Detroit Pistons will exercise the $4 million team option on Daniss Jenkins, league sources told @hoopshype. Jenkins averaged 9.3 points, including shooting 37.4% from 3-point range, with 3.9 assists in 20.2 minutes, while starting 19 of 72 games for the Pistons last season.

item.title: Kings attached a 2033 second-round pick to move Devin Carter
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Sacramento Kings are trading Devin Carter and a 2033 second-round pick to the Atlanta Hawks, league sources told @hoopshype. Carter is owed $5.16 million next season, which now comes off the books for Sacramento next season.

item.title: Bradley Beal has chosen unrestricted free agency The …
item.description: Law Murray: Bradley Beal has chosen unrestricted free agency The LA Clippers will maintain interest in retaining him Beal played six games last season, all as a starter on a strict minute restriction that kept him out of fourth quarters, before undergoing season ending hip surgery in November.

item.title: Mikel Brown Jr. on his vision for the Nets: “I was …
item.description: Erik Slater: Mikel Brown Jr. on his vision for the Nets: “I was talking to Julius Randle about it the other day and he told me, ‘We’re gonna shock the world.’ That’s what we plan on doing.”

item.title: Pistons pick up team option on Daniss Jenkins
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Detroit Pistons will exercise the $4 million team option on Daniss Jenkins, league sources told @hoopshype. Jenkins averaged 9.3 points, including shooting 37.4% from 3-point range, with 3.9 assists in 20.2 minutes, while starting 19 of 72 games for the Pistons last season.

item.title: The Detroit Pistons are exercising the $2.4 million …
item.description: Dushawn London: The Detroit Pistons are exercising the $2.4 million team option on Tolu Smith, per Corey Marcum of EZ Sports.

item.title: Nolan Traore had a scope on his right knee, per Sean …
item.description: C.J. Holmes: Nolan Traore had a scope on his right knee, per Sean Marks, and won’t be available to play in Summer League but should be healthy by the start of training camp.

item.title: Suns picking up team option on Jamaree Bouyea
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Phoenix Suns will exercise the $2.58 million non-guaranteed team option on Jamaree Bouyea for the 2026-27 season, league sources told @hoopshype. Bouyea averaged 5.7 points on 45.8 percent shooting from the field in 46 games with the Suns last season.

item.title: Jalen PIckett enters free agency
item.description: Bennett Durando: The Nuggets are declining Jalen Pickett’s team option, league sources tell @DenverPost. Drafted 32nd overall in 2023, Pickett played in 126 games for Denver including 50 last season.

item.title: Carter has 2 years left: $5.2M and $7.4M
item.description: Bobby Marks: Carter has 2 years left: $5.2M and $7.4M Trade knocks Sacramento out of the second apron.

item.title: 2nd year is a team option that has to be exercised by …
item.description: Bobby Marks: 2nd year is a team option that has to be exercised by 10/31

item.title: Ja Morant heads to Portland for Jerami Grant, Kris Murray
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The Memphis Grizzlies are trading two-time NBA All-Star Ja Morant to the Portland Trail Blazers for Jerami Grant and Kris Murray, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks are declining the team option on Andre …
item.description: Eric Nehm: The Milwaukee Bucks are declining the team option on Andre Jackson Jr. for next season, making him an unrestricted free agent, a team source tells @TheAthletic .

item.title: Jonathan Kuminga enters free agency
item.description: Shams Charania: The Atlanta Hawks are declining Jonathan Kuminga's $24.3 million team option, making him a free agent, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Landry Shamet returning to Knicks on four-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: Free agent guard Landry Shamet intends to sign a new four-year, $24 million deal to return to the New York Knicks, sources tell ESPN. Shamet's agent, George S. Langberg of GSL Sports Group, worked with Knicks executives on Monday to land the long-term commitment.

item.title: Clippers pick up option on Brook Lopez
item.description: Chris Haynes: Los Angeles Clippers are picking up the $9.2M team option on Brook Lopez for the 2026-27 season, league sources tell me.

item.title: Los Angeles will not pick up the options for Nicolas …
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Clippers are exercising Brook Lopez's $9.2 million team option, with the intent on keeping him, source says. Los Angeles will not pick up the options for Nicolas Batum or Bogdan Bogdanovic.

item.title: According to a league source, the Celtics are …
item.description: Adam Himmelsbach: According to a league source, the Celtics are exercising the team options of Neemias Queta, Jordan Walsh and Dalano Banton. Banton remains on a non-guaranteed deal. Per source, the Celtics are declining options of Amari Williams & Max Shulga, but they could still have opps w/Cs.

item.title: Dallas Mavericks have extended a qualifying offer to …
item.description: Mike Curtis: The Dallas Mavericks have extended a qualifying offer to Moussa Cisse, making him a restricted free agent, a person familiar with the situation says. Cisse played on a two-way contract as a rookie last season and averaged 4.5 points, 5.7 rebounds & 1.2 blocks in 38 games.

item.title: Atlanta Hawks will decline the $24.3 million team …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Atlanta Hawks will decline the $24.3 million team option on Jonathan Kuminga for the 2026-27 season, league sources confirm. Kuminga now becomes an unrestricted free agent. ESPN was first to report.

item.title: By declining the Jonathan Kuminga team option, Atlanta …
item.description: Bobby Marks: By declining the Jonathan Kuminga team option, Atlanta now has access to the full $15m non tax midlevel exception. $27M below the tax $35M below the first apron

item.title: The Sacramento Kings were eyeing the possibility of …
item.description: Marc J. Spears: The Sacramento Kings were eyeing the possibility of adding Ja Morant dating back to the last NBA trade deadline, but drafting Darius Acuff, Jr., recently cooled that talk.

item.title: Ja Morant has played 79 total games over the last 3 …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: Ja Morant has played 79 total games over the last 3 seasons, hasn't played in 7 straight games for the Grizzlies since the 2022-23 season.

item.title: Contract details for Memphis/Portland
item.description: Bobby Marks: Contract details for Memphis/Portland To Portland Ja Morant 2026/27: $42.2M 2027/28: $44.9M To Memphis Jerami Grant 2026/27: $34.2M 2027/28: $37.2M (P) Kris Murray 2026/27: $5.3M

item.title: Landry Shamet turned down the opportunity for bigger …
item.description: Ian Begley: Landry Shamet turned down the opportunity for bigger offers from other contenders to re-sign with the Knicks and defend the title, per sources. Those offers were coming if Shamet hit free agency. Instead, he and Knicks agree on 4-year, $24 million deal to bring him back to New York, as ESPN first reported. Bringing back Shamet was a priority for the club. If Knicks stay under second apron, it will be very difficult for them to now retain Mitchell Robinson.

item.title: Contract will allow New York to remain below the …
item.description: Bobby Marks: The contract will allow New York to remain below the second apron after the roster is filled out. The Knicks have 11 players under contract.

item.title: Yessir Lan!!
item.description: Josh Hart: Yessir Lan!!

item.title: Julian Champagnie plans to sign three-year deal to stay with Spurs
item.description: Shams Charania: Just in: San Antonio Spurs forward Julian Champagnie intends to sign a new three-year, $45 million contract with the franchise through 2028-29, sources tell ESPN. The Spurs declined Champagnie’s $3 million team option to give him a new deal. Waived three years ago, Champagnie has become a vital part of the Spurs core.

item.title: Los Angeles Clippers will exercise the $9.19 million …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Los Angeles Clippers will exercise the $9.19 million team option for the 2026-27 season on Brook Lopez and will decline team options on Bogdan Bogdanovic ($16 million) and Nicolas Batum ($5.88 million), making Bogdanovic and Batum unrestricted free agents, sources confirm.

item.title: Oklahoma City Thunder are declining the $7.2M team …
item.description: Chris Haynes: Oklahoma City Thunder are declining the $7.2M team option on Kenrich Williams, making him an unrestricted free agent, league sources tell me.

item.title: Thunder picking up option for Lu Dort
item.description: Shams Charania: The Oklahoma City Thunder are picking up forward Lu Dort's $17.7 million team option for the 2026-27 season, sources tell ESPN.

item.title: Oklahoma City Thunder will exercise their $17.2 …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Oklahoma City Thunder will exercise their $17.2 million team option on Lu Dort for the 2026-27 season, league sources told @hoopshype. ESPN first to report.

item.title: Jusuf Nurkic plans to sign two-year deal with Utah
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent center Jusuf Nurkic intends to sign a two-year, $22 million contract to return to the Utah Jazz, sources tell ESPN. Nurkic thrived in coach Will Hardy's system and was excited about the Jazz new look roster. Utah officials worked through the deal with Klutch Sports CEO Rich Paul on Monday.

item.title: Heat looking to sign-and-trade Norman Powell?
item.description: Heat Central: The Heat are looking to trade Nikola Jovic in addition to a sign and trade for Norman Powell, per @JakeLFischer “Nikola Jovic, his deal is definitely put out there on the trade market… He is a piece in addition to trying to sign and trade Norman Powell to a different destination”

item.title: Jaylen Brown’s market has been described as “tepid” …
item.description: APHoops: Jaylen Brown’s market has been described as “tepid” with teams around the league not valuing Brown highly due to his contract and impending 2-year max contract extension, per @JakeLFischer.

item.title: Amari Williams will stay in Boston
item.description: Michael Scotto: JUST IN: Boston Celtics center Amari Williams has agreed to a deal to return to the Celtics, league sources told @hoopshype . Williams appeared in 22 games for Boston last season. He’s represented by agent George S. Langberg of @GSLSportsGroup .

item.title: Kevin Huerter re-signing with Pistons
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent sharpshooter Kevin Huerter intends to sign a three-year, $27 million deal to return to the Detroit Pistons, sources tell ESPN. The Pistons worked through the deal with Mark Bartelstein and Andy Shiffman of @PrioritySports on Monday.

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks declined to tender Ousmane Dieng a …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Sources: The Milwaukee Bucks declined to tender Ousmane Dieng a qualifying offer, which will make him an unrestricted free agent. Bucks have significant interest in re-signing Dieng in free agency. He averaged 11.0 points, 4.6 rebounds and 3.6 assists last season for Milwaukee.

item.title: I'm told this is a two-way deal for Williams
item.description: John Karalis: I'm told this is a two-way deal for Williams

item.title: Ja Morant has a 15 percent trade bonus. It would be …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Ja Morant has a 15 percent trade bonus. It would be voided if the trade is executed today or tomorrow. If done on July 6, he would be entitled to a $13 million trade bonus. It would increase his $42.2 million salary to $48.7 million. Jerami Grant ($34.2 million) and Kris Murray ($5.3 million) matches for Morant on that $48.7 million salary. We'll see if he gets the entire amount or waives it.

item.title: Shams: Blazers not involved in Jaylen Brown talks, view acquiring Ja Morant as big move
item.description: SM Highlights: The Trail Blazers Aren’t Involved In Any Jaylen Brown Discussions. Per, @ShamsCharania “This was their big move as of right now my understanding is that they are not involved with Jaylen Brown, and they made attempts for Giannis, didn’t fall out.”

item.title: Windhorst: LeBron James has not been available to meet with Lakers
item.description: Lakers Daily: REPORT: LeBron James has "not been available" for a meeting with the Lakers "LeBron has just not been available for the meeting. … I don’t know why LeBron has not been available, but he has not been available for the meeting.” (via @WindhorstESPN)

item.title: Kings expected to be interested in Jonathan Kuminga
item.description: Marc J. Spears: The Sacramento Kings had strong interest in Jonathan Kuminga before he was dealt from the Golden State Warriors to the Atlanta Hawks and are expected to be interested in him now as a free agent.

item.title: Taurean Prince has chosen to opt in to his contract …
item.description: Eric Nehm: Taurean Prince has chosen to opt in to his contract for the 2026-27 season and remain with the Milwaukee Bucks, a league source tells @TheAthletic .

item.title: Timberwolves are declining the team option on Julian …
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: The Timberwolves are declining the team option on Julian Phillips, a source told @TheAthletic.

item.title: Minnesota Timberwolves will decline their $2.41 …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Minnesota Timberwolves will decline their $2.41 million team option on Julian Phillips and will not extend him a qualifying offer, thus making him an unrestricted free agent, league sources told @hoopshype.

item.title: Utah Jazz have declined team options on Bez Mbeng and …
item.description: Tony Jones: The Utah Jazz have declined team options on Bez Mbeng and Hayden Gray, league sources tell The Athletic. Both will be with Utah in summer league, and the Jazz will further evaluate them during that period

item.title: Shelburne: Kawhi Leonard has been very comfortable in Toronto
item.description: Ramona Shelburne: "From what I understand, Kawhi is comfortable going back to places he's been comfortable before. He's been very comfortable in Toronto. There's a guy who runs their performance there and he managed him the entire season with all the load managing and all the injuries he had, all the way to a championship. He's very comfortable with the Raptors organization and he didn't burn any bridges. He never promised them anything. He always was clear about where he was thinking about goin

item.title: That deal works as far as from a financial standpoint, …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "If he is traded, he is eligible to sign a two-year, $124 million extension with the Toronto Raptors. It would have to be Brandon Ingram, who has two years left, there is a player option here, and then to make the math work, it would be Gradey Dick. Now here is where we are kind of in a little bit of a snag, draft picks. Toronto has four first-round picks to trade, and if you are the Clippers, you would certainly probably want 2027, 2029, and 2031. That is awfully rich. If I am Toro

item.title: #Magic have given Colin Castleton a two-way qualifying …
item.description: Jason Beede: The #Magic have given Colin Castleton a two-way qualifying offer, a league source told the @orlandosentinel. He’ll be a restricted free agent.

item.title: Gary Trent Jr. did not opt in to his contract for the …
item.description: Eric Nehm: Gary Trent Jr. did not opt in to his contract for the 2026-27 season with the Milwaukee Bucks before today's deadline, which makes him an unrestricted free agent, a league source tells @TheAthletic.

item.title: Per source, the third year for Kevin Huerter is a team …
item.description: Omari Sankofa II: Per source, the third year for Kevin Huerter is a team option.

item.title: Kings plan to offer Domantas Sabonis in sign-and-trade for Jalen Duren
item.description: League sources say the Kings are scheduled to meet with Duren at the start of free agency on Tuesday. The key here, per sources, is that Sacramento plans to pitch the Pistons on an offer that includes three-time All-Star center Domantas Sabonis heading to Detroit. It’s unclear how the Pistons might see that scenario, but the Kings are clearly serious about the prospect of landing the 22-year-old. To this point, Detroit has been signaling a strong desire to keep Duren. There will likely be other

item.title: Following the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade to the Miami …
item.description: Michael Scotto: Following the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade to the Miami Heat, there’s a growing sense within league circles that All-Star guard Norman Powell won’t return. The Chicago Bulls are among the teams expected to pursue Powell as free agency nears, as first reported in my story Sunday.

item.title: For those who ask 'why not dump Jovic to create more …
item.description: Barry Jackson: Powell can simply sign into Bulls cap space if he chooses. For those who ask 'why not dump Jovic to create more money' -- answer is it's a very hard contract to move and the Heat historically has been opposed to attaching first rounders to a negative asset simply to get off money. That has been a bridge too far for them over the years.

item.title: Porzingis is headed back to the Warriors on a two year …
item.description: Nick Friedell: Porzingis is headed back to the Warriors on a two year deal worth around $40 mil, according to league source. Second year is a player option. Warriors have thought all along that Porzingis fits what they want to do. Porzingis said repeatedly how much he loves Rick Celebrini.

item.title: Likely prohibits Golden State from using the full …
item.description: Shams Charania: The Warriors were eligible to reach an extension with Porzingis ahead of the veteran center hitting the open market on Tuesday night. The deal, according to @BobbyMarks42 , likely prohibits Golden State from using the full non-taxpayer mid-level exception.

item.title: They would presumably swap Jimmy Butler and picks for …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Warriors cap situation after extending Kristaps Porzingis and De'Anthony Melton presumably opting out. The LeBron James-Anthony Davis pipedream is still alive. They would presumably swap Jimmy Butler and picks for Davis. Then they could fit James with $15 million non-taxpayer mid-level under the first apron hard cap IF Draymond Green takes the same contract as Porzingis, and IF they also find a taker for Moses Moody.

item.title: Walker Kessler will hold meetings with teams once free agency starts
item.description: Tony Jones: Utah Jazz restricted free agent center Walker Kessler and his representatives will hold meetings with multiple teams as free agency begins Tuesday evening, league sources tell The Athletic. The meetings will be held in Los Angeles at CAA’s headquarters

item.title: Harrison Barnes staying put in San Antonio
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent forward Harrison Barnes intends to sign a one-year, $8 million deal to stay with the San Antonio Spurs, sources tell ESPN. The Spurs valued Barnes' leadership and production as he enters a third season in San Antonio, finalizing a new deal with agent Jeff Schwartz of Excel Sports Management on Monday night.

item.title: Kristaps Porzingis will return to Golden State
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent center Kristaps Porzingis has agreed to a two-year, $40 million contract extension to return to the Golden State Warriors through 2027-28, with a player option in the second season, agent Jeff Schwartz of Excel Sports Management tells ESPN.

item.title: Jalen Duren will meet with Sacramento when free agency starts
item.description: Chris Haynes: BREAKING: Due to sizable distance apart in negotiations, Detroit Pistons restricted free agent center Jalen Duren will meet with the Sacramento Kings at the start of free agency with the intentions of structuring a sign-&-trade out of Detroit.

item.title: Per league sources, the most likely scenario here …
item.description: Sam Amick: Per league sources, the most likely scenario here would include Kings forward Domantas Sabonis heading to Detroit. The key question now is how the Pistons see the situation, in light of the gap between them and Duren and the chance to get a three-time All-Star in return.

item.title: Norman Powell isn't expecting a sign-and-trade out of …
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Norman Powell isn't expecting a sign-and-trade out of Miami, per a source familiar with where things stand there. Of course, much can change, but that's the intel at this point.

item.title: Kings not expected to pursue Jonathan Kuminga?
item.description: Sam Amick: On a related note, I’m told the Kings are not expected to pursue free agent forward Jonathan Kuminga.

item.title: Pistons uninterested in pursuing sign-and-trade scenarios for Jalen Duren
item.description: Dave McMenamin: Detroit Pistons restricted free agent center Jalen Duren has a planned call with the Los Angeles Lakers on Tuesday, a league source confirmed to ESPN ( @sam_amick first). However DET thus far is uninterested in pursuing a sign and trade with their All-Star big man, sources said.

item.title: Draymond Green expected to stay with Warriors
item.description: Green and the Warriors are expected to reunite, team sources said, with his exact contract -- length, first-year salary and full contract amount -- to be determined after the Warriors determine other business. They are still expected to pursue James, among other unrestricted free agents, once the market opens.

item.title: No tangible movement for Anthony Davis to Golden State talks
item.description: That could also mean exploring a trade for Washington center Anthony Davis to help lure James, though there has yet to be tangible movement on that front, league sources said.

item.title: There was some hope that Wiggins would opt out of his …
item.description: The Heat intends to keep Wiggins as part of this upcoming season’s roster, according to a league source, but he’s still immediately eligible to be traded even after he eventually signs his two-year extension. There was some hope that Wiggins would opt out of his player option to renegotiate his deal into a longer contract with a lower starting salary, giving the Heat more flexibility to operate in free agency this offseason.

item.title: Lakers expected to call Jalen Duren when free agency opens
item.description: Sam Amick: More intel on Jalen Duren coming in: League sources tell @TheAthletic he now has a call with the Lakers scheduled for tomorrow. Another sign-and-trade scenario emerges…

item.title: Nets decline options on Ochai Agbaji, Jalen Wilson
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Brooklyn Nets declined to tender qualifying offers to Ochai Agbaji and Jalen Wilson, making both unrestricted free agents, league sources told @hoopshype . Agbaji averaged 6.7 points in 20 games after being traded to Brooklyn. Wilson averaged 7.4 points over 3 seasons as a Net

item.title: Jalen Duren, Domantas Sabonis both interested in Kings-Pistons swap
item.description: Sam Amick: To add on here, I’m told that Jalen Duren very much wants to play in Sacramento and Kings big man Domantas Sabonis is likewise interested in the prospect of landing with the Pistons.

item.title: Among notable names on #Warriors summer-league roster: …
item.description: Ron Kroichick: Among notable names on #Warriors summer-league roster: Will Richard & LJ Cryer from last season’s GSW team, Yaxel Lendeborg & Lajae Jones from last week’s draft, Graham Ike from Gonzaga and Chance McMillian from Vallejo/Texas Tech

item.title: Interesting twist to the Warriors' summer league …
item.description: Dalton Johnson: Interesting twist to the Warriors' summer league roster: Alex Toohey, their top second-round pick last year, is back after being injured and waived last season

item.title: Davion Mitchell will now wear No. 2 for the Heat to …
item.description: Tim Reynolds: Davion Mitchell will now wear No. 2 for the Heat to honor a late friend, Spud. “We both came from the 912, and he will always be a part of who I am. Wearing this number is my way of keeping his memory close and carrying a piece of him with me every time I step on the court.”

item.title: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander invested into the …
item.description: Justin Martinez: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander invested into the recently-opened TD Coliseum in his hometown of Hamilton, Ontario, in February. The arena’s Shai Legacy Court was unveiled today. It’ll debut when SGA and Team Canada face Puerto Rico (July 3) and Jamaica (July 6) in the FIBA World Cup 2027 Americas Qualifiers.

item.title: De'Anthony Melton becomes unrestricted free agent
item.description: Keith Smith: De'Anthony Melton declined his player option with the Golden State Warriors, a league source told @spotrac . Melton will be an unrestricted free agent this offseason.

item.title: Lakes decline team option on Nick Smith Jr
item.description: Keith Smith: The Los Angeles Lakers declined their team option for Nick Smith Jr., a league source told @spotrac . Smith will be an unrestricted free agent this offseason.

item.title: The Pistons do not intend to trade the 22-year-old …
item.description: The Pistons do not intend to trade the 22-year-old restricted free agent, based on conversations with multiple people who spoke with the Free Press on the condition of anonymity. Duren is reportedly preparing to meet with the Sacramento Kings to open the door for a sign-and-trade, according to NBA insider Chris Haynes. There is a high degree of confidence within the Pistons' organization that Duren will ultimately re-sign with the team.

item.title: Sacramento Kings declined their team option for …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Sacramento Kings declined their team option for Killian Hayes, a league sourced told @spotrac . Hayes will now be an unrestricted free agent this offseason.

item.title: Oklahoma City Thunder have tendered a two-way …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Oklahoma City Thunder have tendered a two-way qualifying offer to Brooks Barnhizer, a league source told @spotrac. Barnhizer will be a restricted free agent this offseason.

item.title: San Antonio Spurs tendered two-way qualifying offers …
item.description: Keith Smith: The San Antonio Spurs tendered two-way qualifying offers to Harrison Ingram and David Jones Garcia, a league source told @spotrac. Both players will now be restricted free agents this offseason.

item.title: Harrison Ingram has a standard qualifying offer
item.description: Keith Smith: Correction: Harrison Ingram has a standard qualifying offer.

item.title: As a result of the trade happening today, he will not …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Ja Morant trade is official. As a result of the trade happening today, he will not receive his trade bonus. He carries a $42.2 million cap hit and the Trail Blazers maintain more cap flexibility. They are $27.6 million under the luxury tax line with 11 players rostered.

item.title: Ja Morant excited to be in Portland
item.description: According to Shams Charania of ESPN, the embattled star guard is reportedly thrilled to welcome new beginnings in Portland. "They felt like they had to take a swing just based on the talent that Ja Morant is and hoping that he has a chip on his shoulder, motivation to turn his career around," the NBA insider said about the Blazers trading for Morant. "The Blazers did speak to Ja Morant today, shortly after the trade. I'm told he expressed to them that he's excited to be in Portland."

item.title: Portland plans on starting Morant and Damian Lillard …
item.description: ESPN's Shams Charania reported Portland acquired Ja Morant via trade from the Memphis Grizzlies in exchange for Jerami Grant and Kris Murray. According to NBA insider Chris Haynes, Portland plans on starting Morant and Damian Lillard in the backcourt alongside Deni Avdija on the wing following "a very unconventional move that the franchise believes has upside."

item.title: One executive from another team I talked to called the …
item.description: The Ja Morant trade caught many around the NBA by surprise, as the oft-injured Memphis Grizzlies guard was traded to the Portland Trail Blazers for Jerami Grant and Kris Murray. One executive from another team I talked to called the deal “confusing," citing the Blazers' glut of guards with Damian Lillard returning from Achilles surgery and Jrue Holiday and Scoot Henderson under contract. He wondered whether there was a corresponding deal the Blazers were working on to clear up the position.

item.title: The Grizzlies saved a $13 million check to Morant by …
item.description: Regarding the Ja Morant trade, there is one important reason to do the deal now rather than in July. That is because Morant is still in year seven of his career rather than year eight, which means his max is capped at 25 percent of the cap (for a player who has completed six full years of service) rather than 30 percent. That matters because Morant has a 15 percent trade kicker on his deal, which doesn't matter between now and June 30 since his 2025-26 salary is already above the six-year-of-ser

item.title: close to $1.3 billion has been committed in salary
item.description: Bobby Marks: As the clock gets closer to the start of free agency (officially), close to $1.3 billion has been committed in salary.

item.title: What he saw, he said, disgusted him. Not the flagrant …
item.description: Five weeks ago I sat across from Dundon in North Carolina and he told me that he had watched “Untold: Jail Blazers,” the Netflix documentary on the era that has defined modern Trail Blazers history on a flight from Dallas to Raleigh. What he saw, he said, disgusted him. Not the flagrant and unapologetic drug use, the rape allegation or the bad attitudes, but the way fans turned on their team. His takeaway: The players didn’t do anything. Indeed, they were not caught on film waving around a handg

item.title: “Hopefully,” Dundon told me, “we’ll create a product …
item.description: When I had that conversation with Dundon in Raleigh, he said that he felt fans of the Portland teams of the early 2000s were too quick to latch onto negativity toward Blazers players. Damon’s weed. Rasheed’s tantrums. Bonzi’s birdie finger. Fans resented it all. “Hopefully,” Dundon told me, “we’ll create a product where that’s not a choice.” Indeed, there should be no choice at all for Blazers fans. The only option is unrestrained fury.

item.title: It should come as no surprise, then, that one of the …
item.description: With the Knicks' long-overdue NBA Finals victory this month came a seemingly endless celebration across New York. It should come as no surprise, then, that one of the Big Apple's "anthems" (as it's been dubbed over the years) has seen a resurgence of late thanks to the team's championship win. Released in 2009, "Empire State of Mind" was one of the smash-hit singles off Jay-Z's acclaimed album The Blueprint III. Hip-hop fans probably know the rap verses by heart, but it's featured artist Alicia

item.title: Shams: Kawhi Leonard's time with the Clippers is nearing its end
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Shams on Kawhi: "Those talks are continuing, they're serious between the Clippers and the Raptors...all signs point to these two sides being motivated and the future is nearing it's end Kawhi Leonard's tenure is nearing it's end in Los Angeles. He has not been given any indication that the Clippers want to give him a contract extension"

item.title: Toronto Raptors are willing to offer Kawhi Leonard the …
item.description: APHoops: The Toronto Raptors are willing to offer Kawhi Leonard the 2-year $126.1 million max contract extension he is demanding, per @ShamsCharania . 2026-27: $50.3M 2027-28: $61.5M 2028-29: $64.5M Total: $176.4M

item.title: Blazers not expected to keep Robert Williams
item.description: The Blazers have immediate needs at center and power forward. They are not expected to retain free agent center Robert Williams III, who is hoping to land a contract in the $15 million a year range, leaving only second-year center Yang Hansen as a backup to Donovan Clingan.

item.title: New York Knicks are not extending a qualifying offer …
item.description: Vincent Goodwill: The New York Knicks are not extending a qualifying offer to center Ariel Hukporti, making him an unrestricted free agent, sources tell ESPN. Hukporti has played 79 across two seasons with the Knicks.

item.title: Milwaukee Bucks are hiring T.J. Ford as part of their …
item.description: Kelly Iko: The Milwaukee Bucks are hiring T.J. Ford as part of their coaching staff, sources tell @YahooSports. Ford, the former Longhorn who played for the franchise from 2003-06, retired from the NBA in 2012.

item.title: "If the price lowers at some point this offseason to 2 …
item.description: Oh No He Didn't: Shams on Jaylen Brown: "If the price lowers at some point this offseason to 2 firsts (round picks) or maybe 2 firsts and then some, then you might see something. My indication right now is they would not want to go that route"

item.title: No picks changed hands in the Ja Morant trade to the …
item.description: Jake Fischer: No picks changed hands in the Ja Morant trade to the Trail Blazers, but the Memphis Grizzlies did send $1 million in cash to Portland, sources say, in exchange for Jerami Grant and Kris Murray.

item.title: Jaylen Brown has grown frustrated with Celtics approach after guiding team to 56 wins
item.description: League sources said that the Celtics have continued to field calls from teams interested in trading for Brown. A league source said that Brown has grown frustrated with the organization’s approach following a season in which he guided the Celtics to 56 wins while Jayson Tatum was sidelined for months with an Achilles injury.

item.title: One league source said Brown could still return to the …
item.description: It remains unclear if the Celtics are urgently attempting to trade Brown or simply listening to pitches from prospective suitors. One league source said Brown could still return to the Celtics next season, even if some uncomfortable conversations must happen first.

item.title: Kings expected to keep exploring trade destinations for Zach LaVine
item.description: LaVine has been the subject of trade rumors and speculation for the past year, but there hasn’t been much of a market for his services due to the size of his contract. The Kings will continue to explore trade possibilities for LaVine as general manager Scott Perry works to overhaul an aging and expensive roster. If they can’t find a deal before the February trade deadline, LaVine’s expiring contract will come off the books next summer, giving Sacramento significant salary cap relief.

item.title: That is because Morant is still in year seven of his …
item.description: Regarding the Ja Morant deal, there is one important reason to do the deal now rather than in July. That is because Morant is still in year seven of his career rather than year eight, which means his max is capped at 25 percent of the max (for a player who has completed six full years of service) rather than 30 percent.
