item.title: 12, thank you for every highlight, every memory, every …
item.description: Memphis Grizzlies: 2x All-Star All-NBA Second Team Rookie of the Year All-Rookie First Team Most Improved Player Franchise record for points in a single game (52) 12, thank you for every highlight, every memory, every unforgettable moment and for all you've given this team, this community and this city. From the with love Thank you @JaMorant for 7 great years.

item.title: Will Richard said one of the biggest things the …
item.description: Nick Friedell: Will Richard said one of the biggest things the coaching staff stressed to him this summer is to get stronger. He said he’s been spending a lot of time in the weight room doing squats and training.

item.title: A lot of guys have mentioned he’s even bigger than …
item.description: Nick Friedell: High praise for Yaxel after first day of practice. A lot of guys have mentioned he’s even bigger than they thought he was. And they can see that he has the ability to defend all over the floor. A lot of hype for his debut in Cali Classic/Vegas.

item.title: Cason Wallace open to leaving OKC?
item.description: There is a belief around the league that Wallace is open to leaving OKC, and he’s up for a big extension the Thunder might be loath to pay. The young guard would cost multiple first-round picks but be the point-of-attack defender the Nets need.

item.title: The Nets are more likely to use the space in trades …
item.description: With their tanking hopefully behind them, Brooklyn will at least try to compete this season. While the past two years have been about taking salary dumps to get draft picks, now they have every intention of improving the roster. League sources told The Post that the Nets are more likely to use the space in trades than chasing free agents, and cap experts Bobby Marks of ESPN and Yossi Gozlan of The Third Apron agreed that’s where the value is. The Nets have shown interest in New Orleans’ Trey Mur

item.title: Robert Parish: 'Don’t get rid of a talent like Jaylen Brown unless he asked to be moved'
item.description: Parish is firmly against the idea of trading Brown, as the four-time champion adamantly expressed during an appearance Monday on SiriusXM NBA Radio. In explaining his position on the matter, Parish called out the Celtics, and specifically team president of basketball operations Brad Stevens, for what he deems a “serious miscalculation” by the organization following their pursuit of Giannis Antetokounmpo. “First of all, you don’t get rid of a talent like Jaylen Brown unless he asked to be moved,

item.title: Jaylen Brown has one of the best shooting touches in …
item.description: Jeremias Engelmann: Jaylen Brown has one of the best shooting touches in the NBA (as measured by actual points vs. expected points, based on shot location and type) Problem is, his shot selection is among the worst, trailing only DeMar DeRozan

item.title: Dusty May could have been an option for Warriors?
item.description: ESPN’s Brian Windhorst revealed on the Hoop Collective podcast that Dusty May -- the newly hired Dallas Mavericks head coach and reigning national championship-winning coach at Michigan -- would have been on the Warriors' coaching short list had Kerr chosen not to return. “I think had Steve Kerr not re-signed in Golden State, Dusty May would have been on the Warriors’ short list,” Windhorst said. “I think he was on the short list a couple of other places, and he was clearly on the short list for

item.title: You combine the contract that he's on, he's got three …
item.description: Shams Charania: "The conversation hasn't been about Jaylen Brown's game. Everyone knows Jaylen Brown is an elite player, but you combine the contract that he's on, he's got three years left on his supermax clip and where we're at in the offseason right now, what you're seeing is a price variance based on the player he is and the contract he's at and the assets that are available. There's a stark variance among those three categories."

item.title: If I'm Jaylen Brown, I feel some type of way coming …
item.description: Kendrick Perkins: "They could come out with all the reports that they want to, but again, some of the stuff, and I'm not saying he's a criminal, but I'm saying the streaming and stuff like that, that has been a turnoff to a lot of people. But if you're Brad Stevens, you cannot bring that energy back into your locker room because if I'm Jaylen Brown, I feel some type of way coming back and I'm saying, 'Well damn, you didn't try to trade me twice. How you expect me to act?' I just came off the bes

item.title: Deron Williams on Jaylen Brown: "Everything that he's …
item.description: Deron Williams on Jaylen Brown: "Everything that he's doing right now is very calculated. I guarantee you it's very calculated. I think he wants out."

item.title: It is my understanding that AD's representatives and …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "Anthony Davis has the same agent, Rich Paul. It is my understanding that AD's representatives and the Wizards met yesterday about a possible contract extension. He can't sign an extension until August, but this is when you would sort of talk about that. AD has one year left on his contract plus a player option, but one guaranteed year.

item.title: "It is possible that the Warriors could call the …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: "It is possible that the Warriors could call the Wizards and make an offer and say we would love to trade for Anthony Davis, and that Anthony Davis could be traded and LeBron could come in free agency and those things could happen. Yes, it is possible... and then Draymond could then resign for a lower number and then add years onto the back to make himself whole. All of those things are possible. I will also tell you that the Warriors routinely, every transaction window, and wha

item.title: Unless LeBron wants to come on the minimum, no, you …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "I never rule anything out in this world, but you just look at the math of it. They are 27 million below the first apron. They are going to bring back Draymond Green. You don't leave 27 on the table to explore free agency. Even if he came back at 20 million, you are 7 million, and anything you use more than 6.1 of that non-tax mid-level, you get hard-capped. So when you look at it from that perspective, unless LeBron wants to come on the minimum, no, you don't have 15 million to off

item.title: The Wizards are over here saying like, what about us? …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "I think what Mike (Dunleavy) and the group don't want to do is trade for Anthony Davis and give up a ton and then don't have LeBron coming. So like, that would be probably the worst-case scenario to think, right? Because it's going to, because here's the thing, and I said this on TV today, it's like, everybody's making it, we're talking about this and it's great. And the Wizards are over here saying like, what about us? You know, like, what is the incentive for the Wizards to trade

item.title: Basically what will happen is you will probably see …
item.description: Bobby Marks: "James Harden declines his $42 million player option. It was a mutual option. There was $13 million guaranteed. It was a no-brainer. Basically what will happen is you will probably see Harden's number between $32-33 million. There will be a second year attached to basically make up the money that he lost. Expect James Harden back."

item.title: Wizards interested in bringing back Russell Westbrook?
item.description: League sources tell The Stein Line that Washington has a level interest in Russell Westbrook. The nine-time former All-Star's final season of the four in his career in which he averaged a triple-double (22.2 PPG, 11.5 RPG and 11.7 APG) came in 2020-21 with the Wizards.

item.title: According to ESPN’s Vincent Goodwill, one league …
item.description: Leonard’s future in Los Angeles is now growing murkier, the Clippers have reportedly emerged as a serious team to watch for Boston Celtics star Jaylen Brown. According to ESPN’s Vincent Goodwill, one league executive said the Clippers “want Brown bad,” adding another major name to what has already become one of the NBA’s most active rumor cycles.

item.title: Multiple executives still believe Portland could be …
item.description: Multiple executives still believe Portland could be involved in trade discussions for the Celtics' Jaylen Brown, and that could be the next big move. Has Morant hit rock bottom? One high-level exec told ESPN over the weekend that he believes Morant’s needs extend beyond basketball. He actually believes, however, that Morant can make a comeback to his old form because of how the guard competes when he’s healthy.

item.title: "This was their big move, Charania said. "As of right …
item.description: Here's what ESPN's Shams Charania had to say on "SportsCenter" when asked whether the Portland Trail Blazers could make a move for the Boston Celtics' Jaylen Brown after they traded for Ja Morant on Monday: "This was their big move, Charania said. "As of right now, my understanding is they're not involved with Jaylen Brown."

item.title: Clippers not involved in Jaylen Brown trade talks?
item.description: Also important: Sources say that the Clippers, whether or not their talks with the Raptors progress all the way to a completed deal, have not pursued Boston's Jaylen Brown this offseason. The sources added that the Clippers aren't planning to make a run at the Celtics' MVP candidate.

item.title: “I find [the Celtics shopping Jaylen Brown] …
item.description: SiriusXM NBA Radio: “I find [the Celtics shopping Jaylen Brown] disturbing” Celtics legend @RobertParish00 tells @AndrewBogusch and @joevardon why he is adamant about Boston keeping Brown and both sides repairing their relationship Hear our ongoing free agency coverage 👇 SXM.app.link/nbaradiolive

item.title: "Those talks are continuing, they're serious between …
item.description: Shams on Kawhi: "Those talks are continuing, they're serious between the Clippers and the Raptors...all signs point to these two sides being motivated and the future is nearing it's end Kawhi Leonard's tenure is nearing it's end in Los Angeles. He has not been given any indication that the Clippers want to give him a contract extension"

item.title: Ja’Kobe Walter would not be part of any deal the Raptors might make for Kawhi Leonard
item.description: Michael Grange: Plenty of noise around Leonard/Clippers/Raptors *potential* deal, but my understanding is second-year wing Ja’Kobe Walter would NOT be part of any deal the Raptors might make, per sources. It almost goes without saying prize rookie Colin Murray-Boyles is off limits also:

item.title: Some rival NBA executives and coaches have mixed …
item.description: The Lakers still have to prove they can maximize Dončić’s championship window. But Dončić, Reaves and James showed they can still thrive together even amid the discomfort when one of them played off the ball. In this case, mostly James. Some rival NBA executives and coaches have mixed feelings on whether James has the appetite to repeat that experience again. Some remain bullish on James’ versatility and unselfishness. Some express skepticism on whether James can stay patient with Dončić b

item.title: League sources say that the Warriors certainly haven't …
item.description: League sources say that the Warriors certainly haven't been operating under the premise that they must adhere to any mandate that James and Davis are somehow a package deal. If James becomes truly available once free agency actually begins and receptive to such pitches, Golden State will be trying to convince him to sign for an estimated $15 million. And Green, to be clear, is going nowhere. He's expected to sign a new multi-year deal after the Warriors sort out the rest of their roster business

item.title: This is going end up with Draymond getting a new …
item.description: [Windhorst] "The people on the league I'm talking to today are giving me the side eye and they're going, this is going end up with Draymond getting a new contract in GS, LeBron getting a new contract in LA and AD getting a new contract in Washington."

item.title: Yaxel said his friends spent all day texting him and …
item.description: Nick Friedell: Yaxel said his friends spent all day texting him and asking how he would fit in playing with Steph, Draymond, LeBron and AD.

item.title: The Lakers, for their part, are saying almost nothing. …
item.description: The Lakers, for their part, are saying almost nothing. No one has offered real hints, according to league sources, about the plans for LeBron James — even to players currently on the roster.

item.title: Warriors unlikely to move Jimmy Butler?
item.description: Behind the scenes, however, Golden State continues to signal a reluctance to use Butler's contract when he's injured for a move like that. Consider the strong relationships with Butler held by Warriors general manager Mike Dunleavy and coach Steve Kerr. Butler and Kerr are frequently in San Diego in the offseason and known to cross paths.

item.title: Kings, Lakers both in the mix for Jalen Duren
item.description: The Jalen Duren situation is getting more interesting by the day. As we first reported Friday, the third-team All-NBA big man was underwhelmed by Detroit’s offer in restricted free agency and almost immediately began strategizing about sign-and-trade scenarios out of town. And as we learned Monday afternoon via league sources, the Sacramento Kings and Los Angeles Lakers are now officially on that list.

item.title: The Kings and Lakers are scheduled to meet with Duren …
item.description: Those sources, who are not authorized to discuss free-agent scenarios before the offseason officially begins, say the Kings and Lakers are scheduled to meet with Duren after free agency opens Tuesday. It’s unclear whether the Kings’ visit will be in person or virtual, but those sources say the Lakers’ meeting is expected to be by phone.

item.title: Luka Doncic a big fan of Jalen Duren's game
item.description: (The Lakers’ incumbent starting center, Deandre Ayton, picked up his $8.1 million player option Monday.) Yet in stark contrast to the Kings’ scenario, the Lakers’ best offerings might be draft capital rather than quality players, as Detroit could trade Duren into the Lakers’ salary-cap space. Lakers star Luka Dončić is known to be a fan of Duren’s game, league sources say, which is a monumental factor for any player who’s trying to be welcomed into Crypto.com Arena these days.

item.title: Luka Doncic for his desire to pair with an "A-list …
item.description: Detroit Pistons center Jalen Duren, one of the top restricted free agents this summer, has a pair of meetings with outside teams scheduled for when free agency officially begins Tuesday. One of the teams is the Los Angeles Lakers, a source familiar with the situation told ESPN. L.A.'s front office has been given feedback from franchise star Luka Doncic for his desire to pair with an "A-list center," a league source told ESPN, and Duren would certainly fit the bill. The other team Duren plans to

item.title: The Blazers now have four point guards — Morant, …
item.description: That leads to the unspoken part of this trade: more deals are on the way. Have to be. The Blazers now have four point guards — Morant, Lillard, Jrue Holiday and Henderson — so unloading one or two seems likely. If I was to guess, I would imagine Holiday, because of his contract and because of his skill set, is the more likely candidate to be traded next.

item.title: They want more big-man depth and realize they have a …
item.description: The Blazers are still in the market to make another deal. They want more big-man depth and realize they have a logjam of four point guards with Morant, Lillard, Jrue Holiday and Scoot Henderson, but the team does not feel it is essential to unload one of its guards. The Blazers believe the four guards can co-exist, in large part because the team believes Holiday can alleviate the redundancy by playing him off the ball as a shooting guard or small forward.

item.title: Donovan Clingan off limits in trade talks
item.description: One thing has become certain about the Trail Blazers after Monday’s trade for point guard Ja Morant: Portland center Donovan Clingan is now essentially off limits in trade talks, according to a team source.

item.title: The Kings are not expected to pursue free agent …
item.description: On a related note, the Kings are not expected to pursue free agent forward Jonathan Kuminga, according to league sources who were granted anonymity to discuss free-agent scenarios before Tuesday. The Atlanta Hawks declined Kuminga’s team option Monday, making the 23-year-old an unrestricted free agent. The Kings attempted to acquire Kuminga last offseason via a sign-and-trade scenario but could not agree to a deal with Kuminga’s former team, the Golden State Warriors.

item.title: Lakers eyeing Sandro Mamukelashvili
item.description: But people around the NBA, tasked with trying to make sense of the evolving free-agency landscape, started to link the Lakers to a wide range of players throughout Monday. One name has come up from multiple league sources: Toronto forward/center Sandro Mamukelashvili. Mamukelashvili declined his $2.8 million option with the Raptors Monday, with league sources believing he could command $10 million or more in free agency.

item.title: #Cavs and Dean Wade are having ongoing negotiations …
item.description: Chris Fedor: On the eve of NBA free agency, sources tell @clevelanddotcom that #Cavs and Dean Wade are having ongoing negotiations and dialogue. Both sides plan to keep working to see if a deal is possible before free agency Tuesday. Neither side has closed the door on a return to Cleveland.

item.title: The Blazers have interest in retaining all three …
item.description: The Blazers’ 15-man regular season roster features 10 players under guaranteed contract and two more — Vit Krejčí and Sidy Cissoko — with team options. The Blazers are expected to exercise both team options, giving them 12 players under contract. This leaves them with three unrestricted free agents: Robert Williams III, Matisse Thybulle and Blake Wesley. A team source told The Oregonian/OregonLive earlier this month the Blazers have interest in retaining all three players “if the deal makes se

item.title: They are not expected to retain free agent center …
item.description: The Blazers have immediate needs at center and power forward. They are not expected to retain free agent center Robert Williams III, who is hoping to land a contract in the $15 million a year range, leaving only second-year center Yang Hansen as a backup to Donovan Clingan. The Blazers are also thin at power forward, with Deni Avdija and Toumani Camara their only options.

item.title: Josh Okogie is among a group of swingmen they are …
item.description: Sources say Josh Okogie is among a group of swingmen they are looking at to support the soon-to-be-back-from-injury Tyrese Haliburton. Indiana can pay part of the non-taxpayer midlevel to compete against other teams' prospective offers at the veteran's minimum and is also said to have Gary Trent Jr. and Kelly Oubre Jr. on its list.

item.title: The Washington Wizards decline their team option for …
item.description: Keith Smith: The Washington Wizards decline their team option for Jamir Watkins, a league source told @spotrac . The Wizards did tender a qualifying offer to Watkins. He'll now be a restricted free agent this offseason.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard trade 'could happen today' as Clippers, Raptors continue discussing draft pick compensation
item.description: Brian Windhorst on potential Kawhi Leonard trade to Raptors: This is lining up. This trade could happen today. I think the Clippers and the Raptors have agreed on player compensation. I think it would be Brandon Ingram based on what I am told that would head to Los Angeles. What they've been haggling over is the draft pick compensation. The Raptors have all of their four first-round picks. They have up to four to trade. Naturally, the Clippers are going to want multiple of those. Let's see if th

item.title: Anthony Davis to Warriors 'unlikely'
item.description: Brian Windhorst: The Warriors still have the ability to re-sign Draymond Green to a reasonable contract number and to try to add LeBron James, but it's really, really narrow and they would really need to make some other moves to build up their team. Right now, I'm taking the Anthony Davis thing off the front burner. I do not have data that says that that's likely to happen.

item.title: The actions were that Draymond Green opted out of …
item.description: Brian Windhorst: I like actions over words. The actions were that Draymond Green opted out of his contract. That gives the Warriors flexibility, but I don't think the Warriors think they're getting Anthony Davis. Do you want to know why I think they will? Because they signed Kristaps Porzingis. And this contract and Porzingis did not play a lot of games last year for anybody, much less for the Warriors. But you've got to have big men to compete in the Western Conference. They had to handle this

item.title: Bronny James' deal with Lakers becomes fully guaranteed
item.description: Keith Smith: Bronny James' contract with the Los Angeles Lakers became fully guaranteed for $2.3M yesterday.

item.title: Players who have contract guarantee triggers today: …
item.description: Keith Smith: Players who have contract guarantee triggers today: 🏀Cam Christie - LAC: full at $2.3M 🏀Kris Dunn - LAC: full at $5.7M 🏀Kyle Filipowski - UTA: full at $3M 🏀Kam Jones - CHI: full at $2.2M 🏀Leonard Miller - CHI: full at $2.4M 🏀Svi Mykhailiuk - UTA: full at $3.8M

item.title: Clippers, Raptors spent Monday nearing agreement on a Kawhi Leonard trade
item.description: Jake Fischer: The Clippers and Raptors spent Monday nearing a framework that would send Kawhi Leonard back to Toronto, sources say. Will those negotiations be completed before free agency officially opens at 6 p.m. ET?

item.title: Kawhi League sources tell The Athletic that Kawhi …
item.description: Kawhi League sources tell The Athletic that Kawhi Leonard’s camp is exploring what is out there. Like in 2023, he’s extension eligible, though the Clippers did not agree to extensions with him or George then. They wound up adding Harden’s contract year shortly after the start of the 2023-24 regular season, despite the presence of Russell Westbrook as a starting point guard. Leonard signed his three-year extension later that season, but the Clippers failed to advance out of the first round twice

item.title: Dusty May said he has already spoken with every player …
item.description: Dusty May said he has already spoken with every player on the Mavericks’ roster, including Kyrie Irving, who will return this fall after missing all of last season with a torn ACL. May called Irving “one of the greatest point guards to ever play the game” and likened him to a “jazz musician.” “How to earn his trust?” May said. “Be yourself and work like crazy for him every single day. Kyrie knows what it takes to win a championship. As a first-time NBA coach, I’m going to lean on him in a lot of

item.title: Nemanja Jokic, the older brother of Denver …
item.description: Nemanja Jokic, the older brother of Denver Nuggets superstar Nikola Jokic, has successfully transitioned from a protective family advisor into a credentialed professional by becoming a certified NBA agent, Serbian outlet Meridian Sports relayed on Tuesday. The middle of the three brothers had to navigate the strict licensing protocols dictated by the National Basketball Players Association, which stands as the sole governing body authorized to issue the official license required to negotiate con

item.title: The Timberwolves were surging during the 2023-24 …
item.description: JC Stroebel and Jesse George started this whole thing on a whim. The Timberwolves were surging during the 2023-24 season, and Stroebel just threw out a post on social media offering to tattoo “Naz Reid.” on any who wanted one for $20. He had no idea what was coming. Wolves fans responded in overwhelming fashion. Stroebel and George estimate they’ve done between, at least, 1,200 to 1,500 “Naz Reid.” tattoos. What started as a lark with the hope of landing tickets to a playoff game became a full-b

item.title: Simone Fontecchio to return to Miami on one-year deal
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent Simone Fontecchio intends to sign a one-year deal to return to the Miami Heat, sources tell ESPN. Heat officials worked with Fontecchio's agent at Excel Sports Management, Sam Goldfeder, to bring back the shooter who averaged 8.5 points and 37.5% 3-point shooting.

item.title: Lakers had meeting with Luka Doncic last summer mapping out 2026 offseason; plans did not include LeBron James
item.description: SM Highlights: The Lakers Had A Meeting Last Summer With Luka Doncic That Mapped Out The 2026 Offseason, LeBron James WASN’T In Those Plans. Per, @ramonashelburne “At that meeting they laid out their plans… in those plans, they did NOT include LeBron James.

item.title: Draymond Green on opting out of his Warriors contract: It allows us to possibly do some things
item.description: Draymond Green: For me personally, you know, I’m always willing to work with the team on whatever is best, especially at this point in my career. So, my decision to opt out, was for a few reasons. As you all know, I have always taken the approach of working with the organization. I’ve been in one place for 14 years. It’s more of a family to me than anything. Like you’re talking people I’ve built relationships with over the course of the past 14 years. I view Golden State more like Michigan State

item.title: Draymond Green on Austin Reaves' new contract: I didn't expect the Lakers to go so high
item.description: Draymond Green: I just didn’t expect the Lakers to go so high. On a team where you had Austin Reaves, you had Luka Doncic, and you had LeBron James, the team didn’t fare as well as one would hope. So, I thought the Lakers would stand pat. A little bit longer maybe try to see if they can negotiate that number down quite a bit, but apparently the Pistons raise the offer.

item.title: Listen, maybe they don’t want Jaylen Brown for other …
item.description: Draymond Green: Listen, maybe they don’t want Jaylen Brown for other reasons. But to say to be competitive, you need to trade Jaylen Brown. I don’t ride with that because I just watched them be competitive and be top two, three seed in the East with Jaylen Brown at the helm and Jayson Tatum pretty much out the entire season. So to say that we need to be competitive and you just won a championship a couple years ago, I don’t buy that. I don’t but in order to be competitive.

item.title: Mavericks have emerged as leading suitor for Marcus Sasser via trade
item.description: Marc Stein: The Mavericks have emerged a leading suitor to acquire Detroit's Marcus Sasser via trade, league sources tell @TheSteinLine .

item.title: Blazers, Robert Williams III agree to three-year, $44 million contract extension
item.description: Shams Charania: Portland Trail Blazers center Robert Williams III has agreed to sign a three-year, $44 million contract extension to return to the Portland Trail Blazers through 2028-29, agent Kevin Bradbury of LIFT Sports Management tells ESPN.

item.title: Celtics actively making teams trade offers involving Jaylen Brown
item.description: Brian Windhorst on Jaylen Brown: When the pursuit of Giannis Antetokounmpo last week failed, the expectation was that the Celtics would have a bespoke trade conversation with Jaylen Brown, targeting a couple of teams where they like the possible trade. And it's not gone that way. The Celtics have ended up canvasing a large part of the league. In some cases, from what I am told, actually actively making offers involving Brown, not receiving offers. When you have a valuable asset, you usually sit

item.title: So, right now, I do think the Celtics are searching a …
item.description: Brian Wimdhorst on Jaylen Brown: So, right now, I do think the Celtics are searching a little bit, and that has left some uncertainty. It has even raised the possibility that they would retreat totally and try to repair the relationship with Jaylen Brown. There's one way to do it, which would be to give him a contract extension next month and a huge amount of money. That said, I do not think that situation is over. I still think they're looking, and I still think he could be traded.

item.title: Rockets expected to land Marcus Smart
item.description: The Houston Rockets have significant interest in free agent guard Marcus Smart and are expected to work out a deal in the three-year, $21 million range that would reunite him with head coach Ime Udoka, sources told Dallas Hoops Journal. Such a deal would fall within the taxpayer mid-level exception threshold.

item.title: Suns have a strong level of interest in Luke Kennard
item.description: Grant Afseth: The Phoenix Suns have a strong level of interest in Luke Kennard ahead of NBA free agency. Kennard averaged 8.4 points, 2.3 rebounds, and 2.2 assists last season, while shooting 47.8% from 3-point range.

item.title: On top of the full non-taxpayer MLE and a $20.3M TPE …
item.description: Marc Stein: On top of the full non-taxpayer MLE and a $20.3M TPE via the Anthony Davis trade, Dallas also generated a $6 million trade exception by trading away Jaden Hardy. Sasser's $5.2M salary for next season could slot into it.

item.title: Simone Fontecchio’s actual salary will be $2.8 million …
item.description: Ira Winderman: Simone Fontecchio’s actual salary will be $2.8 million for next season.

item.title: LeBron James informs Lakers they can move on without him because he will continue career elsewhere
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: LeBron James will continue his NBA career for the 2026-27 season and has informed the Los Angeles Lakers that the franchise can move on without him because he will play elsewhere, Klutch Sports CEO Rich Paul tells ESPN.

item.title: Houston is expected to extend an $8.01 million …
item.description: Houston is expected to extend an $8.01 million qualifying offer to Eason, according to a team source, who was granted anonymity to discuss deliberations still in progress. That move would officially make Eason a restricted free agent. Eason admitted that there were times last season when he struggled mentally with the scrutiny that came with playing a full season knowing his contract situation was unsettled. Unfortunately for him, there’s a chance he won’t be finalizing a new deal anytime soon a

item.title: The Rockets will need to shed payroll if they're going …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Rockets will need to shed payroll if they're going to sign Marcus Smart to a three-year deal with a portion of their mid-level exception and re-sign Tari Eason while staying under the first apron hard cap. Dorian Finney-Smith has been mentioned as a potential cap casualty.

item.title: Lakers told LeBron James they wanted him back, but he decided to move on himself: 'It's time to move on'
item.description: Shams Charania: The Lakers expressed to James that they wanted him back, but the NBA's all-time leading scorer decides to move on elsewhere.

item.title: A source close to LeBron James told ESPN, “it was time …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: A source close to LeBron James told ESPN, “it was time to move on.”

item.title: LeBron James has informed the Los Angeles Lakers that …
item.description: Chris Haynes: BREAKING: LeBron James has informed the Los Angeles Lakers that he will play elsewhere for the 2026-27 season, his agent Rich Paul tells me.

item.title: After eight seasons with the Lakers, including leading …
item.description: Shams Charania: After eight seasons with the Lakers, including leading the franchise to the 2020 NBA championship, James departs and enters free agency set to join a new team. Lakers president Rob Pelinka and Rich Paul spoke a short time ago about the four-time champion's plans, sources said. James chose to inform the Lakers well before start of free agency tonight out of courtesy and appreciation for their run together, and to allow LA to conduct its offseason business.

item.title: LeBron James has informed the Lakers he is leaving and …
item.description: Dan Woike: LeBron James has informed the Lakers he is leaving and will be playing elsewhere next season, sources close to James say. He has not yet decided where he’ll be playing next season and felt it was time to move on.

item.title: Overall Giannis getting to go to one of the places he …
item.description: Draymond Green: As it stands today, I don’t think it leaves Giannis in a much better position next year than he would’ve been with the Bucks. However, the long run I think definitely leaves them in a better position because you can build that team out. We saw even when LeBron and Chris Bosh went to the Miami Heat, that team looked a bit different the second year than the first year. Overall Giannis getting to go to one of the places he preferred to go to, I think it’s great.

item.title: Jalen Brunson says when he signed in New York, he …
item.description: Legion Hoops: Jalen Brunson says when he signed in New York, he always wanted to wear No. 13 to honor his favorite player, Steve Nash, but he wasn’t able to get it from Evan Fournier.

item.title: Warriors 'at the front of the line' in pursuit of LeBron James
item.description: Marc Stein: The Warriors are at the front of the line in pursuit of LeBron James in free agency and league sources tell @TheSteinLine that Golden State is not operating as though it has a mandate to also pursue an Anthony Davis trade.

item.title: Cavaliers have interest in second reunion with LeBron James
item.description: Chris Haynes: Cleveland Cavaliers have interest in a second reunion with LeBron James with the appeal of the franchise’s greatest player finishing his career where it all started, league sources tell me.

item.title: There was nothing percolating on LeBron front for …
item.description: Barry Jackson: There was nothing percolating on LeBron front for Miami when the Heat opted not to create full mid-level money in recent days. They could create full $15.1 mid-level money, if LeBron somewhat surprisingly decides to leave the West and come here, by attaching a draft pick to Jovic with hope of finding a taker.

item.title: Jock Landale to return to Hawks
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent center Jock Landale intends to sign a one-year, $14 million deal to return to the Atlanta Hawks, sources tell ESPN. Landale had a strong finish after being traded to the Hawks, averaging 10.6 points and 5.7 rebounds last season, and his agents Sammy Wloszczowski and Mike Lelchitski of SIG Sports finalized the deal Tuesday.

item.title: "I definitely think it's something to keep an eye on, …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "I definitely think it's something to keep an eye on, that's one to keep an eye on," - @WindhorstESPN on the Cavs potentially trading Max Strus.

item.title: "They are working on trades, I'm not going to tell you …
item.description: ESPN Cleveland: "They are working on trades, I'm not going to tell you who they're working on because I can't deal with it in my life," - @WindhorstESPN on the Cavs right now 🤣🤣🤣

item.title: LeBron James to Warriors not a done deal yet
item.description: Anthony Slater: Warriors decision makers have been given no indication at this hour that they are the firm LeBron James landing spot, per sources. There's a belief that they'll have plenty of appeal, but appears to be some level of recruitment required.

item.title: Golden State will certainly be pitching LeBron James …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Golden State will certainly be pitching LeBron James to join Stephen Curry and Draymond Green with the Warriors. From last night: "Golden State is known to be the league's most interested external suitor for the 41-year-old."

item.title: LeBron James to Miami has been discussed around the league as a possibility
item.description: Heat Central: REPORT: LeBron James has been linked to the Miami Heat after the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade, per @JakeLFischer “He’s been connected to Miami, a potential homecoming there once Giannis was traded to South Beach. There’s plenty talk around the league that they could see that reunion”

item.title: The Hawks will use nearly their entire $15 million …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Hawks will use nearly their entire $15 million mid-level exception to sign Jock Landale to this contract. His Non Bird rights are too low for him to receive this without the MLE. Going rate for premium reserve big men is $14 million annually between him and Robert Williams.

item.title: Cavaliers, Heat, Warriors expected to emerge as suitors for LeBron James
item.description: SportsCenter: I'm told this is a happiness-led decision for LeBron James. ... Wherever he lands in free agency, it will not be driven by money." "It's unclear what the timeline will be. Right now, he's in Ohio spending time with his family. He's gonna make a decision on his own time. But there's no question all of the NBA's contending teams are in the picture for LeBron James. Rich Paul will call LeBron with expressed interest as soon as free agency opens up tonight. Around the league, teams exp

item.title: “You look at teams he’s been too, you look at the …
item.description: Heat Central: “You look at teams he’s been too, you look at the Cavs, and you look at the Heat. He’s been in those places. He knows the terrain. I think it’s too soon to say anyone is the front runner” — @TheSteinLine on LeBron destinations

item.title: LeBron James, Luka Doncic had no personal issues with each other; James departure unrelated to Doncic
item.description: There might be an instinct here to think that Luka Doncic helped push LeBron James out the door, but league sources who know both, who were granted anonymity because they were not authorized to discuss the situation, insist the two players had no personal issues with one another. James, after all, went to the Lakers, and said Dončić should have his name introduced last in the starting lineups. And this summer, LeBron and Luka shared text messages about golf, a hobby they've both picked up in t

item.title: Thank you, LeBron 💜💛
item.description: Los Angeles Lakers: Thank you, LeBron 💜💛

item.title: The Lakers are expected to target Jalen Duren, Walker …
item.description: NBA Central: The Lakers are expected to target Jalen Duren, Walker Kessler, Peyton Watson, Mitchell Robinson, Sandro Mamukelashvili, Luke Kennard, and Rui Hachimura, per @ramonashelburne

item.title: Jock Landale’s new one-year, $14 million deal to …
item.description: Kelly Iko: Jock Landale’s new one-year, $14 million deal to return to Atlanta is fully guaranteed, sources tell @YahooSports .

item.title: Celtics unlikely to trade Jaylen Brown under current asking price
item.description: A quick word on Jaylen Brown, as his sticky situation continues to loom so large over this offseason. Barring a drastic and unforeseen change in the market, the Boston Celtics will have to significantly lower their asking price if they plan on trading him anytime soon.

item.title: League sources have been tying the Blazers to Brown …
item.description: League sources have been tying the Blazers to Brown for some time now, but all indications are that their interest is moderate, at best. And, again, nothing close to what the Celtics have been asking for, according to sources who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing deliberations.

item.title: While the Nuggets have certainly considered the Brown …
item.description: While the Nuggets have certainly considered the Brown option, they haven’t spoken to the Celtics since the weekend and their interest has been significantly overstated, according to team sources who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing negotiations. There’s a familiar obstacle, too, as the Nuggets simply don’t have the draft assets to satisfy the Celtics’ ask.

item.title: NBA Europe franchise bids coming in at at least $500 million
item.description: The latest bids for NBA Europe franchises were due Monday, and they came in at $500 million or higher in each of the 12 target cities, with multiple bids of more than $1 billion. Bids were made by more than 20 existing basketball and soccer clubs, including current EuroLeague teams, according to someone familiar with the details.

item.title: “We’re extremely encouraged by the final bids we …
item.description: For those first dozen teams, the group is speaking with bidders in Athens, Greece; Barcelona, Spain; Berlin; Istanbul; London; Lyon, France; Madrid, Spain; Manchester, England; Milan; Munich; Paris; and Rome. “We’re extremely encouraged by the final bids we received for permanent franchises,” Mark Tatum, NBA deputy commissioner, said in a statement. “This will be the biggest influx of capital European basketball has ever seen, and we have clear front-runners in each of our 12 target cities, incl

item.title: Cavaliers open to the idea of a LeBron James return
item.description: Sources tell cleveland.com that the Cavs are open to the idea of a LeBron James return. Of course, signing the franchise legend for one final stint isn’t exactly straightforward given their salary cap situation. Although cap-strapped Cleveland is currently $29.1 million below the first apron and $42.1 million below the second apron, those numbers don’t reflect the expected multiyear deal James Harden is expected to sign in the near future.

item.title: Max Strus, Dennis Schroeder on the move?
item.description: Since Cleveland’s season ended in a Game 4 loss to the New York Knicks in the Eastern Conference finals, sources say the team has had discussions about the possibility of trading Max Strus and Dennis Schroder. Strus is set to make $16.6 million in the final year of his contract. Schroder is still owed nearly $31 million over the next two seasons. Those moves would help Cleveland’s salary cap situation. If the Cavs get below the punitive aprons, they could also theoretically orchestrate a sign-an

item.title: Moritz Wagner on Nets' radar
item.description: They were reportedly interested in Mitchell Robinson but the Nets also are expected to show strong interest in Orlando’s Mo Wagner in free agency, per sources. Brooklyn obviously wouldn’t sign both Robinson and Wagner.

item.title: The Knicks plan entering free agency for second-round …
item.description: The Knicks plan entering free agency for second-round pick Jack Kayil is that he play next season overseas with his current club in Germany

item.title: Celtics planning to build a new arena in Boston?
item.description: The C’s have another decade on their lease at TD Garden, so Bill Chisholm has some time to decide. But not THAT much, given how long these projects typically take. And whispers are already out there. “They’ve been looking for a long time in Boston,” one political insider said. “Financially, it’s not working for them not to have their own stadium.” The latest rumored location? Outside South Station, on land currently occupied by the US Postal Service annex along Fort Point Channel.

item.title: Other sites beyond South Station are in the air as …
item.description: Other sites beyond South Station are in the air as well. One is the former Boston Flower Exchange in the South End, where The Abbey Group has approvals to develop four buildings. That property, however, is about a mile from four T stations, which might be tough on game days.

item.title: Heat summer roster does not include Keshad Johnson, …
item.description: Ira Winderman: Heat summer roster does not include Keshad Johnson, but does include Myron Gardner, Vlad Goldin, Jahmir Young and Trevor Keels. Of that group, only Gardner presently under contract to Heat. Former Pacer and Net Kendall Brown also on Heat summer roster.

item.title: Coach Corliss Williamson says Jayden Quaintance will …
item.description: Matthew Tynan: Update from Summer Spurs practice: Coach Corliss Williamson says Jayden Quaintance will be traveling with the team throughout Summer League but will not play. Carter Bryant will be playing, but only in Las Vegas.

item.title: Luka Doncic says goodbye to LeBron James: 'An honor to play with and learn from you'
item.description: Law Murray: From Luka Doncic to LeBron James

item.title: Magic Johnson: I want to thank LeBron James and his …
item.description: Magic Johnson: I want to thank LeBron James and his super agent Rich Paul for taking a meeting with me back in 2018 when I was President of the Lakers and deciding to sign with us. He created a whole new era for the Lakers, including leading us to an NBA Championship in 2020 and all the milestones he surpassed in a Lakers jersey. I’ll always love and be grateful for him and wish him all the best in the next phase of his life, on and off the court!

item.title: OG Anunoby's game-winning ball from Finals Game 4 returned to the Knicks, won't be up for auction
item.description: The ball that New York Knicks forward OG Anunoby tipped in to seal the biggest comeback in NBA Finals history and win Game 4 against the San Antonio Spurs was returned to the Knicks shortly before it was scheduled to hit the Sotheby’s auction block Tuesday.

item.title: “The game ball from Game 4 of the 2026 NBA Finals …
item.description: “The game ball from Game 4 of the 2026 NBA Finals holds significant importance to the New York Knicks, their fans, and the city, and will remain with the team as a lasting piece of franchise history. As such, it will not be included in Sotheby’s June 30 auction, which will continue to feature a curated selection of authentic jerseys and memorabilia from the series,” an NBA spokesperson said in a statement to The Athletic when asked why the ball was withdrawn.

item.title: In the absence of the ball, perhaps the standout piece …
item.description: In the absence of the ball, perhaps the standout piece in the auction is Finals MVP Jalen Brunson’s jersey from Game 1, in which he scored 30 points, including 13 in the fourth quarter to help the Knicks jump out to an early series lead. There were 40 bids on the Brunson jersey within the first hour of the auction opening, with a high bid of $110,000.

item.title: Tim Legler says the NBA has never once pushed back on …
item.description: Tim Legler says the NBA has never once pushed back on him for criticizing officiating during a broadcast. Legler was a guest on last week’s edition of the Awful Announcing Podcast and was asked directly whether he’s ever heard from the league after criticizing an official or a call. “No, not once,” he told host Brandon Contes. “They allowed us to have these conversations.”

item.title: “We’re respectful of the process. We’re respectful of …
item.description: “We’re respectful of the process. We’re respectful of the officials,” Legler said. “So at times, yeah, you’re going to comment on a no-call or the physicality overall in a game… I think we addressed it in a respectful way, and we’re on there to do that — but to respect the game, respect the participants — and we both feel that way. I think we were able to pull that off.”

item.title: The LA Clippers will be guaranteeing Kris Dunn’s …
item.description: Law Murray: As expected, the LA Clippers will be guaranteeing Kris Dunn’s contract for the 2026-27 NBA season

item.title: LeBron James to talk to several teams after free agency opens
item.description: Ramona Shelburne: LeBron James and/or his representatives will talk to a few teams after free agency opens in a few hours. A source close to the process says there's “no rush" for him to make a decision.

item.title: LeBron James on social media: Truly a honor to wear the purple and gold
item.description: LeBron James: No, THANK YOU! Truly a honor to wear the 💜💛 while trying to continuing the greatness & legacies that came before me! Hope I made a few proud during my stint. 🙏🏾🫡👑

item.title: Clippers, Raptors finalizing deal to send Kawhi Leonard back to Toronto
item.description: Shams Charania: BREAKING: The Los Angeles Clippers are nearing deal sending Kawhi Leonard to the Toronto Raptors for Brandon Ingram, Gradey Dick, 2 first-round picks, 1 pick swap and 2 second-rounders, sources tell ESPN. A return to Canada for the Raptors champion and two-time Finals MVP.

item.title: Lakers interested in Quentin Grimes
item.description: Marc Stein: Last night we reported the Lakers' rising interest in Toronto free agent Sandro Mamukelashvili. Now league sources tell @JakeLFischer and me that Philadelphia's Quentin Grimes is emerging as another top Lakers target. MORE Intel here: tinyurl.com/bde3f33k

item.title: Precious Achiuwa returns to Sacramento on a two-year deal
item.description: Chris Haynes: Free agent forward Precious Achiuwa has agreed on terms to re-sign with the Sacramento Kings on a two-year, $11.5M deal, league sources tell me.

item.title: Brandon Ingram and Gradey Dick plus picks for Kawhi …
item.description: Michael Grange: Not saying this is not going to happen, but per league sources, Brandon Ingram and Gradey Dick plus picks for Kawhi Leonard is not yet completed. Some details still TBD.

item.title: The Raptors luxury tax/apron situation is fluid due to …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Raptors luxury tax/apron situation is fluid due to Kawhi Leonard's $7.45 million trade bonus. He will need to waive most of it unless Raptors shed more payroll. They'd have enough room to fill their final four roster spots with minimum salaries if he waives it all.

item.title: Wizards shut down trade talks for Anthony Davis
item.description: Marc J. Spears: The Washington Wizards are not interested in trading 10-time NBA All-Star forward Anthony Davis and are not listening to trade offers for him, a source told @espn @andscape. Another NBA team exec says Wizards GM Will Dawkins is a huge fan of AD.

item.title: @clevelanddotcom that Cleveland has remained engaged …
item.description: Chris Fedor: Despite the LeBron James news and #Cavs being interested, sources tell @clevelanddotcom that Cleveland has remained engaged in conversations with free agent Dean Wade about the possibility of him returning.

item.title: Leonard has the 2026-27 season left on his contract at …
item.description: Leonard has the 2026-27 season left on his contract at $50.3 million. He had preferred to stay with the Clippers, but the franchise made no long-term commitments to him this offseason, sources said.

item.title: Familiarity with a Toronto organization that has the …
item.description: Three key reasons Leonard was open to this deal to Toronto, according to sources: Familiarity with a Toronto organization that has the majority of its front office structure in place, now led by executive vice president Bobby Webster. Leonard also likes the city of Toronto and believes the Raptors can contend in the Eastern Conference.

item.title: LeBron James wants Rich Paul to talk to any team interested in signing him
item.description: Dave McMenamin: LeBron James has instructed Rich Paul to talk to everyone around the league who is interested in him playing for them and come back to him with what the options are so he can make his decision, a source familiar with James' thinking told ESPN.

item.title: After taking time to decompress and undergo some …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: After taking time to decompress and undergo some self-assessment, LeBron James came to the conclusion that he wanted to continue playing “meaningful, competitive basketball,” a source familiar with James’ thinking told ESPN.

item.title: While James is committed to returning for a 24th …
item.description: Dave McMenamin: While James is committed to returning for a 24th season, he has not made any decision as to whether that will be his final season in the NBA, the source said.

item.title: James Harden holding off on signing new deal with Cavs to see if they can get LeBron James
item.description: On Monday, Harden declined his $42.3 million player option and is now a free agent. He is expected to return to Cleveland on a new contract, but Harden is waiting to sign to give the Cavs the flexibility they need to make a move like adding LeBron for next season, according to a league source who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing deliberations.

item.title: Cavs looking to move Max Strus
item.description: To that end, the Cavs are trying to trade forward Max Strus, league sources told The Athletic, so they could sign LeBron — or perhaps another player — into that midlevel exception. Strus is slated to make $16.6 million next season. If Harden signs for $30 million, and if Cleveland can deal Strus and take back no salary in return, it would be able to give James the midlevel exception.

item.title: Because of where Toronto is with regard to the first …
item.description: Bobby Marks: Because of where Toronto is with regard to the first apron, the Kawhi Leonard proposed trade will need to wait until July 6. Leonard also has a 15% trade bonus of his remaining salary. The bonus is valued at $7.5M and the Clippers are responsible to pay. He can amend the bonus down or eliminate.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard will work with Raptors to sign two-year extension
item.description: Chris Haynes: Kawhi Leonard and Toronto Raptors will soon engage to structure out the terms on a two-year extension to lock up the future Hall of Famer through the 2028-29 season, sources say.

item.title: Leonard's and Ingram's could take a minute
item.description: Michael Grange: Given the histories of the primary players involved, it's not surprising that the last hurdle to get a deal done is each team signing off on the medicals of the incoming player, per league source. Gradey Dick? Shouldn't be a problem. Leonard's and Ingram's could take a minute.

item.title: The Clippers have 9 first-round picks now, but that …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: Now we await the results of the Aspiration investigation. The Clippers have 9 first-round picks now, but that amount could be reduced if penalized. Between this and the Zubac trade, it makes sense to load up on first-round picks just in case.

item.title: High scoring average in a single postseason in Raptors …
item.description: Keerthika Uthayakumar: High scoring average in a single postseason in Raptors history: Kawhi Leonard - 30.5 in 2019 Vince Carter - 27.3 in 2001 RJ Barrett - 24.1 this year Scottie Barnes - 24.1 this year Chris Bosh - 24.0 in 2008

item.title: Lakers could potentially sign both Sandro …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Lakers could potentially sign both Sandro Mamukelashvili & Quentin Grimes while preserving a $41.25 million max slot. One could go into their surplus cap space (~$11 million, could create more via trade). The other could go into the $9.4 million room mid-level.

item.title: LeBron was a winner on and off the court, and Laker …
item.description: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar: @KingJames LeBron was a winner on and off the court, and Laker fan should appreciate his performance for the last 8 years. If LeBron goes home to Cleveland, it won’t matter if they win the world championship or not because it’s Hometown fans will get their opportunity to say their goodbyes to one of the greatest ever. And who knows one day he may become Mayor!! @Lakers @NBAcom

item.title: The NBA now will shift to finalizing agreements
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: NBA deputy commissioner Mark Tatum said the NBA has landed on clear frontrunners in 12 cities for NBA Europe after it received final bids for the league's launch. That list of includes "existing basketball and football clubs." The NBA now will shift to finalizing agreements.

item.title: Pistons plan to match any potential offer sheet Jalen Duren signs
item.description: Marc J. Spears: The Detroit Pistons have offered what the franchise believes is the most lucrative contract possible for their All-Star restricted free agent Jalen Duren, are not interested in any sign and trade deals and will match any potential offer sheet he signs, sources @espn @andscape .

item.title: National Basketball Association announced today that …
item.description: NBA Communications: The National Basketball Association announced today that the Salary Cap for the 2026-27 season has been set at $164.961 million. The Tax Level for the 2026-27 season is $200.428 million.

item.title: The 3 teams I mentioned (Heat, GSW, CLE) you can …
item.description: Heat Central: LeBron James main priority is going to a team that has a chance at winning, per @ChrisBHaynes "I'm told he's going to be open minded and patient for his next team.... The one component a team is going to have to have to secure his service from what i'm told, is the team has to be competitive and have a realistic chance at winning.... The 3 teams I mentioned (Heat, GSW, CLE) you can pretty much look and see of those 3 who has the best shot at winning... Money wasn't mentioned"

item.title: Timberwolves have interest in LeBron James?
item.description: Cleveland and Miami are often cited as realistic landing spots as well, and the Minnesota Timberwolves are known to have interest if he felt like shocking the basketball world with that sort of move.

item.title: The Warriors are now signaling that Davis isn't in …
item.description: The Warriors are now signaling that Davis isn't in their plans, according to league sources who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing deliberations. Conversely, the Washington Wizards, the club that employs Davis, is very against the prospect of trading him to the Warriors (or, for now, anywhere), those sources said.

item.title: Being told that barring a last minute change here, …
item.description: Jake Fischer: Being told that barring a last minute change here, Dean Wade will test the market in unrestricted free agency at 6 p.m ET, where Wade is expected to field interest from several playoff teams.

item.title: Bones Hyland returning to Minnesota
item.description: Jon Krawczynski: Bones Hyland has agreed to return to the Minnesota Timberwolves next season, sources told @TheAthletic. Hyland established himself as a rotation player, teaming with Ayo Dosunmu in the "Twin Turbos" backcourt.

item.title: DeAndre Jordan re-signs with Pelicans
item.description: Chris Haynes: Free agent center DeAndre Jordan is re-signing with the New Orleans Pelicans for what will be his 19th NBA season, league sources tell me.

item.title: Ousmane Dieng signs three-year deal with Bucks
item.description: Free agent forward Ousmane Dieng has agreed to a three-year, $17.5 million deal to return to the Milwaukee Bucks, sources tell ESPN. Dieng had a strong finish to last season after his trade from OKC, and now Bucks officials finalize the deal with agents Michael Tellem of Excel Sports Management and Marius Rutkauskas.

item.title: LeBron in no rush,
item.description: Barry Jackson: LeBron in no rush, per Shams. "What does happiness mean?" is the most existential question ever uttered on SportsCenter.

item.title: All of that, according to multiple NBA sources, is …
item.description: Is LeBron’s interest contingent on the Warriors acquiring his old Lakers teammate and fellow Paul client Anthony Davis, which would take the Warriors putting Jimmy Butler into any offer with the Wizards for salary-matching purposes? And would the Warriors have to commit to a long-term extension with Davis as part of this mammoth undertaking? All of that, according to multiple NBA sources, is mostly being assumed but has not been explicitly demanded by LeBron or Paul. And the Davis/Butler part of

item.title: But also, what better way for Paul to shake loose the …
item.description: Many league sources have long speculated that if and when LeBron and the Lakers soured on each other, the Warriors would be LeBron’s first choice, if he wanted to continue playing and certain conditions could be met. And I think Monday’s intrigue was an indication that he wants to continue playing, let’s put it that way. But also, what better way for Paul to shake loose the Lakers’ best offer than whispering that LeBron is extremely interested in wandering a bit north and joining up with Curry a

item.title: Those sources indicated many of the undisclosed bids …
item.description: The bidding for NBA Europe franchises ended on Tuesday, with sources saying a few of the various offers are from current EuroLeague teams, former Bucks owner Marc Lasry and existing soccer clubs hoping to launch first-time basketball franchises. Those sources indicated many of the undisclosed bids exceeded the NBA’s preferred range of $500M to $1B and that ownership groups from Athens, Barcelona, Berlin, Istanbul, London, Lyon, Madrid, Manchester, Milan, Munich, Paris and Rome all are in the run

item.title: But he was born in Marrakesh, Morocco, moved to the …
item.description: It is unknown which of those markets Lasry -- who sold the Bucks in 2023 -- is pursuing. But he was born in Marrakesh, Morocco, moved to the U.S. at the age of 7 and, according to sources, is likely not the only former NBA owner to bid for a franchise. Lasry was unavailable for comment.

item.title: The sense, according to sources, is that the winning …
item.description: The NBA’s BOG is scheduled to meet in mid-July, although it is unclear whether the governors will greenlight the franchises in just a matter of two weeks. The sense, according to sources, is that the winning bids will be announced on a rolling basis -- not all at once -- and that the NBA is still trying to sort out a potential EuroLeage partnership.

item.title: For instance, in the first decade of NBA Europe, the …
item.description: Sources said that since the NBA tweaked the league’s financial model in late April, the initial bids (due in late March) have grown by more than 100%. For instance, in the first decade of NBA Europe, the NBA expects to make over $10B in distributions, will invest over $3B right away to offset potential early-stage losses and that the 12 permanent franchises will earn money by Year 3.

item.title: Quentin Grimes to get $10 million annually?
item.description: ‪Adam Aaronson‬: Jake Fischer says on his B/R stream that he expects Quentin Grimes to sign a contract in the range of $10 million annually. www.youtube.com/watch?v=gqJC...

item.title: Bulls project having roughly $31M and the $9.4M room …
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Bulls project having roughly $31M and the $9.4M room exception as free agency begins. With Leonard Miller set to return and Nic Claxton set to arrive, Bulls are at 12 contracts and two of their three 2-way contracts

item.title: Utah Jazz center and restricted free agent Walker …
item.description: Utah Jazz center and restricted free agent Walker Kessler will reportedly meet with the Los Angeles Lakers as the NBA’s free agency window opens. Kessler was spotted arriving in Los Angeles ahead of his meeting with the Lakers’ brass. “I’m here,” Kessler said in a video published by TMZ Sports. The Lakers have more money to play with than previously expected. On Tuesday, it was announced that LeBron James would leave L.A. and join another team. That makes the Lakers — already in need of a center

item.title: “I did see that,” Kessler said when asked about James’ …
item.description: “I did see that,” Kessler said when asked about James’ decision to leave the Lakers on Tuesday. “Obviously, he’s one of the greatest players of all time, so he would have been great to play with, but we just gotta see what happens, man.”

item.title: Non-taxpayer's mid-level exception:
Exhibit 10 bonuses jumping to $91,000, guys who sign …
item.description: Cyro Asseo: Exhibit 10 bonuses jumping to $91,000, guys who sign E-10s, get cut and then play that season with their affiliate G League team for at least 60 days, will now earn $136,000. Not a bad salary to play in the G

item.title: LeBron was a winner on and off the court, and Laker …
item.description: Kareem Abdul-Jabbar: @KingJames LeBron was a winner on and off the court, and Laker fans should appreciate his performance for the last 8 years. If LeBron goes home to Cleveland, it won’t matter if they win the World Championship or not because his hometown fans will get their opportunity to say their goodbyes to one of the greatest ever. And who knows one day he may become Mayor of Cleveland !!

item.title: Lebron farewell tour in Ohio going to be popcorn
item.description: Dwight Howard: Lebron farewell tour in Ohio going to be popcorn 🍿

item.title: Chicago Bulls will fully guarantee the $2.41 million …
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Chicago Bulls will fully guarantee the $2.41 million salary of Leonard Miller for the 2026-27 season, league sources told @hoopshype. Miller averaged 11.7 points on 55.5% shooting from the field with 5.8 rebounds in 27 games, including 12 starts, for Chicago last season.

item.title: Little over $1M guaranteed.
item.description: K.C. Johnson: Little over $1M guaranteed. Bryson Graham didn’t commit to Jones when asked about his future at news conference introducing Caleb Wilson and Dailyn Swain. Bulls still shy of salary floor as FA set to begin.

item.title: Bulls will waive Kam Jones
item.description: Michael Scotto: The Chicago Bulls plan to waive Kam Jones, league sources told @hoopshype . Jones had a deadline today that would’ve fully guaranteed his salary at $2.15 million. He was the 38th pick in the 2025 NBA Draft by the Indiana Pacers and was traded to Chicago on draft night.

item.title: “They (warriors) are at the front of the line, but …
item.description: Heat Central: The Miami Heat have interest in LeBron James, per @TheSteinLine “They (warriors) are at the front of the line, but there is a line…. Cleveland is certainly going to try and convince him of a 3rd marriage, Miami has interest…. This might not necessarily happen tonight”

item.title: Shams: A dozen teams expected to pitch LeBron James
item.description: Fullcourtpass: There is expected to be a DOZEN teams that pitch LeBron James and Rich Paul is already listening to offers, per @ShamsCharania

item.title: Hardaway agreed to a one-year, $6.5 million deal with …
item.description: Shams Charania: Hardaway agreed to a one-year, $6.5 million deal with the Heat, sources said. Miami officials finalized the deal with Mark Bartelstein of @PrioritySports tonight.

item.title: Final numbers on Joel Embiid's three-year supermax …
item.description: ‪Adam Aaronson‬: Final numbers on Joel Embiid's three-year supermax extension: 2026-27: $57,736,350 2027-28: $62,355,258 2028-29: $66,974,166 (player option) Total: three years, $187,065,774

item.title: Tim Hardaway Jr signs with Miami
item.description: Chris Haynes: Free agent sharpshooter Tim Hardaway Jr. will be signing with the Miami Heat to provide the organization with the spacing needed for Giannis Antetokounmpo, league sources tell me.

item.title: Ousmane Dieng and the Milwaukee Bucks have agreed to a …
item.description: ‪Michael Scotto‬: Sources: Ousmane Dieng and the Milwaukee Bucks have agreed to a 3-year, $17.5 million deal, including a team option Year 3. Agent Michael Tellem and Marius Rutkauskas of Excel Sports Management did the deal. ESPN was first to report an agreement. HoopsHype first on a team option.

item.title: "This is a players' league. If you don't have buy-in …
item.description: Mike Brown: "This is a players' league. If you don't have buy-in from your players, you have no shot at coaching anybody else on the team. And these guys made my job easy. I love Josh. You need a guy that wears his emotions on his sleeve, and he does do that. There were a few times I went to him and I said, 'Hey, I'm going to let you do what the hell you want. You do anything you want. Just play hard. I'm going to tell you what I'm thinking. If you don't like it, you have one opportunity right t

item.title: NBA Cup championship game will be at Butler's Hinkle Fieldhouse in Indianapolis
item.description: The NBA is going back to college this coming season, bringing the NBA Cup championship game to Butler’s historic Hinkle Fieldhouse in Indianapolis. Hinkle — one of the oldest arenas in the country — is iconic, and more than fits the billing that NBA Commissioner Adam Silver and other league officials were looking for when he said the league was searching for “storied college arenas” to play host to the Cup final.

item.title: Heat using a portion of its midlevel exception on …
item.description: Anthony Chiang: Tim Hardaway Jr. agreed to a one-year, $6.5 million contract to join the Heat, a league source confirms. Heat using a portion of its midlevel exception on Hardaway.

item.title: Bucks are still $33.2M under the luxury tax with an …
item.description: ‪KeithSmithNBA‬: Bucks are still $33.2M under the luxury tax with an estimate plugged in for Ousmane Dieng. Roster spots are a bigger issue than spending power for Milwaukee right now, factoring in the trade with the Heat.

item.title: I very much enjoyed all our convos, whether about …
item.description: Justin Russo: Last thing on Kawhi Leonard: Covered him for six years and, throughout that, thought we at least had a pretty solid relationship, so it was fun to see him become more open over the years. I very much enjoyed all our convos, whether about basketball or life. Wish him all the best.

item.title: Kawhi Leonard to Raptors trade has been completed
item.description: Shams Charania Tweets‬: Kawhi Leonard to the Raptors has been agreed to, sources tell ESPN. The Clippers will receive unprotected first-round picks in 2031 and 2033, 2030 and 2033 second-round picks and a 2027 first-round swap.

item.title: They'll have to maximize these next few years with …
item.description: Josh Lewenberg: That changes the risk level for the Raptors to HIGH. Leonard will be 40 and 42 in 2031 and 2033. So, likely retired. They'll have to maximize these next few years with Kawhi and figure out a way to remain competitive post-Kawhi or those picks could be juicy. Raps are going for it

item.title: Clippers get the two most valuable first-round picks …
item.description: Yossi Gozlan: The Clippers get the two most valuable first-round picks the Raptors could offer and should move up around 10 spots in the 2027 Draft with that swap. Fantastic return for Kawhi Leonard.

item.title: Kelly Oubre expected to meet with Sixers, Pacers, Blazers, Lakers, others
item.description: Kelly Iko: Free agent wing Kelly Oubre Jr. plans to meet with the 76ers, Pacers, Blazers and Lakers among others, sources tell @YahooSports.

item.title: As I’ve said for months, I think Davis will be on the …
item.description: Are people in the Wizards organization laughing at these Anthony Davis to the Warriors rumors? — @Faazzla The Wizards have no interest in trading Anthony Davis. As I’ve said for months, I think Davis will be on the Wizards’ roster to begin the regular season.

item.title: And Hardaway agrees to terms with Heat. Miami zeroed …
item.description: Barry Jackson: And Hardaway agrees to terms with Heat. Miami zeroed in on Hardaway from the start. Played 80 games last season and 3rd in 6th man voting. Read all about him below.

item.title: LeBron James will be patient and open-minded when selecting next team
item.description: Chris Haynes: Sources: LeBron James will be patient and open minded in selecting his next destination but the priority is having a realistic chance at competing for a championship. He remains committed to playing at the highest level.

item.title: "You're going to be looking for guys on minimum deals …
item.description: Heat Central: Chris Haynes on what's next for the Miami Heat after signing Tim Hardaway Jr.: "You're going to be looking for guys on minimum deals who can be available on the cheap for 1 year deals where the benefit for them is they can come in and maybe have a really good season that contributes to a high success of winning, then hit the market the following summer to increase their value. The thing about Miami and Giannis, It's going to be an attractive place."

item.title: DeAndre Jordan's deal with the Pelicans is a fully …
item.description: ‪Shamit Dua‬: Per league sources, DeAndre Jordan's deal with the Pelicans is a fully guaranteed minimum

item.title: NBA had 7 teams taxpayers for 2025-26 season, while 23 …
item.description: Mike Vorkunov: NBA had 7 teams taxpayers for 2025-26 season, while 23 teams each got a $4.85 million payout. Cavaliers owe NBA-leading $68.671 million in tax payments. Warriors will pay $67.91 million as a repeater tax payer. Knicks owe $44.44 million after finishing $19.5 mil over tax line.

item.title: Western Kentucky’s Grant Newell will join the Chicago …
item.description: Jon Chepkevich: NEWS: Western Kentucky’s Grant Newell will join the Chicago Bulls for NBA Summer League, sources tell DraftExpress. The broad-framed, athletic 6’9”, 225 pound forward averaged 13 points and hit 58 threes for the Hilltoppers this season. Suiting up for his hometown team.

item.title: “Did you know LeBron James loves Charlotte?” McGee …
item.description: ESPN personality Ryan McGee -- who just so happens to have deep Carolina roots -- talked about James’ love for Bojangles during a 2024 appearance on the Pat McAfee Show. “Did you know LeBron James loves Charlotte?” McGee asked McAfee during that appearance. “And the reason he loves Charlotte is because Bojangles -- I’m not making this up -- Bojangles chicken. “When LeBron James was a rookie, the deal was he would leave games at the old Hive ... the takeaway food you get on the plane after a game

item.title: Branden Carlson signs with Blazers
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent 7-footer Branden Carlson has agreed to a one-year, $2.5 million deal to sign with the Portland Trail Blazers, sources tell ESPN. Portland picks up a big man who has developed in the OKC Thunder system over the last two seasons, finalizing a deal with @PrioritySports ' Ross Aroyo and Mark Bartelstein.

item.title: It’s why, according to league sources granted …
item.description: For the Lakers, that open-ended approach doesn’t work with the urgency they need to reconstruct the roster around Dončić and Reaves. For them, James is a massive cap number coming off the books, a pathway to fixing things. He’s no longer a part of the answer to their championship hopes. It’s why, according to league sources granted anonymity to discuss front-office strategy, James’ name hadn’t been mentioned much in the Lakers’ team-building conversations with outside parties. James, it seemed

item.title: Windhorst: If you want LeBron James attention, go out and make a move to improve your team
item.description: Heat Central: "If you want LeBron, you got to go make a move. I don't mean bringing LeBron in.... If you want LeBron James attention, make a move. Make a move to improve your team and then you get his attention" - @WindhorstESPN on Lebron 👀 If LeBron truly isn't worried about the money, the Heat CAN'T be ruled out.

item.title: Raptors sending unprotected first round picks in 2031 …
item.description: ‪Doug Smith‬: NBA sources: Raptors sending unprotected first round picks in 2031 and 2033, second round picks in 2030 and 2033 and the right to swap picks in 2027 to the Clips to finalize Leonard deal

item.title: "I didn't even think about that until you asked me …
item.description: Bennett Durando: First conversation I had with Tim last year, I asked him if any part of his reason for signing with Denver was the track record of guys getting paid more after playing with Jokic. "I didn't even think about that until you asked me that question."

item.title: Pistons have no interest in Domantas Sabonis
item.description: Duren, as The Athletic has reported, is a restricted free agent who is taking meetings with the Los Angeles Lakers and Sacramento Kings Tuesday with the hopes of finding a sign-and-trade pathway out of town. The gap in negotiations between Duren and the Pistons sparked this strategy, but it remains possible the two sides eventually strike a deal — especially considering the signals sent by the Pistons. Detroit does not have interest in the Kings’ scenario, which would send three-time All-Star Do

item.title: Pistons could lose Tobias Harris
item.description: The 33-year-old unrestricted free agent was the Pistons’ second-most productive player in the playoffs (behind Cade Cunningham), averaging 18.1 points and 7.2 rebounds. Yet Harris will be visiting with five teams at the start of free agency, according to a league source who spoke on condition of anonymity to describe ongoing negotiations. (It’s not clear whether the meetings are virtual, phone or in person, or which teams are in play). At minimum, that means the prospect of Detroit losing Harris

item.title: "Boston, I've heard all along, has this kind of …
item.description: Bobby Krivitsky: Jake Fischer on the Celtics and Jaylen Brown: "Boston, I've heard all along, has this kind of feeling in the back of their minds that they were already here in 2022." That's a reference to when the Celtics offered Brown in a deal for Kevin Durant before moving forward together. Fischer says what he initially heard was that Boston would only offer Brown for Giannis, but "the toothpaste is out of the tube." 🎥: NBA Free Agency Live, with Marc Stein and Jake Fischer on Bleacher Repo

item.title: Walker Kessler has multiple offers in the mid-to-high $30 million range annually
item.description: Tony Jones: Utah Jazz restricted free agent Walker Kessler has had meetings with a couple of teams and is headed to another meeting, league sources tell The Athletic. He holds multiple offers in the mid to high 30’s annually, along with structure of his choice, including player options

item.title: One of the teams with interest in Kessler, sources …
item.description: Dan Woike: One of the teams with interest in Kessler, sources tell me, is the Los Angeles Lakers.

item.title: “When I talk to teams, there’s a few that really stand …
item.description: Heat Central: “When I talk to teams, there’s a few that really stand out. The Miami Heat are definitely one of them. I expect the Heat to have interest. You have Giannis & Bam. You have history with LeBron. Warm weather. The market of Miami where he already won 2 championships. That will obviously come calling” — @ShamsCharania on LeBron 👀

item.title: Kobe Sanders agrees to four-year deal with Clippers
item.description: Shams Charania: Free agent forward Kobe Sanders has agreed to a four-year, $11.2 million deal to return to the Los Angeles Clippers, sources tell ESPN. A second round pick in 2025, Sanders averaged 7.3 points and 19.9 minutes over 68 games as a rookie.

item.title: Kobe Sanders: four-year deal, $11.3 million, two …
item.description: Law Murray: Kobe Sanders: four-year deal, $11.3 million, two guaranteed, two NG per team source

item.title: League sources told Sharania and ESPN's Anthony Slater …
item.description: League sources told Sharania and ESPN's Anthony Slater that a Warriors pursuit of Davis could be used as a motivational tactic to lure James to Golden State. The two won a championship together in 2020 with the Lakers and have remained close friends. However, there has yet to be tangible movement on the Davis front, league sources said.

item.title: Yaxel Lendeborg said he didn’t hear from Green, after …
